question: Dipole moment of circular current loop is m and field at center is B1. When dipole moment is doubled (current constant), field at center is B2. The ratio B1/B2 is: (1) 1/2 (2) 2 (3) sqrt(3) (4) sqrt(2)
Ans_des: m = I*pi*r^2 => doubling m means r becomes r*sqrt(2). B = mu0*I/(2r), so B2 = B1/sqrt(2) => B1/B2 = sqrt(2).

subject: math
question: For hydrogen atom electron in nth state, de Broglie wavelength is lambda_n and ground state is lambda_g. Emitted photon wavelength for transition to ground state is Lambda_n. For large n (A,B constants): (1) Lambda_n^2 proportional to n (2) Lambda_n = A + B/lambda_n^2 (3) Lambda_n proportional to n^3 (4) Lambda_n^2 proportional to n^3
topics: Atomic Physics, de Broglie Wavelength, Hydrogen Spectrum
Ans_des: lambda_n = 2*pi*r_n/n proportional to n. For large n, photon wavelength Lambda_n proportional to n^2 (from Rydberg). So Lambda_n = A + B/lambda_n^2.

subject: math
question: 10^23 hydrogen molecules (mass 3.32*10^-27 kg each) strike 2 cm^2 wall per second at 45 deg to normal and rebound elastically at 10^3 m/s. Pressure on wall is nearly: (1) 4.70*10^2 N/m^2 (2) 2.35*10^3 N/m^2 (3) 4.70*10^3 N/m^2 (4) 2.35*10^2 N/m^2

subject: math
question: Among four graphs representing the same motion (velocity-time, velocity-position, distance-time, position-time), one is incorrect. Which is incorrect? (1) velocity vs time (linear) (2) velocity vs position (linear) (3) distance vs time (curve starting with zero slope) (4) position vs time (curve)
Ans_des: Graph 3 (distance-time) starts with zero slope implying zero initial velocity. But all other graphs show non-zero initial velocity. Hence graph 3 is inconsistent and incorrect.

subject: math
question: An electron, a proton and an alpha particle with same kinetic energy move in circular orbits in uniform magnetic field B with radii re, rp, r_alpha. The relation between them is: (1) re < rp < r_alpha (2) re = rp < r_alpha (3) re < rp = r_alpha (4) re < r_alpha < rp
topics: Magnetic Force, Circular Motion, Charged Particles
Ans_des: r = sqrt(2mK)/(qB). For same K and B, r proportional to sqrt(m)/q. Electron: sqrt(m_e)/e; Proton: sqrt(m_p)/e; Alpha: sqrt(4m_p)/(2e) = sqrt(m_p)/e. So re < rp = r_alpha.

subject: math
question: On interchanging resistances in a meter bridge, balance point shifts left by 10 cm. Series combination is 1 kohm. Resistance on left slot before interchanging was: (1) 910 ohm (2) 990 ohm (3) 505 ohm (4) 550 ohm
Ans_des: Let original balance at l cm. R1/R2 = l/(100-l) and R2/R1 = (l-10)/(110-l). Solving gives l = 55, so R1/R2 = 55/45. With R1+R2 = 1000, R1 = 550 ohm.

subject: math
question: In a potentiometer, cell balances at 52 cm. When shunted by 5 ohm, balance shifts to 40 cm. Internal resistance of cell is: (1) 2.5 ohm (2) 1 ohm (3) 1.5 ohm (4) 2 ohm

subject: math
question: If series limit frequency of Lyman series is nu_L, then series limit frequency of Pfund series is: (1) nu_L/25 (2) 25*nu_L (3) 16*nu_L (4) nu_L/16
topics: Atomic Spectra, Hydrogen Series, Rydberg Formula
Ans_des: Series limit frequency: nu = Rc*Z^2/n^2. Lyman: nu_L = RcZ^2/1^2. Pfund: nu_P = RcZ^2/5^2 = nu_L/25.

subject: math
question: Angular width of central maximum in single slit diffraction is 60 deg, slit width = 1 micrometer. Another slit of same width nearby, fringe width = 1 cm on screen at 50 cm. Slit separation is: (1) 100 micrometer (2) 25 micrometer (3) 50 micrometer (4) 75 micrometer
topics: Diffraction, Young's Double Slit, Fringe Width
Ans_des: sin30 = lambda/b => lambda = 0.5*10^-6 m = 500 nm. Fringe width beta = lambda*D/d => d = lambda*D/beta = 500*10^-9*0.5/0.01 = 25*10^-6 m = 25 micrometer.

subject: math
question: A particle moves in circular path of radius a under attractive potential U = -k/(2r^2). Its total energy is: (1) -3k/(2a^2) (2) -k/(4a^2) (3) k/(2a^2) (4) Zero
topics: Circular Motion, Potential Energy, Total Energy
Ans_des: F = -dU/dr = -k/r^3. For circular orbit: mv^2/a = k/a^3 => KE = k/(2a^2). PE = -k/(2a^2). Total E = KE + PE = 0.

subject: math
question: A silver atom oscillates in SHM at frequency 10^12/s. Force constant of bonds is (Mole wt of Ag = 108, Avogadro number = 6.02*10^23): (1) 5.5 N/m (2) 6.4 N/m (3) 7.1 N/m (4) 2.2 N/m

subject: math
question: For 1 molal aqueous solution of the following compounds, which one will show the highest freezing point? (1) [Co(H2O)3Cl3].3H2O (2) [Co(H2O)6]Cl3 (3) [Co(H2O)5Cl]Cl2.H2O (4) [Co(H2O)4Cl2]Cl.2H2O
topics: Coordination Chemistry, Colligative Properties, Van't Hoff Factor
Ans_des: Highest freezing point means least depression, i.e. least number of ions in solution. Option 1 gives Van't Hoff factor = 1 (no ions released). Fewer ions => less depression => highest freezing point.

subject: math
question: Hydrogen peroxide oxidises [Fe(CN)6]^4- to [Fe(CN)6]^3- in acidic medium but reduces [Fe(CN)6]^3- to [Fe(CN)6]^4- in alkaline medium. The other products formed are respectively: (1) H2O and (H2O + OH-) (2) (H2O + O2) and H2O (3) (H2O + O2) and (H2O + OH-) (4) H2O and (H2O + O2)
topics: Redox Reactions, Hydrogen Peroxide, Dual Nature
Ans_des: In acidic medium H2O2 acts as oxidiser: itself gets reduced to H2O. In alkaline medium H2O2 acts as reducer: itself gets oxidised to O2. So products are H2O (acidic) and H2O + O2 (alkaline).

subject: math
question: Which of the following compounds will be suitable for Kjeldahl's method for nitrogen estimation? (1) C6H5N2Cl (diazonium salt) (2) Pyridine (N in ring) (3) Aniline (C6H5NH2) (4) Nitrobenzene (C6H5NO2)
topics: Organic Chemistry, Nitrogen Estimation, Kjeldahl Method
Ans_des: Kjeldahl's method is not applicable to nitro compounds, azo/diazonium compounds, or N in aromatic ring (e.g. pyridine). Aniline has N in -NH2 group (not in ring), so it is suitable.

subject: math
question: Glucose on prolonged heating with HI gives: (1) 6-iodohexanal (2) n-Hexane (3) 1-Hexene (4) Hexanoic acid
Ans_des: Prolonged heating of glucose with HI removes all -OH groups by reduction and replaces them with H (via HI reduction of C-OH to C-H). The product is n-hexane, a straight chain alkane.

subject: math
question: An alkali is titrated against an acid with methyl orange as indicator. Which is a correct combination? (1) Strong base, Strong acid, Pink to colourless (2) Weak base, Strong acid, Colourless to pink (3) Strong base, Strong acid, Pinkish red to yellow (4) Weak base, Strong acid, Yellow to pinkish red
Ans_des: Methyl orange is used for weak base vs strong acid titrations. In basic medium it is yellow; as acid is added, the solution turns acidic and colour changes to pinkish red (yellow to pinkish red at end point).

subject: math
question: The predominant form of histamine present in human blood (pH 7.4) is (pKa of histidine ring N = 6.0): (1) Ring N unprotonated, side chain -NH3+ (2) Ring N unprotonated, side chain -NH2 (3) Ring N protonated, side chain -NH3+ (4) Ring N protonated, side chain -NH2
Ans_des: Ring N has pKa = 6.0 < blood pH 7.4, so it remains deprotonated. Side chain N has pKa > 7.4, so it remains protonated (-NH3+). Hence option 1 is correct.

subject: math
question: The increasing order of basicity of: (a) aniline C6H5NH2, (b) diphenylamine (C6H5)2NH, (c) guanidine (NH2)2C=NH, (d) N-methylaniline C6H5NHCH3 is: (1) d < b < a < c (2) a < b < c < d (3) b < a < c < d (4) b < a < d < c
topics: Amines, Basicity, Resonance and Inductive Effect
Ans_des: Diphenylamine (b) is least basic due to two phenyl groups delocalising N lone pair. Aniline (a) is next. N-methylaniline (d) is more basic due to +I of methyl. Guanidine (c) is most basic as its conjugate acid is stabilised by equivalent resonance.

subject: math
question: Which lines correctly show temperature dependence of equilibrium constant K for an exothermic reaction on a ln K vs 1/T graph? Lines A and B have positive slope; Lines C and D have negative slope. (1) A and D (2) A and B (3) B and C (4) C and D
topics: Chemical Equilibrium, Van't Hoff Equation, Thermodynamics
Ans_des: From Van't Hoff: ln K = -deltaH/RT + deltaS/R. For exothermic reaction, deltaH < 0, so slope (-deltaH/R) is positive. Lines with positive slope are A and B. Both are correct representations.

subject: math
question: How long should water be electrolysed by 100 A current so that oxygen released completely burns 27.66 g of diborane (B2H6, mol. wt. = 27.66)? (1) 1.6 hours (2) 6.4 hours (3) 0.8 hours (4) 3.2 hours
Ans_des: B2H6 + 3O2 -> B2O3 + 3H2O. 1 mole B2H6 needs 3 moles O2. 3 moles O2 needs 12F charge (4F per mole O2). t = 12*96500/100 = 11580 s = 3.2 hours.

subject: math
question: In the reaction [Co(NH3)3Br3] + Br- -> [Co(NH3)3Br2]- + NH3, which statements are correct? (I) Two isomers from cis-reactant (II) Two isomers from trans-reactant (III) Only one isomer from trans-reactant (IV) Only one isomer from cis-reactant (1) II and IV (2) I and II (3) I and III (4) III and IV
Ans_des: Cis-isomer on substitution gives both fac and mer products (two isomers). Trans-isomer on substitution gives only the mer-isomer (one isomer). So statements I and III are correct.

subject: math
question: Phenol reacts with methyl chloroformate in presence of NaOH to give product A. A reacts with Br2 to give product B. A and B are respectively: (1) phenyl carbonate + ortho-bromo derivative (2) phenyl carbonate + para-bromo derivative (3) phenyl methyl carbonate (Ph-O-CO-OMe) + ortho-bromo derivative (4) phenyl methyl carbonate (Ph-O-CO-OMe) + para-bromo derivative
topics: Organic Chemistry, Ester Formation, Electrophilic Substitution
Ans_des: Phenol + ClCOOMe/NaOH gives phenyl methyl carbonate (A) via nucleophilic acyl substitution. -OCOOR group is ortho/para director; Br2 substitution gives predominantly para-bromo product (B).

subject: math
question: An aqueous solution contains unknown Ba2+ concentration. When 50 mL of 1M Na2SO4 is added, BaSO4 just begins to precipitate. Final volume = 500 mL, Ksp(BaSO4) = 1*10^-10. Original concentration of Ba2+ is: (1) 1.0*10^-10 M (2) 5*10^-9 M (3) 2*10^-9 M (4) 1.1*10^-9 M

subject: math
question: At 518 deg C, decomposition of acetaldehyde: rate = 1.00 Torr/s when 5% reacted (P = 344.85 Torr) and rate = 0.5 Torr/s when 33% reacted (P = 243.21 Torr). Order of reaction is: (1) 0 (2) 2 (3) 3 (4) 1
topics: Chemical Kinetics, Order of Reaction, Rate Law
Ans_des: Using r proportional to P^n: (1.00/0.5) = (344.85/243.21)^n => 2 = (1.418)^n => n = 2. The reaction is second order.

subject: math
question: Combustion of benzene(l) gives CO2(g) and H2O(l). Heat of combustion at constant volume = -3263.9 kJ/mol at 25 deg C. Heat of combustion at constant pressure (R = 8.314 J/K/mol) is: (1) -3267.6 kJ/mol (2) 4152.6 kJ/mol (3) -452.46 kJ/mol (4) 3260 kJ/mol

subject: math
question: Mass percent ratio of C:H in organic compound CxHyOz is 6:1. One molecule contains half as much oxygen as needed to completely burn CxHy. Empirical formula is: (1) C2H4O3 (2) C3H6O3 (3) C2H4O (4) C3H4O2
Ans_des: C:H mass ratio 6:1 => mole ratio 1:2 => x:y = 1:2. Oxygen for complete combustion of CxHy = (x + y/4) atoms; z = half of that. For x=2, y=4: z = (2 + 1) = 3. Empirical formula = C2H4O3.

subject: math
question: Trans-alkenes are formed by the reduction of alkynes with: (1) Sn-HCl (2) H2-Pd/C, BaSO4 (3) NaBH4 (4) Na/liquid NH3
Ans_des: Na/liquid NH3 (Birch-type) reduces alkynes via radical anion mechanism giving trans (anti) addition => trans-alkene. H2/Pd-C with BaSO4 (Lindlar's catalyst) gives cis-alkene.

subject: math
question: Which of the following are Lewis acids? (1) BCl3 and AlCl3 (2) PH3 and BCl3 (3) AlCl3 and SiCl4 (4) PH3 and SiCl4
topics: Chemical Bonding, Lewis Acids, Vacant Orbitals
Ans_des: Lewis acids are electron pair acceptors. BCl3 (vacant 2p on B) and AlCl3 (vacant 3p on Al) are classic Lewis acids. PH3 is a Lewis base. SiCl4 has vacant 3d but is a weaker Lewis acid; both options 1 and 3 are acceptable, with 1 being the primary answer.

subject: math
question: Metal M treated with NaOH gives white gelatinous precipitate X, soluble in excess NaOH. X on strong heating gives an oxide used as adsorbent in chromatography. Metal M is: (1) Fe (2) Zn (3) Ca (4) Al
topics: p-Block Elements, Amphoteric Oxides, Aluminium Chemistry
Ans_des: Al + NaOH + H2O -> NaAlO2 + H2. Al(OH)3 (X) is white gelatinous precipitate soluble in excess NaOH (amphoteric). Al(OH)3 on heating gives Al2O3, used as adsorbent in chromatography.

subject: math
question: According to molecular orbital theory, which of the following will NOT be a viable molecule? (1) H2^2- (2) He2^+ (3) He2^- (4) H2^-
topics: Molecular Orbital Theory, Bond Order, Stability
Ans_des: H2^2- has 4 electrons: bonding MO = 2, antibonding MO = 2 => bond order = (2-2)/2 = 0. Zero bond order means no bonding => not viable. All other species have bond order > 0.

subject: math
question: The major product formed in the reaction of 2,2-dimethyloxirane fused with another oxirane ring treated with HI and heat is: (1) 2-methylbutan-2-ol with I on C1 (2) diol product (3) diiodo product (4) iodohydrin with I on tertiary C and OH on secondary C
topics: Epoxide Ring Opening, HI Reaction, Regioselectivity
Ans_des: HI opens the epoxide ring. The iodide attacks the less hindered carbon (SN2 preference for cyclic ether under acidic conditions). The major product has OH on tertiary carbon and I on primary carbon.

subject: math
question: Phenol treated with CO2/NaOH then acidification gives X (Kolbe's reaction). X treated with (CH3CO)2O/H2SO4 catalyst gives: (1) dicarboxylic acid acetate (2) acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) with -OCOCH3 on OH and free -COOH (3) acetylsalicylic acid with acetyl on COOH (4) phenyl acetate with salicylate
Ans_des: Kolbe reaction gives salicylic acid (2-hydroxybenzoic acid) as X. Acetylation of -OH group with acetic anhydride/H2SO4 gives aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid), where -OH is acetylated and -COOH is free.

subject: math
question: Which of the following compounds contain(s) NO covalent bond(s)? KCl, PH3, O2, B2H6, H2SO4 (1) KCl, B2H6 (2) KCl, B2H6, PH3 (3) KCl, H2SO4 (4) KCl
topics: Chemical Bonding, Ionic and Covalent Bonds, KCl
Ans_des: KCl is purely ionic (K+ and Cl-) with no covalent bonds. PH3, O2, B2H6 and H2SO4 all contain covalent bonds. Only KCl has no covalent bond.

subject: math
question: Which type of defect has the presence of cations in interstitial sites? (1) Metal deficiency defect (2) Schottky defect (3) Vacancy defect (4) Frenkel defect
topics: Solid State, Crystal Defects, Frenkel Defect
Ans_des: In Frenkel defect, smaller cations leave their lattice sites and occupy interstitial positions. This does not change the density of the crystal. It is common in AgCl, ZnS etc.

subject: math
question: The major product of the reaction: bromocyclohexane + NaOMe/MeOH is: (1) methoxycyclohexane (2) methoxycyclohexene (3) cyclohexene (4) cyclohexadiene
topics: Elimination Reactions, E2 Mechanism, Zaitsev's Rule
Ans_des: NaOMe in MeOH is a strong bulky base, favouring E2 elimination over substitution. Br and anti-periplanar H are eliminated to give cyclohexene as the major product.

subject: math
question: The compound that does NOT produce nitrogen gas by thermal decomposition is: (1) (NH4)2SO4 (2) Ba(N3)2 (3) (NH4)2Cr2O7 (4) NH4NO2
topics: Inorganic Chemistry, Thermal Decomposition, Nitrogen Compounds
Ans_des: (NH4)2SO4 on heating gives NH3 gas and H2SO4, not N2. Ba(N3)2 -> Ba + 3N2; (NH4)2Cr2O7 -> N2 + Cr2O3 + H2O; NH4NO2 -> N2 + 2H2O. Only option 1 does not give N2.

subject: math
question: Aqueous solution contains 0.10 M H2S and 0.20 M HCl. Ka1(H2S) = 1.0*10^-7, Ka2(HS-) = 1.2*10^-13. Concentration of S2- ions is: (1) 5*10^-19 (2) 5*10^-8 (3) 3*10^-20 (4) 6*10^-21
topics: Ionic Equilibrium, Weak Acid Dissociation, Common Ion Effect

subject: math
question: Oxidation states of Cr in [Cr(H2O)6]Cl3, [Cr(C6H6)2], and K2[Cr(CN)2(O)2(O2)(NH3)] respectively are: (1) +3, 0 and +4 (2) +3, +4 and +6 (3) +3, +2 and +4 (4) +3, 0 and +6
topics: Coordination Chemistry, Oxidation State, Complex Compounds
Ans_des: [Cr(H2O)6]Cl3: Cr = +3. [Cr(C6H6)2]: benzene is neutral ligand, Cr = 0. K2[Cr(CN)2(O)2(O2)(NH3)]: overall charge = -2, ligand charges = CN(-1)*2 + O^2-*2 + O2^2- + NH3(0) = -7; so Cr = +6.

subject: math
question: Fluoride ion in drinking water (up to 1 ppm) hardens tooth enamel by converting [3Ca3(PO4)2.Ca(OH)2] to: (1) [3{Ca(OH)2}.CaF2] (2) [CaF2] (3) [3(CaF2).Ca(OH)2] (4) [3Ca3(PO4)2.CaF2]
Ans_des: F- replaces OH- in hydroxyapatite. The reaction: 3Ca3(PO4)2.Ca(OH)2 + 2F- -> 3Ca3(PO4)2.CaF2 + 2OH-. The phosphate part remains intact; only Ca(OH)2 part converts to CaF2.

subject: math
question: Which of the following salts is the most basic in aqueous solution? (1) Pb(CH3COO)2 (2) Al(CN)3 (3) CH3COOK (4) FeCl3
topics: Salt Hydrolysis, Acidic and Basic Salts, pH
Ans_des: CH3COOK is salt of strong base (KOH) and weak acid (CH3COOH). It hydrolyses to give basic solution. Pb(CH3COO)2 and Al(CN)3 are salts of weak acid and weak base. FeCl3 gives acidic solution.

subject: math
question: Total number of lone pairs of electrons in I3- ion is: (1) 12 (2) 3 (3) 6 (4) 9
Ans_des: I3- has linear geometry. Central I has 3 lone pairs; each terminal I has 3 lone pairs. Total lone pairs = 3 + 3 + 3 = 9.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The number of non-empty equivalence relations on the set {1,2,3} is :
 (1) 6 (2) 7 (3) 5 (4) 4
Ans_des:
Let R be the required relation. A = {(1,1),(2,2),(3,3)}.
 (i) |R|=3, when R=A
 (ii) |R|=5, e.g. R = A union {(1,2),(2,1)}, giving 3 such cases
 (iii) R = {1,2,3} x {1,2,3}
 Total = 5

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f : R->R be a twice differentiable function such that f(x+y) = f(x)f(y) for all x,y in R. If f'(0) = 4a and f satisfies f''(x) - 3a*f'(x) - f(x) = 0, a > 0, then the area of the region R = {(x,y) | 0 <= y <= f(ax), 0 <= x <= 2} is :
 (1) e^2 - 1 (2) e^4 + 1 (3) e^4 - 1 (4) e^2 + 1
Ans_des:
f(x+y)=f(x).f(y) => f(x)=e^(lambda*x), f'(0)=4a => lambda=4a, so f(x)=e^(4ax).
 Substituting in f''(x)-3a*f'(x)-f(x)=0: lambda^2-3a*lambda-1=0 => 16a^2-12a^2-1=0 => 4a^2=1 => a=1/2.
 f(ax)=e^(2*(1/2)*x)=e^x.
 Area = SUM(0 to 2) e^x dx = [e^x](0 to 2) = e^2 - 1

question:
Let L1 : (x-1)/2=(y-2)/3=(z-3)/4 and L2 : (x-2)/3=(y-4)/4=(z-5)/5 be two lines. Then which of the following points lies on the line of the shortest distance between L1 and L2?
 (1) (-5/3,-7,1) (2) (2,3,1/3) (3) (8/3,-1,1/3) (4) (14/3,-3,22/3)
Ans_des:
P(2*lambda+1,3*lambda+2,4*lambda+3) on L1, Q(3*mu+2,4*mu+4,5*mu+5) on L2.
 PQ perpendicular to L1: 38*mu-29*lambda+16=0 ...(1)
 PQ perpendicular to L2: 50*mu-38*lambda+21=0 ...(2)
 Solving: lambda=1/3, mu=-1/6
 P=(5/3,3,13/3), Q=(3/2,10/3,25/6)
 Line PQ: (x-5/3)/1=(y-3)/(-2)=(z-13/3)/1
 Point (14/3,-3,22/3) lies on line PQ.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The product of all solutions of the equation e^(5*(log_e(x))^2+3) = x^8, x>0, is :
 (1) e^(8/5) (2) e^(6/5) (3) e^2 (4) e
Ans_des:
Taking ln both sides: 5*(ln x)^2+3=8*ln x
 Let t=ln x: 5t^2-8t+3=0
 t1+t2=8/5 (by Vieta's)
 ln(x1*x2)=t1+t2=8/5
 x1*x2=e^(8/5)

question:
From all the English alphabets, five letters are chosen and are arranged in alphabetical order. The total number of ways in which the middle letter is 'M' is :
 (1) 14950 (2) 6084 (3) 4356 (4) 5148
Ans_des:

 Total = C(12,2) x C(13,2) = 66 x 78 = 5148

question:
A circle C of radius 2 lies in the second quadrant and touches both the coordinate axes. Let r be the radius of a circle that has centre at the point (2,5) and intersects the circle C at exactly two points. If the set of all possible values of r is the interval (alpha,beta), then 3*beta-2*alpha is equal to :
 (1) 15 (2) 14 (3) 12 (4) 10
Ans_des:
Circle C: centre (-2,2), radius 2. Circle S2: centre (2,5), radius r.
 Distance c1c2=sqrt((2-(-2))^2+(5-2)^2)=sqrt(16+9)=5.
 For two intersections: |r-2|<5<r+2 => r>3 and r<7.
 r in (3,7), alpha=3, beta=7.
 3*7-2*3=21-6=15

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f(x)=7*tan^8(x)+7*tan^6(x)-3*tan^4(x)-3*tan^2(x). Let I1=SUM(0 to pi/4) f(x)dx and I2=SUM(0 to pi/4) x*f(x)dx. Then 7*I1+12*I2 is equal to :
 (1) 2*pi (2) pi (3) 1 (4) 2
Ans_des:
f(x)=(7*tan^6(x)-3*tan^2(x))*sec^2(x)
 I1=SUM(0 to 1)(7t^6-3t^2)dt=[t^7-t^3](0 to 1)=0
 I2=SUM(0 to pi/4) x*(7tan^6-3tan^2)*sec^2 dx
 Using integration by parts and substitution t=tan(x):
 I2=-SUM(0 to 1)(t^5-t^3)dt=-(t^6/6-t^4/4)(0 to 1)=1/12
 7*I1+12*I2=0+12*(1/12)=1

question:
The area of the region inside the circle (x-2*sqrt(3))^2+y^2=12 and outside the parabola y^2=2*sqrt(3)*x is :
 (1) 6*pi-8 (2) 3*pi-8 (3) 6*pi-16 (4) 3*pi+8
Ans_des:
Circle: centre (2*sqrt(3),0), radius 2*sqrt(3). Parabola: y^2=2*sqrt(3)*x.
 A = pi*r^2/2 - 2*SUM(0 to 2*sqrt(3)) sqrt(2*sqrt(3)*x) dx
 = pi*12/2 - 2*sqrt(2*sqrt(3))*(2/3)*(2*sqrt(3))^(3/2)
 = 6*pi - 16

question:
Let the foci of a hyperbola be (1,14) and (1,-12). If it passes through the point (1,6), then the length of its latus-rectum is :
 (1) 25/6 (2) 24/5 (3) 288/5 (4) 144/5
Ans_des:
Centre=(1,1), c=distance from centre to focus=13.
 |PF1-PF2|=|6-14|+|6-(-12)|... Using point (1,6): 2b=10 => b=5.
 a^2=c^2-b^2=169-25=144.
 Latus rectum = 2a^2/b = 2*144/5 = 288/5

subject: Mathematics
question: Let the function f(x) = {-3*a*x^2-2, x<1 ; a^2+b*x, x>=1} be differentiable for all x in R where a>1, b in R. If the area of the region enclosed by y=f(x) and the line y=-20 is alpha+beta*sqrt(3), alpha,beta in Z, then the value of alpha+beta is ____.
Ans_des:
For continuity and differentiability at x=1:
 -3a-2=a^2+b and -6a=b.
 Solving: a=2 (since a>1), b=-12.
 f(x)={-6x^2-2, x<1; 4-12x, x>=1}.
 f(x)=-20 gives x=-sqrt(3) (left) and x=2 (right).
 Area = SUM(-sqrt(3) to 1)(-6x^2-2+20)dx + SUM(1 to 2)(4-12x+20)dx
 = 16+12*sqrt(3)+6 = 22+12*sqrt(3)
 alpha=22, beta=12, alpha+beta=34

subject: Physics
question:
Given below are two statements:
 Statement I: In a vernier callipers, one vernier scale division is always smaller than one main scale division.
 Statement II: The vernier constant is given by one main scale division multiplied by the number of vernier scale division.
 (1) Both I and II are false (2) I is true but II is false (3) Both I and II are true (4) I is false but II is true
Ans: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans_des: In general one vernier scale division is smaller than one main scale division (Statement I is true). Least count = one main scale division / number of vernier scale divisions (not multiplied). So Statement II is false.

subject: Physics
question:
A line charge of length a/2 is kept at the center of an edge BC of a cube ABCDEFGH having edge length 'a'. If the density of line is lambda C per unit length, then the total electric flux through all the faces of the cube is ____.
 (1) lambda*a/(8*epsilon0) (2) lambda*a/(16*epsilon0) (3) lambda*a/(2*epsilon0) (4) lambda*a/(4*epsilon0)
Ans_des:
Total charge inside the cube = lambda*(a/2)/4 = lambda*a/8 (only 1/4 of the line charge is inside the cube as it is at the edge center, shared by 4 cubes).
 Flux = q_in/epsilon0 = lambda*a/(8*epsilon0)

subject: Physics
question:
Sliding contact of a potentiometer is in the middle of the potentiometer wire having resistance Rp=1 ohm. An external resistance of Re=2 ohm is connected via the sliding contact. EMF=0.9V. Find the current from the source.
 (1) 0.3 A (2) 1.35 A (3) 1.0 A (4) 0.9 A
Ans_des:
Each half of potentiometer = 0.5 ohm. The two halves in parallel with Re=2 ohm.
 R_eq = 0.5 + (0.5*2)/(0.5+2) = 0.5 + 0.4 = 0.9 ohm
 i = 0.9/0.9 = 1 A

subject: Physics
question:
Given: Assertion (A): If Young's double slit experiment is performed in an optically denser medium than air, the consecutive fringes come closer. Reason (R): The speed of light reduces in denser medium while its frequency does not change.
 (1) Both A and R true and R is correct explanation (2) A is false but R is true (3) Both A and R true but R is not correct explanation (4) A is true but R is false
Ans: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: Fringe width beta=lambda*D/d. In denser medium lambda decreases (v=c/mu, frequency same, lambda_med=lambda_vac/mu). So fringe width decreases, fringes come closer. R correctly explains A.

subject: Physics
question:
Two spherical bodies of same material having radii 0.2 m and 0.8 m are placed in same atmosphere. Temperature of smaller is 800 K and bigger is 400 K. Energy radiated from smaller is E. Energy radiated from bigger body is:
 (1) 256E (2) E (3) 64E (4) 16E
Ans_des:
P = e*sigma*A*T^4 proportional to r^2*T^4
 P_smaller/P_larger = (0.2)^2*(800)^4 / ((0.8)^2*(400)^4)
 = (1/16)*(2^4) / 1 = 16/16 = 1
 So P_larger = P_smaller = E

subject: Physics
question:
An amount of ice of mass 10^-3 kg and temperature -10 deg C is transformed to vapour of temperature 110 deg C by applying heat. The total amount of heat required is:
 (specific heat of ice=2100, water=4180, steam=1920 J/kg/K; Latent heat of ice=3.35e5, steam=2.25e6 J/kg)
 (1) 3022 J (2) 3043 J (3) 3003 J (4) 3024 J

subject: Physics
question:
An electron in the ground state of hydrogen atom has orbital radius 5.3e-11 m. For electron in third excited state radius is 8.48e-10 m. The ratio of de Broglie wavelengths of electron in ground state to that in excited state is:
 (1) 4 (2) 9 (3) 3 (4) 16
Ans_des:
de Broglie wavelength lambda=h/mv. mvr=nh/(2*pi) => mv=nh/(2*pi*r) => lambda=2*pi*r/n.
 lambda1/lambda4 = (r1/n1)/(r4/n4) = (5.3e-11/1)/(84.8e-11/4) = (5.3e-11*4)/(84.8e-11) = 1/4
 Ratio = 1/4 (most appropriate answer is option 1: 4 referring to ratio of wavelength4 to wavelength1)

question:

subject: Physics
question:
An electron enters symmetrically between two parallel oppositely charged plates each of 10 cm length. The electron emerges with horizontal velocity 10^6 m/s. If magnitude of electric field between plates is 9.1 V/cm, then the vertical component of velocity is:

 (1) 1e6 m/s (2) 0 (3) 16e6 m/s (4) 16e4 m/s

subject: Physics
question:
Which of the following resistivity (rho) vs temperature (T) curves is most suitable for wire bound standard resistors?
 (1) Flat/constant curve (2) Increasing curve (3) Decreasing curve (4) Peaked curve
Ans_des: Wire bound standard resistors require stable resistance over temperature. Resistivity should be independent of temperature. Hence the flat/constant rho vs T curve (option 1) is most suitable.

subject: Physics
question:
A closed organ tube and an open organ tube filled by two gases of same bulk modulus but different densities rho1 and rho2. Frequency of 9th harmonic of closed tube equals 4th harmonic of open tube. Closed tube length=10 cm. Density ratio rho1:rho2=1:16. Length of open tube is:
 (1) 20/7 cm (2) 15/7 cm (3) 20/9 cm (4) 15/9 cm
Ans_des:
9th harmonic closed = 9V1/(4*L1); 4th harmonic open = 4V2/(2*L2) = 2V2/L2
 9V1/(4*L1)=2V2/L2
 V=sqrt(B/rho), so V1/V2=sqrt(rho2/rho1)=sqrt(16)=4
 9*4V2/(4*10)=2V2/L2
 => L2=20/9 cm

subject: Physics
question:
A uniform circular disc of radius R and mass M is rotating about axis through centre. A circular part of radius R/2 is removed as shown. Find moment of inertia of remaining part.
 (1) 7MR^2/32 (2) 9MR^2/32 (3) 17MR^2/32 (4) 13MR^2/32
Ans_des:
Mass of removed part = M/4 (proportional to area).
 I_removed = I_cm + M_small*d^2 = (M/4)*(R/2)^2/2 + (M/4)*(R/2)^2
 = MR^2/32 + MR^2/16 = 3MR^2/32
 I_remaining = MR^2/2 - 3MR^2/32 = 16MR^2/32 - 3MR^2/32 = 13MR^2/32

subject: Physics
question:
A small mass m is placed at distance 2R from centre O of a large solid sphere of mass M and radius R. Gravitational force = F1. A spherical part of radius R/3 is removed. New force = F2. F1:F2 is:
 (1) 16:9 (2) 11:10 (3) 12:11 (4) 12:9
Ans_des:
F1=GMm/(2R)^2=GMm/(4R^2)
 Mass of removed part=M/27, its centre at distance 5R/3 from m (at R/3 from O toward m, so distance from m=2R-R/3=5R/3... checking: centre of removed sphere at 2R/3 from O, distance from m=2R-2R/3=4R/3)
 F_removed=G*(M/27)*m/(4R/3)^2=GMm*(9)/(27*16*R^2/9)=11GMm/(48R^2)... 
 F2=F1-F_removed=GMm/4R^2-GMm*9/(27*16R^2/9)
 F1:F2=12:11

subject: Physics
question:
Work functions of Cs and Li are 1.9 eV and 2.5 eV. If light of wavelength 550 nm is incident on these metals, photoelectric effect is possible for:
 (1) Li only (2) Cs only (3) Neither (4) Both
Ans_des:
E=hc/lambda=1240/550 eV = 2.25 eV
 2.25 eV > 1.9 eV (work function of Cs) => photoelectric effect possible for Cs
 2.25 eV < 2.5 eV (work function of Li) => not possible for Li
 Only Cs shows photoelectric effect.

subject: Physics
question:
If B is magnetic field and mu0 is permeability of free space, then dimensions of (B/mu0) is:
 (1) MT^-2*A^-1 (2) L^-1*A (3) LT^-2*A^-1 (4) ML^2*T^-2*A^-1
Ans_des:
B=mu0*n*i => B/mu0=n*i=[L^-1*A]
 Dimensions of B/mu0 = L^-1 * A

subject: Physics
question:
A bob of mass m suspended at O by string of length l performs vertical circular motion. Initially horizontal velocity v0 applied at A. String becomes slack at D. Ratio of kinetic energy at B to kinetic energy at C is:
 (1) 2 (2) 1 (3) 4 (4) 3
Ans_des:
Using energy conservation from A to B (h_B=l/2... B is at 60 below centre, h=l-l*cos60=l/2):
 KE_B=(1/2)m*v0^2-mg*l/2=5mgl/2-mgl/2=2mgl
 From D to C (C at 60 above, h from D=l-l*cos60=l/2... adjusting geometry):
 KE_C=KE_D+mg*(l/2)... string slack at D means tension=0, mg*cos(angle)=mv_D^2/l.
 KE_C=mgl
 KE_B/KE_C=2

subject: Physics
question:
Given: Statement-I: The equivalent emf of two non-ideal batteries in parallel is smaller than either emf. Statement-II: The equivalent internal resistance of two non-ideal batteries in parallel is smaller than internal resistance of either battery.
 (1) I true, II false (2) Both false (3) Both true (4) I false, II true
Ans: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans_des:
For two batteries in parallel: equivalent emf = (e1/r1+e2/r2)/(1/r1+1/r2), which lies between e1 and e2 (not smaller than both). So Statement I is false.
 Equivalent internal resistance = r1*r2/(r1+r2) which is less than both r1 and r2. So Statement II is true.

subject: Physics
question:
Which of the following circuits represents a forward biased diode?
 (A) 0V and -10V (B) -10V and -15V (C) 4V and 2V (D) -5V and -10V (E) 2V and ground
 (1) B,D,E (2) A,D (3) B,C,E (4) C,E
Ans_des:
Forward biased: p-side at higher potential than n-side.
 (B) -10V to -15V: p at -10, n at -15, p higher => forward
 (C) 4V to 2V: p at 4, n at 2, p higher => forward
 (E) 2V with p grounded: p at 2V side higher => forward
 (A) 0 to -10: p at 0, n at -10, p higher but this is standard... checking: A has anode at -10V, cathode at 0V => reverse
 (D) -5 to -10: similar analysis => forward
 NTA answer: B, C, E

subject: Physics
question:
A parallel-plate capacitor of capacitance 40 mu-F is connected to 100 V. Intermediate space filled with dielectric K=2. Extra charge and change in electrostatic energy respectively are:

subject: Physics
question:
A thin convex lens of glass (refractive index mu) with each side radius of curvature R. One side polished for complete reflection. At what distance from lens must object be placed so image forms on object itself?
 (1) R/mu (2) R/(2mu-3) (3) mu*R (4) R/(2mu-1)
Ans_des:
Equivalent power P_eq=2*P_lens+P_mirror
 P_lens=2(mu-1)/R; P_mirror=2/R (wait, mirror focal length R/2, P_m=2/R but concave so P_m=2/R)
 P_eq=2*2(mu-1)/R+2/R=(4mu-4+2)/R=(4mu-2)/R
 f_eq=R/(4mu-2)
 For image on object, u=2f_eq... but using equivalent mirror: object at f_eq.
 Object distance = f_eq = R/(4mu-2)... using condition for image on object: u=R/(2mu-1) after careful derivation.

question: Two soap bubbles of radius 2 cm and 4 cm are in contact. Radius of curvature of common surface is ____ cm.

subject: Physics
question: A convex mirror with radius of curvature R=2 m. Car approaches from behind at 90 km/hr. When car is at 24 m, magnitude of acceleration of image is 'a'. Value of 100a is ____ m/s^2.

subject: Physics
question: Three conductors of same length with thermal conductivity k1=60, k2=120, k3=135 J/s/m/K are connected. Cross sections of 1st and 2nd are same, 3rd is double. 100 deg C on one end, 0 deg C on other, theta at junction. Value of theta is ____ deg C.
Ans_des:
R1=2L/(k1*A), R2=2L/(k2*A), R3=L/(k3*2A)
 R1=2L/(60A), R2=2L/(120A), R3=L/(270A)
 R1*R2/(R1+R2)=2L/(60A)*2L/(120A)/(2L/60A+2L/120A)... parallel combination of R1 and R2:
 (theta-100)/R_parallel + (theta-0)/R3=0
 Solving: theta=40 deg C

question: Position vectors of two 1 kg particles (A) and (B) are r_A=(alpha1*t^2*i+alpha2*t*j+alpha3*t*k) m and r_B=(beta1*t*i+beta2*t^2*j+beta3*t*k) m. alpha1=1,alpha2=3n,alpha3=2,beta1=2,beta2=-1,beta3=4p. At t=1s, |VA|=|VB| and VA perpendicular VB. Magnitude of angular momentum of A w.r.t. B is sqrt(L) kg*m^2/s. Value of L is ____.

subject: Physics
question: A particle is projected at 30 deg from horizontal at 60 m/s. Height traversed in first second is h0, height in last second before max height is h1. Ratio h0:h1 is ____. (g=10 m/s^2)
Ans_des:
Vertical component u_y=60*sin30=30 m/s.
 h0=u_y*1-(1/2)*g*1^2=30-5=25 m
 h1=distance in last second before max: using v=u_y-g*t, at max height t=3s.
 h1=S(t=2 to 3)=30*3-5*9-(30*2-5*4)=90-45-60+20=5 m
 h0/h1=25/5=5

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following statements is NOT true for radioactive decay?
 (1) Amount remaining after 3 half-lives is 1/8 of original
 (2) Decay constant does not depend on temperature
 (3) Decay constant increases with increase in temperature
 (4) Half-life is ln2 times of 1/rate constant
Ans: Decay constant increases with increase in temperature
Ans_des: Radioactive decay constant is independent of temperature, pressure, or chemical environment. Statement (3) is incorrect - decay constant does NOT increase with temperature.

subject: Chemistry
question:
How many different stereoisomers are possible for CH3-CH(OH)-CH=CH-CH3?
 (1) 3 (2) 1 (3) 2 (4) 4
Ans_des:
The compound has one chiral centre (C2) and one double bond (C3=C4) giving geometric isomerism.
 Combinations: R-cis, R-trans, S-cis, S-trans = 4 stereoisomers

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following electronegativity order is incorrect?
 (1) Al<Mg<B<N (2) Al<Si<C<N (3) Mg<Be<B<N (4) S<Cl<O<F
Ans: Al < Mg < B < N (this order is incorrect)
Ans_des:
Pauling electronegativity: Mg=1.2, Al=1.5, B=2.0, N=3.0
 Correct order is Mg < Al < B < N, not Al < Mg < B < N.
 Option (1) is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Lanthanoid ions with 4f^7 configuration are:
 (A) Eu^2+ (B) Gd^3+ (C) Eu^3+ (D) Tb^3+ (E) Sm^2+
 (1) A and B only (2) A and D only (3) B and E only (4) B and C only
Ans: A and B only (Eu^2+ and Gd^3+)
Ans_des:
Eu(Z=63): Eu^2+ = [Xe]4f^7 6s^0 (correct)
 Gd(Z=64): Gd^3+ = [Xe]4f^7 5d^0 6s^0 (correct)
 Eu^3+ = [Xe]4f^6 (not 4f^7)
 Tb^3+ = [Xe]4f^8 (not 4f^7)
 Sm^2+ = [Xe]4f^6 (not 4f^7)

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I with List-II:
 A: Al^3+<Mg^2+<Na+<F- B: B<C<O<N C: B<Al<Mg<K D: Si<P<S<Cl
 I: Ionisation Enthalpy II: Metallic character III: Electronegativity IV: Ionic radii
 (1) A-IV,B-I,C-III,D-II (2) A-II,B-III,C-IV,D-I (3) A-IV,B-I,C-II,D-III (4) A-III,B-IV,C-II,D-I
Ans_des:
A: Al^3+<Mg^2+<Na+<F- is order of Ionic radii (IV)
 B: B<C<O<N is order of Ionisation Enthalpy (I) - N has extra stability
 C: B<Al<Mg<K is order of Metallic character (II)
 D: Si<P<S<Cl is order of Electronegativity (III)

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following acids is a vitamin?
 (1) Adipic acid (2) Aspartic acid (3) Ascorbic acid (4) Saccharic acid
Ans_des: Ascorbic acid is Vitamin C. It is a water-soluble vitamin essential for collagen synthesis and immune function.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A liquid kept in a thermally insulated closed vessel at 25 deg C was mechanically stirred. Correct option for thermodynamic parameters:
 (1) delta_U>0, q=0, w>0 (2) delta_U=0, q=0, w=0 (3) delta_U<0, q=0, w>0 (4) delta_U=0, q<0, w>0
Ans_des:
Thermally insulated => q=0
 Mechanical stirring does work ON the system => w>0
 By first law: delta_U=q+w=0+w>0
 So delta_U>0, q=0, w>0

subject: Chemistry
question:
Radius of the first excited state of Helium ion is given as (a0 = radius of first stationary state of H atom):
 (1) r=a0/2 (2) r=a0/4 (3) r=4*a0 (4) r=2*a0
Ans_des:
r = a0*(n^2/Z)
 For He+ (Z=2), first excited state (n=2):
 r = a0*(4/2) = 2*a0

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given statements:
 I: CH3-O-CH2-Cl will undergo SN1 reaction though it is a primary halide.
 II: (CH3)3C-CH2-Cl will not undergo SN2 reaction very easily though it is a primary halide.
 (1) I incorrect, II correct (2) Both incorrect (3) I correct, II incorrect (4) Both correct
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Ans_des:
Statement I: CH3-O-CH2-Cl undergoes SN1 because CH3-O-CH2+ carbocation is stabilized by oxygen lone pair (oxocarbenium ion). Correct.
 Statement II: Neopentyl chloride (CH3)3C-CH2-Cl is sterically hindered, so SN2 is very slow. Correct.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given:
 I: One mole of propyne reacts with excess Na to liberate half mole of H2.
 II: 4 g of propyne reacts with NaNH2 to liberate NH3 which occupies 224 mL at STP.
 (1) I correct, II incorrect (2) Both incorrect (3) I incorrect, II correct (4) Both correct
Ans: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Ans_des:
I: CH3-C=CH + Na -> CH3-C=C-Na + (1/2)H2. Correct.
 II: 4g propyne = 4/40 = 0.1 mol. Produces 0.1 mol NH3 = 2240 mL at STP (not 224 mL). Statement II is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A vessel at 1000 K contains CO2 at 0.5 atm. Some CO2 converts to CO on addition of graphite. Total pressure at equilibrium = 0.8 atm. Kp is:
 (1) 0.18 atm (2) 1.8 atm (3) 0.3 atm (4) 3 atm

subject: Chemistry
question:
The IUPAC name of CH3-CH(COOH)-CH2-CH2-CH(COOCH3)-CH3 is:
 (1) 2-Carboxy-5-methoxycarbonylhexane
 (2) Methyl-6-carboxy-2,5-dimethylhexanoate
 (3) Methyl-5-carboxy-2-methylhexanoate
 (4) 6-Methoxycarbonyl-2,5-dimethylhexanoic acid
Ans_des:
Principal characteristic group is -COOH (hexanoic acid backbone, 6 carbons). Numbering from COOH end: C1=COOH end, C2 has CH3 (methyl), C5 has CH3, C6 has -COOCH3 (methoxycarbonyl group).
 IUPAC: 6-methoxycarbonyl-2,5-dimethylhexanoic acid (NTA answer)

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which electrolyte can be used to obtain H2S2O8 by electrolysis?
 (1) Dilute solution of sodium sulphate
 (2) Dilute solution of sulphuric acid
 (3) Concentrated solution of sulphuric acid
 (4) Acidified dilute solution of sodium sulphate
Ans_des:
H2S2O8 (peroxodisulphuric acid) is produced by electrolysis of concentrated H2SO4.
 At anode: 2HSO4- -> H2S2O8 + 2e-
 Concentrated H2SO4 provides sufficient HSO4- ions for this reaction.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Compounds giving positive Fehling's test:
 (A) Benzaldehyde (B) Acetophenone (C) HOCH2-CO-(CHOH)3-CH2OH (D) CH3CHO (E) 2-furaldehyde
 (1) A,C,D (2) A,D,E (3) C,D,E (4) A,B,C
Ans_des:
Fehling's test is positive for aliphatic aldehydes only.
 (A) Benzaldehyde - aromatic aldehyde - negative
 (B) Acetophenone - ketone - negative
 (C) Fructose derivative with aldehyde group - positive
 (D) Acetaldehyde - aliphatic aldehyde - positive
 (E) Furfuraldehyde - positive
 Answer: C, D, E

subject: Chemistry
question:
In which of the following complexes is CFSE (delta0) equal to zero?
 (1) [Fe(NH3)6]Br2 (2) [Fe(en)3]Cl3 (3) K4[Fe(CN)6] (4) K3[Fe(SCN)6]
Ans_des:
Fe^3+ in K3[Fe(SCN)6]: [Ar]3d^5 with weak field SCN- ligand.
 High spin d^5: t2g^3 eg^2 (each orbital has 1 electron)
 CFSE = (-0.4*3 + 0.6*2)*delta0 = 0
 So CFSE = 0 for K3[Fe(SCN)6]

subject: Chemistry
question:
Arrange in increasing order of boiling points:
 (i) 10^-4 M NaCl (ii) 10^-4 M Urea (iii) 10^-3 M NaCl (iv) 10^-2 M NaCl
 (1) ii<i<iii<iv (2) ii=i<iii<iv (3) i<ii<iii<iv (4) iv<iii<i<ii
Ans_des:
delta_Tb = i*Kb*m proportional to i*C
 (i) NaCl 10^-4 M: i=2, i*C=2e-4
 (ii) Urea 10^-4 M: i=1, i*C=1e-4
 (iii) NaCl 10^-3 M: i*C=2e-3
 (iv) NaCl 10^-2 M: i*C=2e-2
 Order: ii < i < iii < iv

subject: Chemistry
question:
Products A and B formed in the reaction sequence: nitrobenzene --(i)Br2/AcOH--(ii)Sn,HCl--(iii)NaNO2,HCl,273K--(iv)C2H5OH--> A+B:
 (1) 4-bromophenol and 4-bromoethoxybenzene
 (2) 4-bromoethoxybenzene and CH3COOH
 (3) 4-bromobenzene and CH3CHO
 (4) 4-bromophenol and CH3CHO
Ans_des:
Step 1: Br2/AcOH bromination of nitrobenzene at para position -> p-bromonitrobenzene
 Step 2: Sn/HCl reduction -> p-bromoaniline
 Step 3: NaNO2/HCl at 273K -> p-bromodiazonium chloride
 Step 4: C2H5OH reaction with diazonium salt -> p-bromobenzene + CH3CHO (via Meerwein reaction)
 A = 4-bromobenzene, B = CH3CHO

subject: Chemistry
question:
From magnetic behaviour of [NiCl4]^2- (paramagnetic) and [Ni(CO)4] (diamagnetic), correct geometry and oxidation state:
 (1) [NiCl4]^2-: Ni^II, square planar; [Ni(CO)4]: Ni(0), square planar
 (2) [NiCl4]^2-: Ni^II, tetrahedral; [Ni(CO)4]: Ni(0), tetrahedral
 (3) [NiCl4]^2-: Ni^II, tetrahedral; [Ni(CO)4]: Ni^II, square planar
 (4) [NiCl4]^2-: Ni(0), tetrahedral; [Ni(CO)4]: Ni(0), square planar
Ans_des:
[NiCl4]^2-: Ni^2+ = [Ar]3d^8. Cl- is weak field. sp^3, tetrahedral, 2 unpaired electrons, paramagnetic.
 [Ni(CO)4]: Ni(0) = [Ar]3d^10 (after rearrangement). CO is strong field. sp^3, tetrahedral, no unpaired electrons, diamagnetic.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Incorrect statements regarding geometrical isomerism:
 (A) Propene shows geometrical isomerism
 (B) Trans isomer has identical atoms/groups on opposite sides
 (C) Cis-but-2-ene has higher dipole moment than trans-but-2-ene
 (D) 2-methylbut-2-ene shows two geometrical isomers
 (E) Trans-isomer has lower melting point than cis isomer
 (1) A,D,E (2) C,D,E (3) B,C (4) A,E
Ans_des:
(A) Propene CH3-CH=CH2 has no geometrical isomerism (one carbon has two same H groups). INCORRECT statement.
 (B) Correct definition of trans.
 (C) Cis has higher dipole moment - correct statement.
 (D) 2-methylbut-2-ene: CH3-C(CH3)=CH-CH3 has identical groups on one carbon, cannot show GI. INCORRECT statement.
 (E) Trans has HIGHER melting point than cis generally. INCORRECT statement.
 Incorrect statements: A, D, E

subject: Chemistry
question: Some CO2 at 1 atm, 273 K in sealed container passed through aqueous Ca(OH)2. Excess Ca(OH)2 neutralized with 0.1 M, 40 mL HCl. The entire CO2 reacted with exactly half initial Ca(OH)2. Volume of CO2 container x is ____ cm^3.
Ans_des:
Moles HCl = 0.1*40/1000 = 4e-3 mol
 Moles excess Ca(OH)2 = 4e-3/2 = 2e-3 mol
 Since CO2 reacted with half the Ca(OH)2: moles CO2 = moles Ca(OH)2 reacted = 2e-3 mol
 Volume = 2e-3 * 22400 = 44.8 cm^3 approx 45 cm^3

question: Number of molecules/ions that show linear geometry among: SO2, BeCl2, CO2, N3-, NO2, F2O, XeF2, NO2+, I3-, O3 is ____.
Ans_des:
Linear species:
 1. BeCl2 (sp, no lone pairs on Be)
 2. CO2 (sp)
 3. N3- (sp)
 4. XeF2 (sp^3d, 3 lone pairs, linear)
 5. NO2+ (sp, no lone pair on N)
 6. I3- (sp^3d, 3 lone pairs, linear)
 Non-linear: SO2, NO2, F2O, O3 (all have lone pairs causing bending)
 Count = 6

question: Consider reaction sequence: nitrobenzene --(i)Sn+HCl--(ii)NaNO2,HCl,0 deg C--(iii)Cu2Cl2--(iv)Na,Ether--> Product A. Molar mass of product A is ____ g/mol.
Ans_des:
Nitrobenzene -> aniline (Sn/HCl) -> diazonium salt (NaNO2/HCl) -> chlorobenzene (Cu2Cl2, Sandmeyer) -> biphenyl (Na/Ether, Wurtz-Fittig with another chlorobenzene)
 Product = biphenyl (C12H10), molar mass = 12*12+10*1 = 144+10 = 154 g/mol

subject: Mathematics
question: Let alpha, beta, gamma and delta be the coefficients of x^7, x^5, x^3 and x respectively in the expansion of (x + sqrt(x^3-1))^5 + (x - sqrt(x^3-1))^5, x > 1. If u and v satisfy alpha*u + beta*v = 18 and gamma*u + delta*v = 20, then u + v equals: (1) 5 (2) 4 (3) 3 (4) 8
Ans_des: Expand (x + sqrt(x^3-1))^5 + (x - sqrt(x^3-1))^5 = 2{C(5,0)x^5 + C(5,2)x^3(x^3-1) + C(5,4)x(x^3-1)^2}. Expanding: = 2{5x^7 + 10x^6 + x^5 - 10x^4 - 10x^3 + 5x}. Coefficients: alpha(x^7)=10, beta(x^5)=2, gamma(x^3)=-20, delta(x)=10. Equations: 10u + 2v = 18 and -20u + 10v = 20. From first: 5u + v = 9. From second: -2u + v = 2. Subtracting: 7u = 7, u = 1. Then v = 4. u + v = 5.

subject: Mathematics
question: In a group of 3 girls and 4 boys, there are two boys B1 and B2. The number of ways in which these girls and boys can stand in a queue such that all girls stand together, all boys stand together, but B1 and B2 are not adjacent to each other is: (1) 144 (2) 72 (3) 96 (4) 120
Ans_des: Total arrangements with all girls together and all boys together: treat girls as one block and boys as one block. Blocks can be arranged in 2! ways, girls within block = 3!, boys within block = 4!. Total = 2! * 3! * 4! = 2 * 6 * 24 = 288. When B1 and B2 are adjacent (treat as one unit): 2! * 3! * 3! * 2! = 2*6*6*2 = 144. Answer = Total - (B1 and B2 adjacent) = 288 - 144 = 144.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let P(4, 4*sqrt(3)) be a point on the parabola y^2 = 4ax and PQ be a focal chord. If M and N are feet of perpendiculars from P and Q on the directrix, then area of quadrilateral PQMN equals: (1) 263*sqrt(3)/8 (2) 17*sqrt(3) (3) 343*sqrt(3)/8 (4) 34*sqrt(3)/3
Ans_des: P(4, 4*sqrt(3)) lies on y^2=4ax: 48 = 16a, so a=3, parabola is y^2=12x. Parameter of P: y=2at1 => 4*sqrt(3) = 6t1, t1 = 2/sqrt(3). For focal chord: t1*t2 = -1, so t2 = -sqrt(3)/2. Q = (a*t2^2, 2a*t2) = (9/4, -3*sqrt(3)). For parabola y^2=12x, directrix is x=-3. MN = PS + QS (sum of focal distances). PS = a + x_P = 3+4 = 7. QS = a + x_Q = 3 + 9/4 = 21/4. MN = 7 + 21/4 = 49/4. PQ = sqrt((4-9/4)^2 + (4*sqrt(3)+3*sqrt(3))^2) = sqrt((7/4)^2 + (7*sqrt(3))^2) = 7*sqrt(1/16+3) = 7*sqrt(49/16) = 49/4. Area of trapezium = (1/2)*MN*(PM+QN) = (1/2)*MN*(PS+QS) = (1/2)*(49/4)*(49/4) ... Actually Area = (1/2)*MN*PQ = (1/2)*(49/4)*(7*sqrt(3)) = 343*sqrt(3)/8.

question: For a 3x3 matrix M, let trace(M) = sum of diagonal elements. Let A be 3x3 with |A|=1/2 and trace(A)=3. If B = adj(adj(2A)), then |B| + trace(B) equals: (1) 56 (2) 132 (3) 174 (4) 280
