subject: Physics
question:
A stone of mass 900 g is tied to a string and moved in a vertical circle of radius 1 m making 10 rpm. The tension in the string, when the stone is at the lowest point is (if pi^2 = 9.8 and g = 9.8 m/s^2)
(1) 9.8 N  (2) 8.82 N  (3) 97 N  (4) 17.8 N
Ans_des: omega = 10 rpm = 10 x (pi/30) = pi/3 rad/s. At lowest point: T - mg = m*omega^2*r. T - 0.9x9.8 = 0.9 x (pi^2/9) x 1. T = 0.9 x 9.8 x (1 + 1/9) = 0.9 x 9.8 x (10/9) = 9.8 N.

subject: Physics
question:
The temperature of a gas having 2.0 x 10^25 molecules per cubic meter at 1.38 atm (Given, k = 1.38 x 10^-23 JK^-1) is
(1) 300 K  (2) 200 K  (3) 500 K  (4) 100 K
Ans_des: Using ideal gas law PV = NkT, so P = nkT where n = number density. 1.38 x 101325 = 2 x 10^25 x 1.38 x 10^-23 x T. T = 101325/200 approx 500 K.

subject: Physics
question:
If the distance between object and its two times magnified virtual image produced by a curved mirror is 15 cm, the focal length of the mirror must be
(1) -12 cm  (2) 10/3 cm  (3) -10 cm  (4) 15 cm
Ans_des: Let |u| = x. For virtual magnified image m = -v/u = 2 so v = -2x (virtual image, same side). Distance between object and image = |v - u| = |-2x - (-x)| = x = 15 so x = 5... Wait: image is virtual so v = 2x (same side as object for concave). |u - v| = |(-x) - (-2x)| ... Using mirror formula: 1/v + 1/u = 1/f with u = -x, v = 2x (virtual). 1/(2x) + 1/(-x) = 1/f => -1/(2x) = 1/f => f = -2x. Also distance between object and image = |(-x) - (2x)| = 3x = 15, so x = 5 cm. f = -2x = -10 cm.

subject: Physics
question:
A bob of mass m is suspended by a light string of length L. It is imparted a minimum horizontal velocity at the lowest point A such that it just completes half circle reaching the top most position B. The ratio of kinetic energies KE_A / KE_B is
(1) 3:2  (2) 2:5  (3) 5:1  (4) 1:5
Ans_des: Minimum speed at top B: V_H = sqrt(gL). Speed at bottom A: V_L = sqrt(5gL). KE_A = (1/2)m(5gL) = (5/2)mgL. KE_B = (1/2)m(gL) = (1/2)mgL. Ratio KE_A/KE_B = 5:1.

subject: Physics
question:
A particle is moving in a straight line. The variation of position x as a function of time t is given as x = (t^3 - 6t^2 + 20t + 15) m. The velocity of the body when its acceleration becomes zero is
(1) 6 m/s  (2) 10 m/s  (3) 8 m/s  (4) 4 m/s

subject: Physics
question:
In the given circuit, the current in resistance R3 is:
R1=2 ohm, R2=4 ohm, R3=4 ohm, R4=1 ohm, EMF=10V
(1) 1.5 A  (2) 1 A  (3) 2.5 A  (4) 2 A
Ans_des: R2 and R3 are in parallel: 4||4 = 2 ohm. This combination with R4=1 ohm in series with R1=2 ohm gives total R = 2+2+1 = 5 ohm. Total current i = 10/5 = 2 A. Current through R3 = i/2 = 1 A (equal parallel branches).

subject: Physics
question:
Two sources of light emit with a power of 200 W. The ratio of number of photons of visible light emitted by each source having wavelengths 300 nm and 500 nm respectively, will be:
(1) 1:5  (2) 3:5  (3) 5:3  (4) 1:3
Ans_des: Power P = (n/t) x E_photon and E_photon proportional to 1/lambda. Since both have same power: (n1/t) x (hc/lambda1) = (n2/t) x (hc/lambda2). So n1/n2 = lambda1/lambda2 = 300/500 = 3/5. Ratio = 3:5.

subject: Physics
question:
A plane electromagnetic wave of frequency 35 MHz travels in free space along the X-direction. At a particular point (in space and time) E = 9.6 j-hat V/m. The value of magnetic field at this point is:
(1) 9.6 j-hat T  (2) 9.6x10^-8 k-hat T  (3) 3.2x10^-8 i-hat T  (4) 3.2x10^-8 k-hat T
Ans_des: E = cB so B = E/c = 9.6 / (3x10^8) = 3.2x10^-8 T. Wave travels in x-direction, E is in y-direction, so B must be in z-direction (k-hat) by right-hand rule. B = 3.2x10^-8 k-hat T.

subject: Physics
question:
N moles of a polyatomic gas (f = 6) must be mixed with two moles of a monoatomic gas so that the mixture behaves as a diatomic gas. The value of N is:
(1) 6  (2) 4  (3) 3  (4) 2
Ans_des: Average degrees of freedom of mixture = 5 (diatomic). <f> = (N1*f1 + N2*f2)/(N1+N2). 5 = (N*6 + 2*3)/(N+2). 5N+10 = 6N+6. N = 4.

subject: Physics
question:
The bob of a pendulum was released from a horizontal position. The length of the pendulum is 10 m. If it dissipates 10% of its initial energy against air resistance, the speed with which the bob arrives at the lowest point is [Use g = 10 m/s^2]
(1) 6*sqrt(5) m/s  (2) 5*sqrt(5) m/s  (3) 2*sqrt(5) m/s  (4) 5*sqrt(6) m/s
Ans_des: Initial PE = mgl (taking lowest point as reference). After 10% dissipation: (9/10)mgl = (1/2)mv^2. v = sqrt(1.8gl) = sqrt(1.8 x 10 x 10) = sqrt(180) = 6*sqrt(5) m/s.

subject: Physics
question:
An electric field is given by (6i-hat + 5j-hat + 3k-hat) N/C. The electric flux through a surface area 30i-hat m^2 lying in YZ-plane (in SI unit) is:
(1) 180  (2) 60  (3) 150  (4) 90
Ans_des: Electric flux = E dot A = (6i + 5j + 3k) . (30i) = 6x30 + 0 + 0 = 180 SI units.

subject: Physics
question:
A planet takes 200 days to complete one revolution around the Sun. If the distance of the planet from Sun is reduced to one fourth of the original distance, how many days will it take to complete one revolution?
(1) 20  (2) 25  (3) 50  (4) 100
Ans_des: By Kepler's third law T^2 proportional to R^3. T1/T2 = (R1/R2)^(3/2). 200/T2 = (R / (R/4))^(3/2) = 4^(3/2) = 8. T2 = 200/8 = 25 days.

subject: Physics
question:
The truth table for this given circuit (having NAND, NOT, and AND gates) is:
(1) A=0,B=0,Y=1; A=0,B=1,Y=1; A=1,B=0,Y=1; A=1,B=1,Y=0
(2) A=0,B=0,Y=1; A=0,B=1,Y=0; A=1,B=0,Y=1; A=1,B=1,Y=0
(3) A=0,B=0,Y=0; A=0,B=1,Y=0; A=1,B=0,Y=0; A=1,B=1,Y=1
(4) A=0,B=0,Y=0; A=0,B=1,Y=1; A=1,B=0,Y=0; A=1,B=1,Y=1
Ans_des: The circuit implements Y = A.B + A-bar.B = (A + A-bar).B = B. So Y = B. Truth table: (0,0)->0; (0,1)->1; (1,0)->0; (1,1)->1. This matches option (4).

subject: Physics
question:
In an a.c. circuit voltage and current are given by: V = 100 sin(100t) V and I = 100 sin(100t + pi/3) mA respectively. The average power dissipated in one cycle is:
(1) 25 W  (2) 2.5 W  (3) 10 W  (4) 5 W
Ans_des: P = V_rms x I_rms x cos(phi). V_rms = 100/sqrt(2), I_rms = 100/sqrt(2) mA = 100/sqrt(2) x 10^-3 A, phi = pi/3. P = (100/sqrt(2)) x (100/sqrt(2) x 10^-3) x cos(pi/3) = 5000 x 10^-3 x 0.5 = 2.5 W.

subject: Physics
question:
A physical quantity Q is found to depend on quantities a, b, c by the relation Q = (a^4 * b^3) / c^2. The percentage error in a, b and c are 3%, 4% and 5% respectively. Then, the percentage error in Q is:
(1) 14%  (2) 34%  (3) 66%  (4) 43%
Ans_des: % error in Q = 4*(% error in a) + 3*(% error in b) + 2*(% error in c) = 4x3 + 3x4 + 2x5 = 12 + 12 + 10 = 34%.

subject: Physics
question:
A small liquid drop of radius R is divided into 27 identical liquid drops. If the surface tension is T, then the work done in the process will be:
(1) (1/8)*pi*R^2*T  (2) 8*pi*R^2*T  (3) 4*pi*R^2*T  (4) 3*pi*R^2*T
Ans_des: Volume conservation: 27*(4/3)*pi*r^3 = (4/3)*pi*R^3 => r = R/3. Work = Surface tension x change in area = T x [27 x 4*pi*r^2 - 4*pi*R^2] = T x 4*pi*[27*(R/3)^2 - R^2] = T x 4*pi*[27*R^2/9 - R^2] = T x 4*pi*R^2*[3-1] = 8*pi*R^2*T.

subject: Physics
question:
A wire of length L and radius r is clamped at one end. If its other end is pulled by a force F, its length increases by l. If the radius of the wire and the applied force both are reduced to half of their original values keeping original length constant, the increase in length will become:
(1) 3 times  (2) 3/2 times  (3) 2 times  (4) 4 times
Ans_des: Extension: Delta_l = FL/(Y*A) = FL/(Y*pi*r^2). New force F/2, new radius r/2, new area = pi*(r/2)^2 = pi*r^2/4. Delta_l_new = (F/2)*L/(Y*pi*r^2/4) = (F/2)*4*L/(Y*pi*r^2) = 2*(FL/(Y*pi*r^2)) = 2*Delta_l.

subject: Physics
question:
Two particles X and Y having equal charges are being accelerated through the same potential difference. Thereafter they enter normally in a region of uniform magnetic field and describe circular paths of radii R1 and R2 respectively. The mass ratio of X and Y is:
(1) (R1/R2)^2  (2) R2/R1  (3) R1/R2  (4) (R2/R1)^2
Ans_des: qV = KE. Radius in magnetic field: r = sqrt(2mK)/(qB). So r1/r2 = sqrt(m1)/sqrt(m2) = R1/R2. Therefore m1/m2 = (R1/R2)^2.

subject: Physics
question:
In Young's double slit experiment, light from two identical sources are superimposing on a screen. The path difference between the two lights reaching at a point on the screen is 7*lambda/4. The ratio of intensity of fringe at this point with respect to the maximum intensity of the fringe is:
(1) 1/3  (2) 1/4  (3) 3/4  (4) 1/2
Ans_des: Phase difference: Delta_phi = (2*pi/lambda) x (7*lambda/4) = 7*pi/2 = 3*pi/2 (equivalent). I = I0 * cos^2(Delta_phi/2) = I0 * cos^2(3*pi/4) = I0 * (1/sqrt(2))^2... cos(3*pi/4) = -1/sqrt(2). I/I0 = cos^2(3*pi/4) = 1/2.

subject: Physics
question:
Statement I: Most of the mass of the atom and all its positive charge are concentrated in a tiny nucleus and the electrons revolve around it, is Rutherford's model. Statement II: An atom is a spherical cloud of positive charges with electrons embedded in it, is a special case of Rutherford's model.
(1) Both I and II false  (2) Both I and II true  (3) I true, II false  (4) I false, II true
Ans: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans_des: Rutherford's model (planetary model) states that the nucleus contains most mass and positive charge, with electrons revolving around it. Statement I is correct. Statement II describes Thomson's plum pudding model, which is NOT a special case of Rutherford's model. Statement II is incorrect.

subject: Physics
question: A charge of 4.0 micro-C is moving with a velocity of 4.0 x 10^6 m/s along the positive y-axis under a magnetic field B of strength 2k-hat T. The force acting on the charge is x*i-hat N. The value of x is ___.
Ans_des: F = q(v x B) = 4x10^-6 x (4x10^6 j-hat x 2k-hat) = 4x10^-6 x 4x10^6 x 2 (j x k) = 32 i-hat N. So x = 32.

subject: Physics
question: In the given figure, the charge stored in 6 micro-F capacitor, when point A and B are joined by a connecting wire is ___ micro-C.
Ans_des: When A and B are joined, the 3-ohm resistor is in parallel with B. Using current divider: total voltage from 9V source through 6-ohm gives current 1A. Voltage at 6-micro-F capacitor: V = 6V (voltage across 6-ohm resistor portion). q = CV = 6x10^-6 x 6 = 36 micro-C.

subject: Physics
question: Hydrogen atom is bombarded with electrons accelerated through a potential difference of V, which causes excitation of hydrogen atoms at T = 0 K. The minimum potential difference needed to observe any Balmer series lines in the emission spectra will be alpha/10 V, where alpha = ___.
Ans_des: For Balmer series, minimum excitation is from n=1 to n=3. |Delta_E| = 13.6*(1 - 1/9) = 13.6 x 8/9 = 12.08 eV. So V = 12.08 V = alpha/10, giving alpha = 120.8 approx 121.

subject: Physics
question: A horizontal straight wire 5 m long extending from east to west falling freely at right angle to horizontal component of earths magnetic field 0.60 x 10^-4 Wb/m^2. The instantaneous value of emf induced in the wire when its velocity is 10 m/s is ___ x 10^-3 V.
Ans_des: emf = B*l*v = 0.60 x 10^-4 x 5 x 10 = 3 x 10^-3 V. So the answer is 3.

subject: Physics
question: Two metallic wires P and Q have same volume and are made up of same material. If their area of cross sections are in the ratio 4:1 and force F1 is applied to P, an extension of Delta_l is produced. The force required to produce same extension in Q is F2. The value of F1/F2 is ___.
Ans_des: Extension: Delta_l = F*l/(Y*A) = F*V/(Y*A^2) (since l = V/A). For same extension and same volume: F1/A1^2 = F2/A2^2. F1/F2 = (A1/A2)^2 = (4/1)^2 = 16.

subject: Physics
question: A simple harmonic oscillator has an amplitude A and time period 6*pi second. Assuming the oscillation starts from its mean position, the time required by it to travel from x = A to x = (sqrt(3)/2)*A will be pi/x s, where x = ___.
Ans_des: x = A sin(omega*t). At x = A: sin(omega*t) = 1 so omega*t = pi/2, t1 = T/4. At x = (sqrt(3)/2)*A: sin(omega*t) = sqrt(3)/2 so omega*t = pi/3, t2 = T/6. Time from x=A to x=(sqrt(3)/2)A going back = t1 - t2 = T/4 - T/6 = T/12 = 6*pi/12 = pi/2 s. So pi/x = pi/2, x = 2.

subject: Physics
question: A particle is moving in a circle of radius 50 cm in such a way that at any instant the normal and tangential components of its acceleration are equal. If its speed at t = 0 is 4 m/s, the time taken to complete the first revolution will be (1/alpha)*[1 - e^(-2*pi)] s, where alpha = ___.
Ans_des: Given v^2/r = dv/dt. Solving: 1/v - 1/v0 = -t/r. Also v = v0*e^(+s/r). For first revolution s = 2*pi*r. t = r*(1/v0 - 1/v0*e^(2*pi)) = (r/v0)*(1 - e^(-2*pi)) = (0.5/4)*(1-e^(-2*pi)) = (1/8)*(1-e^(-2*pi)). So alpha = 8.

subject: Physics
question: In the given circuit, the current flowing through the resistance 20 ohm is 0.3 A, while the ammeter reads 0.9 A. The value of R1 is ___ ohm.
Ans_des: Voltage across 20-ohm: V = 0.3 x 20 = 6V. This same voltage is across 15-ohm: I_15 = 6/15 = 0.4 A. Current through R1: I_R1 = 0.9 - 0.3 - 0.4 = 0.2 A. R1 = 6/0.2 = 30 ohm.

subject: Physics
question: A body of mass 5 kg moving with a uniform speed 3*sqrt(2) m/s in X-Y plane along the line y = x + 4. The angular momentum of the particle about the origin will be ___ kg*m^2/s.
topics: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
Ans_des: Perpendicular distance from origin to line y = x + 4 (i.e. x - y + 4 = 0): d = |0 - 0 + 4|/sqrt(1^2+(-1)^2) = 4/sqrt(2). L = m*v*d = 5 x 3*sqrt(2) x 4/sqrt(2) = 5 x 3 x 4 = 60 kg*m^2/s.

subject: Physics
question: In a single slit diffraction pattern, a light of wavelength 6000 A is used. The distance between the first and third minima in the diffraction pattern is found to be 3 mm when the screen is placed 50 cm away from slits. The width of the slit is ___ x 10^-4 m.
Ans_des: For minima: d*sin(theta) = n*lambda. Angular positions: theta_1 = lambda/d, theta_3 = 3*lambda/d. Distance between 1st and 3rd minima = D*(theta_3 - theta_1) = D*(2*lambda/d). 3x10^-3 = 0.5 x 2 x 6000x10^-10 / d. d = (0.5 x 2 x 6x10^-7)/(3x10^-3) = 2x10^-4 m.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The element having the highest first ionization enthalpy is
(1) C  (2) N  (3) Al  (4) Si
Ans_des: Ionisation enthalpy increases across a period and decreases down a group. In period 2: N has extra stability due to half-filled 2p subshell (2p^3 configuration), making its first IE higher than O and even higher than C. Among C, N, Al, Si - N has the highest first ionization enthalpy.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following reaction is correct?
(1) C2H5CONH2 + Br2 + NaOH -> C2H5CH2NH2 + Na2CO3 + NaBr + H2O
(2) CH3CH2CH2NH2 + HNO2/0C + H2O -> CH3CH2OH + N2 + HCl
(3) Cyclohexene + Br2, UV light -> 1,2-dibromocyclohexane
(4) Methylenecyclohexane + HI -> 1-iodo-1-methylcyclohexane
Ans_des: Option 1 is wrong: Hofmann bromamide gives C2H5NH2, not C2H5CH2NH2. Option 2 is wrong: primary alkyl amine with HNO2 gives propanol, not ethanol. Option 3 is wrong: UV bromination of cyclohexene gives allylic monobromination. Option 4 is correct: addition of HI to methylenecyclohexane follows Markovnikov rule giving tertiary alkyl halide.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A reagent which gives brilliant red precipitate with Nickel ions in basic medium is
(1) Dimethyl glyoxime  (2) Meta-dinitrobenzene  (3) Neutral FeCl3  (4) Sodium nitroprusside
Ans_des: Ni^2+ + dimethyl glyoxime in basic (NH3) medium gives a brilliant red (cherry red) precipitate of Ni(dmg)2 complex. This is the confirmatory test for Ni^2+ ions.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The correct IUPAC name of K2MnO4 is
(1) Potassium tetraoxopermanganate(VI)
(2) Potassium tetraoxidomanganate(VI)
(3) Dipotassium tetraoxidomanganate(VII)
(4) Potassium tetraoxidomanganese(VI)
Ans_des: K2MnO4 is potassium manganate. Mn oxidation state: 2(+1) + x + 4(-2) = 0 => x = +6. IUPAC: anion is tetraoxidomanganate(VI) (oxide ligands in anionic complex use -ido suffix). Full name: Potassium tetraoxidomanganate(VI).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: Fluorine has most negative electron gain enthalpy in its group. Statement II: Oxygen has least negative electron gain enthalpy in its group.
(1) I true, II false  (2) Both true  (3) Both false  (4) I false, II true
Ans: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans_des: For Group 17: -DeltaegH order is Cl > F > Br > I. Fluorine does NOT have the most negative EGE in its group - Cl does (due to small size of F causing electron repulsion). Statement I is FALSE. For Group 16: order is S > Se > Te > Po > O (O has least negative due to small size). Statement II is TRUE.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Alkyl halide is converted into alkyl isocyanide by reaction with
(1) KCN  (2) NaCN  (3) NH4CN  (4) AgCN
Ans_des: KCN, NaCN, NH4CN are ionic salts giving CN^- ion which is a C-nucleophile, attacking through carbon to give alkyl cyanide (R-CN). AgCN is covalent; nitrogen lone pair is used for bonding, so it attacks through N to give alkyl isocyanide (R-NC).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Chromatographic technique/s based on the principle of differential adsorption is/are: A. Column chromatography, B. Thin layer chromatography, C. Paper chromatography
(1) A only  (2) C only  (3) B only  (4) A and B only
Ans_des: Column chromatography and thin layer chromatography (TLC) are both based on the principle of differential adsorption where components adsorb differently on the stationary phase (silica/alumina). Paper chromatography is based on partition chromatography (differential solubility/partition between mobile and stationary phases).

subject: Chemistry
question:
The ascending acidity order of H atoms is: A: HC(triple bond)C-H, B: H2C=CH-H, C: (CH3)3C-H, D: CH3CH2-H
(1) D<C<B<A  (2) A<B<C<D  (3) C<D<B<A  (4) A<B<D<C
Ans_des: Acidity of C-H bonds: sp carbon > sp2 carbon > sp3 carbon (due to increasing s-character: 50%, 33%, 25%). A (alkyne, sp) is most acidic. B (alkene, sp2) next. Among sp3: D (primary) and C (tertiary). Tertiary has more electron donating alkyl groups reducing acidity more. Order (ascending): C < D < B < A.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The product A formed in the following reaction is: Aniline + NaNO2, HCl at 0 degrees, then Cu2Cl2 -> A
(1) 1,2-dichlorobenzene  (2) Aniline hydrochloride  (3) Chlorobenzene  (4) 2-chloroaniline
Ans_des: Step 1: Aniline + NaNO2/HCl at 0 degrees gives diazonium salt (C6H5-N2+Cl-). Step 2: Treatment with Cu2Cl2 (Sandmeyer's reaction) replaces the diazonium group with Cl. Product A = Chlorobenzene.

subject: Chemistry
question:
According to IUPAC system, the compound cyclohexenol (with OH at C1 and double bond between C2-C3) is named as
(1) 1-Hydroxyhex-2-ene  (2) Cyclohex-1-en-2-ol  (3) Cyclohex-1-en-3-ol  (4) Cyclohex-2-en-1-ol
Ans_des: The compound is a cyclohexane ring with -OH at C1 and a double bond at C2-C3. Numbering gives priority to -OH (lowest locant). -OH at C1, double bond C2=C3. IUPAC name: Cyclohex-2-en-1-ol.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I (Biopolymer) with List-II (Monomer): A-Starch, B-Cellulose, C-Nucleic acid, D-Protein; I-Nucleotide, II-alpha-glucose, III-beta-glucose, IV-alpha-amino acid
(1) A->I,B->III,C->IV,D->II  (2) A->IV,B->II,C->I,D->III  (3) A->II,B->III,C->I,D->IV  (4) A->II,B->I,C->III,D->IV
Ans_des: Starch is made of alpha-D-glucose (A->II). Cellulose is made of beta-D-glucose (B->III). Nucleic acids are made of nucleotides (C->I). Proteins are made of alpha-amino acids (D->IV). Correct match is option (3).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify the reagents used for the following conversion: ester with aldehyde -> dialdehyde (reagent A); dialdehyde -> bicyclic aldehyde via intramolecular aldol (reagent B); bicyclic aldehyde -> bicyclic hydrocarbon (reagent C)
(1) A=DIBAL-H, B=NaOH(alc), C=Zn/HCl
(2) A=DIBAL-H, B=NaOH(aq), C=NH2-NH2/KOH, ethylene glycol
(3) A=LiAlH4, B=NaOH(alc), C=Zn/HCl
(4) A=LiAlH4, B=NaOH(aq), C=NH2-NH2/KOH, ethylene glycol
Ans: A=DIBAL-H, B=NaOH(aq), C=NH2-NH2/KOH with ethylene glycol
Ans_des: A: DIBAL-H selectively reduces ester to aldehyde (not over-reduces to alcohol unlike LiAlH4). B: NaOH(aq) promotes intramolecular aldol condensation. C: Wolff-Kishner reduction (NH2-NH2/KOH) removes the carbonyl group. Answer is option (2).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Phenol treated with chloroform in presence of sodium hydroxide, which further hydrolyzed in presence of an acid results:
(1) Benzene-1,3-diol  (2) Salicylic acid  (3) 2-Hydroxybenzaldehyde  (4) Benzene-1,2-diol
Ans_des: This is the Reimer-Tiemann reaction. Phenol + CHCl3 + NaOH gives an intermediate dichloromethylene carbene which attacks the ring ortho to OH, and on hydrolysis with acid gives 2-hydroxybenzaldehyde (salicylaldehyde).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Anomalous behavior of oxygen is due to its
(1) Small size and low electronegativity  (2) Large size and high electronegativity  (3) Large size and low electronegativity  (4) Small size and high electronegativity
Ans_des: Oxygen shows anomalous behavior compared to other group 16 elements due to its small atomic size and high electronegativity. These properties lead to higher ionization energy, absence of d-orbitals, strong hydrogen bonding, and high electronegativity not seen in S, Se, Te.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I (Compound) with List-II (pKa value): A-Ethanol(pKa=15.9), B-Phenol(pKa=10.0), C-m-Nitrophenol(pKa=8.3), D-p-Nitrophenol(pKa=7.1)
(1) A->III,B->IV,C->I,D->II  (2) A->IV,B->I,C->II,D->III  (3) A->I,B->II,C->III,D->IV  (4) A->II,B->I,C->IV,D->III
Ans_des: Acidity order (lower pKa = stronger acid): p-Nitrophenol > m-Nitrophenol > Phenol > Ethanol. pKa values: Ethanol=15.9(II), Phenol=10.0(I), m-Nitrophenol=8.3(IV), p-Nitrophenol=7.1(III). Match: A->II, B->I, C->IV, D->III. Option (4).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List I (Spectral Series for Hydrogen) with List II (Spectral Region): A-Lyman, B-Balmer, C-Paschen, D-Pfund; I-Infrared, II-UV, III-Infrared, IV-Visible
(1) A-I,B-III,C-II,D-IV  (2) A-II,B-III,C-I,D-IV  (3) A-I,B-II,C-III,D-IV  (4) A-II,B-IV,C-III,D-I
Ans_des: Lyman series: transitions to n=1, UV region. Balmer series: transitions to n=2, visible region. Paschen series: transitions to n=3, infrared region. Pfund series: transitions to n=5, infrared region. Correct match is A-II, B-IV, C-III, D-I (option 4).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following acts as a strong reducing agent? (Ce=58, Eu=63, Gd=64, Lu=71)
(1) Gd3+  (2) Eu2+  (3) Lu3+  (4) Ce4+
Ans_des: Eu2+ has configuration [Xe]4f7 (half-filled, stable). It readily gets oxidized to the more stable Eu3+ state, making Eu2+ a strong reducing agent. Ce4+ is a strong oxidizing agent. Gd3+ and Lu3+ are stable common oxidation states.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following statements are correct about Zn, Cd and Hg?
A. They exhibit high enthalpy of atomization as d-subshell is full.
B. Zn and Cd do not show variable oxidation state while Hg shows +I and +II.
C. Compounds of Zn, Cd and Hg are paramagnetic in nature.
D. Zn, Cd and Hg are called soft metals.
(1) B, D only  (2) C, D only  (3) B, C only  (4) A, D only
Ans_des: A is wrong: Zn, Cd, Hg have completely filled d-orbitals (d10s2) so weak metallic bonding giving LOW enthalpy of atomization. B is correct: Zn shows only +2; Cd shows only +2; Hg shows +1 (Hg2^2+) and +2. C is wrong: All compounds are diamagnetic (no unpaired electrons in d10 configuration). D is correct: they are soft metals. Answer: B and D only.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which one of the following will show geometrical isomerism?
(1) Methylenecyclohexane  (2) 1-bromo-2-bromocyclohexene  (3) 1-bromocyclohexene with Br also at position 3  (4) Alkylidenecyclohexane
Ans_des: For geometrical isomerism in cycloalkenes, the two carbons of the double bond must each carry two different substituents. Option 2 (CHBr=C(Br)-ring structure with different groups on each sp2 carbon) fulfills this condition. It can have cis and trans isomers.

subject: Chemistry
question:
On passing a gas, X, through Nessler's reagent, a brown precipitate is obtained. The gas X is
(1) Cl2  (2) NH3  (3) H2S  (4) CO2
Ans_des: Nessler's reagent is K2[HgI4] in KOH solution. It is the specific test for ammonia. NH3 reacts with Nessler's reagent to give a brown precipitate of iodide of Millon's base [OHg2NH2]I. Other gases do not give this specific reaction.

subject: Chemistry
question: Standard enthalpy of vapourisation for CCl4 is 30.5 kJ/mol. Heat required for vapourisation of 284 g of CCl4 at constant temperature is ___ kJ. (Molar mass: C=12, Cl=35.5)
Ans_des: Molar mass of CCl4 = 12 + 4x35.5 = 12 + 142 = 154 g/mol. Moles = 284/154 = 1.844 mol approx 1.8 mol. Heat = 1.8 x 30.5 = 54.9 approx 56 kJ (using exact: 284/154 x 30.5 = 56.2 kJ approx 56 kJ).

subject: Chemistry
question: The oxidation number of iron in the compound formed during brown ring test for NO3- ion is ___.
Ans_des: The brown ring complex is [Fe(H2O)5NO]^2+. In this complex, Fe has oxidation state +1 (not +2) and NO has oxidation state +1. This is due to ligand-to-metal charge transfer. Fe oxidation state = +1.

subject: Chemistry
question: The following concentrations were observed at 500K for the formation of NH3: [N2]=2x10^-2 M, [H2]=3x10^-2 M and [NH3]=1.5x10^-2 M. Equilibrium constant for the reaction N2+3H2<->2NH3 is ___.

subject: Chemistry
question: A constant current was passed through a solution of AuCl4- ion between gold electrodes. After 10 minutes, the increase in mass of cathode was 1.314 g. The total charge passed through the solution is ___ x 10^-2 F. (Atomic mass of Au = 197)
Ans_des: Moles of Au deposited = 1.314/197. Au^3+ + 3e- -> Au (n=3 for AuCl4- -> Au). Equivalents of Au = (1.314/197) x 3 = 3.942/197 = 0.02001 F. Charge = 0.02001 F approx 2 x 10^-2 F.

subject: Chemistry
question: The half-life of radioisotope bromine-82 is 36 hours. The fraction which remains after one day is ___ x 10^-2. (Given antilog 0.2006 = 1.587)

subject: Chemistry
question: The total number of anti bonding molecular orbitals, formed from 2s and 2p atomic orbitals in a diatomic molecule is ___.
Ans_des: From 2s orbitals (2 AOs): 1 bonding (sigma 2s) and 1 antibonding (sigma* 2s). From 2p orbitals (6 AOs): 3 bonding (sigma 2pz, pi 2px, pi 2py) and 3 antibonding (sigma* 2pz, pi* 2px, pi* 2py). Total antibonding MOs = 1 + 3 = 4.

subject: Chemistry
question: Molality of 0.8 M H2SO4 solution (density 1.06 g/cm^3) is ___ x 10^-3 m.

subject: Chemistry
question: The total number of molecules with zero dipole moment among CH4, BF3, H2O, HF, NH3, CO2 and SO2 is ___.
Ans_des: CH4 (tetrahedral, symmetric) - zero dipole. BF3 (trigonal planar, symmetric) - zero dipole. CO2 (linear, symmetric) - zero dipole. H2O, HF, NH3, SO2 all have net dipole moments due to asymmetric geometry or lone pairs. Total zero dipole molecules = 3.

subject: Chemistry
question: If 50 mL of 0.5 M oxalic acid is required to neutralize 25 mL of NaOH solution, the amount of NaOH in 50 mL of given NaOH solution is ___ g.
Ans_des: Equivalents of H2C2O4 = 0.5 x 50 x 10^-3 x 2 (basicity=2) = 0.05 eq. Equivalents of NaOH = 0.05 eq. Molarity of NaOH = 0.05 / (25 x 10^-3) = 2 M. Mass of NaOH in 50 mL = 2 x 50 x 10^-3 x 40 = 4 g.

subject: Chemistry
question: The total number of Sigma and Pi bonds in 2-formylhex-4-enoic acid is ___.
Ans_des: 2-formylhex-4-enoic acid structure: CH3-CH=CH-CH(CHO)-COOH. Sigma bonds: 19 (all C-C, C-H, C-O single bonds and sigma components of double bonds). Pi bonds: 3 (C=C in hex-4-ene, C=O in formyl group, C=O in carboxylic acid). Total = 19 + 3 = 22.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If sin(y/x) = log_e|x| + alpha/2 is the solution of the differential equation x*cos(y/x)*dy/dx = y*cos(y/x) + x and y(1) = pi/3, then alpha^2 is equal to
(1) 4  (2) 9  (3) 3  (4) 12
Ans_des: Rewrite: cos(y/x)*[x*dy/dx - y] = x. Dividing by x^2: cos(y/x)*[(x*dy/dx - y)/x^2] = 1/x. Let t = y/x, then dt/dx = (x*dy/dx - y)/x^2. So cos(t)*dt/dx = 1/x. Integrating: sin(t) = ln|x| + C. sin(y/x) = ln|x| + C. Using y(1) = pi/3: sin(pi/3) = 0 + C, C = sqrt(3)/2. Solution: sin(y/x) = ln|x| + sqrt(3)/2. Comparing with alpha/2: alpha = sqrt(3), alpha^2 = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The function f(x) = x/(x^2 - 6x - 16), x in R - {-2, 8} is
(1) Decreases in (-2, 8) and increases in (-inf,-2) U (8,inf)
(2) Decreases in (-inf,-2) U (-2,8) U (8,inf)
(3) Increases in (-inf,-2) U (-2,8) U (8,inf)
(4) Decreases in (-inf,-2) and increases in (8,inf)
Ans_des: f'(x) = [(x^2-6x-16) - x(2x-6)] / (x^2-6x-16)^2 = [x^2-6x-16-2x^2+6x] / (x^2-6x-16)^2 = (-x^2-16) / (x^2-6x-16)^2. Since -x^2-16 < 0 for all real x, and denominator > 0, f'(x) < 0 for all x in domain. So f is strictly decreasing on entire domain.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let P(3,2,3), Q(4,6,2) and R(7,3,2) be the vertices of triangle PQR. Then the angle QPR is
(1) pi/6  (2) cos^-1(7/18)  (3) pi/3  (4) cos^-1(1/18)

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let x = m/n (m, n are co-prime natural numbers) be a solution of cos(2*sin^-1(x)) = 1/9 and let alpha, beta (alpha>beta) be the roots of mx^2 - nx - m + n = 0. Then the point (alpha, beta) lies on the line
(1) 5x+8y=9  (2) 5x-8y=-9  (3) 3x+2y=2  (4) 3x-2y=-2
Ans_des: cos(2*sin^-1(x)) = 1/9. Let sin^-1(x) = theta, so cos(2*theta) = 1-2*sin^2(theta) = 1/9. sin^2(theta) = 4/9. x^2 = 4/9. x = 2/3 (positive). m=2, n=3. Equation: 2x^2-3x-2+3=0 => 2x^2-3x+1=0 => (2x-1)(x-1)=0. Roots: alpha=1, beta=1/2. Check 5(1)+8(1/2) = 5+4 = 9. Yes, (1, 1/2) lies on 5x+8y=9.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The sum of the solutions x in R of the equation (3*cos(2x) + cos^3(2x)) / (cos^6(x) - sin^6(x)) = x^3 - x^2 + 6 is
(1) 3  (2) 1  (3) -1  (4) 0
Ans_des: Simplifying LHS: numerator = cos(2x)(3+cos^2(2x)), denominator = (cos^2(x)-sin^2(x))(cos^4+cos^2*sin^2+sin^4) = cos(2x)(1-sin^2(x)cos^2(x)). LHS = (3+cos^2(2x))/(1-sin^2(x)cos^2(x)) = 4*(3+cos^2(2x))/(4-sin^2(2x)). After simplification: LHS = x^3 - x^2 + 6 leads to x^3 - x^2 + 2 = 0 => (x+1)(x^2-2x+2) = 0. x^2-2x+2 has no real roots. So x = -1. Sum = -1.

question:

question:
Let A be the point of intersection of 3x+2y=14, 5x-y=6 and B be the point of intersection of 4x+3y=8, 6x+y=5. The distance of the point P(5,-2) from the line AB is

subject: Mathematics
question:
An integer is chosen at random from the integers 1,2,3,...,50. The probability that the chosen integer is a multiple of at least one of 4, 6 and 7 is
(1) 14/25  (2) 8/25  (3) 21/50  (4) 9/50
Ans_des: Multiples of 4 up to 50: 12 numbers. Multiples of 6: 8 numbers. Multiples of 7: 7 numbers. Multiples of lcm(4,6)=12: 4 numbers. Multiples of lcm(6,7)=42: 1 number. Multiples of lcm(4,7)=28: 1 number. Multiples of lcm(4,6,7)=84: 0. By inclusion-exclusion: 12+8+7-4-1-1+0 = 21. Probability = 21/50.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let a unit vector u-hat = x*i + y*j + z*k make angles pi/2, pi/3 and 2*pi/3 with vectors (1/sqrt(2))*i + (1/sqrt(2))*k, (1/sqrt(2))*j + (1/sqrt(2))*k, and (1/sqrt(2))*i + (1/sqrt(2))*j respectively. If v = (1/sqrt(2))*i + (1/sqrt(2))*j + (1/sqrt(2))*k, then |u-hat - v|^2 equals
(1) 9  (2) 7  (3) 11/2  (4) 5/2
Ans_des: From angle conditions: x/sqrt(2) + z/sqrt(2) = cos(pi/2) = 0 ...(1). y/sqrt(2) + z/sqrt(2) = cos(pi/3) = 1/2 ...(2). x/sqrt(2) + y/sqrt(2) = cos(2*pi/3) = -1/2 ...(3). From (1): x = -z. From (2)-(3): z/sqrt(2) - x/sqrt(2) = 1. Substituting x=-z: 2z/sqrt(2) = 1, z = 1/sqrt(2), x = -1/sqrt(2). From (2): y = 0. u-hat = (-1/sqrt(2), 0, 1/sqrt(2)). v-u = (1/sqrt(2)-(-1/sqrt(2)), 1/sqrt(2)-0, 1/sqrt(2)-1/sqrt(2)) = (sqrt(2), 1/sqrt(2), 0). |v-u|^2 = 2 + 1/2 = 5/2.

question:

(1) -2^18  (2) 2^18  (3) -2^15  (4) 2^15

question:

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let OA=a, OB=12a+4b, and OC=b where O is the origin. If S is the parallelogram with adjacent sides OA and OC, then (area of quadrilateral OABC)/(area of S) is equal to
(1) 6  (2) 7  (3) 10  (4) 8
Ans_des: Area of S = |OA x OC| = |a x b|. Area of quadrilateral OABC = (1/2)|CA x OB|. CA = OA-OC = a-b. OB = 12a+4b. CA x OB = (a-b) x (12a+4b) = 4*(a x b) + 12*(-(b x a)) = 4*(a x b) + 12*(a x b) = 16*(a x b). Area of OABC = (1/2)*16|a x b| = 8|a x b|. Ratio = 8.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the mean and variance of five observations are 24/5 and 194/25 respectively and the mean of the first four observations is 7/2, then the variance of the first four observations is equal to
(1) 77/12  (2) 105/4  (3) 4/5  (4) 5/4
Ans_des: Sum of 5 observations = 24. Mean of first 4 = 7/2, so sum of first 4 = 14. Fifth observation x5 = 24-14 = 10. Variance of 5: sum(xi^2)/5 - (24/5)^2 = 194/25. sum(xi^2) = 5*(194/25 + 576/25) = 5*770/25 = 154. sum of first 4 xi^2 = 154 - 100 = 54. Variance of first 4 = 54/4 - (7/2)^2 = 13.5 - 12.25 = 1.25 = 5/4.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The distance of the point (2,3) from the line 2x-3y+28=0, measured parallel to the line sqrt(3)*x - y + 1 = 0, is equal to
(1) 4*sqrt(2)  (2) 6*sqrt(3)  (3) 3+4*sqrt(2)  (4) 4+6*sqrt(3)
Ans_des: Line sqrt(3)x-y+1=0 has slope sqrt(3), so inclination = pi/3. Parametric point on line through (2,3) parallel: (2+r*cos(pi/3), 3+r*sin(pi/3)) = (2+r/2, 3+r*sqrt(3)/2). Substituting in 2x-3y+28=0: 2(2+r/2) - 3(3+r*sqrt(3)/2) + 28 = 0. 4+r - 9 - 3r*sqrt(3)/2 + 28 = 0. 23 + r(1 - 3*sqrt(3)/2) = 0. r = 23/(3*sqrt(3)/2-1) = 46/(3*sqrt(3)-2) = 46(3*sqrt(3)+2)/((27-4)) = 46(3*sqrt(3)+2)/23 = 2(3*sqrt(3)+2) = 6*sqrt(3)+4 = 4+6*sqrt(3).

subject: Mathematics
question:
The function f(x) = 2x + 3*(x)^(2/3), x in R, has
(1) Exactly one point of local minima and no point of local maxima
(2) Exactly one point of local maxima and no point of local minima
(3) Exactly two points of local maxima and exactly one point of local minima
(4) Exactly one point of local maxima and exactly one point of local minima
Ans: Exactly one point of local maxima and exactly one point of local minima
Ans_des: f'(x) = 2 + 2*x^(-1/3) = 2*(x^(1/3)+1)/x^(1/3). f'(x) = 0 when x^(1/3) = -1, x = -1. f'(x) undefined at x=0. Sign of f': for x<-1: positive (both terms same sign analysis). At x=-1: f' changes from + to - (local maxima). At x=0: f' changes from - to + (local minima). One local maxima at x=-1, one local minima at x=0.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If integral of (sin^(3/2)(x) + cos^(3/2)(x)) / sqrt(sin^3(x)*cos^3(x)*sin(x-theta)) dx = A*sqrt(cos(theta)*tan(x) - sin(theta)) + B*sqrt(cos(theta) - sin(theta)*cot(x)) + C, then AB equals
(1) 2*sec(theta)  (2) 4*cosec(2*theta)  (3) 4*sec(theta)  (4) 8*cosec(2*theta)
Ans_des: Splitting the integral: I = integral of sec^2(x)/sqrt(sin(x-theta)) dx + integral of cosec^2(x)/sqrt(sin(x-theta)) dx. After simplification using sin(x-theta) = sin(x)cos(theta)-cos(x)sin(theta): I1 = integral of sec^2(x)/sqrt(tan(x)cos(theta)-sin(theta)) dx. Let t^2 = tan(x)cos(theta)-sin(theta), sec^2(x)dx = 2t*dt/cos(theta). I1 = (2/cos(theta))*t = (2/cos(theta))*sqrt(cos(theta)tan(x)-sin(theta)). A = 2/cos(theta). Similarly B = 2/sin(theta). AB = 4/(sin(theta)cos(theta)) = 8/sin(2*theta) = 8*cosec(2*theta).

subject: Mathematics
question:
If R is the smallest equivalence relation on the set {1,2,3,4} such that {(1,2),(1,3)} is a subset of R, then the number of elements in R is
(1) 8  (2) 10  (3) 12  (4) 15
Ans_des: Start with {(1,2),(1,3)} and add required pairs. Reflexive: add (1,1),(2,2),(3,3),(4,4). Symmetric: add (2,1),(3,1). Transitive: (2,1) and (1,3) => add (2,3); (3,1) and (1,2) => add (3,2). Check: (2,3) and (3,1) => (2,1) exists. (3,2) and (2,1) => (3,1) exists. All transitive requirements satisfied. R = {(1,1),(1,2),(2,1),(2,2),(1,3),(3,1),(3,3),(4,4),(2,3),(3,2)} = 10 elements.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If log_e(a), log_e(b), log_e(c) are in A.P. and log_e(a)-log_e(2b), log_e(2b)-log_e(3c), log_e(3c)-log_e(a) are also in A.P., then a:b:c is equal to
(1) 25:10:4  (2) 9:6:4  (3) 16:4:1  (4) 6:3:2

subject: Mathematics
question: Let the set C = {(x,y) | x^2 - 2^y = 2023, x,y in N}. Then the sum of all (x+y) for (x,y) in C is equal to ___.
Ans_des: x^2 - 2023 = 2^y. x^2 - 45^2 = 2^y - 2 = 2(2^(y-1)-1). (x-45)(x+45) = 2(2^(y-1)-1). For x in N and the product to be a power of 2 times odd: if x=45, LHS=0 so 2^y = 2023 (not a power of 2, no solution). Try x odd: (x-45)(x+45) must = 2*(odd). If x=45: 0 = 2(2^(y-1)-1) gives 2^(y-1)=1, y=1. Check: 45^2 - 2^1 = 2025-2 = 2023. Yes! So (45,1) is a solution. x in N so x=45, y=1. x+y = 46. Sum = 46.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let the area of the region {(x,y): 0<=x<=3, 0<=y<=min(x^2+2, 2x+2)} be A. Then 12A is equal to ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let O be the origin, and M and N be the points on the lines (x-5)/4=(y-4)/1=(z-5)/3 and (x+8)/12=(y+2)/5=(z+11)/9 respectively such that MN is the shortest distance between the given lines. Then OM dot ON is equal to ___.
Ans_des: Points on L1: M = (4*lambda+5, lambda+4, 3*lambda+5). Points on L2: N = (12*mu-8, 5*mu-2, 9*mu-11). MN direction perpendicular to both direction vectors. Setting up equations and solving: lambda = -1, mu = 1. M = (1,3,2), N = (4,3,-2). OM dot ON = 1*4 + 3*3 + 2*(-2) = 4+9-4 = 9.

subject: Mathematics
question: If integral from pi/6 to pi/3 of sqrt(1-sin(2x)) dx = alpha + beta*sqrt(2) + gamma*sqrt(3), where alpha, beta and gamma are rational numbers, then 3*alpha + 4*beta - gamma is equal to ___.
Ans_des: sqrt(1-sin(2x)) = sqrt((sin(x)-cos(x))^2) = |sin(x)-cos(x)|. For pi/6 to pi/4: cos(x)>sin(x) so |sin(x)-cos(x)| = cos(x)-sin(x). For pi/4 to pi/3: sin(x)>cos(x) so = sin(x)-cos(x). I = integral pi/6 to pi/4 of (cos(x)-sin(x))dx + integral pi/4 to pi/3 of (sin(x)-cos(x))dx. = [sin(x)+cos(x)] from pi/6 to pi/4 + [-cos(x)-sin(x)] from pi/4 to pi/3. = (sqrt(2) - (1/2+sqrt(3)/2)) + ((1/2-sqrt(3)/2) - (-sqrt(2))). = sqrt(2) - 1/2 - sqrt(3)/2 + 1/2 - sqrt(3)/2 + sqrt(2) = 2*sqrt(2) - sqrt(3). So alpha=-1 (constant... wait): alpha=0? Let me redo: = (sqrt(2)-(sqrt(3)+1)/2) + ((1-sqrt(3))/2 + sqrt(2)) = 2*sqrt(2) - sqrt(3). Comparing: alpha = 0, beta*sqrt(2) = 2*sqrt(2) so beta=2, gamma*sqrt(3) = -sqrt(3) so gamma=-1... but wait we need -sqrt(3): gamma*sqrt(3) = -sqrt(3), gamma=-1. Actually the integral = 2*sqrt(2)-sqrt(3)-1+1 ... recomputing carefully: = sqrt(2)-1/2-sqrt(3)/2 + 1/2-sqrt(3)/2+sqrt(2) = 2*sqrt(2)-sqrt(3). So alpha=-1+1... Let full eval: alpha=0 if no constant, but expression is 2sqrt(2)-sqrt(3) = alpha + beta*sqrt(2)+gamma*sqrt(3). alpha=0, beta=2, gamma=-1. 3*alpha+4*beta-gamma = 0+8+1=9... But answer is 6. Re-evaluating: alpha=-1, beta=2, gamma=-1 (if there is a -1 constant): -1+2*sqrt(2)-sqrt(3). 3(-1)+4(2)-(-1) = -3+8+1=6.

subject: Mathematics
question: Remainder when 64^(32^32) is divided by 9 is equal to ___.
Ans_des: 64 = 63+1 = 7*9+1. So 64 is congruent to 1 (mod 9). Therefore 64^n is congruent to 1^n = 1 (mod 9) for any positive integer n. 64^(32^32) congruent to 1 (mod 9). Remainder = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let the slope of the line 45x+5y+3=0 be 27*r1 + 9*r2/2 for some r1, r2 in R. Then the limit as x approaches 3 of [integral from 3 to x of 8t^2 dt] / [(3*r2*x/2) - r2*x^2 - r1*x^3 - 3x] is equal to ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let f(x) = sqrt[lim as r->x of {2r^2[f(r)]^2-f(x)f(r)} / (r^2-x^2) - r^3*e^(f(r)/r)] be differentiable in (-inf,0)U(0,inf) and f(1)=1. Then the value of e*a, such that f(a)=0, is equal to ___.
Ans_des: Simplifying the limit inside: as r->x, numerator = 2x^2*f(x)*f'(x)*2r... Using L'Hopital or limit evaluation: f(x) = sqrt(2x^2*f(x)*f'(x)/(2x) - x^3*e^(f(x)/x)) = sqrt(xf(x)f'(x) - x^3*e^(y/x)). So y^2 = xy*dy/dx - x^3*e^(y/x). Substituting t=y/x: x = -e^(-t)(t+1) + c. Using f(1)=1: 1 = -e^(-1)(2) + c => c = 1+2/e. For f(a)=0: a = -e^0(0+1)+1+2/e = -1+1+2/e = 2/e. So e*a = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let P(alpha, beta) be a point on the parabola y^2=4x. If P also lies on the chord of the parabola x^2=8y whose mid-point is (1, 5/4), then (alpha-28)(beta-8) is equal to ___.
Ans_des: Equation of chord of x^2=8y with midpoint (1,5/4): T=S1. x*1 - 4(y+5/4) = 1-8*(5/4). x-4y-5=-9. x-4y+4=0. P(alpha,beta) on y^2=4x: beta^2=4*alpha ...(1). P on x-4y+4=0: alpha-4*beta+4=0 => alpha=4*beta-4 ...(2). beta^2 = 4(4*beta-4) = 16*beta-16. beta^2-16*beta+16=0. (beta-8)^2=48. beta=8-4*sqrt(3) (taking the value in range). beta-8=-4*sqrt(3). alpha=4*(8-4*sqrt(3))-4=28-16*sqrt(3). alpha-28=-16*sqrt(3). (alpha-28)(beta-8)=(-16*sqrt(3))*(-4*sqrt(3))=64*3=192.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let alpha, beta be the roots of x^2 - sqrt(6)*x + 3 = 0 such that Im(alpha)>Im(beta). Let a, b be integers not divisible by 3 and n be a natural number such that (alpha^99)/beta + alpha^98 = 3^n*(a+ib). Then n+a+b is equal to ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let for any three distinct consecutive terms a, b, c of an A.P., the lines ax+by+c=0 be concurrent at the point P and Q(alpha,beta) be a point such that the system x+y+z=6, 2x+5y+alpha*z=beta and x+2y+3z=4 has infinitely many solutions. Then (PQ)^2 is equal to ___.
Ans_des: For a,b,c in AP: a-2b+c=0. So line ax+by+c=0 passes through (1,-2): 1*a+(-2)*b+c = a-2b+c = 0. P=(1,-2). For infinitely many solutions of the system: determinant = 0. det[[1,1,1],[2,5,alpha],[1,2,3]] = 1*(15-2*alpha) - 1*(6-alpha) + 1*(4-5) = 15-2*alpha-6+alpha-1 = 8-alpha = 0, so alpha=8. For D1=0 (replacing first column with constants): det[[6,1,1],[beta,5,8],[4,2,3]] = 6*(15-16)-1*(3*beta-32)+1*(2*beta-20) = -6 - 3*beta+32+2*beta-20 = 6-beta = 0, so beta=6. Q=(8,6), P=(1,-2). (PQ)^2 = (8-1)^2+(6-(-2))^2 = 49+64 = 113.

subject: Physics
question:
The electrostatic potential due to an electric dipole at a distance r varies as:
(1) 1/r^3  (2) 1/r^2  (3) r  (4) 1/r
Ans_des: For an electric dipole, V = kp*cos(theta)/r^2. Therefore V is proportional to 1/r^2.

subject: Physics
question:
The work function of a substance is 3.0 eV. The longest wavelength of light that can cause the emission of photoelectrons from this substance is approximately:
(1) 414 nm  (2) 400 nm  (3) 215 nm  (4) 200 nm
Ans_des: Threshold wavelength: lambda_th = hc/phi_0 = 1240/3 nm = 413.3 nm approx 414 nm.

subject: Physics
question:
Two thermodynamical processes are shown in the figure. The molar heat capacity for process A and B are CA and CB. The molar heat capacity at constant pressure and constant volume are represented by CP and CV. Choose the correct statement.
(1) CA > CP > CV  (2) CB = inf, CA = 0  (3) CA = 0 and CB = inf  (4) CP > CV > CA = CB
Ans: None of the given options match
Ans_des: For polytropic process PV^(-x) = const. For A, x = 1 (isothermal-like slope 45 deg), CA = CV + R/2. For B, x = gamma, CB = CV + R/(gamma+1). None of the four options correctly match both values.

subject: Physics
question:
The diffraction pattern of a light of wavelength 400 nm diffracting from a slit of width 0.2 mm is focused on the focal plane of a convex lens of focal length 100 cm. The width of the 1st secondary maximum is:
(1) 0.2 mm  (2) 2 mm  (3) 0.02 mm  (4) 2 cm
Ans_des: Angular width of first secondary maximum = lambda/a. Linear width = (lambda * f) / a = (400x10^-9 * 1) / (0.2x10^-3) = 2x10^-3 m = 2 mm.

subject: Physics
question:
Primary coil of a transformer is connected to 220 V ac. Primary and secondary turns are 100 and 10 respectively. Secondary coil is connected to two series resistances of 15 kOhm and 7 kOhm. The output voltage V0 across 7 kOhm is:
(1) 15 V  (2) 22 V  (3) 44 V  (4) 7 V
Ans_des: Secondary EMF: E2 = (N2/N1)*E1 = (10/100)*220 = 22 V. V0 = 22 * (7000/22000) = 7 V. The voltage divides across 15k and 7k in series, so V0 = 22*(7/22) = 7 V.

subject: Physics
question:
The ratio of the magnitude of the kinetic energy to the potential energy of an electron in the 5th excited state of a hydrogen atom is:
(1) 4  (2) 1  (3) 1/4  (4) 1/2
Ans_des: For hydrogen atom, by virial theorem: KE = -(1/2)*PE (in magnitude). So |KE|/|PE| = 1/2. This is independent of the quantum state including the 5th excited state (n=6).

subject: Physics
question:
A particle of mass m is projected with a velocity u making an angle of 30 degrees with the horizontal. The magnitude of angular momentum of the projectile about the point of projection when the particle is at its maximum height h is:
(1) Zero  (2) mu^3/(sqrt(2)*g)  (3) (sqrt(3)/16)*(mu^3/g)  (4) (sqrt(3)/2)*(mu^2/g)
Ans_des: At maximum height, only horizontal velocity component acts: v_x = u*cos(30). Height H = u^2*sin^2(30)/(2g) = u^2/(8g). L = m*v_x*H = m*(u*sqrt(3)/2)*(u^2/(8g)) = (sqrt(3)/16)*(mu^3/g).

subject: Physics
question:
A Zener diode of breakdown voltage 10 V is used as a voltage regulator. The circuit has a 20V source, 200 ohm series resistance and 500 ohm load. The current through the Zener diode is:
(1) 30 mA  (2) 20 mA  (3) 50 mA  (4) 0
Ans_des: Voltage across Zener = 10 V (breakdown). Current through 500 ohm: i500 = 10/500 = 20 mA. Voltage across 200 ohm = 20-10 = 10 V. Current through 200 ohm: i200 = 10/200 = 50 mA. By KCL: i_Z = i200 - i500 = 50-20 = 30 mA.

subject: Physics
question:
Match List-I with List-II:
(A) Coefficient of viscosity - (I) [ML^2T^-2]
(B) Surface tension - (II) [ML^2T^-1]
(C) Angular momentum - (III) [ML^-1T^-1]
(D) Rotational kinetic energy - (IV) [ML^0T^-2]
(1) A-I,B-II,C-III,D-IV  (2) A-III,B-IV,C-II,D-I  (3) A-IV,B-III,C-II,D-I  (4) A-II,B-I,C-IV,D-III
Ans_des: Dimensions: Viscosity [eta] = [ML^-1T^-1] -> III. Surface tension [S] = [MT^-2] = [ML^0T^-2] -> IV. Angular momentum [L] = [ML^2T^-1] -> II. Rotational KE = [ML^2T^-2] -> I. Correct match: A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I.

subject: Physics
question:
Two insulated circular loops A and B of radius a carrying current I in anticlockwise direction are placed perpendicular to each other sharing the same centre. The magnitude of the magnetic induction at the centre is:
(1) sqrt(2)*mu0*I/a  (2) mu0*I/(2a)  (3) mu0*I/(sqrt(2)*a)  (4) 2*mu0*I/a
Ans_des: Each loop produces B = mu0*I/(2a) at centre. The two fields are perpendicular (loops are perpendicular to each other). Resultant B = sqrt(B1^2+B2^2) = sqrt(2)*mu0*I/(2a) = mu0*I/(sqrt(2)*a).

subject: Physics
question:
A series LR circuit connected with ac source E = 25*sin(1000t) V has a power factor of 1/sqrt(2). If the source is changed to E = 20*sin(2000t) V, the new power factor of the circuit will be:
(1) 1/sqrt(7)  (2) 1/sqrt(2)  (3) 1/sqrt(5)  (4) 1/sqrt(3)
Ans_des: For LR circuit: cos(phi) = R/sqrt(R^2+X_L^2) = 1/sqrt(2) => R = X_L. When omega doubles: X_L' = 2*X_L = 2R. New power factor = R/sqrt(R^2+(2R)^2) = R/sqrt(5R^2) = 1/sqrt(5).

subject: Physics
question:
A spherical body of mass 100 g is dropped from height 10 m, rebounds to height 5 m. The impulse of force imparted by the ground to the body is (g = 9.8 m/s^2):
(1) 43.2 kg*m/s  (2) 23.9 kg*m/s  (3) 2.39 kg*m/s  (4) 4.32 kg*m/s

subject: Physics
question:
Young's modulus of material of a wire of length L and cross-sectional area A is Y. If the length is doubled and cross-sectional area is halved then Young's modulus will be:
(1) Y  (2) 2Y  (3) Y/4  (4) 4Y
Ans_des: Young's modulus is an intrinsic property of the material, independent of its dimensions (length, cross-sectional area). So it remains Y.

subject: Physics
question:
A particle is placed at the point A of a frictionless track ABC. It is gently pushed towards right. The speed of the particle when it reaches point B is (g = 10 m/s^2, height difference A to B is 0.5 m downward):
(1) 2*sqrt(10) m/s  (2) 10 m/s  (3) sqrt(10) m/s  (4) 20 m/s
Ans_des: By conservation of mechanical energy: (1/2)mv^2 = mg*Delta_h. v = sqrt(2*g*Delta_h) = sqrt(2*10*0.5) = sqrt(10) m/s.

question:

(1) g  (2) g/2  (3) g/4  (4) g/3

subject: Physics
question:
At which temperature is the rms velocity of a hydrogen molecule equal to that of an oxygen molecule at 47 degrees C?
(1) 80 K  (2) 20 K  (3) 4 K  (4) -73 K
Ans_des: v_rms = sqrt(3RT/M). For equal v_rms: T_H2/M_H2 = T_O2/M_O2. T_H2/2 = (273+47)/32 = 320/32 = 10. T_H2 = 20 K.

subject: Physics
question:
The gravitational potential at a point above the surface of earth is -5.12x10^7 J/kg and the acceleration due to gravity at that point is 6.4 m/s^2. The height of this point above the earth's surface is (mean radius of earth = 6400 km):
(1) 540 km  (2) 1200 km  (3) 1600 km  (4) 1000 km

subject: Physics
question:
The electric field of an EM wave in free space is E = E0*cos(omega*t - k*z) i-hat. The corresponding magnetic induction vector will be:
(1) B = E0*C*cos(omega*t+kz) j-hat  (2) B = E0*C*cos(omega*t-kz) j-hat  (3) B = (E0/C)*cos(omega*t-kz) j-hat  (4) B = (E0/C)*cos(omega*t+kz) j-hat
Ans_des: E cross B must be along +z direction. E is along i-hat and wave travels in +z. By right-hand rule, B is along j-hat. Magnitude B = E0/C. Both E and B have same phase (cos(omega*t-kz)). So B = (E0/C)*cos(omega*t-kz) j-hat.

subject: Physics
question:
An electric toaster has resistance 60 ohm at room temperature 27 degrees C. It is connected to 220 V. If current reaches 2.75 A, the temperature attained is (alpha = 2x10^-4 per degree C):
(1) 1667 deg C  (2) 694 deg C  (3) 1235 deg C  (4) 1694 deg C

subject: Physics
question:
A potential divider circuit has 4V source, 3.3 kOhm series resistance, and seven 100 ohm resistors in series with output V0 taken across the last five 100 ohm resistors. The output voltage V0 is:
(1) 2 mV  (2) 0.5 V  (3) 4 V  (4) 12 mV
Ans_des: Total resistance in voltage divider chain: 3300 + 7*100 = 4000 ohm. V0 is across 5*100 = 500 ohm. V0 = 4 * (500/4000) = 0.5 V.

subject: Physics
question: Each of three blocks P, Q and R has a mass of 3 kg. Wires A and B have cross-sectional area 0.005 cm^2 and Young's modulus 2x10^11 N/m^2. Neglecting friction, the longitudinal strain on wire B is ___ x10^-4. (g = 10 m/s^2)

subject: Physics
question: The distance between object and its two times magnified real image as produced by a convex lens is 45 cm. The focal length of the lens used is ___ cm.

subject: Physics
question: A capacitor of capacitance C and potential V has energy E. It is connected to another capacitor of capacitance 2C and potential 2V. Then the loss of energy is (x/3)*E, where x is ___.
Ans_des: Initial energy: E = (1/2)CV^2 + (1/2)(2C)(2V)^2 = (1/2)CV^2 + 4CV^2 = (9/2)CV^2. Wait: E = (1/2)CV^2 (for first capacitor only). Common voltage V_eq = (Q1+Q2)/(C+2C) = (CV+4CV)/3C = 5V/3. Final energy E' = (1/2)(3C)(5V/3)^2 = (25/6)CV^2. Energy loss = (1/2)CV^2 + 4CV^2 - (25/6)CV^2 = (3+24-25)CV^2/6 = (2/6)CV^2 = (1/3)CV^2 = (2/3)*E/... = 2E/3 = (2/3)E. So x = 2.

subject: Physics
question: In a closed organ pipe, fundamental frequency is 30 Hz. Water is poured so frequency increases to 110 Hz. If pipe has cross-sectional area 2 cm^2, the amount of water poured is ___ g. (Speed of sound = 330 m/s)

subject: Physics
question: An electron of hydrogen atom in an excited state has energy En = -0.85 eV. The maximum number of allowed transitions to lower energy levels is ___.
Ans_des: En = -13.6/n^2 = -0.85 eV => n^2 = 16 => n = 4. Number of possible transitions from n=4 to lower levels = n*(n-1)/2 = 4*3/2 = 6.

subject: Physics
question: A disc of mass 5 kg, radius 2 m rotates at angular velocity 10 rad/s. An identical disc is gently placed on it along the same axis. The energy dissipated so that both rotate together is ___ J.
Ans_des: By conservation of angular momentum: I*omega = 2I*omega'. omega' = omega/2 = 5 rad/s. I = MR^2/2 = 5*4/2 = 10 kg*m^2. E_initial = (1/2)*I*omega^2 = (1/2)*10*100 = 500 J. E_final = (1/2)*2I*omega'^2 = (1/2)*20*25 = 250 J. E_loss = 500 - 250 = 250 J.

subject: Physics
question: The horizontal component of earth's magnetic field at a place is 3.5x10^-5 T. A long straight conductor carrying current of sqrt(2) A from South east to North west is placed. The force per unit length experienced by the conductor is ___ x10^-6 N/m.
Ans_des: The conductor makes 45 degrees with the horizontal magnetic field. F/l = B*I*sin(45) = 3.5x10^-5 * sqrt(2) * sin(45) = 3.5x10^-5 * sqrt(2) * (1/sqrt(2)) = 3.5x10^-5 N/m = 35x10^-6 N/m.

subject: Physics
question: The displacement and increase in velocity of a moving particle in the time interval t to (t+1)s are 125 m and 50 m/s respectively. The distance travelled by the particle in the (t+2)th second is ___ m.

subject: Physics
question: Two cells E1 (8V, 2 ohm) and E2 (2V, 4 ohm) are connected in opposition. The terminal potential difference of cell E2 is ___ V.
Ans_des: Net EMF = E1 - E2 = 8-2 = 6 V. Total resistance = r1+r2 = 2+4 = 6 ohm. Current i = 6/6 = 1 A. Terminal PD of E2 = E2 + i*r2 = 2 + 1*4 = 6 V (current charges E2).

subject: Physics
question: A ceiling fan has 3 blades of length 80 cm each rotating at 1200 rpm. Earth's magnetic field is 0.5 G and angle of dip is 30 degrees. The emf induced across the blades is N*pi x10^-5 V. The value of N is ___.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Example of vinylic halide is:
(1) Cyclohexyl halide (X on carbon attached to ring)  (2) Cyclohexenyl halide with X on C=C  (3) Cyclohexyl halide (X on ring carbon)  (4) Cyclohexylmethyl halide (CH2X group)
Ans: Vinyl halide: X directly attached to a sp2 carbon of C=C double bond (option 2)
Ans_des: A vinylic halide has the halogen directly attached to a carbon that is part of a C=C double bond (sp2 carbon). Option 2 shows X on the sp2 carbon of the double bond in cyclohexene. Options 1 and 3 are allylic halides (X on sp3 carbon adjacent to double bond). Option 4 is also allylic.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: The gas liberated on warming a salt with dil H2SO4, turns paper dipped in lead acetate black - this is a confirmatory test for sulphide ion.
Statement II: The colour turns black because of formation of lead sulphite.
(1) Both I and II false  (2) I true, II false  (3) Both true  (4) I false, II true
Ans: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans_des: H2S gas turns lead acetate paper black due to formation of PbS (lead sulphide), not lead sulphite. Pb(CH3COO)2 + H2S -> PbS (black) + 2CH3COOH. Statement I is correct as it is a confirmatory test for S^2-. Statement II is false because PbS (sulphide) forms, not PbSO3 (sulphite).

subject: Chemistry
question:
The reaction of benzoyl chloride with H2/Pd-BaSO4 to give benzaldehyde is known as:
(1) Etard reduction  (2) Wolff-Kishner reduction  (3) Stephen reduction  (4) Rosenmund reduction
Ans_des: Rosenmund reduction converts acid chlorides (acyl halides) to aldehydes using H2 gas over Pd-BaSO4 catalyst (poisoned catalyst to prevent over-reduction). C6H5COCl + H2 (Pd/BaSO4) -> C6H5CHO.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The final product A formed in the multistep reaction: Bromobenzene + (i) Mg/ether/CO2/H+ (ii) NH3/heat (iii) Br2/NaOH is:
(1) Aniline  (2) Benzamide  (3) Benzoic acid  (4) Bromobenzoyl compound
Ans_des: C6H5Br + Mg/ether, then CO2/H+ -> C6H5COOH (benzoic acid). + NH3/heat -> C6H5CONH2 (benzamide). + Br2/NaOH (Hofmann bromamide reaction) -> C6H5NH2 (aniline). Final product is aniline.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify reagent A and reagent B for converting toluene to benzaldehyde:
A + (CH3CO)2O at 273-283 K -> intermediate, then H3O+ -> benzaldehyde
B + CS2 -> intermediate, then H3O+/heat -> benzaldehyde
(1) A=CrO2Cl2, B=CrO3  (2) A=CrO3, B=CrO2Cl2  (3) A=CrO2Cl2, B=CrO2Cl2  (4) A=CrO3, B=CrO3
Ans_des: CrO3/(CH3CO)2O at low temperature gives chromate ester intermediate which on hydrolysis gives aldehyde (Etard-like, CrO3 method). CrO2Cl2 in CS2 (Etard reaction) also oxidizes methyl to aldehyde via complex formation. A = CrO3, B = CrO2Cl2.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I (Species) with List-II (Electronic distribution):
(A) Cr+2 - (I) 3d^8
(B) Mn+ - (II) 3d^3*4s^1
(C) Ni+2 - (III) 3d^4
(D) V+ - (IV) 3d^5*4s^1
(1) A-II,B-I,C-IV,D-III  (2) A-III,B-IV,C-I,D-II  (3) A-IV,B-III,C-I,D-II  (4) A-I,B-II,C-III,D-IV
Ans_des: Cr(Z=24): [Ar]3d^5 4s^1. Cr^2+: loses 4s^1 and one 3d -> [Ar]3d^4 = III. Mn(Z=25): [Ar]3d^5 4s^2. Mn+: loses 4s^1 -> [Ar]3d^5 4s^1 = IV. Ni(Z=28): [Ar]3d^8 4s^2. Ni^2+: loses 4s^2 -> [Ar]3d^8 = I. V(Z=23): [Ar]3d^3 4s^2. V+: loses 4s^1 -> [Ar]3d^3 4s^1 = II. Match: A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Diamagnetic Lanthanoid ions are:
(1) Nd3+ and Ce4+  (2) La3+ and Ce4+  (3) Nd3+ and Eu3+  (4) Lu3+ and Eu3+
Ans_des: Diamagnetic means no unpaired electrons. La(Z=57): [Xe]4f^0 -> La^3+: [Xe]4f^0 (diamagnetic). Ce(Z=58): [Xe]4f^1 5d^1 6s^2 -> Ce^4+: [Xe]4f^0 (diamagnetic). Nd^3+ has 3 unpaired 4f electrons (paramagnetic). Eu^3+ has 6 unpaired electrons (paramagnetic).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Sugar which does not give reddish brown precipitate with Fehling's reagent is:
(1) Maltose  (2) Glucose  (3) Lactose  (4) Sucrose
Ans_des: Fehling's reagent tests for reducing sugars (free aldehyde or ketone group). In sucrose, the aldehyde group of glucose and ketone group of fructose are both involved in the glycosidic linkage, so there is no free carbonyl group. Sucrose is a non-reducing sugar and does not react with Fehling's reagent.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I (Molecule) with List-II (Shape):
(A) BrF5 - (I) T-shape
(B) H2O - (II) See saw
(C) ClF3 - (III) Bent
(D) SF4 - (IV) Square pyramidal
(1) A-I,B-II,C-IV,D-III  (2) A-II,B-I,C-III,D-IV  (3) A-III,B-IV,C-I,D-II  (4) A-IV,B-III,C-I,D-II
Ans_des: BrF5: 6 electron pairs (5 bonds + 1 lone pair) -> octahedral electron geometry -> square pyramidal shape (IV). H2O: 4 electron pairs (2 bonds + 2 lone pairs) -> tetrahedral electron geometry -> bent shape (III). ClF3: 5 electron pairs (3 bonds + 2 lone pairs) -> trigonal bipyramidal -> T-shape (I). SF4: 5 electron pairs (4 bonds + 1 lone pair) -> see saw (II). Match: A-IV, B-III, C-I, D-II.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Structure of 4-Methylpent-2-enal is:
(1) CH3-CH2-C(CH3)=CH-CHO  (2) CH3-CH(CH3)-CH=CH-CHO  (3) H2C=C(H)-CH(CH3)-CH2-CHO  (4) CH3-CH2-CH=C(CH3)-CHO
Ans_des: 4-Methylpent-2-enal: parent chain = pentanal (5C, -al ending), double bond at C2, methyl group at C4. Numbering from CHO end: C1=CHO, C2=CH=, C3=CH-, C4=CH(CH3)-, C5=CH3. Structure: CH3-CH(CH3)-CH=CH-CHO. This is option (2).

subject: Chemistry
question:
In the reaction CH3-C(triple bond)CH + Na -> A, then A + B -> CH3-C(triple bond)C-CH2CH2CH3 + NaBr. Find A and B:
(1) A=CH3-C(triple bond)C-Na+, B=CH3CH2CH2Br
(2) A=CH3-C(triple bond)C-Na+, B=CH3CH2CH3
(3) A=CH3CH2CH3, B=CH3-C(triple bond)CH
(4) A=CH3-CH=CH2, B=CH3CH2CH2Br
Ans_des: CH3-C(triple bond)CH + Na -> CH3-C(triple bond)C-Na+ (sodium propynide, A, a strong base/nucleophile). A + CH3CH2CH2Br (B) -> CH3-C(triple bond)C-CH2CH2CH3 + NaBr (nucleophilic substitution). Answer is option (1).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Choose the correct statements:
(A) Ethane-1,2-diamine is a chelating ligand
(B) Metallic aluminium is produced by electrolysis of Al2O3 in presence of cryolite
(C) Cyanide ion is used as ligand for leaching of silver
(D) Phosphine acts as a ligand in Wilkinson catalyst
(E) Stability constants of Ca2+ and Mg2+ are similar with EDTA
(1) C,D,E only  (2) A,B,C only  (3) A,D,E only  (4) B,C,E only
Ans_des: A: en (H2N-CH2-CH2-NH2) is bidentate chelating ligand - TRUE. B: Hall-Heroult process uses electrolysis of Al2O3 dissolved in molten cryolite - TRUE. C: Ag2S + CN- -> [Ag(CN)2]- (cyanide leaches silver) - TRUE. D: Wilkinson catalyst is RhCl(PPh3)3 - phosphine (PPh3, not PH3) - D is debatable but PH3 is not the ligand here - partially FALSE. E: Ca2+-EDTA and Mg2+-EDTA have different stability constants - FALSE. Correct: A, B, C.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: Orbitals having same energy are called degenerate orbitals.
Statement II: In hydrogen atom, 3p and 3d orbitals are not degenerate.
(1) Both true  (2) Both false  (3) I true, II false  (4) I false, II true
Ans: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans_des: Statement I: Correct definition - degenerate orbitals have same energy. Statement II: In hydrogen atom (one-electron system), energy depends only on n (principal quantum number). So 3s, 3p, and 3d all have the same energy (n=3) and are degenerate. Statement II is FALSE.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion (A): CH2=CH-CH2-Cl is an example of allyl halide.
Reason (R): Allyl halides are compounds in which halogen atom is attached to sp2 hybridised carbon.
(1) Both A and R true, R correct explanation  (2) A false, R true  (3) A true, R false  (4) Both true, R not correct explanation
Ans: A is true but R is false
Ans_des: A: CH2=CH-CH2-Cl has Cl on the carbon adjacent to double bond (allylic position). This IS an allyl halide - TRUE. R: In allyl halides, halogen is attached to sp3 hybridised carbon (the allylic carbon), NOT sp2 carbon. The sp3 carbon is adjacent to the C=C. R is FALSE.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Confirmatory test for aromatic primary amines. Identify reagent A and B:
Aromatic amine + A -> diazonium salt + NaOH with B -> scarlet red dye
(1) A=HNO3/H2SO4; B=phenol  (2) A=NaNO2+HCl,0-5C; B=aniline  (3) A=NaNO2+HCl,0-5C; B=alpha-naphthol  (4) A=NaNO2+HCl,0-5C; B=beta-naphthol
Ans: A = NaNO2 + HCl at 0-5 degrees C; B = beta-naphthol
Ans_des: Step A: Diazotization - aromatic primary amine + NaNO2/HCl at 0-5 degrees C gives diazonium salt. Step B: Coupling reaction - diazonium salt + beta-naphthol in NaOH gives azo dye (scarlet red). This is the coupling reaction, and beta-naphthol gives the most intense scarlet red colour.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The Lassaigne's extract is boiled with dil HNO3 before testing for halogens because:
(1) Silver halides are soluble in HNO3  (2) Ag2S is soluble in HNO3  (3) Na2S and NaCN are decomposed by HNO3  (4) AgCN is soluble in HNO3
Ans: Na2S and NaCN are decomposed by HNO3
Ans_des: The Lassaigne's extract may contain Na2S and NaCN (if S and N are present in compound). If not removed, Na2S would give black Ag2S precipitate and NaCN would give white AgCN precipitate, interfering with halide testing. Boiling with dil HNO3 decomposes: NaCN + HNO3 -> NaNO3 + HCN (gas); Na2S + 2HNO3 -> 2NaNO3 + H2S (gas).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: Considerable increase in covalent radius from N to P. However from As to Bi only small increase in covalent radius.
Reason R: Covalent and ionic radii in a particular oxidation state increase down the group.
(1) Both true, R not correct explanation  (2) A true, R false  (3) Both true, R correct explanation  (4) A false, R true
Ans: Both A and R are true and R is not the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: A: True - N to P shows large increase; As to Bi shows very small increase due to d and f orbital electrons increasing effective nuclear charge. R: True - radii generally increase down group due to increasing shell number. But R does not explain why As to Bi has smaller increment (that is due to d/f orbital shielding effects, not captured by R). So both true but R is not the correct explanation.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Aluminium chloride in acidified aqueous solution forms an ion having geometry:
(1) Octahedral  (2) Tetrahedral  (3) Square planar  (4) Trigonal bipyramidal
Ans_des: AlCl3 in acidified aqueous solution forms [Al(H2O)6]^3+ ion. Al^3+ is coordinated to 6 water molecules. Hybridisation of Al^3+ = sp3d2, geometry = octahedral.

subject: Chemistry
question:
What happens to freezing point of benzene when small quantity of naphthalene is added?
(1) First decreases then increases  (2) Increases  (3) Decreases  (4) Remains unchanged
Ans_des: Adding a non-volatile solute (naphthalene) to a solvent (benzene) lowers the vapour pressure of the solution. Since vapour pressure decreases, the freezing point also decreases (colligative property - depression in freezing point). Delta_Tf = Kf * m.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following molecule/species is most stable?
(1) Cyclopropenium cation (3-membered ring with +)  (2) Cyclopentadienyl cation (5-membered with +)  (3) Cyclopropene with lone pair  (4) Cyclohexadiene
Ans_des: Aromaticity requires planar, cyclic, conjugated system with 4n+2 pi electrons (Huckel rule). Cyclopropenium cation (C3H3+): 3-membered ring, 2 pi electrons (n=0, 4*0+2=2) - AROMATIC, most stable. Cyclopentadienyl cation: 4 pi electrons - anti-aromatic. Cyclopropene with lone pair: 4 pi electrons - anti-aromatic. Cyclohexadiene: non-aromatic (not fully conjugated).

subject: Chemistry
question: On a thin layer chromatographic plate, an organic compound moved by 3.5 cm while the solvent moved by 5 cm. The retardation factor is ___ x10^-1.
Ans_des: Rf = distance travelled by solute / distance travelled by solvent = 3.5/5 = 0.7 = 7 x10^-1. Answer = 7.

subject: Chemistry
question: 2MnO4- + bI- + cH2O -> xI2 + yMnO2 + zOH-. If balanced with integer coefficients, the value of z is ___.
Ans_des: Mn goes from +7 to +4 (gain 3e- each, total 6e- for 2 Mn). I- goes from -1 to 0 (lose 1e- each). 6 I- needed. Balanced: 2MnO4- + 6I- + 4H2O -> 3I2 + 2MnO2 + 8OH-. z = 8.

subject: Chemistry
question: An ideal gas undergoes cyclic transformation A->B->C->A (triangle on P-V diagram with A at (10,10), B at (10,30), C at (30,10) in kPa and dm^3). The total work done in the process is ___ J.
Ans_des: Work done in a cyclic process = area enclosed in P-V diagram. Shape is a right triangle with base = 20 dm^3 = 0.02 m^3 and height = 20 kPa = 20000 Pa. Area = (1/2) x 0.02 x 20000 = 200 J.

subject: Chemistry
question: The mass of sodium acetate (CH3COONa) required to prepare 250 mL of 0.35 M aqueous solution is ___ g. (Molar mass = 82.02 g/mol)
Ans_des: Moles of CH3COONa = Molarity x Volume (L) = 0.35 x 0.250 = 0.0875 mol. Mass = 0.0875 x 82.02 = 7.18 g approx 7 g.

subject: Chemistry
question: If IUPAC name of an element is Unununnium then the element belongs to the nth group of Periodic table. The value of n is ___.
Ans_des: IUPAC name Unununnium: un=1, un=1, un=1, ium=element. Atomic number = 111. Element 111 (Roentgenium) is in Group 11 of the periodic table (same group as Cu, Ag, Au). n = 11.

subject: Chemistry
question: The compound formed by the reaction of ethanal with semicarbazide contains ___ number of nitrogen atoms.
Ans_des: Ethanal (CH3CHO) + semicarbazide (H2N-NH-CO-NH2) -> semicarbazone (CH3CH=N-NH-CO-NH2) + H2O. The product semicarbazone contains 3 nitrogen atoms: N (=N-), N (-NH-), N (-NH2). Answer = 3.

subject: Chemistry
question: The total number of molecular orbitals from 2s and 2p atomic orbitals of a diatomic molecule is ___.
Ans_des: From 2 atoms: 2 x 2s = 2 AOs -> 2 MOs (sigma2s, sigma*2s). 2 x 2p (3 each) = 6 AOs -> 6 MOs (sigma2pz, pi2px, pi2py, sigma*2pz, pi*2px, pi*2py). Total = 2 + 6 = 8 MOs.

subject: Chemistry
question: The pH at which Mg(OH)2 (Ksp = 1x10^-11) begins to precipitate from a solution containing 0.10 M Mg2+ ions is ___.
Ans_des: For precipitation to begin: Ksp = [Mg2+][OH-]^2. 1x10^-11 = 0.1 x [OH-]^2. [OH-]^2 = 10^-10. [OH-] = 10^-5 M. pOH = 5. pH = 14 - 5 = 9.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If f(x) is a 3x3 determinant with entries involving cos^4(x), sin^4(x), sin^2(2x), then (1/5)*f'(0) is equal to:
(1) 1  (2) 2  (3) 6  (4) 0
Ans_des: f(x) = det of 3x3 matrix. When we differentiate det and evaluate at x=0, each derivative term produces a matrix where one row is differentiated. At x=0: cos^4(0)=1, sin^4(0)=0, sin^2(0)=0. The differentiated row at x=0 gives factors of 0 from sin terms, making each 3x3 determinant = 0. Therefore f'(0) = 0 and (1/5)*f'(0) = 0.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f: [-pi/2, pi/2] -> R be differentiable with f(0) = 1/2. If the limit as x->0 of [x * integral from 0 to x of f(t)dt] / (e^(x^2) - 1) = alpha, then 8*alpha^2 equals:
(1) 1  (2) 16  (3) 4  (4) 2
Ans_des: The limit is 0/0 form. Applying L'Hopital: [integral(0 to x)f(t)dt + x*f(x)] / (2x*e^(x^2)). This is still 0/0. Apply again: [f(x) + f(x) + x*f'(x)] / (2*e^(x^2) + 4x^2*e^(x^2)) -> [2*f(0)] / 2 = f(0) = 1/2. So alpha = 1/2 and 8*alpha^2 = 8*(1/4) = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If 2*sin^3(x) + sin(2x)*cos(x) + 4*sin(x) - 4 = 0 has exactly 3 solutions in [0, n*pi/2], n in N, then roots of x^2 + n*x + (n-3) = 0 belong to:
(1) (-inf, 0)  (2) Z  (3) (-sqrt(17)/2, sqrt(17)/2)  (4) (0, inf)
Ans_des: Simplify: 2sin^3(x) + 2sin(x)cos^2(x) + 4sin(x) - 4 = 0 => 2sin(x)(sin^2+cos^2) + 4sin(x) = 4 => 2sin(x) + 4sin(x) = 4... Wait: 2sin^3+2sinx*cos^2x = 2sinx(sin^2+cos^2) = 2sinx. So 2sinx + 4sinx - 4 = 0... recalculate: sin^3+sinx(1-sin^2x)+2sinx-2=0 => 3sinx=2 => sinx=2/3. For exactly 3 solutions in [0, n*pi/2]: n=5. Equation: x^2+5x+2=0. x = (-5+-sqrt(17))/2, both negative. Roots in (-inf, 0).

subject: Mathematics
question:
If domain of f(x) = cos^-1((2-|x|)/4) + (log_e(3-x))^-1 is [-alpha, beta) - {gamma}, then alpha + beta + gamma equals:
(1) 8  (2) 12  (3) 9  (4) 11
Ans_des: For cos^-1: -1 <= (2-|x|)/4 <= 1 => |x| <= 6 => x in [-6,6]. For log: 3-x > 0 => x < 3. Also 3-x != 1 => x != 2. Combined: x in [-6,3) - {2}. alpha=6, beta=3, gamma=2. alpha+beta+gamma = 11.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If circles (x+1)^2+(y+2)^2=r^2 and x^2+y^2-4x-4y+4=0 intersect at exactly two distinct points, then:
(1) 1/2 < r < 7  (2) 3 < r < 7  (3) 5 < r < 9  (4) 0 < r < 7
Ans_des: Circle 2: centre (2,2), radius = sqrt(4+4-4) = 2. Circle 1: centre (-1,-2), radius r. Distance between centres c1c2 = sqrt(9+16) = 5. For two distinct intersections: |r1-r2| < c1c2 < r1+r2. |r-2| < 5 and r+2 > 5. From |r-2|<5: -3<r<7. From r>3. Combined: 3<r<7.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the length of the minor axis of an ellipse equals half the distance between the foci, then eccentricity is:
(1) 1/sqrt(3)  (2) 2/sqrt(5)  (3) sqrt(3)/2  (4) sqrt(5)/3
Ans_des: 2b = 2ae => ae = 2b... Wait: minor axis length = 2b, distance between foci = 2ae. Condition: 2b = (1/2)*(2ae) => 2b = ae. So 4b^2 = a^2*e^2. Using b^2 = a^2(1-e^2): 4a^2(1-e^2) = a^2*e^2 => 4-4e^2 = e^2 => 5e^2 = 4 => e = 2/sqrt(5).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Value of limit as n->inf of sum(k=1 to n) of n^3/[(n^2+k^2)(n^2+3k^2)] is:
(1) pi/(8*(2*sqrt(3)+3))  (2) (2*sqrt(3)+3)*pi/24  (3) 13*pi/(8*(4*sqrt(3)+3))  (4) 13*(2*sqrt(3)-3)*pi/8
Ans_des: Rewrite sum as (1/n)*sum[1/((1+k^2/n^2)(1+3k^2/n^2))]. This converts to integral from 0 to 1 of 1/[(1+x^2)(1+3x^2)]dx. Using partial fractions and evaluating: = (1/2)*[sqrt(3)*tan^-1(sqrt(3)*x) - tan^-1(x)] from 0 to 1 = (1/2)*(sqrt(3)*pi/3 - pi/4) = pi/(2*sqrt(3)) - pi/8 = 13*pi/(8*(4*sqrt(3)+3)).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Consider the system: x+y+z=4mu, x+2y+2*lambda*z=10*mu, x+3y+4*lambda^2*z=mu^2+15. Which statement is NOT correct?
(1) Infinite solutions if lambda=1/2 and mu=15
(2) Consistent if lambda not=1/2
(3) Inconsistent if lambda=1/2 and mu not=1
(4) Unique solution if lambda not=1/2 and mu not=1,15
Ans: The system is inconsistent if lambda=1/2 and mu not=1
Ans_des: Determinant = (2*lambda-1)^2. For lambda not=1/2: det not=0, unique solution (options 2 and 4 correct). For lambda=1/2: system reduces to conditions. Solving gives (mu-1)(mu-15)=0. So for lambda=1/2 and mu not=1 or 15: inconsistent. But option 3 says mu not=1 (missing mu not=15 case). If lambda=1/2 and mu=15, system is consistent with infinite solutions (option 1 correct). If mu not=1 AND mu not=15, then inconsistent. Statement 3 is NOT fully correct (ignores mu=15 case). Ans: Option 3 is NOT correct.

Ans: f'(x) = 0 for at least two x in (0, 2)

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let y=y(x) be solution of sec(x)*dy + {2(1-x)tan(x) + x(2-x)}dx = 0 with y(0)=2. Then y(2) equals:
(1) 2{sin(2)+1}  (2) 1  (3) 2{1-sin(2)}  (4) 2
Ans_des: Rewrite: dy = [2(x-1)sin(x) + x(x-2)cos(x)]dx. Integrating: y = integral of d[(x^2-2x)*sin(x)] = (x^2-2x)*sin(x) + C. At y(0)=2: C=2. y(x) = (x^2-2x)*sin(x) + 2. y(2) = (4-4)*sin(2) + 2 = 0 + 2 = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
A line through A(9,0) makes 30 degrees with positive x-axis. It is rotated 15 degrees clockwise about A. Equation in new position:
(1) y/(sqrt(3)-2) + x = 9  (2) y/(sqrt(3)-2) + y = 9  (3) y/(sqrt(3)+2) + x = 9  (4) y/(sqrt(3)+2) + y = 9
Ans_des: New inclination = 30-15 = 15 degrees. Slope = tan(15) = 2-sqrt(3). Equation: y-0 = (2-sqrt(3))*(x-9). y = (2-sqrt(3))*(x-9). Rearranging: x + y/(sqrt(3)-2) = 9 (since 1/(2-sqrt(3)) = 2+sqrt(3)... multiply: y/(2-sqrt(3)) = y*(2+sqrt(3)) and x = 9 + y/(sqrt(3)-2) => x + y/(sqrt(3)-2) = 9).

subject: Mathematics
question:
If z = x+iy (xy not=0) satisfies z^2 + i*z-bar = 0, then |z^2| equals:
(1) 4  (2) 9  (3) 1  (4) 1/4
Ans_des: z^2 + i*z-bar = 0 => z^2 = -i*z-bar => |z^2| = |i||z-bar| = |z|. So |z|^2 = |z| => |z|(|z|-1) = 0. Since xy not=0, z not=0, so |z| = 1. Therefore |z^2| = |z|^2 = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question: A group of 40 students took exam in 3 subjects. All passed at least one. n(M)=20, n(P)=25, n(C)=16. n(M^P)<=11, n(P^C)<=15, n(M^C)<=15. Maximum students who passed all three is ___.
Ans_des: Using inclusion-exclusion: n(M)+n(P)+n(C) - n(M^P) - n(P^C) - n(M^C) + n(M^P^C) = 40. 20+25+16 - n(M^P) - n(P^C) - n(M^C) + x = 40. 61 - (n(M^P)+n(P^C)+n(M^C)) + x = 40. To maximize x, minimize (n(M^P)+n(P^C)+n(M^C)). Setting each at maximum allowed: 11+15+15=41. 61-41+x=40 => x=20? But must check each region is non-negative. Trying x=10: 61-11-15-15+10=30 not 40. Setting up correctly: with x=10, n(M^P)=11, n(P^C)=15, n(M^C)=15: 61-11-15-15+10=30 not 40. After careful Venn diagram analysis with all constraints satisfied, maximum x=10.

question: Value of 9 * integral from 0 to 9 of [sqrt(10x/(x+1))] dx, where [t] = greatest integer function, is ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Number of integral terms in the expansion of {7^(1/2) + 11^(1/6)}^824 equals ___.
Ans_des: General term: T_(r+1) = C(824,r) * 7^((824-r)/2) * 11^(r/6). For integral terms: (824-r)/2 and r/6 both integers. r/6 integer => r = 0,6,12,...,822. (824-r)/2 integer: 824-r even => r even. All multiples of 6 are even. So r = 0,6,12,...,822. Number of terms = 822/6 + 1 = 137+1 = 138.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let A = {1,2,3,...,7} and P(A) its power set. Number of functions f: A->P(A) such that a in f(a) for all a in A is m^n. Then m+n equals ___.
Ans_des: For each a in A, f(a) must be a subset of A containing a. Number of subsets of A containing a = 2^(7-1) = 2^6 = 64 (choose freely among the other 6 elements). For 7 elements: total functions = 64^7 = (2^6)^7 = 2^42. m^n = 2^42. m=2 (smallest), n=42. m+n = 44.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let alpha = 1^2+4^2+8^2+13^2+19^2+26^2+... (10 terms) and beta = sum(n=1 to 10) of n^4. If 4*alpha - beta = 55k+40, then k equals ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: If d1 is shortest distance between lines x+1=2y=-12z, x=y+2=6z-6 and d2 is shortest distance between (x-1)/2=(y+8)/(-7)=(z-4)/5 and (x-1)/2=(y-2)/1=(z-6)/(-3), then 32*sqrt(3)*d1/d2 equals ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: If f(x) = 1/|x| for |x|>=2 and ax^2+2b for |x|<2 is differentiable on R, then 48*(a+b) equals ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let latus rectum of hyperbola x^2/9 - y^2/b^2 = 1 subtend angle pi/3 at centre. If b^2 = (A/m)*(1+sqrt(n)), A and m co-prime, then A^2+m^2+n^2 equals ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let y=y(x) be solution of (1-x^2)dy = [xy + (x^3+2)*sqrt(3(1-x^2))]dx, -1<x<1, y(0)=0. If y(1/2) = m/n (co-prime), then m+n equals ___.
Ans_des: Rewrite: dy/dx + (x/(x^2-1))*y = (x^3+2)*sqrt(3)/(sqrt(1-x^2)). IF = e^(integral x/(x^2-1)dx) = e^(1/2*ln|x^2-1|) = sqrt(1-x^2) (taking negative). Solution: y*sqrt(1-x^2) = sqrt(3)*integral(x^3+2)dx + C = sqrt(3)*(x^4/4+2x) + C. y(0)=0 => C=0. y(1/2)*sqrt(3/4) = sqrt(3)*(1/64+1) = sqrt(3)*65/64. y(1/2) = (sqrt(3)*65/64)/(sqrt(3)/2) = 65/32. m=65, n=32 (co-prime). m+n=97.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let alpha, beta in N be roots of x^2-70x+lambda=0 where lambda/2, lambda/3 not in N. If lambda is minimum possible, (sqrt(alpha-1)+sqrt(beta-1))*(lambda+35)/|alpha-beta| equals ___.
Ans_des: alpha+beta=70, alpha*beta=lambda. lambda not divisible by 2 or 3 => alpha, beta not divisible by 2 or 3. Minimize lambda = alpha*(70-alpha). Lambda increasing for alpha<=35. Minimum with constraints: alpha=1,beta=69 (69 div by 3, rejected). alpha=5,beta=65: lambda=5*65=325. Check: 325/2=162.5 not N; 325/3=108.3 not N. Valid! |alpha-beta|=60. sqrt(alpha-1)+sqrt(beta-1)=sqrt(4)+sqrt(64)=2+8=10. lambda+35=360. Result = 10*360/60 = 60.
