Ans_des: Ans_des

subject: Physics
question:
For the photoelectric effect, the maximum kinetic energy (Ek) of the photoelectrons is plotted against the frequency (v) of the incident photons. The slope of the graph gives:
(1) Charge of electron
(2) Work function of the metal
(3) Ratio of Planck's constant to electric charge
(4) Planck's constant
Ans_des: For photoelectric effect: hv = Ek + phi_0, so Ek = hv - phi_0. Comparing with y = mx + c, slope m = h (Planck's constant).

subject: Physics
question:
An alternating voltage V(t) = 220 sin(100*pi*t) volt is applied to a purely resistive load of 50 ohm. The time taken for the current to rise from half of the peak value to the peak value is:
(1) 3.3 ms
(2) 2.2 ms
(3) 5 ms
(4) 7.2 ms
Ans_des: i = I0 sin(100*pi*t). Time to reach I0/2 from 0: I0/2 = I0 sin(100*pi*t1), so t1 = 1/600 s. Time to reach I0 from 0: t2 = T/4 = 1/200 s. Delta_t = 1/200 - 1/600 = 3.3 x 10^-3 s = 3.3 ms.

subject: Physics
question:
Projectiles A and B are thrown at angles of 45 degrees and 60 degrees with the vertical respectively from the top of a 400 m high tower. If their ranges and times of flight are the same, the ratio of their speeds of projection vA : vB is: [Take g = 10 m/s^2]
(1) 1 : sqrt(3)
(2) 1 : sqrt(2)
(3) sqrt(2) : 1
(4) 1 : 2
Ans_des: For same time of flight, vertical component of velocity must be same. vA sin45 = vB sin30, so vA*(1/sqrt(2)) = vB*(1/2), giving vA/vB = 1/sqrt(2), i.e., vA : vB = 1 : sqrt(2).

subject: Physics
question:
If mass is written as m = k * c^p * G^(-1/2) * h^(1/2), then the value of P will be: (Constants have their usual meaning with k a dimensionless constant)
(1) -1/3
(2) 1/2
(3) 2
(4) 1/3
Ans_des: [M] = [LT^-1]^P * [M^-1 L^3 T^-2]^(-1/2) * [ML^2 T^-1]^(1/2). Solving the dimensional equation gives P = 1/2.

subject: Physics
question:
A particle of charge '-q' and mass 'm' moves in a circle of radius 'r' around an infinitely long line charge of linear charge density '+lambda'. The time period will be: (Consider k as Coulomb's constant)
(1) T = (1/2pi*r) * sqrt(m / 2k*lambda*q)
(2) T^2 = (4pi^2 * m / 2k*lambda*q) * r^3
(3) T = 2pi*r * sqrt(m / 2k*lambda*q)
(4) T = (1/2pi) * sqrt(2k*lambda*q / m)
Ans_des: Centripetal force = Electric force: mv^2/r = lambda*q / (2*pi*epsilon_0*r). Solving: v = sqrt(lambda*q / (2*m*epsilon_0)). Time period T = 2*pi*r / v = 2*pi*r * sqrt(m / (2k*lambda*q)).

subject: Physics
question:
Match List I with List II:
List I: A. Gauss's law of magnetostatics, B. Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, C. Ampere's law, D. Gauss's law of electrostatics
List II: I. Closed integral E.da = (1/epsilon_0) * integral rho dv, II. Closed integral B.da = 0, III. Closed integral E.dl = -d/dt (integral B.da), IV. Closed integral B.dl = mu_0 * I
Options:
(1) A-I, B-III, C-IV, D-II
(2) A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
(3) A-IV, B-II, C-III, D-I
(4) A-II, B-III, C-IV, D-I
Ans_des: Gauss's law of magnetostatics: Closed integral B.da = 0 (II). Faraday's law: Closed integral E.dl = -d/dt (integral B.da) (III). Ampere's law: Closed integral B.dl = mu_0*I (IV). Gauss's law of electrostatics: Closed integral E.da = (1/epsilon_0) integral rho dv (I).

subject: Physics
question:
A beam of unpolarised light of intensity I0 is passed through a Polaroid A and then through another Polaroid B which is oriented so that its principal plane makes an angle of 45 degrees relative to that of A. The intensity of emergent light is:
(1) I0/8
(2) I0/4
(3) I0/2
(4) I0
Ans_des: After Polaroid A: I1 = I0/2. After Polaroid B (Malus's law): I2 = I1 * cos^2(45 deg) = (I0/2) * (1/2) = I0/4.

subject: Physics
question:
An electron revolving in the nth Bohr orbit has magnetic moment mu_n. If mu_n is proportional to n^x, the value of x is:
(1) 1
(2) 3
(3) 0
(4) 2
Ans_des: Magnetic moment of electron in nth orbit = ehn / (4*pi*m_e), which is directly proportional to n. Therefore x = 1.

subject: Physics
question:
If the total energy transferred to a surface in time t is 6.48 x 10^5 J, then the magnitude of the total momentum delivered to this surface for complete absorption will be:
(1) 2.46 x 10^-3 kg m/s
(2) 4.32 x 10^-3 kg m/s
(3) 1.58 x 10^-3 kg m/s
(4) 2.16 x 10^-3 kg m/s
Ans_des: For complete absorption: E = p*c, so p = E/c = (6.48 x 10^5) / (3 x 10^8) = 2.16 x 10^-3 kg m/s.

subject: Physics
question:
In a nuclear fission reaction of an isotope of mass M, three similar daughter nuclei of the same mass are formed. The speed of a daughter nucleus in terms of mass defect delta_M will be:
(1) c * sqrt(3*delta_M / M)
(2) sqrt(2c*delta_M / M)
(3) delta_M*c^2 / 3
(4) c * sqrt(2*delta_M / M)
Ans_des: (M - 3m)c^2 = 3 * (1/2) * m*V^2. Also delta_M = M - 3m, m = (M - delta_M)/3. Solving: V = c * sqrt(2*delta_M / M).

subject: Physics
question:
Escape velocity of a body from earth is 11.2 km/s. If the radius of a planet be one third the radius of earth and mass be one-sixth that of earth, the escape velocity from the planet is:
(1) 4.2 km/s
(2) 8.4 km/s
(3) 7.9 km/s
(4) 11.2 km/s
Ans_des: Escape velocity V = sqrt(2GM/R). Vp = sqrt(2G*(Me/6) / (Re/3)) = sqrt(2GMe / (2Re)) = Ve / sqrt(2) = 11.2 / sqrt(2) = approx 7.9 km/s.

subject: Physics
question:
Choose the correct statement for processes A and B shown in a P-V diagram where curve B has steeper slope than curve A:
(1) PV = k for process B and A
(2) P^(gamma-1) / T^gamma = k for process B and T = k for process A
(3) T^gamma / P^(gamma-1) = k for process A and PV = k for process B
(4) PV^gamma = k for process B and PV = k for process A
Ans: PV^gamma = k for process B and PV = k for process A; also P^(gamma-1)/T^gamma = k for process B and T = k for process A
Ans_des: Slope of curve B > slope of curve A, so A is isothermal and B is adiabatic. For adiabatic (B): PV^gamma = constant, which also gives T^gamma / P^(gamma-1) = constant. For isothermal (A): T = constant, so PV = constant.

subject: Physics
question:
A block of mass m is placed on a surface having vertical cross-section given by y = x^2/4. If the coefficient of friction is 0.5, the maximum height above the ground at which block can be placed without slipping is:
(1) 1/2 m
(2) 1/6 m
(3) 1/3 m
(4) 1/4 m
Ans_des: At equilibrium, tan(theta) = mu = 0.5. dy/dx = x/2 = 0.5, so x = 1. y = (1)^2/4 = 1/4 m.

subject: Physics
question:
In the given circuit with diodes D1 (Ge) and D2 (Si) in series with a 1.5 kohm resistor, and a load resistance RL = 2.5 kohm, connected to a 15 V source, the voltage across RL is:
(1) 14.00 V
(2) 9.00 V
(3) 8.50 V
(4) 8.75 V

subject: Physics
question:
If three moles of monoatomic gas (gamma = 5/3) is mixed with two moles of a diatomic gas (gamma = 7/5), the value of adiabatic exponent gamma for the mixture is:
(1) 1.35
(2) 1.52
(3) 1.75
(4) 1.40

subject: Physics
question:
A block of mass 1 kg is pushed up a surface inclined at 60 degrees by a force of 10 N parallel to the surface. When the block is pushed up 10 m along the inclined surface, the work done against frictional force is [g = 10 m/s^2, mu_s = 0.1]:
(1) 5 J
(2) 5*sqrt(3) J
(3) 5 x 10^3 J
(4) 10 J
Ans_des: Friction force f = mu * m * g * cos(theta) = 0.1 * 1 * 10 * cos(60 deg) = 0.1 * 10 * 0.5 = 0.5 N. Work done against friction = f * d = 0.5 * 10 = 5 J.

subject: Physics
question:
A block of ice at -10 degrees C is slowly heated and converted to steam at 100 degrees C. Which of the following curves represents the phenomenon qualitatively (Temperature vs Heat supplied)?
(1) Curve starting high then gradual slope
(2) Curve with two plateaus and steep segments
(3) Curve with two plateaus at lower temperatures
(4) Curve with one plateau
Ans: Option (3): Two horizontal plateaus (at 0 deg C and 100 deg C) with sloped segments in between
Ans_des: Ice(-10 C) heats up (T rises) -> Ice melts at 0 C (T constant) -> Water heats up (T rises) -> Water boils at 100 C (T constant) -> Steam forms. So the graph has two plateaus at 0 C and 100 C with sloped regions in between.

subject: Physics
question:
Three blocks A, B and C are pulled on a horizontal smooth surface by a force of 80 N. Masses: A = 5 kg, B = 3 kg, C = 2 kg. The tensions T1 (between A and B) and T2 (between B and C) in the strings are respectively:
(1) 80 N, 100 N
(2) 88 N, 96 N
(3) 40 N, 64 N
(4) 60 N, 80 N
Ans_des: Total mass = 5+3+2 = 10 kg. Acceleration a = 80/10 = 8 m/s^2. T1 pulls A (5 kg): T1 = 5*8 = 40 N. T2 pulls A and B together (8 kg): T2 = 8*8 = 64 N.

subject: Physics
question:
If 50 Vernier divisions are equal to 49 main scale divisions of a traveling microscope and one smallest reading of main scale is 0.5 mm, the Vernier constant of the traveling microscope is:
(1) 0.01 mm
(2) 0.1 cm
(3) 0.01 cm
(4) 0.1 mm
Ans_des: MSD = 0.5 mm. Least count LC = MSD - VSD = MSD/50 = 0.5/50 = 0.01 mm.

subject: Physics
question:
When a potential difference V is applied across a wire of resistance R, it dissipates energy at a rate W. If the wire is cut into two halves and these halves are connected mutually parallel across the same supply, the energy dissipation rate will become:
(1) W/2
(2) 4W
(3) W/4
(4) 2W
Ans_des: Initially W = V^2/R. After cutting into two halves and connecting in parallel, equivalent resistance = R/4. New power W' = V^2 / (R/4) = 4V^2/R = 4W.

subject: Physics
question: Two identical charged spheres are suspended by strings of equal lengths making an angle of 37 degrees with each other. When suspended in a liquid of density 0.7 g/cm^3, the angle remains same. If density of material of sphere is 1.4 g/cm^3, the dielectric constant of the liquid is ___.
Ans_des: For angle to remain same in liquid: Fe_liquid / W_liquid = Fe_air / W_air. Dielectric constant k = rho / (rho - sigma) = 1.4 / (1.4 - 0.7) = 1.4 / 0.7 = 2.

subject: Physics
question: A point source is emitting sound waves of intensity 16 x 10^-8 W/m^2 at the origin. The difference in intensity (magnitude only) at two points located at distances of 2 m and 4 m from the origin respectively will be ___ x 10^-8 W/m^2.

subject: Physics
question: A power transmission line feeds input power at 2.3 kV to a step-down transformer with 3000 primary turns. Output is at 230 V. Primary current is 5 A and efficiency is 90%. The output current of the transformer is ___ A.
Ans_des: Input power Pi = Vi * Ii = 2300 * 5 = 11500 W. Output power Po = 0.9 * 11500 = 10350 W. Output current Io = Po / Vo = 10350 / 230 = 45 A.

subject: Physics
question: In an experiment to measure the focal length (f) of a convex lens, magnitudes of object distance (x) and image distance (y) are measured with reference to the focal point. The y-x plot is given. The focal length of the lens is ___ cm.
Ans_des: Using Newton's lens formula: x1 * x2 = f^2. From the graph, x1*x2 = f^2 gives f = 20 cm.

subject: Physics
question: The current of 5 A flows in a square loop of sides 1 m placed in air. The magnetic field at the centre of the loop is X*sqrt(2) x 10^-7 T. The value of X is ___.

subject: Physics
question: Two discs of moment of inertia I1 = 4 kg m^2 and I2 = 2 kg m^2, rotating with angular speeds 10 rad/s and 4 rad/s respectively, are brought into contact face to face with their axes coincident. The loss in kinetic energy of the system in the process is ___ J.
topics: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

subject: Physics
question: Two resistances of 100 ohm and 200 ohm are connected in series with a battery of 4 V and negligible internal resistance. A voltmeter measures voltage across 100 ohm resistance and gives 1 V. The resistance of the voltmeter must be ___ ohm.
Ans_des: Voltage across 200 ohm = 3 V. Current in circuit = 3/200 A. Parallel combination of 100 ohm and voltmeter R gives: [100R/(100+R)] * (3/200) = 1. Solving: 100R/(100+R) = 200/3... actually 300R = 200(100+R), 300R = 20000+200R, 100R = 20000, R = 200 ohm.

subject: Physics
question: A vector has magnitude same as that of A = 3i + 4j and is parallel to B = 4i + 3j. The x and y components of this vector in the first quadrant are x and 3 respectively where x = ___.
Ans_des: |A| = sqrt(9+16) = 5. The required vector C is parallel to B = 4i+3j with magnitude 5. Unit vector of B = (4i+3j)/5. C = 5*(4i+3j)/5 = 4i+3j. x-component = 4.

subject: Physics
question: A simple pendulum is placed at a place where its distance from earth's surface is equal to the radius of the earth. If the length of the string is 4 m, the time period of small oscillations will be ___ s. [Take g = pi^2 m/s^2]
Ans_des: At height R from surface (R = Earth's radius): g' = g/(1+1)^2 = g/4. T = 2*pi*sqrt(L/g') = 2*pi*sqrt(4*4/pi^2) = 2*pi*(4/pi) = 8 s.

subject: Physics
question: A big drop is formed by coalescing 1000 small identical drops of water. If E1 be the total surface energy of 1000 small drops and E2 be the surface energy of the single big drop, then E1 : E2 = x : 1 where x = ___.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Choose the correct statements about the hydrides of group 15 elements:
A. Stability decreases: NH3 > PH3 > AsH3 > SbH3 > BiH3
B. Reducing ability increases: NH3 < PH3 < AsH3 < SbH3 < BiH3
C. NH3 is strong reducing agent while BiH3 is mild reducing agent
D. Basicity increases: NH3 < PH3 < AsH3 < SbH3 < BiH3
Options:
(1) B and C only
(2) A and B only
(3) A and D only
(4) C and D only
Ans_des: BiH3 is a stronger reducing agent than NH3, so C is wrong. Basicity of hydrides decreases down the group (NH3 > PH3 > AsH3...), so D is wrong. Stability decreases and reducing ability increases going down group 15, so A and B are correct.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given:
Statement I: Since F is more electronegative than N, the net dipole moment of NF3 is greater than NH3.
Statement II: In NH3, orbital dipole due to lone pair and NH bond dipoles are in opposite direction, but in NF3 the orbital dipole due to lone pair and N-F bond dipoles are in the same direction.
(1) Both I and II are true
(2) Both I and II are false
(3) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(4) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans_des: In NF3, N-F bond dipoles point away from N (towards F due to high electronegativity) and the lone pair dipole on N points upward; they are in opposite directions. In NH3, the lone pair dipole and N-H bond dipoles are in the same direction, adding up. So NH3 has higher dipole moment than NF3. Statement I is false, Statement II is true.

subject: Chemistry
question:
m-Chlorobenzaldehyde, on treatment with 50% KOH solution yields:
(1) m-chlorobenzoate ion + m-chlorobenzyl alcohol
(2) Aldol condensation product
(3) Two benzaldehyde-like products with OH groups
(4) Benzoate + benzyl alcohol with OH groups
Ans: m-Chlorobenzoate ion and m-chlorobenzyl alcohol (Cannizzaro reaction products)
Ans_des: m-Chlorobenzaldehyde has no alpha-hydrogen, so with 50% KOH it undergoes Cannizzaro reaction (disproportionation). One molecule is oxidized to carboxylate (m-chlorobenzoate) and another is reduced to alcohol (m-chlorobenzyl alcohol).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Reduction potentials of ions are given:
ClO4^- : E = 1.19 V, IO4^- : E = 1.65 V, BrO4^- : E = 1.74 V
The correct order of their oxidising power is:
(1) IO4^- > BrO4^- > ClO4^-
(2) BrO4^- > IO4^- > ClO4^-
(3) ClO4^- > IO4^- > BrO4^-
(4) BrO4^- > ClO4^- > IO4^-
Ans_des: Higher reduction potential means stronger oxidizing agent. E(BrO4^-) = 1.74 V > E(IO4^-) = 1.65 V > E(ClO4^-) = 1.19 V. So oxidizing power: BrO4^- > IO4^- > ClO4^-.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Products A and B formed in the following reactions are:
Reaction pathway: Cyclohexene -> B2H6/H2O2/NaOH -> B; Cyclohexene -> H+/H2O -> A
(1) A = CH2OH-substituted cyclohexanol, B = CH2OH-substituted cyclohexanol
(2) A = 1-methylcyclohexanol, B = cyclohexanol-methyl (same)
(3) A = CH2OH-cyclohexanol, B = CH3-cyclohexanol
(4) Same as (3) but reversed
Ans: A = 1-methylcyclohexanol (Markovnikov), B = cyclohexanol with OH on less substituted carbon (anti-Markovnikov)
Ans_des: H+/H2O (Markovnikov addition) gives A = 1-methylcyclohexanol. B2H6 then H2O2/NaOH (hydroboration-oxidation, anti-Markovnikov) gives B = cyclohexanol with OH on less substituted C.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given:
Statement I: High concentration of strong nucleophilic reagent with secondary alkyl halides without bulky substituents follows SN2 mechanism.
Statement II: A secondary alkyl halide treated with excess ethanol follows SN1 mechanism.
(1) I true but II false
(2) Both I and II are true
(3) I false but II false
(4) I false but II true
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Ans_des: High concentration of strong nucleophile and absence of bulky groups favor SN2 mechanism (Statement I is true). Ethanol is a weak nucleophile/polar protic solvent, so it favors SN1 mechanism for secondary alkyl halides (Statement II is true).

subject: Chemistry
question:
The correct stability order of carbocations is:
(1) CH3+ > CH3CH2+ > (CH3)2CH+ > (CH3)3C+
(2) (CH3)3C+ > CH3CH2+ > (CH3)2CH+ > CH3+
(3) CH3+ > (CH3)2CH+ > CH3CH2+ > (CH3)3C+
(4) (CH3)3C+ > (CH3)2CH+ > CH3CH2+ > CH3+
Ans_des: Stability order of carbocations: tertiary > secondary > primary > methyl. More alkyl substituents provide greater hyperconjugation and inductive effect, increasing stability.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The solution with the highest depression in freezing point is:
(1) 180 g of acetic acid dissolved in benzene
(2) 180 g of acetic acid dissolved in water
(3) 180 g of benzoic acid dissolved in benzene
(4) 180 g of glucose dissolved in water
Ans: 180 g of acetic acid dissolved in water
Ans_des: 180 g acetic acid = 3 mol (MW = 60). In water, acetic acid partially dissociates, increasing number of solute particles. Benzoic acid in benzene dimerizes (fewer particles). Glucose = 1 mol. So 180 g acetic acid in water has most solute particles, giving greatest freezing point depression.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The coordination geometry around the manganese in decacarbonyldimanganese(0) is:
(1) Square pyramidal
(2) Square planar
(3) Octahedral
(4) Trigonal bipyramidal
Ans_des: In Mn2(CO)10, each Mn atom is bonded to 5 terminal CO ligands and 1 Mn-Mn bond (or bridging CO). Each Mn has 6 atoms surrounding it in an octahedral arrangement.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given:
Statement I: Along a period, chemical reactivity of elements gradually increases from group 1 to group 18.
Statement II: Nature of oxides formed by group 1 elements is basic while that of group 17 elements is acidic.
(1) Both I and II true
(2) Both I and II false
(3) Statement I false but Statement II true
(4) Statement I true but Statement II false
Ans: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans_des: Chemical reactivity does NOT increase uniformly across a period; noble gases (group 18) are least reactive. So Statement I is false. Group 1 metals form basic oxides; group 17 non-metals form acidic oxides. So Statement II is true.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A and B formed in the following reactions:
CrO2Cl2 + 4NaOH -> A + 2NaCl + 2H2O
A + 2HCl + 2H2O2 -> B + 3H2O
(1) A = Na2Cr2O7, B = CrO3
(2) A = Na2Cr2O7, B = CrO5
(3) A = Na2CrO4, B = CrO4
(4) A = Na2CrO4, B = CrO5
Ans_des: CrO2Cl2 reacts with NaOH to form sodium chromate (A = Na2CrO4). Na2CrO4 reacts with HCl and H2O2 to form CrO5 (chromium pentoxide, a blue peroxo compound) (B = CrO5).

subject: Chemistry
question:
If substance 'A' dissolves in a mixture of 'B' and 'C' with moles nA, nB, nC respectively, the mole fraction of C in the solution is:
(1) nB / (nA + nB)
(2) nC / (nA - nB - nC)
(3) nC / (nA + nB + nC)
(4) nC / (nA x nB x nC)
Ans_des: Mole fraction = moles of component / total moles of all components = nC / (nA + nB + nC).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Alkaline oxidative fusion of MnO2 gives 'A', which on electrolytic oxidation in alkaline solution produces B. A and B respectively are:
(1) MnO4^2- and Mn2O7
(2) Mn2O7 and MnO4^-
(3) Mn2O3 and MnO4^2-
(4) MnO4^2- and MnO4^-
Ans_des: 2MnO2 + 4KOH + O2 -> 2K2MnO4 + 2H2O. So A = K2MnO4 (MnO4^2-). MnO4^2- on electrolytic oxidation gives MnO4^- (permanganate). So B = MnO4^-.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The molecule/ion with square pyramidal shape is:
(1) PCl5
(2) PF5
(3) [Ni(CN)4]^2-
(4) BrF5
Ans_des: BrF5 has 5 bonding pairs and 1 lone pair (total 6 electron pairs = octahedral electron geometry). With one lone pair, the shape is square pyramidal. PCl5 and PF5 are trigonal bipyramidal; [Ni(CN)4]^2- is square planar.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Salicylaldehyde is synthesized from phenol when reacted with:
(1) CO2, NaOH
(2) Acid chloride, NaOH
(3) CCl4, NaOH
(4) HCCl3, NaOH
Ans_des: Phenol reacts with CHCl3 (chloroform) and NaOH to give salicylaldehyde (2-hydroxybenzaldehyde). This is the Reimer-Tiemann reaction.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The products A and B formed in the following reaction scheme:
Benzene + conc. HNO3/conc. H2SO4 (323-333 K) -> nitrobenzene; nitrobenzene + Sn/HCl -> A; A + NaNO2/HCl (273-278 K) -> diazonium salt; diazonium + Phenol -> B
(1) A = aniline, B = 4-hydroxyazobenzene (para azo dye)
(2) A = aniline, B = diphenylamine
(3) A = nitrobenzene, B = azo compound with OH
(4) A = chloronitrobenzene, B = nitrophenol azo compound
Ans_des: Nitration gives nitrobenzene. Sn/HCl reduces it to aniline (A). Aniline reacts with NaNO2/HCl at 273-278 K to give diazonium salt. Coupling with phenol gives 4-hydroxyazobenzene (B), an azo dye.

subject: Chemistry
question:
IUPAC name of the compound CH3-CH(NH2)-CH2-CN is:
(1) 2-Aminopentanenitrile
(2) 2-Aminobutanenitrile
(3) 3-Aminopropanenitrile
(4) 3-Aminobutanenitrile
Ans_des: The chain: CN (C1) - CH2 (C2) - CH(NH2) (C3) - CH3 (C4). Principal group is -CN (nitrile), so chain is 4 carbons = butanenitrile. NH2 at C3. Name: 3-Aminobutanenitrile.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The orange colour of K2Cr2O7 and purple colour of KMnO4 is due to:
(1) d->d transitions in K2Cr2O7 and charge transfer in KMnO4
(2) d->d transitions in KMnO4 and charge transfer in K2Cr2O7
(3) Charge transfer transitions in both
(4) d->d transitions in both
Ans_des: Both K2Cr2O7 and KMnO4 have d^0 metal centers (Cr^6+ and Mn^7+ respectively). Since there are no d electrons, d->d transitions are not possible. Their colours are due to ligand-to-metal charge transfer (LMCT) transitions.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which among the following purification methods is based on the principle of 'Solubility' in two different solvents?
(1) Distillation
(2) Differential Extraction
(3) Column Chromatography
(4) Sublimation
topics: General Principles of Isolation of Elements
Ans_des: Differential extraction (solvent extraction) is based on the different solubility of a substance in two immiscible solvents. The solute partitions between the two solvents based on its solubility in each.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: H2Te is more acidic than H2S.
Reason R: Bond dissociation enthalpy of H2Te is lower than H2S.
(1) A is false but R is true
(2) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(3) A is true but R is false
(4) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: H2Te has weaker H-Te bond (lower bond dissociation enthalpy) compared to H-S bond, making it easier to release H+. Therefore H2Te is a stronger acid than H2S. R correctly explains A.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The total number of correct statements regarding nucleic acids is ___.
A. RNA is regarded as the reserve of genetic information.
B. DNA molecule self-duplicates during cell division.
C. DNA synthesizes proteins in the cell.
D. The message for the synthesis of particular proteins is present in DNA.
E. Identical DNA strands are transferred to daughter cells.
Ans_des: A is wrong (DNA is the reserve of genetic info, not RNA). B is correct (DNA self-replicates). C is wrong (proteins are synthesized by mRNA/ribosomes, not directly by DNA). D is correct (genetic code in DNA). E is correct (identical DNA to daughter cells). Correct: B, D, E = 3 statements.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Total number of species from the following which can undergo disproportionation reaction is ___:
H2O2, ClO3^-, P4, Cl2, Ag, Cu^+, F2, NO2, K^+
Ans_des: Disproportionation occurs when atoms are in intermediate oxidation states. H2O2 (O is -1), ClO3^- (Cl is +5), P4 (P is 0), Cl2 (Cl is 0), Cu^+ (Cu is +1), NO2 (N is +4) can all disproportionate. Ag, F2 (fluorine is most electronegative, cannot be oxidized), K^+ cannot disproportionate. Count = 6.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Number of complexes which show optical isomerism among the following is ___:
cis-[Cr(ox)2Cl2]^3-, [Co(en)3]^3+, cis-[Pt(en)2Cl2]^2+, cis-[Co(en)2Cl2]^+, trans-[Pt(en)2Cl2]^2+, trans-[Cr(ox)2Cl2]^3-
Ans_des: Optical isomerism requires non-superimposable mirror images (chiral). cis-[Cr(ox)2Cl2]^3- (chiral), [Co(en)3]^3+ (chiral, D3 symmetry), cis-[Pt(en)2Cl2]^2+ (chiral), cis-[Co(en)2Cl2]^+ (chiral) show optical isomerism. Trans isomers have planes of symmetry, no optical isomerism. Count = 4.

subject: Chemistry
question:
NO2 required for a reaction is produced by decomposition of N2O5 in CCl4:
2N2O5(g) -> 4NO2(g) + O2(g)
Initial [N2O5] = 3 mol/L, after 30 min [N2O5] = 2.75 mol/L.
The rate of formation of NO2 is x x 10^-3 mol/L/min. Value of x is ___.
Ans_des: Rate of disappearance of N2O5 = (3 - 2.75)/30 = 0.25/30 mol/L/min. Rate of formation of NO2 = 2 * (rate of disappearance of N2O5) = 2 * 0.25/30 = 0.5/30 = 0.01667 mol/L/min = 17 x 10^-3 mol/L/min. x = 17.

subject: Chemistry
question: Number of geometrical isomers possible for the compound with structure: H-C=C-C=C-H with D substituents on alternate carbons (structure has 3 double bonds with H and D groups) is ___.
Ans_des: For the given structure with 3 double bonds (left, middle, right), possible E/Z combinations: E-E, Z-Z, E-Z-Z, Z-E-Z (middle DB has no geometrical isomerism if substituents same; only left and right DB contribute). Total 4 geometrical isomers.

subject: Chemistry
question: Number of spectral lines obtained in He+ spectra when an electron makes transition from fifth excited state to first excited state will be ___.
topics: Atoms and Nuclei / Structure of Atom
Ans_des: Fifth excited state = n = 6; first excited state = n = 2. Number of spectral lines = (n2 - n1)(n2 - n1 + 1)/2 = (6-2)(6-2+1)/2 = 4*5/2 = 10.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Number of metal ions characterized by flame test among the following is ___:
Sr^2+, Ba^2+, Ca^2+, Cu^2+, Zn^2+, Co^2+, Fe^2+
Ans_des: Flame test is characteristic for: Ca^2+ (brick red), Sr^2+ (crimson red), Ba^2+ (grassy green), Cu^2+ (blue-green). Zn^2+, Co^2+, Fe^2+ do not give characteristic flame colours. Count = 4.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Two reactions:
2Fe(s) + 3/2 O2(g) -> Fe2O3(s), delta_H = -822 kJ/mol
C(s) + 1/2 O2(g) -> CO(g), delta_H = -110 kJ/mol
Enthalpy change for: 3C(s) + Fe2O3(s) -> 2Fe(s) + 3CO(g) is ___ kJ/mol.
Ans_des: Target reaction = -(reaction 1) + 3*(reaction 2). delta_H = +822 + 3*(-110) = 822 - 330 = 492 kJ/mol.

question: 2-chlorobutane + Cl2 -> C4H8Cl2 (isomers). Total number of optically active isomers shown by C4H8Cl2 obtained in the above reaction is ___.
Ans_des: 2-chlorobutane has positions A(C1), B(C2, has Cl), C(C3), D(C4). Adding Cl to A gives 2 optically active isomers; B gives no new optically active isomers; C gives 2 optically active isomers; D gives 2 optically active isomers. Total = 2+0+2+2 = 6.

subject: Chemistry
question: The pH of a buffer containing 1 M benzoic acid (pKa = 4.20) and 1 M sodium benzoate is 4.5. The volume of benzoic acid solution in 300 mL of this buffer is ___ mL. [log 2 = 0.3]
Ans_des: Using Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([A^-]/[HA]). 4.5 = 4.2 + log((300-V)/V). log((300-V)/V) = 0.3 = log2. So (300-V)/V = 2, giving 300-V = 2V, 3V = 300, V = 100 mL.

question:
Let a and b be two vectors such that |b| = 1 and |b x a| = 2. Then |(b x a) - b|^2 is equal to:
(1) 5
(2) 1
(3) 3
(4) 4
Ans_des: |(b x a) - b|^2 = |b x a|^2 + |b|^2 - 2*b.(b x a). Since b.(b x a) = 0 (scalar triple product with repeated vector), we get (2)^2 + (1)^2 - 0 = 4 + 1 = 5.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let y = f(x) be thrice differentiable in (-5,5). Tangents at (1,f(1)) and (3,f(3)) make angles pi/6 and pi/4 with positive x-axis. If 27 * integral from 1 to 3 of [(f'(t))^2 + 1]*f''(t) dt = alpha + beta*sqrt(3), where alpha, beta are integers, then alpha + beta equals:
(1) -14
(2) -16
(3) 36
(4) 26
Ans_des: f'(1) = tan(pi/6) = 1/sqrt(3), f'(3) = tan(pi/4) = 1. Let u = f'(t), du = f''(t)dt. I = 27 * integral from 1/sqrt(3) to 1 of (u^2+1)du = 27*[u^3/3 + u] from 1/sqrt(3) to 1 = 27*[4/3 - 10/(9*sqrt(3))] = 36 - 10*sqrt(3). alpha=36, beta=-10. alpha+beta=26.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let a and b be real constants such that f(x) = {x^2+3x+a, x<=1; bx+2, x>1} is differentiable on R. Then integral from -2 to 2 of f(x)dx equals:
(1) 15/6
(2) 17
(3) 21
(4) 19/6
Ans_des: Continuity at x=1: a+4=b+2, so b-a=2. Differentiability: 2(1)+3=b, b=5, a=3. Integral = integral(-2 to 1) of (x^2+3x+3)dx + integral(1 to 2) of (5x+2)dx = [x^3/3+3x^2/2+3x] from -2 to 1 + [5x^2/2+2x] from 1 to 2 = (1/3+3/2+3)-(-8/3+6-6) + (10+4)-(5/2+2) = 6+3/2+12-5/2 = 18-1 = 17.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the domain of f(x) = log_e[(2x+3)/(4x^2+x-3)] + cos^(-1)[(2x-1)/(x+2)] is (alpha, beta], then the value of 5*beta - 4*alpha is equal to:
(1) 10
(2) 11
(3) 12
(4) 9
Ans_des: For log: (2x+3)/[(4x-3)(x+1)] > 0 gives x in (-3/2, -1) union (3/4, inf). For cos^(-1): -1 <= (2x-1)/(x+2) <= 1, which gives x in (-inf,-2) union [-1/3, inf) intersected with (-2,3]. Combined: x in (3/4, 3] = (alpha, beta]. 5*beta - 4*alpha = 5*3 - 4*(3/4) = 15 - 3 = 12.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If z is a complex number, then the number of common roots of z^1985 + z^100 + 1 = 0 and z^3 + 2z^2 + 2z + 1 = 0 is equal to:
(1) 2
(2) 1
(3) 0
(4) 3
Ans_des: z^3+2z^2+2z+1 = (z+1)(z^2+z+1) = 0. Roots: z = -1, omega, omega^2 (cube roots of unity). Testing z=-1: (-1)^1985+(-1)^100+1 = -1+1+1 = 1 not 0. For omega: omega^3=1, so omega^1985 = omega^2, omega^100 = omega. Sum = omega^2+omega+1 = 0. Similarly omega^2 works. Both omega and omega^2 are common roots. Count = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let R = diag(x, y, z) (3x3 diagonal matrix) where x*sin(theta) = y*sin(theta+2pi/3) = z*sin(theta+4pi/3) not equal to 0, theta in (0,2pi). Among the statements:
(I) Trace(R) = 0
(II) If Trace(adj(adj(R))) = 0, then R has exactly one non-zero entry.
(1) Only II is true
(2) Only I is true
(3) Both I and II are true
(4) Neither I nor II is true
Ans: Neither I nor II is true
Ans_des: x+y+z = -3x / [4sin(theta+2pi/3)*sin(theta+4pi/3)] which is not 0. Statement I is wrong. adj(adj(R)) has trace = xyz(x+y+z) which is not 0, so statement II is also wrong. Neither is true.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let L1: r = (i-j+2k) + lambda*(i-j+2k), L2: r = (j-k) + mu*(3i+j+pk), L3: r = delta*(li+mj+nk) be three lines such that L1 is perpendicular to L2 and L3 is perpendicular to both L1 and L2. The point which lies on L3 is:
(1) (-1, 7, 4)
(2) (1, -7, 4)
(3) (1, 7, -4)
(4) (-1, -7, 4)
Ans_des: L1 perpendicular to L2: 1*3 + (-1)*1 + 2*p = 0, so p = -1. L3 perpendicular to L1: l-m+2n = 0 (eq.1). L3 perpendicular to L2: 3l+m-n = 0 (eq.2). Checking (-1,7,4): l=-1,m=7,n=4. Eq.1: -1-7+8=0 (check). Eq.2: -3+7-4=0 (check). So (-1,7,4) lies on L3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Consider the system: x+y+z=5, x+2y+lambda^2*z=9, x+3y+lambda*z=mu. Which statement is NOT correct?
(1) System has unique solution if lambda not equal to 1 and mu not equal to 13
(2) System is inconsistent if lambda = 1 and mu not equal to 13
(3) System has infinite solutions if lambda = 1 and mu = 13
(4) System is consistent if lambda not equal to 1 and mu = 13
Ans: System has unique solution if lambda not equal to 1 and mu not equal to 13
Ans_des: Delta = -(2*lambda+1)*(lambda+1). Delta = 0 when lambda = -1/2 or lambda = -1. So for lambda not equal to -1/2 and lambda not equal to -1 (not necessarily lambda not equal to 1), unique solution exists. For lambda = -1/2 (which is not equal to 1) and mu not equal to 13, there is no solution. Statement (1) is not always correct.

question:

(1) 22
(2) 20
(3) 26

Ans_des: e = sqrt(1+4/9) = sqrt(13)/3. Foci F1=(sqrt(13),0), F2=(-sqrt(13),0). Area = (1/2)*|F1F2|*y-coord of P = (1/2)*(2*sqrt(13))*beta = 2*sqrt(13). So beta = 2. On hyperbola: alpha^2/9 - 4/4 = 1, alpha^2/9 = 2, alpha = 3*sqrt(2). Distance^2 = 18+4 = 22.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Suppose 2-p, p, 2-alpha, alpha are the coefficients of four consecutive terms in expansion of (1+x)^n. Then the value of p^2 - alpha^2 + 6*alpha + 2p equals:
(1) 8
(2) 4
(3) 10
(4) 6
Ans_des: Let the four consecutive terms be nCr, nCr+1, nCr+2, nCr+3. Then 2-p = nCr, p = nCr+1, 2-alpha = nCr+2, alpha = nCr+3. nCr + nCr+1 = n+1Cr+1 = 2 and nCr+2 + nCr+3 = n+1Cr+3 = 2. This gives r+1 = r+3 (contradiction) or n = 2r+3. Substituting leads to no valid integer solution. Data is inconsistent, so this is a bonus question.

subject: Mathematics
question:
For alpha, beta in (0, pi/2), let 3*sin(alpha+beta) = 2*sin(alpha-beta) and a real number k such that tan(alpha) = k*tan(beta). Then k equals:
(1) 2/3
(2) -5
(3) 5
(4) -2/3
Ans_des: 3sin(alpha+beta) = 2sin(alpha-beta). Using componendo-dividendo: [sin(alpha+beta)+sin(alpha-beta)] / [sin(alpha+beta)-sin(alpha-beta)] = (2+3)/(2-3) = -5. This gives [2sin(alpha)cos(beta)] / [2cos(alpha)sin(beta)] = -5, so tan(alpha)/tan(beta) = -5, k = -5. But alpha, beta in (0, pi/2) means tan(alpha) and tan(beta) are both positive, so k cannot be -5. Data is inconsistent, so this is a bonus question.

subject: Mathematics
question: The number of real solutions of the equation x*(x^2 + 3|x| + 5|x-1| + 6|x-2|) = 0 is ___.
Ans_des: Either x=0 or x^2+3|x|+5|x-1|+6|x-2|=0. All terms in the second factor are non-negative and for any real x, the expression is always positive (x^2>=0 and each term is >=0, not all zero simultaneously). So only x=0 is a solution. Number of solutions = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question: The area of the region enclosed by the parabola (y-2)^2 = x-1, the line x-2y+4 = 0 and the positive coordinate axes is ___.
Ans_des: Parabola vertex: (1,2). Line x=2y-4. Intersection: substituting x=2y-4 into (y-2)^2=x-1 gives (y-2)^2=2y-5, y^2-6y+9=2y-5, y^2-8y+14... solving properly gives y=3,x=2. Region: integrate (x_parabola - x_line) from y=0 to y=3, minus triangle area. Area = integral from 0 to 3 of [(y-2)^2+1 - (2y-4)] dy - triangle = 9 - (1/2)*4*2 = 9-4 = 5.

subject: Mathematics
question: The number of symmetric relations defined on the set {1, 2, 3, 4} which are not reflexive is ___.
Ans_des: For set of n=4 elements: Total symmetric relations = 2^(n(n+1)/2) = 2^10 = 1024. Reflexive and symmetric relations = 2^(n(n-1)/2) = 2^6 = 64. Symmetric but not reflexive = 1024 - 64 = 960.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let alpha = sum from k=0 to n of [(nCk)^2 / (k+1)] and beta = sum from k=0 to n-1 of [nCk * nCk+1 / (k+2)]. If 5*alpha = 6*beta, then n equals ___.
Ans_des: alpha = (1/(n+1)) * 2n+1Cn+1. beta = (1/(n+1)) * 2n+1Cn+2. beta/alpha = 2n+1Cn+2 / 2n+1Cn+1 = n/(n+2). Given 5*alpha = 6*beta, so beta/alpha = 5/6. Thus n/(n+2) = 5/6, 6n = 5n+10, n = 10.

question: Let a line passing through (-1,2,3) intersect L1: (x-1)/3 = (y-2)/2 = (z+1)/(-2) at M(alpha,beta,gamma) and L2: (x+2)/(-3) = (y-2)/(-2) = (z-1)/4 at N(a,b,c). Then [(alpha+beta+gamma)/(a+b+c)]^2 equals ___.

Solving: lambda=4, t=2. M=(13,10,-9), N=(-8,-2,9). (alpha+beta+gamma) = 14, (a+b+c) = -1. [(14)/(-1)]^2 = 196.

question: Consider circles C1: x^2+y^2=25 and C2: (x-alpha)^2+y^2=16, alpha in (5,9). The angle between radii drawn to one of the intersection points is sin^(-1)(sqrt(63)/8). If common chord length is beta, then (alpha*beta)^2 equals ___.
Ans_des: Let P be an intersection point. Radii are AP=5 (from C1) and BP=4 (from C2). sin(theta) = sqrt(63)/8. Area of triangle ABP = (1/2)*alpha*beta/2 = (1/2)*5*4*sin(theta) = (1/2)*5*4*(sqrt(63)/8) = 5*sqrt(63)/4. So alpha*beta = 5*sqrt(63). (alpha*beta)^2 = 25*63 = 1575.

subject: Physics
question:
In the given circuit if the power rating of Zener diode is 10 mW, the value of series resistance RS to regulate the input unregulated supply is:
Vz = 5V, RL = 1 kohm, VS = 8V
(1) 5 kohm
(2) 10 ohm
(3) 10 kohm
(4) 1 kohm
Ans_des: Voltage across RS = 8 - 5 = 3 V. Zener current Iz = P/Vz = 10 mW / 5 V = 2 mA. Load current IL = Vz/RL = 5V/1kohm = 5 mA. Total current = 5 + 2 = 7 mA... Wait: 3/RS = Iz + IL = 10mW/5V + 5/1k = 2mA + 5mA = 7mA is not consistent. Re-checking: 3/RS = 3 mA (from solution: 3/RS = 3mA), RS = 1 kohm. The Zener carries 2 mA (10mW/5V) and load carries 5 mA, total = 7 mA. But solution says 3/RS = 3mA. Checking: if RS=1k, 3V/1kohm=3mA, load=5mA, Zener gets 3-5=-2mA which is not possible. The correct reading: VS=8V, Vz=5V, drop on RS=3V. I_total = I_z + I_L. P_z=10mW, Vz=5V so I_z=2mA. I_L=5V/1kohm=5mA. I_total=7mA. RS=3V/7mA=428 ohm, not matching options. Official answer is RS=1 kohm.

subject: Physics
question:
A particle moving in a circle of radius R with uniform speed takes time T to complete one revolution. If this particle is projected with the same speed at an angle theta to the horizontal, the maximum height attained by it is equal to 4R. The angle of projection theta is then given by:
(1) cos^-1[(2gT^2 / (pi^2 * R))^(1/2)]
(2) sin^-1[(2gT^2 / (pi^2 * R))^(1/2)]
(3) sin^-1[(pi^2 * R / (2gT^2))^(1/2)]
(4) cos^-1[(pi * R / (2gT^2))^(1/2)]
Ans_des: T = 2*pi*R/V, so V = 2*pi*R/T. Maximum height Hmax = V^2*sin^2(theta)/(2g) = 4R. sin^2(theta) = 8gR/V^2 = 8gR / (4*pi^2*R^2/T^2) = 2gT^2 / (pi^2*R). theta = sin^-1[(2gT^2 / (pi^2*R))^(1/2)].

subject: Physics
question:
Two identical capacitors have same capacitance C. One of them is charged to the potential V and other to the potential 2V. The negative ends of both are connected together. When the positive ends are also joined together, the decrease in energy of the combined system is:
(1) (1/4)CV^2
(2) (3/4)CV^2
(3) 2CV^2
(4) (1/2)CV^2
Ans_des: Initial energy Ei = (1/2)CV^2 + (1/2)C(2V)^2 = (5/2)CV^2. When connected, total charge Q = CV + 2CV = 3CV on two capacitors in parallel (2C). Final voltage Vf = 3CV/(2C) = 3V/2. Ef = (1/2)(2C)(3V/2)^2 = (9/4)CV^2. Decrease = Ei - Ef = (5/2 - 9/4)CV^2 = (1/4)CV^2.

subject: Physics
question:
A galvanometer has a resistance of 50 ohm and it allows maximum current of 5 mA. It can be converted into a voltmeter to measure up to 100 V by connecting in series a resistor of resistance:
(1) 20050 ohm
(2) 5975 ohm
(3) 19950 ohm
(4) 19500 ohm

subject: Physics
question:
The reading in the ideal voltmeter (V) shown in the given circuit diagram is:
(Circuit has 8 cells each of emf 5V with internal resistance 0.2 ohm each, connected in a specific configuration with an ideal voltmeter)
(1) 0 V
(2) 5 V
(3) 10 V
(4) 3 V
Ans_des: Total emf = 8*5 = 40 V (but arrangement causes cancellation). Total 8 cells: net emf around loop = 40 - i*(8*0.2) = 0, i = 25 A. For the voltmeter branch: VB - (25*0.2) + 5 = VA, VB + 0 = VA, so VA - VB = 0 V.

subject: Physics
question:
A ball of mass 0.5 kg on a string of length 50 cm is rotated on a horizontal circular path about its vertical axis. The maximum tension that the string can bear is 400 N. The maximum possible value of angular velocity of the ball in rad/s is:
(1) 1000
(2) 40
(3) 20
(4) 1500
Ans_des: T*cos(theta) = mg, T*sin(theta) = m*omega^2*L*sin(theta). So T = m*omega^2*L. 400 = 0.5*omega^2*0.5. omega^2 = 1600. omega = 40 rad/s.

subject: Physics
question:
The minimum energy required by a hydrogen atom in ground state to emit radiation in Balmer series is nearly:
(1) 13.6 eV
(2) 1.5 eV
(3) 12.1 eV
(4) 1.9 eV
Ans_des: Balmer series requires transition to n=2. Minimum energy is needed for n=1 to n=3 transition (as n=3 to n=2 emits Balmer radiation). Delta_E = E_ground - E(n=3) = -13.6 - (-13.6/9) = 13.6*(1 - 1/9) = 13.6*(8/9) = 12.09 approximately 12.1 eV.

subject: Physics
question:
The dimensional formula of angular impulse is:
(1) [ML^2 T^-2]
(2) [ML^-2 T^-1]
(3) [ML^2 T^-1]
(4) [MLT^-1]
Ans_des: Angular impulse = Torque * time = Force * distance * time. [Angular impulse] = [Force][Length][Time] = [MLT^-2][L][T] = [ML^2T^-1].

subject: Physics
question:
The pressure and volume of an ideal gas are related as PV^(3/2) = K (Constant). The work done when the gas is taken from state A(P1, V1, T1) to state B(P2, V2, T2) is:
(1) 2(P2*sqrt(V2) - P1*sqrt(V1))
(2) 2(P1*V1 - P2*V2)
(3) 2(P2*V2 - P1*V1)
(4) 2(sqrt(P1)*V1 - sqrt(P2)*V2)
Ans_des: For process PV^gamma = K with gamma = 3/2: W = (P_f*V_f - P_i*V_i)/(1 - gamma) = (P2*V2 - P1*V1)/(1 - 3/2) = (P2*V2 - P1*V1)/(-1/2) = -2(P2*V2 - P1*V1) = 2(P1*V1 - P2*V2).

subject: Physics
question:
With rise in temperature, the Young's modulus of elasticity:
(1) Decreases
(2) Remains unchanged
(3) Increases
(4) Changes erratically
Ans_des: With rise in temperature, Young's modulus of elasticity decreases because increased atomic vibrations reduce the interatomic forces, making the material less stiff and easier to deform.

subject: Physics
question:
Two moles of a monoatomic gas is mixed with six moles of a diatomic gas. The molar specific heat of the mixture at constant volume is:
(1) (3/2)R
(2) (9/4)R
(3) (7/4)R
(4) (5/2)R

subject: Physics
question:
A simple pendulum of length 1 m has a wooden bob of mass 1 kg. It is struck by a bullet of mass 10^-2 kg moving with a speed of 2*10^2 m/s. The bullet gets embedded into the bob. The height to which the bob rises before swinging back is (use g = 10 m/s^2):
(1) 0.20 m
(2) 0.35 m
(3) 0.30 m
(4) 0.40 m
Ans_des: By momentum conservation: 10^-2 * 2*10^2 = (1 + 0.01)*v. 2 = 1.01*v, v = 2/1.01 approx 2 m/s. By energy conservation: (1/2)*(1.01)*v^2 = (1.01)*10*h. h = v^2/(2g) = 4/20 = 0.20 m.

subject: Physics
question:
A parallel plate capacitor has a capacitance C = 200 pF. It is connected to 230 V ac supply with an angular frequency 300 rad/s. The rms value of conduction current in the circuit and displacement current in the capacitor respectively are:
(1) 14.3 microA and 143 microA
(2) 13.8 microA and 138 microA
(3) 13.8 microA and 13.8 microA
(4) 1.38 microA and 1.38 microA
Ans_des: Displacement current (rms) = C*omega*Erms = 200*10^-12 * 300 * 230 = 13.8*10^-6 A = 13.8 microA. Conduction current (rms) = Erms * omega*C = same = 13.8 microA. Both are equal in a capacitor circuit.

subject: Physics
question:
The de Broglie wavelengths of a proton and an alpha particle are lambda and 2*lambda respectively. The ratio of the velocities of proton and alpha particle will be:
(1) 4 : 1
(2) 1 : 8
(3) 8 : 1
(4) 1 : 2
topics: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
Ans_des: de Broglie wavelength: lambda = h/(m*v), so v = h/(m*lambda). v proportional to 1/(m*lambda). Vp/Valpha = (m_alpha * lambda_alpha) / (m_p * lambda_p) = (4*m_p * 2*lambda) / (m_p * lambda) = 8/1. Ratio = 8:1.

subject: Physics
question:
In series LCR circuit, the capacitance is changed from C to 4C. To keep the resonance frequency unchanged, the new inductance should be:
(1) Increased to 4L
(2) Increased by 2L
(3) Reduced by (1/4)L
(4) Reduced by (3/4)L
Ans_des: Resonance frequency omega = 1/sqrt(LC). For omega to remain same: L*C = L'*4C. L' = L/4. Change in inductance = L - L/4 = 3L/4. So inductance is reduced by (3/4)L.

subject: Physics
question:
If R is the radius of the earth and the acceleration due to gravity on the surface of earth is g = pi^2 m/s^2, then the length of the second's pendulum at a height h = 2R from the surface of earth will be:
(1) 4/9 m
(2) 8/9 m
(3) 1/9 m
(4) 2/9 m
Ans_des: At height h=2R: g' = GM/(R+2R)^2 = GM/(9R^2) = g/9. Time period of second's pendulum T=2s: T = 2*pi*sqrt(L/g'). 2 = 2*pi*sqrt(L*9/g) = 2*pi*sqrt(9L/pi^2) = 2*pi*3*sqrt(L)/pi = 6*sqrt(L). sqrt(L) = 1/3. L = 1/9 m.

subject: Physics
question:
A monochromatic light of wavelength 6000 angstrom is incident on the single slit of width 0.01 mm. If the diffraction pattern is formed at the focus of the convex lens of focal length 20 cm, the linear width of the central maximum is:
(1) 12 mm
(2) 120 mm
(3) 60 mm
(4) 24 mm

subject: Physics
question:
10 divisions on the main scale of a Vernier caliper coincide with 11 divisions on the Vernier scale. If each division on the main scale is of 5 units, the least count of the instrument is:
(1) 10/11
(2) 1/2
(3) 5/11
(4) 50/11
Ans_des: 10 MSD = 11 VSD, so 1 VSD = 10/11 MSD. VSD value = (10/11)*5 = 50/11 units. Least count = MSD - VSD = 5 - 50/11 = (55-50)/11 = 5/11 units.

subject: Physics
question:
The radius (r), length (l) and resistance (R) of a metal wire were measured as:
r = (0.35 +/- 0.05) cm, R = (100 +/- 10) ohm, l = (15 +/- 0.2) cm.
The percentage error in resistivity of the material of the wire is:
(1) 35.6%
(2) 39.9%
(3) 25.6%
(4) 37.3%

subject: Physics
question:
Consider a block and trolley system. A 20 kg trolley is on a horizontal surface with kinetic friction coefficient 0.04. A 6 kg block hangs from a string over a pulley providing 60 N force. The acceleration of the system in m/s^2 is:
(1) 4
(2) 3
(3) 2
(4) 1.2
Ans_des: Friction force fk = mu_k * N = 0.04 * (20+6) * 10 = wait: fk = 0.04 * 20 * 10 = 8 N (normal force from trolley only). Net force = 60 - 8 = 52 N... Official: 60 - fk = (20+6)*a. 60 - 200*0.04 = 26a. 60 - 8 = 52 = 26a, a = 2 m/s^2.

subject: Physics
question: A rectangular loop of sides 12 cm and 5 cm, with its sides parallel to x and y axes respectively, moves with velocity 5 cm/s in positive x direction in a space with variable magnetic field in positive z direction. The field has gradient 10^-3 T/cm along negative x direction and decreasing at 10^-3 T/s. If resistance of loop is 6 milliohm, power dissipated as heat is ___ x 10^-9 W.

subject: Physics
question: A plane is in level flight at constant speed and each of its two wings has area 40 m^2. Speed of air is 180 km/h over lower wing surface and 252 km/h over upper wing surface. The mass of the plane is ___ kg. (air density = 1 kg/m^3, g = 10 m/s^2)

subject: Physics
question: Two identical charged spheres are suspended by strings of equal lengths. The strings make angle theta with each other. When suspended in water the angle remains same. If density of material of sphere is 1.5 g/cc, the dielectric constant of water will be ___. (density of water = 1 g/cc)
Ans_des: For angle to remain same: tan(theta) same in both cases. Electric force/weight = same. In water: Fe/(K) divided by (mg - buoyancy) = Fe/mg in air. 1/(K*(1 - sigma/rho)) = 1. K = 1/(1 - 1/1.5) = 1/(1 - 2/3) = 1/(1/3) = 3.

subject: Physics
question: A particle is moving in one dimension (along x-axis). Its initial position was 16 m right of origin. The variation of its position with time is: x = -3t^3 + 18t^2 + 16t, where x is in m and t in s. The velocity of the particle when its acceleration becomes zero is ___ m/s.

subject: Physics
question: The current in a conductor is expressed as I = 3t^2 + 4t^3, where I is in Ampere and t is in second. The amount of electric charge that flows through a section of the conductor during t = 1 s to t = 2 s is ___ C.
Ans_des: q = integral of I dt from 1 to 2 = integral of (3t^2 + 4t^3) dt from 1 to 2 = [t^3 + t^4] from 1 to 2 = (8 + 16) - (1 + 1) = 24 - 2 = 22 C.

subject: Physics
question: The distance between object and its 3 times magnified virtual image as produced by a convex lens is 20 cm. The focal length of the lens used is ___ cm.
Ans_des: |m| = v/u = 3 (virtual image, so v = 3u with same sign as u for virtual). For virtual image: v = 3u (both same side). Distance |v| - |u| = 20. 3|u| - |u| = 20 (no wait: object at -10, image at +30 for virtual). u = -10 cm, v = +30 cm. 1/f = 1/v - 1/u = 1/30 - 1/(-10) = 1/30 + 1/10 = 4/30. f = 30/4... But answer is 15. u=-10, v=30: 1/f=1/30+1/10=1/30+3/30=4/30, f=7.5. Let v=3u (virtual, same side): |v|-|u|=20, 2|u|=20, |u|=10. u=-10, v=-30: 1/f=1/(-30)-1/(-10)=-1/30+1/10=2/30=1/15. f=15 cm.

subject: Physics
question: Three identical spheres each of mass 2M are placed at corners of a right angled triangle with mutually perpendicular sides equal to 4 m each. Taking point of intersection of the two perpendicular sides as origin, the magnitude of position vector of centre of mass of system is 4*sqrt(2)/x, where x = ___.
topics: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

subject: Physics
question: A tuning fork resonates with a sonometer wire of length 1 m stretched with a tension of 6 N. When the tension in the wire is changed to 54 N, the same tuning fork produces 12 beats per second with it. The frequency of the tuning fork is ___ Hz.

subject: Physics
question: The radius of a nucleus of mass number 64 is 4.8 fermi. Then the mass number of another nucleus having radius of 4 fermi is 1000/x, where x = ___.

subject: Physics
question: A regular polygon of 6 sides is formed by bending a wire of length 4*pi meter. If an electric current of 4*pi*sqrt(3) A is flowing through the sides of the polygon, the magnetic field at the centre of the polygon would be x * 10^-7 T. The value of x is ___.
Ans_des: Perimeter = 4*pi m, so each side = 4*pi/6 = 2*pi/3 m. Distance from centre to side d = (2*pi/3)/(2*tan(30)) = (2*pi/3)/(2/sqrt(3)) = pi*sqrt(3)/3 = pi/sqrt(3). B for one side = (mu_0*I)/(4*pi*d)*(2*sin30). B_total = 6*(mu_0*4*pi*sqrt(3))/(4*pi*(pi/sqrt(3)))*sin30 = 6*(10^-7*4*pi*sqrt(3)*sqrt(3))/(pi)*0.5... Working: B = 6*(mu_0*3)/(pi) = 6*4*pi*10^-7*3/pi = 72*10^-7 T. x = 72.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Assertion (A): PH3 has lower boiling point than NH3.
Reason (R): In liquid state NH3 molecules are associated through van der Waal's forces, but PH3 molecules are associated through hydrogen bonding.
(1) Both A and R are correct and R is correct explanation of A.
(2) Both A and R are correct but R is not correct explanation of A.
(3) A is correct but R is not correct.
(4) A is not correct but R is correct.
Ans: A is correct but R is not correct
Ans_des: PH3 has lower boiling point than NH3 is TRUE. But the Reason is WRONG: It is NH3 that forms hydrogen bonds (not van der Waals forces), while PH3 cannot form hydrogen bonds (P is not electronegative enough). So A is correct, R is not correct.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following complex is homoleptic?
(1) [Co(NH3)4Cl2]+
(2) [Ni(NH3)2Cl2]
(3) [Fe(NH3)4Cl2]+
(4) [Ni(CN)4]^2-
Ans_des: A homoleptic complex has all identical ligands. [Ni(CN)4]^2- has all CN^- ligands (same type). The other complexes have mixed ligands (NH3 and Cl^-).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I with List-II:
List-I (Reactions): A. Ethyl hexanoate -> hexanal, B. Benzophenone -> diphenylmethane, C. Benzaldehyde -> 1-phenylethanol, D. Ethyl acetoacetate -> 3-hydroxybutanoate ester
List-II (Reagents): (i) CH3MgBr, H2O; (ii) Zn(Hg) and conc. HCl; (iii) NaBH4, H+; (iv) DIBAL-H, H2O
(1) A-IV, B-II, C-III, D-I
(2) A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I
(3) A-IV, B-II, C-I, D-III
(4) A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
Ans_des: A: Ester to aldehyde requires DIBAL-H (iv). B: Ketone to alkane (deoxygenation) requires Clemmensen reduction - Zn(Hg)/conc. HCl (ii). C: Aldehyde + CH3MgBr gives secondary alcohol (i). D: Selective reduction of keto group with NaBH4 (iii).

subject: Chemistry
question:
We have three aqueous solutions of NaCl labeled 'A', 'B' and 'C' with concentrations 0.1 M, 0.01 M and 0.001 M respectively. The value of van't Hoff factor (i) for these solutions will be in the order:
(1) iA > iB > iC
(2) iA < iB < iC
(3) iA = iB = iC
(4) iA < iC < iB
Ans_des: NaCl is a strong electrolyte that completely dissociates (NaCl -> Na+ + Cl-) giving i = 2 regardless of concentration. So iA = iB = iC = 2 for all concentrations.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following reactions are disproportionation reactions?
A. Cu+ -> Cu^2+ + Cu
B. 3MnO4^2- + 4H+ -> 2MnO4^- + MnO2 + 2H2O
C. 3KMnO4 -> K2MnO4 + MnO2 + O2
D. 2MnO4^- + 3Mn^2+ + 2H2O -> 5MnO2 + 4H+
Choose the correct answer:
(1) B, C, D
(2) A, D
(3) A, B
(4) A, B, C
Ans_des: Disproportionation: same element simultaneously oxidized and reduced. A: Cu+ (+1) -> Cu^2+ (oxidized) and Cu^0 (reduced). YES. B: MnO4^2- (+6) -> MnO4^- (+7, oxidized) and MnO2 (+4, reduced). YES. C: KMnO4 (+7) -> K2MnO4 (+6) and MnO2 (+4), Mn only reduces, not disproportionation. D: Different species react (comproportionation). So A and B are disproportionation reactions.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following compound will most easily be attacked by an electrophile?
(1) Chlorobenzene
(2) Benzene
(3) Toluene
(4) Phenol
Ans_des: Phenol has the -OH group which strongly activates the benzene ring by donating electron density through resonance to ortho and para positions. This makes phenol the most reactive towards electrophilic aromatic substitution. Order: Phenol > Toluene > Benzene > Chlorobenzene.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: The NH2 group in aniline is ortho and para directing and a powerful activating group.
Statement II: Aniline does not undergo Friedel-Craft's reaction (alkylation and acylation).
(1) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
(2) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(3) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
(4) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Ans_des: Statement I is true: NH2 is strongly electron-donating by resonance, making it ortho/para director and activating group. Statement II is true: Aniline does not undergo Friedel-Craft's reaction because NH2 reacts with AlCl3 (Lewis acid catalyst) to form a salt, deactivating the ring.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: A solution of [Ni(H2O)6]^2+ is green in colour.
Statement II: A solution of [Ni(CN)4]^2- is colourless.
(1) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(2) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
(3) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
(4) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Ans_des: Statement I is true: [Ni(H2O)6]^2+ is green due to d-d transitions (Ni^2+ has 3d^8 config, H2O is weak field ligand, no pairing). Statement II is true: [Ni(CN)4]^2- is colourless because CN^- is strong field ligand causing complete pairing, so no d-d transition is possible.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In case of isoelectronic species the size of F^-, Ne and Na+ is affected by:
(1) Electron-electron interaction in the outer orbitals.
(2) Principal quantum number (n)
(3) Nuclear charge (z)
(4) None of the factors because their size is the same
Ans_des: For isoelectronic species (same number of electrons), the size depends on nuclear charge Z. Greater nuclear charge means greater attraction on the same number of electrons, hence smaller size. Order: F^- > Ne > Na+ as Z increases from 9 to 10 to 11.

subject: Chemistry
question:
According to the wave-particle duality of matter by de Broglie, which graph plot presents the most appropriate relationship between wavelength of electron (lambda) and momentum of electron (p)?
(1) p vs lambda: linear increasing
(2) 1/p vs lambda: curved
(3) p vs lambda: linear increasing from origin
(4) p vs lambda: rectangular hyperbola (lambda decreases as p increases)
Ans: Graph (4): rectangular hyperbola - lambda decreases as p increases
Ans_des: de Broglie relation: lambda = h/p, so lambda is inversely proportional to p. The graph of lambda vs p is a rectangular hyperbola (as p increases, lambda decreases). This is option (4).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Arrange the bonds in order of increasing ionic character in the molecules LiF, K2O, N2, SO2 and ClF3:
(1) LiF < K2O < ClF3 < SO2 < N2
(2) N2 < SO2 < ClF3 < K2O < LiF
(3) ClF3 < N2 < SO2 < K2O < LiF
(4) N2 < ClF3 < SO2 < K2O < LiF
Ans_des: Ionic character depends on electronegativity difference. N2 (same atoms, zero difference), SO2 (S-O, moderate), ClF3 (Cl-F, moderate-high), K2O (K-O, large difference, K is electropositive metal), LiF (Li-F, largest difference). Order: N2 < SO2 < ClF3 < K2O < LiF.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Assertion (A): Haloalkanes react with KCN to form alkyl cyanides as main product while with AgCN form isocyanide as the main product.
Reason (R): KCN and AgCN both are highly ionic compounds.
(1) A is not correct but R is correct.
(2) Both A and R correct and R is correct explanation of A.
(3) Both A and R correct but R is not correct explanation of A.
(4) A is correct but R is not correct.
Ans: A is correct but R is not correct
Ans_des: Assertion A is correct: KCN (ionic, gives CN^- which attacks via C) gives alkyl cyanides; AgCN (covalent, attacks via N) gives isocyanides. Reason R is WRONG: AgCN is NOT ionic - it is covalent. So A is correct but R is not correct.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In acidic medium, K2Cr2O7 shows oxidising action as represented in the half reaction:
Cr2O7^2- + XH+ + Ye^- -> 2A + ZH2O
X, Y, Z and A are respectively:
(1) 8, 4, 6 and Cr2O3
(2) 14, 6, 7 and Cr^3+
(3) 14, 7, 6 and Cr^3+
(4) 8, 6, 4 and Cr2O3
Ans_des: Cr2O7^2- + 14H+ + 6e^- -> 2Cr^3+ + 7H2O. Cr goes from +6 to +3 (gain of 3 electrons per Cr, 6 total for 2 Cr). X=14, Y=6, Z=7, A=Cr^3+.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Aminobenzene and aniline are same organic compounds.
Statement II: Aminobenzene and aniline are different organic compounds.
(1) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
(2) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(3) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
(4) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Ans: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Ans_des: Aniline (C6H5NH2) is also known as aminobenzene or phenylamine. Both names refer to the same compound: benzene ring with an amino group (-NH2). Statement I is correct; Statement II is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In Kjeldahl's method for estimation of nitrogen, CuSO4 acts as:
(1) Oxidising agent
(2) Catalytic agent
(3) Reducing agent
(4) Hydrolysis agent
Ans_des: In Kjeldahl's method, the organic compound is heated with concentrated H2SO4. CuSO4 acts as a catalyst to speed up the digestion/oxidation process. The reaction: organic compound + H2SO4 (with CuSO4 as catalyst) -> (NH4)2SO4.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Potassium hydrogen phthalate is a primary standard for standardisation of sodium hydroxide solution.
Statement II: In this titration phenolphthalein can be used as indicator.
(1) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(2) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
(3) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
(4) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Ans_des: Statement I is true: Potassium hydrogen phthalate (KHP) is a widely used primary standard for NaOH standardization. Statement II is true: Phenolphthalein is suitable as indicator for this weak acid-strong base titration (changes from colourless in acid to pink in base at pH ~8.2-10).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Choose the correct option for free expansion of an ideal gas under adiabatic condition:
(1) q = 0, delta_T < 0, w not equal to 0
(2) q not equal to 0, delta_T = 0, w = 0
(3) q = 0, delta_T not equal to 0, w = 0
(4) q = 0, delta_T = 0, w = 0
Ans_des: Free expansion means expansion against zero external pressure. So w = -P_ext*dV = 0. Adiabatic means q = 0. By first law: delta_U = q + w = 0. For ideal gas, internal energy depends only on temperature, so delta_U = 0 means delta_T = 0.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify A and B in the following sequence of reaction:
Methylbenzene + Cl2/hv -> A + H2O (373K) -> B
(1) A = benzal chloride (CHCl2), B = benzoic acid (COOH)
(2) A = benzal chloride (CHCl2), B = benzaldehyde (CHO)
(3) A = benzoyl chloride (COCl), B = benzaldehyde (CHO)
(4) A = benzyl chloride (CH2Cl), B = benzaldehyde (CHO)
Ans: A = benzal chloride (CHCl2 at CH3 position), B = benzaldehyde (CHO)
Ans_des: CH3-benzene + Cl2/hv (free radical chlorination) -> CHCl2-benzene (benzal dichloride, A). A + H2O at 373K -> gem-diol -> loses H2O to give CHO-benzene (benzaldehyde, B). Chlorination at side chain gives CHCl2 which hydrolyzes to CHO.

subject: Chemistry
question:
If one strand of a DNA has the sequence ATGCTTCA, the sequence of bases in the complementary strand is:
(1) TACGAAGT
(2) CATTAGCT
(3) GTACTTAC
(4) ATGCGACT
Ans_des: In DNA base pairing: A pairs with T, T pairs with A, G pairs with C, C pairs with G. Given strand: A-T-G-C-T-T-C-A. Complementary: T-A-C-G-A-A-G-T = TACGAAGT.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Ionic reactions with organic compounds proceed through:
A. Homolytic bond cleavage
B. Heterolytic bond cleavage
C. Free radical formation
D. Primary free radical
E. Secondary free radical
Choose the correct answer:
(1) C only
(2) D and E only
(3) A only
(4) B only
Ans_des: Ionic reactions (involving ions/charged species) occur through heterolytic bond cleavage where both electrons go to one atom, forming ions (carbocations or carbanions). Example: CH3-Br -> CH3+ + Br^-. Homolytic cleavage produces free radicals, which are involved in free radical reactions, not ionic reactions.

Ans_des: Amphoteric oxides react with both acids and bases. PbO2, Al2O3, and SnO2 are amphoteric. CO, NO, N2O are neutral. Cl2O7, SiO2, N2O5 are acidic. Count = 3.

question: Number of optical isomers possible for 2-chlorobutane is ___.
Ans_des: 2-chlorobutane: CH3-CHCl-CH2-CH3. C2 is a chiral centre (bonded to H, Cl, CH3, CH2CH3 - all four different groups). One chiral centre gives 2^1 = 2 optical isomers (R and S or d and l forms).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Consider the following reaction:
3PbCl2 + 2(NH4)3PO4 -> Pb3(PO4)2 + 6NH4Cl
If 72 mmol of PbCl2 is mixed with 50 mmol of (NH4)3PO4, then the amount of Pb3(PO4)2 formed is ___ mmol.
Ans_des: For reaction: 3 mol PbCl2 reacts with 2 mol (NH4)3PO4. Moles needed for 72 mmol PbCl2: (NH4)3PO4 needed = 72*(2/3) = 48 mmol < 50 mmol available. PbCl2 is limiting reagent. Pb3(PO4)2 formed = 72*(1/3) = 24 mmol.

a) SrSO4, b) Mg(NH4)PO4, c) BaCrO4, d) Mn(OH)2, e) PbSO4, f) PbCrO4, g) AgBr, h) PbI2, i) CaC2O4, j) [Fe(OH)2(CH3COO)]
Ans_des: White coloured salts: SrSO4 (white), Mg(NH4)PO4 (white), Mn(OH)2 (white/pale pink), PbSO4 (white), CaC2O4 (white). Non-white: BaCrO4 (yellow), PbCrO4 (yellow), AgBr (pale yellow), PbI2 (yellow), [Fe(OH)2(CH3COO)] (greenish). Count = 5.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Total number of deactivating groups in aromatic electrophilic substitution reaction among the following is ___:
-C(=O)-OCH3, -C(=O)-N(H)CH3, -N(H)CH3, -C(triple bond)N, -OCH3
Ans_des: Deactivating groups in EAS are those that withdraw electron density from the ring (-I or -R effect). -C(=O)-OCH3 (ester group) is deactivating (-R effect). -C triple bond N (nitrile) is deactivating (-R effect). -C(=O)-N(H)CH3 (amide) is slightly deactivating. -N(H)CH3 and -OCH3 are activating. Count of clearly deactivating = 2 (ester and nitrile).

The number of molecules/ions having trigonal bipyramidal shape is ___:

Ans_des: PF5: sp3d hybridization, trigonal bipyramidal. PCl5: sp3d, trigonal bipyramidal. Fe(CO)5: sp3d, trigonal bipyramidal. BrF5: sp3d2, square pyramidal. [PtCl4]^2-: dsp2, square planar. BF3: sp2, trigonal planar. Trigonal bipyramidal count = 3 (PF5, PCl5, Fe(CO)5).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let S = {z in C : |z-1|=1 and (sqrt(2)-1)*(z+z_bar) - i*(z-z_bar) = 2*sqrt(2)}. Let z1, z2 in S such that |z1| = max|z| and |z2| = min|z| in S. Then |sqrt(2)*z1 - z2|^2 equals:
(1) 2
(2) 4
(3) 3
(4) 1
Ans_des: Let z = x+iy. |z-1|=1 gives circle: (x-1)^2+y^2=1. The second condition: (sqrt(2)-1)*2x + 2y = 2*sqrt(2), giving (sqrt(2)-1)*x + y = sqrt(2). Solving these simultaneously gives x=1,y=1 (z2=1+i) and x=1/(2-sqrt(2)), y=sqrt(2)-1/sqrt(2) (z1). |sqrt(2)*z1 - z2|^2 = (sqrt(2))^2 = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let S = {x in R : (sqrt(3)+sqrt(2))^x + (sqrt(3)-sqrt(2))^x = 10}. Then the number of elements in S is:
(1) 2
(2) 1
(3) 4
(4) 0
Ans_des: Let t = (sqrt(3)+sqrt(2))^x. Then (sqrt(3)-sqrt(2))^x = 1/t (since product = 1). So t + 1/t = 10. t^2 - 10t + 1 = 0. t = (10 +/- sqrt(96))/2 = 5 +/- 2*sqrt(6). Both positive values for t. (sqrt(3)+sqrt(2))^x = 5+2*sqrt(6) gives x=2; = 5-2*sqrt(6) gives x=-2. So S = {2, -2}, 2 elements.

subject: Mathematics
question:
For 0 < theta < pi/2, if eccentricity of hyperbola x^2 - y^2*cosec^2(theta) = 5 is sqrt(7) times eccentricity of ellipse x^2*cosec^2(theta) + y^2 = 5, then theta is:
(1) pi/4
(2) 5*pi/12
(3) pi/3
(4) pi/6
Ans_des: Hyperbola: x^2/5 - y^2/(5*sin^2(theta)) = 1. e_H = sqrt(1 + sin^2(theta)). Ellipse: x^2/(5*sin^2(theta)) + y^2/5 = 1. e_E = sqrt(1 - sin^2(theta)) = |cos(theta)|. Given: sqrt(1+sin^2(theta)) = sqrt(7)*cos(theta). 1+sin^2(theta) = 7*cos^2(theta) = 7(1-sin^2(theta)). 8*sin^2(theta) = 6. sin(theta) = sqrt(3)/2. theta = pi/3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f: R->R and g: R->R be defined as f(x) = {log_e(x) for x>0; e^(-x) for x<=0} and g(x) = {x for x>=0; e^x for x<0}. Then gof: R->R is:
(1) Neither one-one nor onto
(2) Onto but not one-one
(3) Both one-one and onto
(4) One-one but not onto
Ans_des: gof(x) = g(f(x)). For x<=0: f(x)=e^(-x)>=1>0, so g(f(x))=f(x)=e^(-x). For 0<x<1: f(x)=ln(x)<0, so g(f(x))=e^(f(x))=e^(ln(x))=x. For x>=1: f(x)=ln(x)>=0, so g(f(x))=ln(x). So gof = {e^(-x) for x<=0; x for 0<x<1; ln(x) for x>=1}. Range: (0,1) from x in (0,1) is not covered by x<=0 branch which gives (1,inf). Not onto (-inf,0). Not one-one since multiple x can give same value. Neither one-one nor onto.

If n is the number of ways five different employees can sit into four indistinguishable offices where any office may have any number of persons including zero, then n is equal to:
(1) 43
(2) 53
(3) 47
(4) 51
Ans_des: This is distributing 5 distinct objects into 4 identical boxes (Stirling numbers of second kind). Distributions: (5,0,0,0)=1, (4,1,0,0)=5, (3,2,0,0)=10, (3,1,1,0)=10, (2,2,1,0)=15, (2,1,1,1)=10. Total = 1+5+10+10+15+10 = 51.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the system of equations:
2x + 3y - z = 5
x + alpha*y + 3z = -4
3x - y + beta*z = 7
has infinitely many solutions, then 13*alpha*beta is equal to:
(1) 1220
(2) 1110
(3) 1120
(4) 1210
Ans_des: For infinitely many solutions, Delta = 0 and Delta_2 = Delta_3 = 0. From Delta_2 = 0: 13*beta + 16 = 0, beta = -16/13. From Delta_3 = 0: -alpha - 70 = 0, alpha = -70. 13*alpha*beta = 13*(-70)*(-16/13) = 70*16 = 1120.

subject: Mathematics
question:
A bag contains 8 balls, colours either white or black. 4 balls are drawn without replacement and 2 are white and 2 are black. The probability that the bag contains equal number (4W, 4B) of white and black balls is:
(1) 2/5
(2) 1/7
(3) 1/5
(4) 2/7
Ans_des: Possible compositions: (2W,6B), (3W,5B), (4W,4B), (5W,3B), (6W,2B) each with prior probability 1/9 (assuming uniform prior). P(2W 2B drawn | 4W 4B) = C(4,2)*C(4,2)/C(8,4) = 36/70. Summing all terms and applying Bayes theorem: P(4W4B|2W2B drawn) = 36/126 = 2/7.

(4) sqrt(2)*pi^2/16

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let 3, a, b, c be in AP and 3, a-1, b+1, c+9 be in GP. Then the arithmetic mean of a, b and c is:
(1) -4
(2) -1
(3) 11
(4) 13
Ans_des: In AP: a-3 = b-a = c-b = d (common diff). In GP: (a-1)^2 = 3*(b+1). With a=7 (from solving a^2-8a+7=0, a not equal to 1): b=2*7-3=11, c=2*11-7=15. AM = (7+11+15)/3 = 33/3 = 11.

question:
Let a = -5i + j - 3k, b = i + 2j - 4k and c = (((a x b) x i) x i) x i. Then c.(-i + j + k) is equal to:
(1) -10
(2) -15
(3) -12
(4) -13
Ans_des: c = (((a x b) x i) x i) x i. Using vector triple product: ((a x b) x i) = (a.i)*b - (b.i)*a = -5b - a. Let u = -(5b+a) = 0i - 11j + 23k. (u x i) x i = (u.i)*i - (i.i)*u = 0 - u = 11j - 23k. c = 0i + 11j - 23k. c.(-i+j+k) = 0 + 11 - 23 = -12.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the shortest distance between lines (x-lambda)/(-2) = (y-2)/1 = (z-1)/1 and (x-sqrt(3))/1 = (y-1)/(-2) = (z-2)/1 is 1, then the sum of all possible values of lambda is:
(1) 0
(2) 2*sqrt(3)
(3) 3*sqrt(3)
(4) -2*sqrt(3)
Ans_des: Direction vectors: V1=(-2,1,1), V2=(1,-2,1). V1 x V2 = (3,3,3) = 3(1,1,1). |V1 x V2| = 3*sqrt(3). AB = (lambda-sqrt(3), 1, -1). Shortest distance = |AB.(V1 x V2)| / |V1 x V2| = |(lambda-sqrt(3)+1-1)|/sqrt(3) = |lambda-sqrt(3)|/sqrt(3) = 1. |lambda-sqrt(3)| = sqrt(3). lambda = 2*sqrt(3) or 0. Sum = 2*sqrt(3).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f: R->R be defined as:
f(x) = {(a - b*cos(2x))/x^2 for x<0; x^2 + cx + 2 for 0<=x<=1; 2x+1 for x>1}.
If f is continuous everywhere and m is the number of points where f is NOT differentiable, then m+a+b+c equals:
(1) 4
(2) 2
(3) 1
(4) 3
Ans_des: Continuity at x=0: limit as x->0- of (a-b*cos(2x))/x^2 = limit (a-b*(1-2x^2+...))/x^2. For finite limit: a=b. Then limit = 2b*x^2/x^2 = 2b = 2, so b=1=a. Continuity at x=1: 1+c+2=3, c=0. f'(x) at x=0: left = 2b*x/x... = 0 matching 2x|_0=0. At x=1: left f'(1-)=2, right f'(1+)=2. So m=0. m+a+b+c=0+1+1+0=2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let y=y(x) be the solution of the differential equation dy/dx = 2x*(x+y)^3 - x*(x+y)^(-1), y(0)=1. Then (1/sqrt(2) + y(1/sqrt(2)))^2 equals:
(1) 2/(1+sqrt(e))
(2) 3/(3-sqrt(e))
(3) 1/(2-sqrt(e))
(4) 4/(4+sqrt(e))
Ans_des: Let t=x+y. dt/dx = 1+dy/dx = 1+2x*t^3-x*t^(-1). dt/(2t^3-t) = x dx. Integrating: ln|(t^2-1/2)/t^2| = x^2+c. y(0)=1 gives t(0)=1, ln(1/2)=c. At x=1/sqrt(2): (x+y)^2 = 1/(2-sqrt(e)). So (1/sqrt(2)+y(1/sqrt(2)))^2 = 1/(2-sqrt(e)).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let C: x^2+y^2=4 and C': x^2+y^2-4*lambda*x+9=0 be two circles. If the set of all values of lambda so that C and C' intersect at two distinct points is R-[a,b], then the point (8a+12, 16b-20) lies on the curve:
(1) 5x^2 - y = -11
(2) x^2 + 2y^2 - 5x + 6y = 3
(3) x^2 - 4y^2 = 7
(4) 6x^2 + y^2 = 42
Ans_des: C: centre (0,0) r1=2. C': centre (2*lambda,0) r2=sqrt(4*lambda^2-9). For intersection: |r1-r2|<d<r1+r2. Working through inequalities gives lambda in R-[-13/8, 13/8]. So a=-13/8, b=13/8. (8a+12, 16b-20) = (-13+12, 26-20) = (-1, 6). Check: 6*(-1)^2+(6)^2=6+36=42. Yes, lies on 6x^2+y^2=42.

question: Let P={z in C: |z+2-3i|<=1} and Q={z in C: z*(1+i)+z_bar*(1-i)<=-8}. Let in P intersection Q, |z-3+2i| be maximum and minimum at z1 and z2 respectively. If |z1|^2 + 2*|z2|^2 = alpha + beta*sqrt(2), where alpha, beta are integers, then alpha+beta equals ___.
Ans_des: P: circle (x+2)^2+(y-3)^2<=1, centre C=(-2,3), radius 1. Q: 2(x-y)<=-8, so x-y<=-4. Distance from C=(-2,3) to P=reference point (3,-2): slope=-1, line y=-x+1. Max |z-3+2i| at z1=(-2-1/sqrt(2), 3+1/sqrt(2)), min at z2=(-3/2, 5/2). |z1|^2+2|z2|^2 = 31+5*sqrt(2). alpha+beta=31+5=36.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let 3, 7, 11, 15, ..., 403 and 2, 5, 8, 11, ..., 404 be two arithmetic progressions. Then the sum of the common terms in them is equal to ___.
Ans_des: First AP: a1=3, d1=4. Second AP: a2=2, d2=3. LCM(4,3)=12. Common terms start at 11 (first common), with common difference 12: 11, 23, 35, ..., 395. Last term: 11+(n-1)*12=395, n=33. Sum = (33/2)*(11+395) = (33/2)*406 = 33*203 = 6699.

subject: Mathematics
question: If the integral from -pi/2 to pi/2 of [8*sqrt(2)*cos(x) / ((1+e^(sin x))*(1+sin^4(x)))] dx = alpha*pi + beta*ln(3+2*sqrt(2)), where alpha, beta are integers, then alpha^2 + beta^2 equals ___.
Ans_des: Using f(x)+f(-x) property to eliminate e^(sin x) term: integral becomes 8*sqrt(2) * integral(0 to pi/2) cos(x)/(1+sin^4(x)) dx. Let t=sin(x): 8*sqrt(2)*integral(0 to 1) dt/(1+t^4). Evaluating: = 2*pi + 2*ln(3+2*sqrt(2)) = alpha*pi + beta*ln(3+2*sqrt(2)). alpha=2, beta=2. alpha^2+beta^2=4+4=8.

subject: Mathematics
question: If the coefficient of x^30 in the expansion of (1+1/x)^6 * (1+x^2)^7 * (1-x^3)^8, where x not equal to 0, is alpha, then |alpha| equals ___.
Ans_des: Coefficient of x^30 in (1+1/x)^6*(1+x^2)^7*(1-x^3)^8 = coefficient of x^36 in (1+x)^6*(1+x^2)^7*(1-x^3)^8. General term: 6Cr1 * 7Cr2 * 8Cr3 * (-1)^r3 * x^(r1+2r2+3r3). Need r1+2r2+3r3=36. Summing over all valid combinations: alpha = 882 - 8*272 + 28*22 = 882 - 2176 + 616 = -678. |alpha|=678.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let the line L: sqrt(2)*x+y=alpha pass through P (intersection of circle x^2+y^2=3 and parabola x^2=2y in first quadrant). Line L touches two circles C1 and C2 of equal radius 2*sqrt(3) with centres Q1 and Q2 on y-axis. The square of area of triangle PQ1Q2 equals ___.
Ans_des: Solving x^2+y^2=3 and x^2=2y: y=1 (taking first quadrant). P=(sqrt(2),1). Line L: sqrt(2)*x+y=sqrt(2)*sqrt(2)+1=3, so alpha=3. Centres on y-axis: (0,beta). Distance from (0,beta) to line sqrt(2)x+y=3 equals 2*sqrt(3): |beta-3|/sqrt(3) = 2*sqrt(3), |beta-3|=6, beta=9 or beta=-3. Q1=(0,9), Q2=(0,-3). |Q1Q2|=12. Base = sqrt(2) (perpendicular from P to y-axis). Area = (1/2)*12*sqrt(2) = 6*sqrt(2). Area^2 = 72.

subject: Physics
question:
A disc of radius R and mass M is rolling horizontally without slipping with speed v. It then moves up an inclined smooth surface. The maximum height that the disc can go up the incline is:
(1) 2v^2/(3g)
(2) v^2/g
(3) 3v^2/(4g)
(4) v^2/(2g)
topics: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
Ans_des: When the disc moves up the smooth incline, there is no friction so the rotational KE remains unchanged. Only translational KE converts to PE. (1/2)Mv^2 = Mgh. Therefore h = v^2/(2g).

subject: Physics
question:
A light planet is revolving around a massive star in a circular orbit of radius R with a period of revolution T. If the force of attraction between planet and star is proportional to R^(-3/2), then:
(1) T^2 proportional to R^(5/2)
(2) T^2 proportional to R^3
(3) T^2 proportional to R^(3/2)
(4) T^2 proportional to R^(7/2)
Ans_des: F = K/R^(3/2) = mv^2/R. So v = K'/R^(3/4). T = 2*pi*R/v = (2*pi/K)*R^(5/4). Therefore T proportional to R^(5/4) and T^2 proportional to R^(5/2).
