question:
A clock has 75 cm second hand and 60 cm minute hand. In 30 minutes, the tip of second hand will travel x distance more than the tip of minute hand. Value of x in meters is nearly (pi = 3.14):
(1) 139.4  (2) 140.5  (3) 220.0  (4) 118.9
Ans_des: In 30 min: minute hand completes half revolution, x_min = pi x r_min = pi x (60/100) m. Second hand completes 30 revolutions, x_second = 30 x 2pi x (75/100) m. x = x_second - x_min = 30 x 2 x 3.14 x 0.75 - 3.14 x 0.60 = 141.3 - 1.884 = 139.4 m.

subject: Physics
question:
Young's modulus Y = 49000 M/l (in dyne/cm^2). Smallest scale divisions: 5 g (load axis), 0.02 cm (extension axis). M = 500 g, l = 2 cm. Percentage error of Y is:
(1) 0.2%  (2) 0.02%  (3) 2%  (4) 0.5%
Ans_des: deltaY/Y = deltaM/M + deltal/l = 5/500 + 0.02/2 = 0.01 + 0.01 = 0.02. Percentage error = 2%.

subject: Physics
question:
Two different adiabatic paths for same gas intersect two isothermal curves in P-V diagram (points a, b, c, d). The relation between Va/Vd and Vb/Vc is:
(1) Va/Vd = (Vb/Vc)^-1  (2) Va/Vd not equal to Vb/Vc  (3) Va/Vd = Vb/Vc  (4) Va/Vd = (Vb/Vc)^2
Ans_des: For adiabatic process: TV^(gamma-1) = constant. On first adiabatic: Ta x Va^(gamma-1) = Td x Vd^(gamma-1), so (Va/Vd)^(gamma-1) = Td/Ta. On second adiabatic: Tb x Vb^(gamma-1) = Tc x Vc^(gamma-1), so (Vb/Vc)^(gamma-1) = Tc/Tb. Since Td = Tc (same isotherm) and Ta = Tb (same isotherm), Va/Vd = Vb/Vc.

subject: Physics
question:
Two planets A and B with masses m1, m2 move around sun in circular orbits r1, r2. Angular momentum of A is L, B is 3L. The ratio TA/TB is:
(1) (r2/r1)^(3/2)  (2) (r1/r2)^3  (3) (1/27)(m2/m1)^3  (4) 27(m1/m2)^3
Ans_des: Angular momentum L = m x v x r = m x (2*pi*r/T) x r = 2*pi*m*r^2/T. So T = 2*pi*m*r^2/L. TA/TB = (m1*r1^2/L) / (m2*r2^2/3L) = 3*(m1/m2)*(r1/r2)^2. From Kepler's third law r^3 proportional to T^2. After solving using both relations: TA/TB = (1/27)*(m2/m1)^3.

subject: Physics
question:
A LCR circuit at resonance has resistance R halved (other parameters same). Current amplitude at resonance will be:
(1) Zero  (2) double  (3) same  (4) halved
Ans_des: At resonance, impedance Z = R. Current amplitude I = V/R. If R is halved, I = V/(R/2) = 2V/R = 2I. Current doubles.

subject: Physics
question:
The output Y of given logic circuit (NAND followed by NOR) for inputs A and B:
(1) A.B.(A+B)  (2) A.B  (3) 0  (4) A_bar.B
Ans_des: By truth table analysis: For any combination of A and B, the output Y = 0. The circuit is designed such that the output is always 0 regardless of inputs A and B.

subject: Physics
question:
Two charged conducting spheres of radii a and b are connected by conducting wire. The ratio of charges (q1:q2) is:
(1) sqrt(ab)  (2) ab  (3) a/b  (4) b/a
Ans_des: When connected, they reach same potential. V1 = V2. Kq1/a = Kq2/b. q1/q2 = a/b.

subject: Physics
question:
Correct Bernoulli's equation (symbols have usual meaning):
(1) P + mgh + (1/2)mv^2 = constant
(2) P + rho*g*h + (1/2)*rho*v^2 = constant
(3) P + rho*g*h + rho*v^2 = constant
(4) P + (1/2)rho*g*h + (1/2)rho*v^2 = constant
Ans_des: Bernoulli's equation per unit volume: P + rho*g*h + (1/2)*rho*v^2 = constant. This uses fluid density rho (not mass m), and has the factor 1/2 for kinetic energy term.

subject: Physics
question:
A player catches a cricket ball of mass 150 g at speed 20 m/s. Catching process takes 0.1 s. Magnitude of force exerted by ball on hand is:
(1) 150 N  (2) 3 N  (3) 30 N  (4) 300 N

subject: Physics
question:
A stationary particle breaks into two parts of masses mA and mB with velocities vA and vB. Ratio of kinetic energies (KB:KA) is:
(1) vB:vA  (2) mB:mA  (3) mB*vB : mA*vA  (4) 1:1
topics: Work, Energy and Power / Conservation of Momentum
Ans_des: Initial momentum = 0, so |pA| = |pB|, i.e., mA*vA = mB*vB. KE_A = (1/2)*mA*vA^2, KE_B = (1/2)*mB*vB^2. KE_A/KE_B = (mA*vA^2)/(mB*vB^2) = (mA*vA x vA)/(mB*vB x vB) = (pA x vA)/(pB x vB) = vA/vB (since pA=pB). So KB/KA = vB/vA.

subject: Physics
question:
Critical angle of incidence for a pair of optical media is 45 deg. Refractive indices of first and second media are in the ratio:
(1) sqrt(2):1  (2) 1:2  (3) 1:sqrt(2)  (4) 2:1
Ans_des: sin(critical angle) = mu2/mu1 (rarer/denser). sin(45 deg) = 1/sqrt(2) = mu2/mu1. So mu1/mu2 = sqrt(2)/1. Ratio mu1:mu2 = sqrt(2):1.

subject: Physics
question:
Vernier caliper: 9 MSD = 10 VSD, shortest MSD = 1 mm. MSR = 2 cm, 2nd VSD coincides. Mass of sphere = 8.635 g. Density of sphere is:
(1) 2.5 g/cm^3  (2) 1.7 g/cm^3  (3) 2.2 g/cm^3  (4) 2.0 g/cm^3

subject: Physics
question: A uniform thin metal plate of mass 10 kg with dimensions shown (L-shaped region from (0,0) to (3,2) minus region (1,1) to (2,2)). The ratio of x and y coordinates of center of mass is n/9. Value of n is ______.
topics: System of Particles / Centre of Mass
Ans_des: Total mass m3 = 12 kg (full 3x2 rectangle), m2 = 2 kg (missing 1x1 square at top centre). For x: 10*x1 + 2*(1.5) = 12*(1.5), so x1 = 1.5 cm. For y: 10*y1 + 2*(1.5) = 12*1.0, so y1 = 0.9 cm. Ratio x1/y1 = 1.5/0.9 = 15/9. n = 15.

subject: Physics
question: An electron with kinetic energy 5 eV enters region of uniform magnetic field 3 micro T perpendicular to its direction. Electric field E is applied perpendicular to both velocity and magnetic field so electron moves straight. Value of E is ______ N/C. (m_e = 9 x 10^-31 kg, e = 1.6 x 10^-19 C)
Ans_des: For straight path: qE = qvB, so E = vB. v = sqrt(2KE/m) = sqrt(2 x 5 x 1.6 x 10^-19 / 9 x 10^-31) = sqrt(1.778 x 10^12) approx 1.333 x 10^6 m/s. E = v x B = 1.333 x 10^6 x 3 x 10^-6 = 4 N/C.

subject: Physics
question: Square loop PQRS: 10 turns, area 3.6 x 10^-3 m^2, resistance 100 ohm. Being pulled out of magnetic field B = 0.5 T uniformly in 1.0 s. Work done in pulling out is ______ x 10^-6 J.

subject: Physics
question: Resistance of wire at 0 deg C, 100 deg C and t deg C is 10 ohm, 10.2 ohm and 10.95 ohm respectively. Temperature t in Kelvin scale is ______.

subject: Physics
question: Electric field E = (2i+6j+8k)/sqrt(6) passes through surface of 4 m^2 area with unit vector n = (2i+j+k)/sqrt(6). Electric flux is ______ V-m.

subject: Physics
question: Liquid column of height 0.04 cm balances excess pressure of soap bubble. Density of liquid = 8 x 10^3 kg/m^3, surface tension = 0.28 N/m. Diameter of soap bubble is ______ cm. (g = 10 m/s^2)
Ans_des: Excess pressure in soap bubble = 4S/R. Balancing with liquid column: rho*g*h = 4S/R. R = 4S/(rho*g*h) = 4*0.28/(8000*10*0.04*10^-2) = 1.12/(32) = 0.035 m = 3.5 cm. Diameter = 7 cm.

subject: Physics
question: Closed and open organ pipes have same lengths. Ratio of frequencies of their seventh overtones is (a-1)/a. Value of a is ______.
Ans_des: 7th overtone of closed pipe = (2*7+1)th harmonic = 15th harmonic: fc = 15v/(4L). 7th overtone of open pipe = 8th harmonic: fo = 8v/(2L) = 16v/(4L). fc/fo = 15/16 = (a-1)/a. So a = 16.

subject: Physics
question: Three vectors OP, OQ and OR each of magnitude A are acting: OQ along +y (90 deg), OP at 45 deg below x-axis (in first quadrant), OR at 45 deg below x-axis (going down). Resultant is A*sqrt(x). Value of x is ______.
Ans_des: From figure: OQ along +y direction (magnitude A), OP along +x direction (magnitude A), OR at 45 deg below x-axis (components A/sqrt(2) in +x and -A/sqrt(2) in y). Resultant R = (A + A/sqrt(2))i + (A - A/sqrt(2))j. |R| = sqrt((A+A/sqrt(2))^2 + (A-A/sqrt(2))^2) = sqrt(A^2+sqrt(2)A^2+A^2/2 + A^2-sqrt(2)A^2+A^2/2) = sqrt(3A^2) = A*sqrt(3). x = 3.

subject: Physics
question: Parallel beam of monochromatic light wavelength 600 nm passes through single slit 0.4 mm wide. Angular divergence corresponding to second order minima is ______ x 10^-3 rad.
Ans_des: For minima: sin(theta) = m*lambda/b. For 2nd order: sin(theta) = 2*600*10^-9/(0.4*10^-3) = 3*10^-3 rad (one side). Total angular divergence for 2nd order = 2 x 3*10^-3 = 6*10^-3 rad.

subject: Physics
question: In alpha particle scattering experiment, distance of closest approach is 4.5 x 10^-14 m. Target nucleus atomic number = 80. Maximum velocity of alpha particle is ______ x 10^5 m/s. (1/(4*pi*epsilon_0) = 9 x 10^9, m_alpha = 6.72 x 10^-27 kg)

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: IUPAC name of Compound A (chloro with two nitro groups on benzene) is 4-chloro-1,3-dinitrobenzene. Statement II: IUPAC name of Compound B (aniline with CH3 and C2H5 substituents) is 4-ethyl-2-methylaniline.
(1) Both correct  (2) I incorrect, II correct  (3) I correct, II incorrect  (4) Both incorrect
Ans: Statement I incorrect, Statement II correct
Ans_des: For Compound A: Cl is at position 1, NO2 groups at positions 2 and 4. Correct name = 1-chloro-2,4-dinitrobenzene (not 4-chloro-1,3-dinitrobenzene). Statement I is incorrect. For Compound B: NH2 at C1, CH3 at C2, C2H5 at C4. Name = 4-ethyl-2-methylaniline. Statement II is correct.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which compound will undergo fastest SN2 reaction among four alkyl bromides? (shown as primary, secondary, tertiary alkyl bromides and neopentyl type)
(1) Option 1  (2) Option 2  (3) Option 3  (4) Option 4
Ans_des: SN2 rate order: methyl > primary > secondary > tertiary. Neopentyl type (option 4) is very hindered. Option 3 is a primary (1 degree) alkyl bromide with least steric hindrance, giving fastest SN2 reaction.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Combustion of glucose (C6H12O6) produces CO2 and water. Amount of oxygen (in g) required for complete combustion of 900 g of glucose: (Molar mass = 180 g/mol)
(1) 480  (2) 960  (3) 800  (4) 32
Ans_des: C6H12O6 + 6O2 -> 6CO2 + 6H2O. Moles of glucose = 900/180 = 5 mol. Moles of O2 needed = 5 x 6 = 30 mol. Mass of O2 = 30 x 32 = 960 g.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify major products A and B in: 2-methylcyclohexanol treated with conc. H2SO4/delta gives A (E1 product); A treated with CH3COCl/pyridine gives B (acetylation).
(1) A = 1-methylcyclohexene, B = its acetate  (2) A = methylenecyclohexane  (3) Option 3  (4) Option 4
Ans_des: 2-methylcyclohexanol with conc. H2SO4 and heat undergoes E1 elimination: most substituted alkene (1-methylcyclohexene) formed as major product A (Zaitsev rule). A then reacts with CH3COCl/pyridine: but wait, A is an alkene. The alcohol reacts with CH3COCl/pyridine (acetylation of -OH): B = 1-methylcyclohexyl acetate (acetate ester). A = 1-methylcyclohexene, B = 1-methylcyclohexyl acetate.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: Stability order of +1 oxidation state of Ga, In and Tl is Ga < In < Tl. Reason R: Inert pair effect stabilizes lower oxidation state down the group.
(1) Both A and R true, R explains A  (2) A true, R false  (3) Both true, R does not explain A  (4) A false, R true
Ans: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: Inert pair effect increases down group 13: Tl+1 is most stable, In+1 more stable than Ga+1. So stability: Ga+1 < In+1 < Tl+1. This is because the 6s electrons in Tl are more penetrating and are held more tightly (relativistic effect/inert pair effect), making Tl prefer +1 state. R correctly explains A.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match tests with reaction sequences:
A. Borax bead test - B. Charcoal cavity test - C. Cobalt nitrate test - D. Flame test
I. MCO3->MO-(Co(NO3)2)->CoO.MO  II. MCO3->MCl2->M^2+  III. MSO4-(Na2B4O7)->M(BO2)2->MBO2->M  IV. MSO4-(Na2CO3)->MCO3->MO->M
Ans_des: Borax bead test (A): uses Na2B4O7, forms M(BO2)2 -> MBO2 -> M (III). Charcoal cavity test (B): uses Na2CO3, forms MCO3 -> MO -> M (IV). Cobalt nitrate test (C): MCO3->MO then Co(NO3)2 gives CoO.MO (I). Flame test (D): MCO3->MCl2->M^2+ gives characteristic flame color (II). Answer: A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match molecules with shapes:
A. NH3 - B. BrF5 - C. PCl5 - D. CH4
I. Square pyramid  II. Tetrahedral  III. Trigonal pyramidal  IV. Trigonal bipyramidal
(1) A-IV,B-III,C-I,D-II  (2) A-II,B-IV,C-I,D-III  (3) A-III,B-I,C-IV,D-II  (4) A-III,B-IV,C-I,D-II
Ans_des: NH3: sp3 hybridization, one lone pair, trigonal pyramidal shape (III). BrF5: sp3d2, one lone pair, square pyramidal shape (I). PCl5: sp3d, no lone pairs, trigonal bipyramidal (IV). CH4: sp3, no lone pairs, tetrahedral (II). Answer: A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II.

subject: Chemistry
question:
For hypothetical reactions: X <-> Y, K1=1.0; Y <-> Z, K2=2.0; Z <-> W, K3=4.0. Equilibrium constant for X <-> W is:
(1) 6.0  (2) 12.0  (3) 8.0  (4) 7.0
Ans_des: For overall reaction X <-> W (sum of three reactions), K = K1 x K2 x K3 = 1.0 x 2.0 x 4.0 = 8.0.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Thiosulphate reacts with I2 giving S4O6^2- and with Br2 giving SO4^2-. Which statement justifies this dual behaviour?
(1) Br2 undergoes oxidation; I2 undergoes reduction
(2) Thiosulphate oxidized by Br2 and reduced by I2
(3) Br2 is stronger oxidant than I2
(4) Br2 is weaker oxidant than I2
Ans: Bromine is a stronger oxidant than iodine
Ans_des: With I2: S oxidation state changes from +2 to +2.5 (partial oxidation, S2O3^2- -> S4O6^2-). With Br2: S oxidation state changes from +2 to +6 (complete oxidation, S2O3^2- -> SO4^2-). Both I2 and Br2 are oxidants, but Br2 is stronger oxidant (can fully oxidize to +6), while I2 is weaker (only partially oxidizes to +2.5).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Octahedral complex CoCl3.nNH3, with excess AgNO3 gives 2 moles AgCl. Oxidation state of Co is x. Value of x + n is:
(1) 3  (2) 6  (3) 8  (4) 5
Ans_des: 2 moles AgCl means 2 Cl- ions are outside coordination sphere (ionizable). Complex is [Co(NH3)5Cl]Cl2. Oxidation state: x + 0 - 1 = +2 overall, so x = +3. n = 5 (5 NH3 ligands). x + n = 3 + 5 = 8.

subject: Chemistry
question:
For the given open-chain glucose structure (CHO-CHOH-CHOH-CHOH-CHOH-CH2OH), the INCORRECT statement is:
(1) Can be oxidized to dicarboxylic acid with Br2 water
(2) Despite CHO does not give Schiff's test
(3) Has 4 asymmetric carbon atoms
(4) Will coexist in equilibrium with 2 other cyclic structures
Ans: Can be oxidized to a dicarboxylic acid with Br2 water
Ans_des: Statement 1 is incorrect: Br2 water oxidizes only the aldehyde (-CHO) to carboxylic acid (-COOH), giving a monocarboxylic acid (gluconic acid), not dicarboxylic acid. Statement 2 is correct: glucose in its cyclic hemiacetal form does not give Schiff's test. Statement 3 is correct: glucose has 4 chiral carbons. Statement 4 is correct: open chain and alpha/beta cyclic forms are in equilibrium.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In compound CH3-C(CH3)2-CH(H)-C(CH3)2-CH3 (2,2,4,4-tetramethylpentane-like), number of 2 degree (secondary) carbon atoms is:
(1) Three  (2) One  (3) Two  (4) Four
Ans_des: In CH3-C(CH3)2-CH-C(CH3)2-CH3: The central CH is bonded to two tertiary carbons - it is a secondary carbon (bonded to 2 C, 1 H). The C(CH3)2 carbons are tertiary (bonded to 3 C). The CH3 groups are primary. Only one secondary carbon is present.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following are aromatic? A. naphthalene-like fused ring, B. azulene-like compound with 7+5 ring fusion, C. larger fused ring, D. bridged bicyclic compound.
(1) B and D only  (2) A and C only  (3) A and B only  (4) C and D only
Ans_des: Aromaticity requires: planar structure, cyclic pi system, (4n+2) pi electrons (Huckel's rule). From the figures: A is non-aromatic (not fulfilling 4n+2), B is aromatic (fulfills conditions), C is non-aromatic, D is aromatic. Answer: B and D only.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Among F2, Cl2, Br2 and I2, which can undergo disproportionation reaction?
(1) Only I2  (2) Cl2, Br2 and I2  (3) F2, Cl2 and Br2  (4) F2 and Cl2
Ans_des: Disproportionation requires the element to exist in both higher and lower oxidation states simultaneously. F2 cannot disproportionate because fluorine is the most electronegative element and cannot take positive oxidation state (no d-orbitals). Cl2, Br2 and I2 can disproportionate in alkaline solution.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: N(CH3)3 and P(CH3)3 can act as ligands to form transition metal complexes. Statement II: As N and P are in same group, nature of bonding of N(CH3)3 and P(CH3)3 is always same with transition metals.
(1) I incorrect, II correct  (2) Both correct  (3) I correct, II incorrect  (4) Both incorrect
Ans: Statement I correct, Statement II incorrect
Ans_des: Both N(CH3)3 (trimethylamine) and P(CH3)3 (trimethylphosphine) have lone pairs and can act as sigma-donor ligands - Statement I is correct. However, P(CH3)3 has empty d-orbitals and can additionally act as a pi-acceptor (back bonding from metal d to P d), while N(CH3)3 cannot (no d-orbitals available). So their bonding with transition metals is NOT always same - Statement II is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match elements with properties:
A. Cl, S - B. Ge, As - C. Fr, Ra - D. F, O
I. Highest electronegativity  II. Largest atomic size  III. Properties of both metals and non-metals  IV. Highest negative electron gain enthalpy
Ans_des: Cl and S: highest negative electron gain enthalpy in their groups (not F and O due to small size and electron repulsion) - IV. Ge and As: metalloids, show properties of both metals and non-metals - III. Fr and Ra: heaviest elements in their groups, largest atomic size - II. F and O: highest electronegativity in their groups (and overall) - I. Answer: A-IV, B-III, C-II, D-I.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Iron(III) catalyses reaction between iodide and persulphate ions:
A. Fe^3+ oxidises iodide  B. Fe^3+ oxidises persulphate  C. Fe^2+ reduces iodide  D. Fe^2+ reduces persulphate
Choose most appropriate:
(1) B and C only  (2) B only  (3) A only  (4) A and D only
Ans_des: Mechanism: 2Fe^3+ + 2I^- -> 2Fe^2+ + I2 (Fe^3+ oxidises I^-, A is correct). 2Fe^2+ + S2O8^2- -> 2Fe^3+ + 2SO4^2- (Fe^2+ reduces persulphate by accepting electrons, D is correct). Fe^3+ does not directly oxidise persulphate and Fe^2+ does not reduce iodide. Answer: A and D only.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match compounds with colours:
A. Fe4[Fe(CN)6]3.xH2O - B. [Fe(CN)5NOS]^4- - C. [Fe(SCN)]^2+ - D. (NH4)3PO4.12MoO3
I. Violet  II. Blood Red  III. Prussian Blue  IV. Yellow
Ans_des: Fe4[Fe(CN)6]3.xH2O is Prussian Blue (III). [Fe(CN)5NOS]^4- is nitroprusside ion, gives violet colour (I). [Fe(SCN)]^2+ gives blood red colour (II) - used in iron detection test. (NH4)3PO4.12MoO3 is ammonium phosphomolybdate, yellow precipitate (IV). Answer: A-III, B-I, C-II, D-IV.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Number of complexes with even number of electrons in t2g orbitals from: [Fe(H2O)6]^2+, [Co(H2O)6]^2+, [Co(H2O)6]^3+, [Cu(H2O)6]^2+, [Cr(H2O)6]^2+:
(1) 1  (2) 3  (3) 2  (4) 5
Ans_des: [Fe(H2O)6]^2+: Fe^2+ is d^6, high spin (weak field H2O): t2g^4 -> 4 electrons in t2g (even). [Co(H2O)6]^2+: Co^2+ is d^7, high spin: t2g^5 -> 5 electrons (odd). [Co(H2O)6]^3+: Co^3+ is d^6, high spin: t2g^6 -> 6 electrons (even). [Cu(H2O)6]^2+: Cu^2+ is d^9, high spin: t2g^6 -> 6 electrons (even). [Cr(H2O)6]^2+: Cr^2+ is d^4, high spin: t2g^3 -> 3 electrons (odd). Even t2g electrons: Fe^2+(4), Co^3+(6), Cu^2+(6) = 3 complexes.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify product P in reaction: cyclopentane-COOH with (i) Br2/Red P (ii) H2O gives P (HVZ reaction):
(1) alpha-bromo cyclopentane carboxylic acid  (2) COBr compound  (3) CHO compound  (4) COOH with Br at different position
Ans_des: HVZ (Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky) reaction: Br2/Red P followed by H2O causes alpha-bromination of carboxylic acid. The bromine substitutes at the alpha-carbon (adjacent to COOH). Product P = alpha-bromocyclopentane carboxylic acid (2-bromocyclopentane-1-carboxylic acid).

subject: Chemistry
question: A hypothetical electromagnetic wave has wavelength 1.5 x 4 pm = 6 pm. The frequency of the wave is x x 10^19 Hz. x = ______.
Ans_des: Wavelength lambda = 6 x 10^-12 m (from figure: half wavelength shown as 1.5 pm x 4). Frequency v = c/lambda = 3 x 10^8 / (6 x 10^-12) = 5 x 10^19 Hz. x = 5.

subject: Chemistry
question: 1 mol ideal gas in cylinder at 18 deg C. Piston moved from position A (V1 = 10 L) to B (V2 = 90 L) isothermally and reversibly. Work done x = ______ L atm. (R = 0.08206 L atm mol^-1 K^-1)
Ans_des: Work done by system in reversible isothermal expansion: w = -nRT*ln(V2/V1) = -1 x 0.08206 x (18+273.15) x ln(90/10) = -0.08206 x 291.15 x ln(9) = -0.08206 x 291.15 x 2.1972 = -55.0 L atm. Work done on system = 55 L atm (magnitude).

subject: Chemistry
question: Number of amine compounds from the given list (aniline, phenylhydrazine, H2N-CO-NH2, N-methylaniline, 4-methoxyaniline, propylamine, diphenylamine, H2N-CO-NH2, morpholine, piperidine, cyclopentylamine) giving solids soluble in NaOH upon reaction with Hinsberg's reagent is ______.
Ans_des: Hinsberg's reagent (benzenesulfonyl chloride) reacts with primary amines to give sulfonamides that are soluble in NaOH (ionic solid formed). Secondary amines give insoluble sulfonamides. Tertiary amines do not react. Primary amines in the list that give NaOH-soluble solids: aniline, 4-methoxyaniline, diphenylamine-NH2 (if primary), propylamine, cyclopentylamine = 5 primary amines.

subject: Chemistry
question: The number of optical isomers in a compound with Br and CH3 substituents on a bicyclic ring system with 5 chiral centres is ______.
Ans_des: The compound has 5 chiral (asymmetric) carbon atoms as marked in the structure. Number of optical isomers = 2^n = 2^5 = 32 (assuming no meso forms with this structure).

subject: Chemistry
question: Spin-only magnetic moment of MO4^2- where M is the metal with least metallic radius among Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Zn is ______ BM.
Ans_des: Among Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Zn, the metal with least metallic radius is Cr (due to poor shielding by d-electrons and maximum effective nuclear charge around the middle of the series). In CrO4^2-, Cr is in +6 oxidation state: [Ar]4s^0 3d^0 = d^0 configuration. No unpaired electrons. Spin-only magnetic moment = sqrt(n(n+2)) = sqrt(0) = 0 BM.

subject: Chemistry
question: Number of molecules from CO2, NO2, H2SO4, BF3, CH4, SiF4, ClO2, PCl5, BeF2, C2H6, CHCl3, CBr4 which are exceptions to octet rule is ______.
Ans_des: Exceptions to octet rule: NO2 (odd electron, 17 electrons around N), H2SO4 (S has expanded octet, 12 electrons), BF3 (B has incomplete octet, 6 electrons), ClO2 (odd electron), PCl5 (P has expanded octet, 10 electrons), BeF2 (Be has incomplete octet, 4 electrons). Total = 6 exceptions. CO2, CH4, SiF4, C2H6, CHCl3, CBr4 all satisfy octet rule.

subject: Chemistry
question: If 279 g of aniline is reacted with one equivalent of benzenediazonium chloride, the maximum amount of aniline yellow formed is ______ g. (consider complete conversion)
Ans_des: Aniline (MW=93) + PhN2+Cl- -> aniline yellow (azo dye). Moles of aniline = 279/93 = 3 mol. Since 1:1 ratio, 3 mol product formed. MW of aniline yellow (Ph-N=N-Ph-NH2) = 197 g/mol. Amount = 3 x 197 = 591 g.

subject: Chemistry
question: For A + B -> C: time for A to become 1/4th initial is twice time to become 1/2. Concentration of B vs time gives straight line with negative slope and positive intercept. Overall order of reaction is ______.
Ans_des: For A becoming 1/4th initial in time = 2 x (half-life): this is characteristic of 1st order reaction (t_3/4 = 2 x t_1/2). So order with respect to A = 1. For B: concentration vs time is linear with negative slope (straight line with positive intercept) means B decreases linearly = zero order with respect to B. Overall order = 1 + 0 = 1.

subject: Chemistry
question: Major product B of the reaction: ethylbenzene treated with KMnO4/KOH/delta gives A (benzoate), then A treated with HNO3/H2SO4 gives B (nitrobenzoic acid). Number of pi bonds in B is ______.
Ans_des: Ethylbenzene + KMnO4/KOH/delta -> potassium benzoate (A). A + HNO3/H2SO4 -> nitrobenzoic acid (B = 4-nitrobenzoic acid). Pi bonds in B: benzene ring has 3 pi bonds (delocalized), -COOH has 1 pi bond (C=O), -NO2 has 1 pi bond (N=O counted as one effective pi bond in resonance). Total pi bonds = 3 + 1 + 1 = 5.

The value of k in N for which the integral I_n = integral from 0 to 1 of (1-x^k)^n dx satisfies 147*I20 = 148*I21 is:
(1) 10  (2) 8  (3) 14  (4) 7
Ans_des: Using integration by parts: I_n/I_(n-1) = nk/(nk+1). So I_21/I_20 = 21k/(21k+1) = 147/148. Cross multiplying: 21k x 148 = 147 x (21k+1). 3108k = 3087k + 147. 21k = 147. k = 7.

question:
Let P(x,y,z) be in first octant, Q is projection in xy-plane, OP = gamma, angle OQ with x-axis = theta, angle OP with z-axis = phi. Distance of P from x-axis is:
(1) gamma*sqrt(1-sin^2(phi)*cos^2(theta))  (2) gamma*sqrt(1+cos^2(theta)*sin^2(phi))  (3) gamma*sqrt(1-sin^2(theta)*cos^2(phi))  (4) gamma*sqrt(1+cos^2(phi)*sin^2(theta))
Ans_des: x^2+y^2+z^2 = gamma^2. cos(theta) = x/sqrt(x^2+y^2), cos(phi) = z/gamma. So sin^2(phi) = (x^2+y^2)/gamma^2. x = sqrt(x^2+y^2)*cos(theta). Distance from x-axis = sqrt(y^2+z^2) = sqrt(gamma^2 - x^2) = gamma*sqrt(1-x^2/gamma^2). x^2/gamma^2 = (x/(sqrt(x^2+y^2)))^2 * (x^2+y^2)/gamma^2 = cos^2(theta)*sin^2(phi). Distance = gamma*sqrt(1-cos^2(theta)*sin^2(phi)).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Number of critical points of f(x) = (x-2)^(2/3) * (2x+1) is:
(1) 2  (2) 0  (3) 1  (4) 3
Ans_des: f'(x) = (2/3)*(x-2)^(-1/3)*(2x+1) + (x-2)^(2/3)*2 = 2*(3x-1) / (3*(x-2)^(1/3)). Setting numerator = 0: 3x-1 = 0, x = 1/3. Denominator undefined at x = 2 (f' undefined but f is defined, so also a critical point). Critical points: x = 1/3 and x = 2. Total = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f(x) be positive such that area bounded by y=f(x) from 0 to a is e^(-a) + 4a^2 + a - 1. Differential equation whose general solution is y = c1*f(x) + c2 is:
(1) (8e^x-1)*y'' + y' = 0  (2) (8e^x+1)*y'' - y' = 0  (3) (8e^x+1)*y'' + y' = 0  (4) (8e^x-1)*y'' - y' = 0
Ans_des: Area from 0 to a = e^(-a)+4a^2+a-1. Differentiating: f(a) = -e^(-a)+8a+1. So f(x) = -e^(-x)+8x+1. y = C1*f(x)+C2. dy/dx = C1*f'(x) = C1*(e^(-x)+8). d^2y/dx^2 = -C1*e^(-x). So C1 = -e^x*d^2y/dx^2. Substituting: dy/dx = -e^x*d^2y/dx^2*(e^(-x)+8) = -d^2y/dx^2*(1+8e^x). Rearranging: (8e^x+1)*d^2y/dx^2 + dy/dx = 0.

subject: Mathematics
question:
f(x) = 4cos^3(x) + 3*sqrt(3)*cos^2(x) - 10. Number of points of local maxima of f in interval (0, 2*pi) is:
(1) 1  (2) 2  (3) 3  (4) 4
Ans_des: f'(x) = -12cos^2(x)*sin(x) + 3*sqrt(3)*2cos(x)*(-sin(x)) = -6sin(x)*cos(x)*(2cos(x)+sqrt(3)). f'(x) = 0 when: sin(x)=0, or cos(x)=0, or cos(x)=-sqrt(3)/2. Sign analysis shows local maxima at x = 5*pi/6 and x = 7*pi/6 in (0, 2*pi). Total = 2 points of local maxima.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let A = [[2,a,0],[1,3,1],[0,5,b]]. If A^3 = 4A^2 - A - 21I, then 2a + 3b is:
(1) -10  (2) -13  (3) -9  (4) -12
Ans_des: From A^3 - 4A^2 + A + 21I = 0, by Cayley-Hamilton theorem, characteristic polynomial has roots satisfying this. Trace(A) = 2+3+b = 5+b = 4 (coefficient gives b = -1). det(A) = 2(3b-5) - a(b-0) + 0 = 6b-10-ab = -21. With b=-1: -6-10+a = -21, a = -21+16 = -5. So 2a+3b = 2(-5)+3(-1) = -10-3 = -13.

question:
Sum of two positive integers = 24. Probability that product is not less than (3/4) times greatest possible product is m/n, gcd(m,n)=1. n-m equals:
(1) 9  (2) 11  (3) 8  (4) 10
Ans_des: x+y=24. Max product by AM-GM when x=y=12, product=144. (3/4)*144=108. Need xy >= 108. For x+y=24: xy = x(24-x) >= 108, so x^2-24x+108 <= 0, (x-6)(x-18) <= 0, 6 <= x <= 18. Favourable: x in {6,7,...,18}, i.e., 13 values. Total: x from 1 to 23 (23 values). Probability = 13/23. m=13, n=23. n-m=10.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let I(x) = integral of 6/[sin^2(x)*(1-cot(x))^2] dx. If I(0) = 3, then I(pi/12) is:
(1) sqrt(3)  (2) 3*sqrt(3)  (3) 6*sqrt(3)  (4) 2*sqrt(3)
Ans_des: I(x) = integral of 6*cosec^2(x)/(1-cot(x))^2 dx. Let t = 1-cot(x), dt = cosec^2(x)dx. I = integral of 6/t^2 dt = -6/t + C = -6/(1-cot(x)) + C. I(x) = C - 6/(1-cot(x)). At x=0: cot(0)=inf, I(0)=C+0=C=3. I(x) = 3 - 6/(1-cot(x)). At x=pi/12: cot(pi/12) = cot(15 deg) = 2+sqrt(3). I(pi/12) = 3-6/(1-(2+sqrt(3))) = 3-6/(-1-sqrt(3)) = 3+6/(1+sqrt(3)) = 3+6(sqrt(3)-1)/2 = 3+3*sqrt(3)-3 = 3*sqrt(3).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let z be complex with |z+2|=1 and Im((z+1)/(z+2)) = 1/5. Then |Re(conjugate of (z+2))| is:
(1) sqrt(6)/5  (2) (1+sqrt(6))/5  (3) 24/5  (4) 2*sqrt(6)/5
Ans_des: Let z+2 = cos(theta)+i*sin(theta). (z+1)/(z+2) = 1 - 1/(z+2) = 1 - (cos(theta)-i*sin(theta)) = (1-cos(theta))+i*sin(theta). Im = sin(theta) = 1/5. cos(theta) = +-sqrt(1-1/25) = +-sqrt(24/25) = +-2*sqrt(6)/5. Re(conj(z+2)) = Re(cos(theta)-i*sin(theta)) = cos(theta). |Re(conj(z+2))| = |cos(theta)| = 2*sqrt(6)/5.

subject: Mathematics
question:
For f(x) = cos(x) - x + 1, x in R:
S1: f(x)=0 for only one value of x in [0,pi]
S2: f(x) decreasing in [0,pi/2] and increasing in [pi/2,pi]
(1) Both correct  (2) Only S1 correct  (3) Both incorrect  (4) Only S2 correct
Ans_des: f'(x) = -sin(x)-1. Since sin(x) >= 0 in [0,pi], f'(x) = -(sin(x)+1) <= -1 < 0 for all x. So f is strictly decreasing on all of R, including [0,pi]. S2 is INCORRECT (f is not increasing anywhere). f(0) = 1+1 = 2 > 0, f(pi) = -1-pi+1 = -pi < 0. Since f is continuous and strictly decreasing, exactly one zero in [0,pi]. S1 is CORRECT.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Set of all alpha for which vectors a = alpha*t*i + 6j - 3k and b = t*i - 2j - 2*alpha*t*k are inclined at obtuse angle for all t in R is:
(1) [0,1)  (2) (-2,0]  (3) (-4/3, 0]  (4) (-4/3, 1)
Ans_des: a.b = alpha*t^2 - 12 + 6*alpha*t^2 = alpha*t^2*(1+6) - 12... Let me redo: a.b = (alpha*t)*t + 6*(-2) + (-3)*(-2*alpha*t) = alpha*t^2 - 12 + 6*alpha*t = alpha*t^2 + 6*alpha*t - 12. For obtuse angle for all t: a.b < 0 for all t in R. alpha*t^2 + 6*alpha*t - 12 < 0 for all t. Need alpha < 0 and discriminant < 0. 36*alpha^2 + 48*alpha < 0. alpha*(3*alpha+4) < 0. -4/3 < alpha < 0. Also at alpha=0, a.b = -12 < 0 (included). So alpha in (-4/3, 0].

subject: Mathematics
question:
Solution y(x) of (1+y^2)*e^(tan x)*dx + cos^2(x)*(1+e^(2tanx))*dy = 0, y(0)=1. Then y(pi/4) is:
(1) 2/e  (2) 1/e^2  (3) 1/e  (4) 2/e^2
Ans_des: Separating: [sec^2(x)*e^(tanx)/(1+e^(2tanx))]dx + dy/(1+y^2) = 0. Let u = e^(tanx): integral gives arctan(e^(tanx)) + arctan(y) = C. At x=0, y=1: arctan(e^0)+arctan(1) = pi/4+pi/4 = pi/2. C = pi/2. At x=pi: arctan(e^(tan(pi)))+arctan(y) = pi/2. arctan(e^0)+arctan(y) = pi/2. pi/4+arctan(y) = pi/2. arctan(y) = pi/4. y = 1. Hmm - at x=pi/4: arctan(e^(tan(pi/4)))+arctan(y) = pi/2. arctan(e)+arctan(y) = pi/2. arctan(y) = pi/2-arctan(e) = arccot(e). y = cot(arctan(e)) = 1/e.

question: Orthocentre of triangle formed by 2x+3y-1=0, x+2y-1=0, ax+by-1=0 is centroid of another triangle with circumcentre (3,4) and orthocentre (-6,-8). Value of |a-b| is ______.
Ans_des: Centroid lies on Euler line: G divides O to H in ratio 1:2 from O. Centroid = ((3+2*(-6))/3, (4+2*(-8))/3) = ((3-12)/3, (4-16)/3) = (-3, -4). Wait: G = (2*O+H... no, OG:GH = 1:2). G = (3+2*(-6)/3... G = ((3x3+(-6))/3, (3*4+(-8))/3) hmm. G = (O+2H)/3... Actually G = (2*O+H)/3? No: H, G, O are collinear with HG:GO = 2:1. G = (H+2*O)/3... checking: centroid divides segment from H to O in ratio 2:1 from H. G = (H + 2*O)/3. Hmm, actually G = (2*O + H)/3: (2*3+(-6))/3=0, (2*4+(-8))/3=0. So G=(0,0). Orthocentre of first triangle = (0,0). Finding intersection of 2x+3y=1 and x+2y=1: x=-1, y=1. Vertex at (-1,1). Line ax+by=1 passes through (0,0)? a*0+b*0=0 not 1. So (0,0) is orthocentre. Altitude from (-1,1) perpendicular to ax+by=1 passes through (0,0). Foot on ax+by=1: slope of ax+by=1 is -a/b. Altitude through (-1,1) with slope b/a. Passes through (0,0): 0 = (b/a)(-1+... ) line through (0,0) with slope b/a: y=bx/a. This must be altitude from (-1,1). Vector (-1,1) to (0,0) has slope (0-1)/(0-(-1))=-1. So b/a = -1, b=-a. Also altitude from (0,0) perpendicular to ax+by=1 means direction (a,b) and passes through vertex: vertex on ax+by=1 perpendicular to altitude. Since b=-a: ax-ay=1. Vertex of first triangle not on first two lines: intersection of ax-ay=1 with 2x+3y=1. a(x-y)=1 and 2x+3y=1. From perpendicularity condition: slope of ax-ay=1 is 1, altitude from (-1,1): y-1=1*(x+1), y=x+2. Check passes through (0,0): 0=0+2? No. So need another approach. Final answer: a=-8, b=8. |a-b| = 16.

question: Number of 3-digit numbers formed using digits 2,3,4,5,7 (no repetition) which are NOT divisible by 3 is ______.
Ans_des: Total 3-digit numbers without repetition from {2,3,4,5,7} = P(5,3) = 60. A number divisible by 3 iff digit sum divisible by 3. Sum of all 5 digits = 2+3+4+5+7=21. Valid digit-sum-divisible-by-3 triplets: {2,5,7}->14 (no), {3,4,7}->14 (no), {4,5,7}->16 (no)... Let me list: {2,3,4}->9 (yes), {2,4,5}->11(no), {2,5,7}->14(no), {3,5,4}->12(yes? yes), {2,3,7}->12(yes), {3,4,5}->12(yes? yes), {3,5,7}->15(yes), {4,5,7}->16(no), {2,4,7}->13(no), {2,3,5}->10(no). Divisible groups: {2,3,4},{2,5,4}... Let me recount: {2,3,4}sum=9, {2,3,7}sum=12, {3,4,5}sum=12, {3,5,7}sum=15, {4,5,7}sum=16(no), {2,5,7}sum=14(no), wait. Actually from PDF: groups not divisible = {4,5,7},{3,4,7},{2,5,7},{2,4,7},{2,4,5},{2,3,5} = 6 groups x 3! = 36.

subject: Mathematics
question: Positive integers written in triangular array (row 1: 1; row 2: 2,3; row 3: 4,5,6; ...). The row in which number 5310 appears is ______.
Ans_des: Last number in row n = n(n+1)/2. First number in row n = n(n-1)/2 + 1. For row n: T_n = n(n+1)/2. Need n such that T_(n-1) < 5310 <= T_n. n(n+1)/2 >= 5310, n^2+n >= 10620. n approx 102.5. n=103: T_103 = 103*104/2 = 5356. T_102 = 102*103/2 = 5253. Since 5253 < 5310 <= 5356, number 5310 is in row 103.

subject: Mathematics
question: Range of f(theta) = (sin^4(theta)+3cos^2(theta))/(sin^4(theta)+cos^2(theta)) is [alpha, beta]. Sum of infinite GP with first term 64 and common ratio alpha/beta equals ______.
Ans_des: f(theta) = (sin^4(theta)+3cos^2(theta))/(sin^4(theta)+cos^2(theta)). Let t = cos^2(theta) in [0,1], s = sin^4(theta) = (1-t)^2. f = ((1-t)^2+3t)/((1-t)^2+t). Min when t=1: f=(0+3)/(0+1)=3. Wait at t=0: f=(1+0)/(1+0)=1. At t=1: f=3/1=3. So range is [1,3], alpha=1, beta=3. Sum = a/(1-r) = 64/(1-1/3) = 64/(2/3) = 96.

subject: Mathematics
question: Area of region enclosed by y = min{sin x, cos x} and x-axis between x=-pi to x=pi is A. Then A^2 = ______.
Ans_des: y = min{sin x, cos x}. From x=-pi to x=-3pi/4: min is sin x (negative). From x=-3pi/4 to x=pi/4: min is sin x. From x=pi/4 to x=pi: min is cos x. Area above x-axis: from 0 to pi/4, sinx is positive and is the min: area = integral_0^{pi/4} sinx dx = 1-1/sqrt(2). Negative areas below x-axis: from -pi to -3pi/4 (sinx-cosx region) and pi/4 to pi (cosx region). Total area = 4. A=4. A^2=16.

question: The value of the limit as x->0 of 2*(1 - cos(x)*cos(2x)^(1/2)*cos(3x)^(1/3)*...*cos(10x)^(1/10)) / x^2 is ______.
Ans_des: Using expansion cos^(1/k)(kx) approx 1-(kx)^2/(2k!) approx 1-kx^2/2 for small x. Product approx product_{k=1}^{10}(1-kx^2/2) approx 1 - (sum_{k=1}^{10} k)*x^2/2. Sum = 1+2+...+10 = 55. Product approx 1 - 55x^2/2. So 1-product approx 55x^2/2. Limit = 2*(55x^2/2)/x^2 = 55.

subject: Physics
question:
A block of 5 kg is released from the top of an inclined plane (length 10 m, angle 30 degrees, mu=0) and hits a spring (k=100 N/m) at the bottom. The horizontal surface has mu=0.5 (length 2 m). The maximum compression in the spring is:
(1) sqrt(6) m  (2) 2 m  (3) 1 m  (4) sqrt(5) m
Ans_des: Using work-energy theorem: Wg + Wfriction + Wspring = Delta(KE). With m=5 kg, g=10 m/s^2, height = 10*sin30 = 5 m: 5*10*5 - 0.5*5*10*x - (1/2)*100*x^2 = 0. 250 = 25x + 50x^2. 2x^2 + x - 10 = 0. Solving: x = 2 m.

subject: Physics
question:
In a hypothetical fission reaction: 92X^236 -> 56Y^141 + 36Z^92 + 3R. The identity of emitted particles (R) is:
(1) Proton  (2) Electron  (3) Neutron  (4) Gamma-radiations
Ans_des: Balancing atomic number (Z): LHS = 92, RHS = 56 + 36 = 92. Balanced. Balancing mass number (A): LHS = 236, RHS = 141 + 92 = 233. Deficit = 3. So 3 particles with A=1, Z=0 are emitted. These are neutrons.

subject: Physics
question:
If epsilon-0 is the permittivity of free space and E is the electric field, then epsilon-0 * E^2 has the dimensions:
(1) [M^0 L^-2 T A]  (2) [M L^-1 T^-2]  (3) [M^-1 L^-3 T^4 A^2]  (4) [M L^2 T^-2]
Ans_des: E = KQ/R^2 = Q/(4*pi*epsilon-0*R^2), so epsilon-0 = Q/(4*pi*R^2*E). epsilon-0*E^2 = Q*E/R^2. Dimensions: [Q][E]/[R^2] = [Q][W]/([R^2][Q][R]) = [W]/[R^3] = [M L^2 T^-2]/[L^3] = [M L^-1 T^-2].

subject: Physics
question:
Three lenses with f1=10 cm, f2=-10 cm, f3=30 cm are separated as follows: 30 cm between L1 and L2, 5 cm between L2 and L3, 10 cm gap after L3. Object is placed 30 cm to the left of L1. The position of the final image is:
(1) 30 cm right of third lens  (2) 15 cm left of second lens  (3) 30 cm left of third lens  (4) 15 cm right of second lens
Ans_des:
Lens 1: f=10, u=-30, v = uf/(u+f) = (-30*10)/(-30+10) = 15 cm. Image is 15 cm right of L1 = 20 cm to right of L2 (but L2 is 30 cm from L1), so object for L2 is at u = 15-30 = -15... Actually: image from L1 at v=15 (right of L1). L2 is 30 cm right of L1, so u for L2 = 15-30 = -15+... Correcting: v1=15 means image 15 cm right of L1. L2 is 35 cm (30+5) from L1, so u2 = 15-35 = ...
Simplified per solution: For L2, u=10 (5 cm after image from L1 at 15, plus 5 to L2 = 10), v = 10*(-10)/(10-10) = infinity. For L3: u = -inf, v = f3 = 30 cm. Final image is 30 cm to the right of L3.

question:
A thin circular disc of mass M and radius R rotating with angular velocity omega. Another disc of same dimensions but mass M/2 is placed coaxially on it. The new angular velocity is:
(1) (4/5)*omega  (2) (5/4)*omega  (3) (2/3)*omega  (4) (3/2)*omega
Ans_des: By conservation of angular momentum: I1*omega = I2*omega2. I1 = MR^2/2. I2 = MR^2/2 + (M/2)*R^2/2 = MR^2/2 + MR^2/4 = 3MR^2/4. So (MR^2/2)*omega = (3MR^2/4)*omega2. omega2 = (2/3)*omega.

subject: Physics
question:
A cube of ice floats partly in water and partly in kerosene oil. Given: specific gravity of kerosene = 0.8, specific gravity of ice = 0.9. The ratio of volume of ice immersed in water to that in kerosene oil is:
(1) 8:9  (2) 5:4  (3) 9:10  (4) 1:1
Ans_des: Let v1 = volume in water, v2 = volume in oil. Buoyancy balance: v1*rho_w*g + v2*rho_oil*g = (v1+v2)*rho_ice*g. Dividing by rho_w: v1 + 0.8*v2 = 0.9*(v1+v2). v1 + 0.8*v2 = 0.9*v1 + 0.9*v2. 0.1*v1 = 0.1*v2. v1:v2 = 1:1.

subject: Physics
question:
Given: Statement I - The mean free path of gas molecules is inversely proportional to the square of molecular diameter. Statement II - Average kinetic energy of gas molecules is directly proportional to absolute temperature.
(1) I false, II true  (2) I true, II false  (3) Both false  (4) Both true
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Ans_des: Mean free path: lambda = RT/(sqrt(2)*pi*d^2*N_A*P), which is inversely proportional to d^2. True. Average KE = (f/2)*nRT, which is directly proportional to T. Both statements are true.

subject: Physics
question:
Two satellites A and B orbit a planet in circular orbits of radii 4R and R respectively. If speed of A is 3v, the speed of B is:
(1) (4/3)v  (2) 3v  (3) 6v  (4) 12v
Ans_des: Orbital speed v = sqrt(GM/r). So vA/vB = sqrt(R_B/R_A) = sqrt(R/4R) = 1/2. Therefore vB = 2*vA = 2*3v = 6v.

subject: Physics
question:
A long straight wire of radius a carries steady current I uniformly distributed across cross-section. The ratio of magnetic field at distance a/2 and 2a from axis is:
(1) 1:4  (2) 4:1  (3) 1:1  (4) 3:4
Ans_des: For r = a/2 (inside): B1*(2*pi*a/2) = mu_0*I*(a/2)^2/(a^2). B1 = mu_0*I/(4*pi*a). For r = 2a (outside): B2*(2*pi*2a) = mu_0*I. B2 = mu_0*I/(4*pi*a). Ratio B1:B2 = 1:1.

subject: Physics
question:
The angle of projection for a projectile to have the same horizontal range and maximum height is:
(1) tan^-1(2)  (2) tan^-1(4)  (3) tan^-1(1/4)  (4) tan^-1(1/2)
Ans_des: Range R = u^2*sin(2*theta)/g. Max height H = u^2*sin^2(theta)/(2g). Setting R = H: u^2*sin(2*theta)/g = u^2*sin^2(theta)/(2g). 2*sin(theta)*cos(theta)*2 = sin^2(theta). 4*cos(theta) = sin(theta). tan(theta) = 4. theta = tan^-1(4).

subject: Physics
question:
Water boils in an electric kettle in 20 minutes. Using the same supply, the length of the heating element should be changed to boil water in 15 minutes:
(1) increased, 3/4  (2) increased, 4/3  (3) decreased, 3/4  (4) decreased, 4/3
Ans: decreased to 3/4 of its initial length
Ans_des: Power P = V^2/R = V^2*A/(rho*l), so P is inversely proportional to l. P1/P2 = t2/t1 = 15/20 = l2/l1. l2 = (3/4)*l1. The length should be decreased to 3/4 of initial.

subject: Physics
question:
A capacitor with air dielectric has plate area 12 cm^2 and plate separation 0.6 cm. A dielectric slab of same area and 0.6 cm thickness is inserted. One plate is moved by 0.2 cm to keep capacitance the same. The dielectric constant is: (epsilon_0 = 8.834e-12 F/m)
(1) 1.50  (2) 1.33  (3) 0.66  (4) 1
Ans_des: Initial: C = A*epsilon_0/d where d = 0.6 cm. After insertion (plate moved 0.2 cm, so effective air gap = 0.2 cm, slab thickness = 0.6 cm): C = A*epsilon_0/(0.2 + 0.6/k). Setting equal: 0.6 = 0.2 + 0.6/k. 0.4 = 0.6/k. k = 0.6/0.4 = 3/2 = 1.5.

subject: Physics
question:
A given object takes n times as long to slide down a 45-degree rough inclined plane as it takes to slide down an identical smooth 45-degree inclined plane. The coefficient of kinetic friction is:
(1) 1 - 1/n^2  (2) 1 - n^2  (3) sqrt(1 - 1/n^2)  (4) sqrt(1 - n^2)
Ans_des: Smooth plane: a1 = g*sin(45). Distance l = (1/2)*a1*t1^2, so t1 = sqrt(2l/(g*sin45)). Rough plane: a2 = g*sin45 - mu*g*cos45. t2 = n*t1. Since l = (1/2)*a2*t2^2: t2/t1 = sqrt(a1/a2) = n. So a2 = a1/n^2. g*sin45 - mu*g*cos45 = g*sin45/n^2. At 45 deg, sin=cos: 1 - mu = 1/n^2. mu = 1 - 1/n^2.

subject: Physics
question:
A coil of negligible resistance is connected in series with 90 ohm resistor across 120 V, 60 Hz supply. A voltmeter reads 36 V across resistance. Inductance of the coil is:
(1) 0.76 H  (2) 2.86 H  (3) 0.286 H  (4) 0.91 H

subject: Physics
question:
A screw gauge has 100 circular divisions, pitch 1 mm. Zero of circular scale is 5 divisions below reference line (zero error). For a wire, 4 linear scale divisions are visible and 60 circular scale divisions coincide with reference. Diameter of wire:
(1) 4.65 mm  (2) 4.55 mm  (3) 4.60 mm  (4) 3.35 mm
Ans_des: Least count = 1/100 mm = 0.01 mm. Zero error = +0.05 mm (5 divisions below = positive error). Reading = 4*1 mm + 60*0.01 mm - 0.05 mm = 4 + 0.60 - 0.05 = 4.55 mm.

subject: Physics
question:
A proton and an electron have the same de Broglie wavelength. If Kp and Ke are their kinetic energies respectively, then:
(1) Kp > Ke  (2) Kp = Ke  (3) Kp = Ke^2  (4) Kp < Ke
Ans_des: Same de Broglie wavelength lambda = h/p means same momentum p. Kinetic energy KE = p^2/(2m). Since m_proton >> m_electron, KE_proton < KE_electron. Therefore Kp < Ke.

subject: Physics
question:
Least count of a vernier caliper is 1/(20N) cm. Value of one main scale division is 1 mm. The number of main scale divisions that coincide with N vernier scale divisions is:
(1) (2N-1)/(20N)  (2) (2N-1)/2  (3) (2N-1)  (4) (2N-1)/(2N)
Ans_des: Let x divisions of main scale coincide with N vernier divisions. 1 VSD = x*1mm/N. Least count = 1 MSD - 1 VSD = 1mm - x*mm/N. Setting equal to 1/(20N) cm = 1/(2N) mm: 1/(2N) = 1 - x/N. x/N = 1 - 1/(2N) = (2N-1)/(2N). x = (2N-1)/2.

subject: Physics
question:
If Mo is the mass of isotope 5^12 B, Mp and Mn are masses of proton and neutron respectively, the nuclear binding energy of the isotope is:
(1) (5Mp + 7Mn - Mo)C^2  (2) (Mo - 5Mp)C^2  (3) (Mo - 12Mn)C^2  (4) (Mo - 5Mp - 7Mn)C^2
Ans_des: Boron-12 has Z=5 protons and N=12-5=7 neutrons. Mass defect = Delta(m) = 5*Mp + 7*Mn - Mo. Binding energy = Delta(m)*C^2 = (5Mp + 7Mn - Mo)*C^2.

subject: Physics
question:
A diatomic gas (gamma = 1.4) does 100 J of work in an isobaric expansion. The heat given to the gas is:
(1) 350 J  (2) 490 J  (3) 150 J  (4) 250 J
Ans_des: For isobaric process: W = nR*Delta(T) = 100 J. Q = Delta(U) + W = (f/2)*nR*Delta(T) + nR*Delta(T) = (f/2 + 1)*nR*Delta(T). For diatomic gas f=5: Q = (5/2 + 1)*100 = (7/2)*100 = 350 J.

subject: Physics
question: The coercivity of a magnet is 5x10^3 A/m. A solenoid of length 30 cm with 150 turns is used to demagnetize it. The current required is ______ A.
Ans_des: Coercivity Hc = n*i, where n = N/L = 150/0.30 = 500 turns/m. 5*10^3 = 500*i. i = 10 A.

subject: Physics
question: Small water droplets of radius 0.01 mm fall with terminal velocity 10 cm/s. 8 such droplets coalesce into a larger drop. The new terminal velocity is ______ cm/s.
Ans_des: Terminal velocity Vt is proportional to R^2. 8 small drops coalesce: 8*(4/3)*pi*r^3 = (4/3)*pi*R^3. R = 2r. So new Vt = (R/r)^2 * old Vt = 4 * 10 = 40 cm/s.

subject: Physics
question: If the net electric field at point P along Y-axis is zero (charges +q2 at distance sqrt(20) and -q3 at distance sqrt(25) from P, horizontal distances 2 cm and 3 cm), then the ratio |q2/q3| = 8/(5*sqrt(x)), where x = ______.
Ans_des: Balancing horizontal (x) components of E-field at P: Kq2*cos(beta)/20 = Kq3*cos(theta)/25. cos(beta) = 4/sqrt(20), cos(theta) = 4/sqrt(25). q2*4/(20*sqrt(20)) = q3*4/(25*sqrt(25)). q2/q3 = (20*sqrt(20))/(25*sqrt(25)) = (20/25)*sqrt(20/25) = (4/5)*sqrt(4/5) = 8/(5*sqrt(5)). So x = 5.

subject: Physics
question: A heater (1000 W at 100 V) is connected with a 10 ohm resistor and resistance R to 100 V mains. For heater to operate at 62.5 W, value of R is ______ ohm.

subject: Physics
question: An alternating emf E = 110*sqrt(2)*sin(100t) V is applied to a capacitor of 2 microF. The rms value of current in the circuit is ______ mA.

subject: Physics
question: Two slits are 1 mm apart, screen 1 m away, wavelength 500 nm. Width of each slit to obtain 10 double-slit maxima within central maximum of single-slit pattern is ______ x 10^-4 m.
Ans_des: 10 double-slit fringes fit in central single-slit maximum means: 10*(lambda*D/d) = 2*(lambda*D/a). So a = 2d/10 = d/5 = 10^-3/5 = 2*10^-4 m.

subject: Physics
question: An object of mass 0.2 kg executes SHM along x-axis with frequency 25/pi Hz. At x = 0.04 m, KE = 0.5 J and PE = 0.4 J. Amplitude of oscillation is ______ cm.

subject: Physics
question: A potential divider circuit has 20 V DC source, LED (glow voltage 1.8 V), zener diode (breakdown 3.2 V). Resistive wire length PR = 20 cm. Minimum length PQ to just glow the LED is ______ cm.
Ans_des: Zener maintains 3.2 V. Voltage needed to light LED = 1.8 V. Total voltage across wire = 20 V. But zener clamps lower portion at 3.2 V. Minimum voltage needed at PQ = zener + LED = 3.2 + 1.8 = 5 V. PQ/PR = 5/20 = 1/4. PQ = (1/4)*20 = 5 cm.

subject: Physics
question: A body of mass M is thrown horizontally with velocity v from height H and lands 100 m from base. A body of mass 2M is thrown horizontally with v/2 from height 4H. It lands ______ m from base.
Ans_des: Horizontal distance x = v*sqrt(2H/g). For case 1: 100 = v*sqrt(2H/g). For case 2: x = (v/2)*sqrt(2*4H/g) = (v/2)*2*sqrt(2H/g) = v*sqrt(2H/g) = 100 m.

subject: Physics
question: A circular table rotates with angular velocity omega rad/s. A steel ball placed at 1 m in a radial groove. Table radius is 3 m. Radial velocity of ball when it leaves table is x*sqrt(2)*omega m/s. Value of x is ______.
Ans_des: In rotating frame, centrifugal acceleration a_c = omega^2*r. v*dv/dr = omega^2*r. Integrating from r=1 to r=3: v^2/2 = omega^2*(r^2/2) evaluated from 1 to 3 = omega^2*(9-1)/2 = 4*omega^2. v = 2*sqrt(2)*omega. So x = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In qualitative test for phosphorous, the compound is heated with oxidising agent, treated with HNO3 and ammonium molybdate. The yellow coloured precipitate obtained is:
(1) Na3PO4.12MoO3  (2) (NH4)3PO4.12(NH4)2MoO4  (3) (NH4)3PO4.12MoO3  (4) MoPO4.21NH4NO3
Ans_des: PO4^3- (or HPO4^2-) + (NH4)2MoO4 (ammonium molybdate) in acidic (H+) conditions gives (NH4)3PO4.12MoO3 (canary yellow precipitate), also called ammonium phosphomolybdate.

subject: Chemistry
question:
For reaction A ->k1-> B ->k2-> C. If rate of formation of B is set to zero, concentration of B is:
(1) K1*K2*[A]  (2) (K1-K2)*[A]  (3) (K1+K2)*[A]  (4) (K1/K2)*[A]
Ans_des: Rate of formation of B: d[B]/dt = K1[A] - K2[B] = 0. K1[A] = K2[B]. [B] = (K1/K2)*[A].

subject: Chemistry
question:
When psi_A and psi_B are wave functions of atomic orbitals, the antibonding molecular orbital sigma* is represented by:
(1) psi_A - 2*psi_B  (2) psi_A - psi_B  (3) psi_A + 2*psi_B  (4) psi_A + psi_B
Ans_des: Antibonding molecular orbitals are formed by destructive interference of wave functions. sigma* = psi_A - psi_B (linear combination with subtraction). Bonding orbitals use addition (sigma = psi_A + psi_B).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which compound readily reacts with dilute NaOH?
(1) C6H5CH2OH  (2) C2H5OH  (3) (CH3)3COH  (4) C6H5OH
Ans_des: Phenol (C6H5OH) is a stronger acid than water and reacts with dilute NaOH to form sodium phenoxide: C6H5OH + NaOH -> C6H5O^-Na^+ + H2O. Alcohols (pKa ~16-18) are much weaker acids and do not react with dilute NaOH.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The shape of a carbocation is:
(1) Trigonal planar  (2) Diagonal pyramidal  (3) Tetrahedral  (4) Diagonal
Ans_des: A carbocation has 3 bonds and one empty p-orbital. The central carbon is sp2 hybridized, giving trigonal planar geometry with bond angles of 120 degrees.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: SN2 reactions are stereospecific, resulting in only one stereoisomer. Statement II: SN1 reactions generally give racemic mixtures.
(1) I true, II false  (2) I false, II true  (3) Both true  (4) Both false
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Ans_des: SN2 proceeds with inversion of configuration (Walden inversion), giving one specific stereoisomer - stereospecific. SN1 forms a planar carbocation intermediate, allowing attack from both sides, giving racemic mixture. Both statements are true.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match reactions with products:
(A) Aniline + NaNO2/HCl, H2O warm  (B) Phenol + Na2Cr2O7/H2SO4  (C) Phenol + CHCl3/NaOH, H+  (D) Phenol + NaOH, CO2, H+
Choose correct matching:
(1) A-III,B-II,C-I,D-IV  (2) A-IV,B-II,C-III,D-I  (3) A-I,B-IV,C-II,D-III  (4) A-II,B-IV,C-I,D-III
Ans_des: (A) Aniline + NaNO2/HCl then H2O/warm (Gattermann reaction variant) gives phenol (II). (B) Phenol + Na2Cr2O7/H2SO4 (oxidation) gives benzoquinone (IV). (C) Phenol + CHCl3/NaOH then H+ (Reimer-Tiemann) gives 2-hydroxybenzaldehyde (I). (D) Phenol + NaOH, CO2, H+ (Kolbe-Schmitt) gives 2-hydroxybenzoic acid (salicylic acid) (III).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match tests with identifications:
(A) Bayer's test  (B) Ceric ammonium nitrate test  (C) Phthalein dye test  (D) Schiff's test
With: (I) Phenol  (II) Aldehyde  (III) Alcoholic-OH  (IV) Unsaturation
Correct match:
(1) A-III,B-I,C-IV,D-II  (2) A-II,B-III,C-IV,D-I  (3) A-IV,B-I,C-II,D-III  (4) A-IV,B-III,C-I,D-II
Ans_des: (A) Bayer's test (KMnO4) detects unsaturation (IV). (B) Ceric ammonium nitrate test detects alcoholic -OH group (III). (C) Phthalein dye test detects phenol (I). (D) Schiff's test detects aldehydes (II).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify incorrect statements about group 15 elements:
(A) Dinitrogen acts like inert gas at room temperature  (B) Common oxidation states are -3, +3, +5  (C) Nitrogen forms pi-pi multiple bonds  (D) Stability of +5 state increases down group  (E) Nitrogen shows maximum covalency of 6
Choose correct:
(1) A,B,D only  (2) A,C,E only  (3) B,D,E only  (4) D and E only
Ans_des: (D) is incorrect: due to inert pair effect, +5 state becomes less stable going down the group. (E) is incorrect: Nitrogen is in 2nd period, cannot expand octet, so maximum covalency is 4 (not 6). Statements A, B, C are correct.

subject: Chemistry
question:
IUPAC name of CH3-CH(CH3)-CH2-CH2-CH(CH3)-CH(CH3)-CH2-CH3:
(1) 2-Ethyl-3,6-dimethylheptane  (2) 2-Ethyl-2,6-diethylheptane  (3) 2,5,6-Trimethyloctane  (4) 3,4,7-Trimethyloctane
Ans_des: Number the longest chain (8 carbons = octane) to give lowest locants to substituents. Methyl groups at positions 2, 5, and 6. Name: 2,5,6-Trimethyloctane.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The equilibrium Cr2O7^2- <-> 2CrO4^2- is shifted to the right in:
(1) Acidic medium  (2) Basic medium  (3) Weakly acidic medium  (4) Neutral medium
Ans_des: Cr2O7^2- (dichromate, orange) + OH^- -> 2CrO4^2- (chromate, yellow). In basic (alkaline) medium, the equilibrium shifts right toward CrO4^2-. In acidic medium, it shifts back to Cr2O7^2-.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: A buffer solution is a mixture of salt and acid or base in any quantities. Statement II: Blood is a naturally occurring buffer whose pH is maintained by H2CO3/HCO3^- concentrations.
(1) I false, II true  (2) Both true  (3) Both false  (4) I true, II false
Ans: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans_des: Statement I is false: a buffer is a mixture of a weak acid/base with its conjugate base/acid in specific ratio (not any quantities). Statement II is true: blood is buffered by H2CO3/HCO3^- system maintaining pH ~7.4.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Correct decreasing order of acidic strength of CH3CH2COOH, CH3COOH, CH3CH2CH2COOH, HCOOH:
(1) HCOOH > CH3COOH > CH3CH2COOH > CH3CH2CH2COOH
(2) HCOOH > CH3CH2CH2COOH > CH3CH2COOH > CH3COOH
(3) CH3CH2CH2COOH > CH3CH2COOH > CH3COOH > HCOOH
(4) CH3COOH > CH3CH2COOH > CH3CH2CH2COOH > HCOOH
Ans_des: Increasing alkyl chain length increases electron-donating inductive effect (+I), destabilizing the carboxylate anion, reducing acidity. HCOOH (formic acid) has no alkyl group and is most acidic. Acidity decreases as chain length increases.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: All of C6H5NH2, (C6H5)2NH, (C6H5)3N react with p-toluenesulfonyl chloride. Statement II: Their products are soluble in NaOH.
(1) Both false  (2) I true, II false  (3) I false, II true  (4) Both true
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Ans_des: Hinsberg test: only primary amines (1 degree, like C6H5NH2) react with p-toluenesulfonyl chloride to give sulfonamides soluble in NaOH. Secondary amine (C6H5)2NH gives insoluble product. Tertiary amine (C6H5)3N does not react. Statement I is false. Statement II is also false.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The EMF of cell Tl|Tl+(0.001M)||Cu2+(0.01M)|Cu is 0.83 V at 298 K. It could be increased by:
(1) Increasing [Tl+]  (2) Increasing both [Tl+] and [Cu2+]  (3) Decreasing both  (4) Increasing [Cu2+]
Ans_des: Cell reaction: 2Tl + Cu2+ -> 2Tl+ + Cu. E_cell = E_cell^0 - (0.0591/2)*log([Tl+]^2/[Cu2+]). To increase E_cell, the log term must decrease, meaning [Tl+]^2/[Cu2+] must decrease. This can be achieved by increasing [Cu2+].

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify correct statements about p-block elements:
(A) Non-metals have higher electronegativity than metals  (B) Non-metals have lower ionisation enthalpy than metals  (C) Compounds between highly reactive nonmetals and metals are ionic  (D) Non-metal oxides are basic  (E) Metal oxides are acidic or neutral
Choose correct:
(1) D and E only  (2) A and C only  (3) B and E only  (4) B and D only
Ans_des: (A) Correct: non-metals have higher electronegativity. (B) Incorrect: non-metals generally have higher ionisation enthalpy than metals. (C) Correct: large electronegativity difference leads to ionic bond. (D) Incorrect: non-metal oxides are acidic, not basic. (E) Incorrect: metal oxides are generally basic, not acidic/neutral.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: Kjeldahl method is applicable to estimate nitrogen in pyridine. Statement II: Nitrogen in pyridine can easily be converted to ammonium sulphate in Kjeldahl method.
(1) Both false  (2) I false, II true  (3) Both true  (4) I true, II false
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Ans_des: Kjeldahl method cannot be applied to nitrogen present in aromatic rings (like pyridine) or nitro/azo compounds because this nitrogen cannot be easily converted to ammonium sulphate. Both statements are false.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The reaction (1/2)H2(g) + AgCl(s) -> H+(aq) + Cl-(aq) + Ag(s) occurs in which galvanic cell:
(1) Pt|H2(g)|HCl(soln)|AgCl(s)|Ag  (2) Pt|H2(g)|HCl(soln)|AgNO3(aq)|Ag
(3) Pt|H2(g)|KCl(soln)|AgCl(s)|Ag  (4) Ag|AgCl(s)|KCl(soln)|AgNO3(aq)|Ag
Ans_des: Anode: (1/2)H2 -> H+ + e^-. Cathode: AgCl(s) + e^- -> Ag(s) + Cl^-. Overall: (1/2)H2 + AgCl -> H+ + Cl^- + Ag. Cl^- comes from KCl salt bridge. This matches cell notation Pt|H2(g)|KCl(soln)|AgCl(s)|Ag.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: Fusion of MnO2 with KOH and oxidising agent gives dark green K2MnO4. Statement II: Manganate ion on electrolytic oxidation in alkaline medium gives permanganate ion.
(1) Both true  (2) Both false  (3) I true, II false  (4) I false, II true
Ans: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Ans_des: MnO2 + 4KOH + O2 -> 2K2MnO4 + 2H2O (dark green). Statement I is true. Electrolytic oxidation at anode: MnO4^2- -> MnO4^- + e^- (manganate to permanganate). Statement II is true.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match complex ions with spin-only magnetic moments:
(A) [Cr(NH3)6]3+  (B) [NiCl4]2-  (C) [CoF6]3-  (D) [Ni(CN)4]2-
With: (I) 4.90 BM  (II) 3.87 BM  (III) 0.0 BM  (IV) 2.83 BM
Choose correct:
(1) A-I,B-IV,C-II,D-III  (2) A-IV,B-III,C-I,D-II  (3) A-II,B-IV,C-I,D-III  (4) A-II,B-III,C-I,D-IV
Ans_des: (A) Cr3+ = 3d3, n=3 unpaired, mu = sqrt(3*5) = 3.87 BM (II). (B) [NiCl4]2- (tetrahedral, weak field): Ni2+ = 3d8, n=2, mu = sqrt(2*4) = 2.83 BM (IV). (C) [CoF6]3- (weak field): Co3+ = 3d6 high spin, n=4, mu = sqrt(4*6) = 4.90 BM (I). (D) [Ni(CN)4]2- (strong field, square planar): n=0, mu = 0 BM (III).

subject: Chemistry
question: Delta_vap H (theta) for water is +40.49 kJ/mol at 1 bar and 100 degrees C. Change in internal energy for vaporisation under same conditions is ______ kJ/mol. (R = 8.3 J/(K*mol))
Ans_des: Delta_H = Delta_U + Delta_n_g * R * T. Delta_n_g = 1 (liquid to gas). T = 373.15 K. Delta_H = 40.49 kJ/mol (note: solution uses 40.79, slight discrepancy in problem). Delta_U = 40.79 - (1 * 8.3 * 373.15)/1000 = 40.79 - 3.097 = 37.69 approximately 38 kJ/mol.

subject: Chemistry
question: Number of molecules having bond order 2 from: C2, O2, Be2, Li2, Ne2, N2, He2 is ______.
Ans_des: C2: BO = (8-4)/2 = 2. O2: BO = (10-6)/2 = 2. Be2: BO = 0. Li2: BO = 1. Ne2: BO = 0. N2: BO = 3. He2: BO = 0. Only C2 and O2 have bond order 2. Count = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question: Total number of optically active compounds from the given set (includes CH3-CH2-CH(Cl)-CH3 and others) is ______.
Ans_des: CH3-CH2-CH(Cl)-CH3 (2-chlorobutane) has one chiral centre (C2 bonded to CH3, C2H5, Cl, H - all different), so it is optically active. Other compounds listed lack chiral centres or have planes of symmetry. Total = 1.

subject: Chemistry
question: The total number of carbon atoms present in tyrosine (an amino acid) is ______.
Ans_des: Tyrosine structure: HO-C6H4-CH2-CH(NH2)-COOH (4-hydroxyphenylalanine). Carbon count: benzene ring (6C) + CH2 (1C) + CH (1C) + COOH (1C) = 9 carbon atoms.

subject: Chemistry
question: Two moles of benzaldehyde and one mole of acetone under alkaline NaOH conditions with heating gives product x. The number of pi bonds in x is ______.
Ans_des: Aldol condensation: 2 PhCHO + CH3COCH3 -> Ph-CH=CH-CO-CH=CH-Ph (dibenzylideneacetone, or dibenzalacetone). Product has 2 phenyl rings (3 pi bonds each = 6) + 2 C=C bonds + 1 C=O = 6 + 2 + 1 = 9 pi bonds.

subject: Chemistry
question: Total number of aromatic compounds among given structures (including cyclopentadienyl cation, pyridinium, cycloheptatrienyl anion, cyclobutadiene dianion, pyrrole, cyclohexadiene) is ______.
Ans_des: Aromatic compounds require 4n+2 pi electrons (Huckel rule), planarity, and cyclic conjugation. Among the given set, pyrrole has 6 pi electrons (4*1+2) and is aromatic. Only 1 compound is aromatic per the answer.

subject: Chemistry
question: Molality of an aqueous solution of urea is 4.44 m. Mole fraction of urea in solution is x * 10^-3. Value of x is ______.
Ans_des: 4.44 m means 4.44 mol urea in 1000 g water. Moles of water = 1000/18 = 55.56 mol. Mole fraction of urea = 4.44/(4.44 + 55.56) = 4.44/60 = 0.0740 = 74 * 10^-3. x = 74.

subject: Chemistry
question: Total number of unpaired electrons in [Co(NH3)6]3+ and [NiCl4]2- combined is ______.
Ans_des: [Co(NH3)6]3+: Co3+ = 3d6, strong field (NH3), t2g^6 eg^0 = 0 unpaired electrons. [NiCl4]2-: Ni2+ = 3d8, weak field (Cl^-), tetrahedral = 2 unpaired electrons. Total = 0 + 2 = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question: A solution is prepared by adding 1 mole ethyl alcohol in 9 moles water. Mass percent of solute in solution is ______ (molar mass: ethanol = 46, water = 18).
Ans_des: Mass of ethanol = 1*46 = 46 g. Mass of water = 9*18 = 162 g. Total mass = 208 g. Mass percent = (46/208)*100 = 22.11 approximately 22%.

question:
Let a = i + 2j + 3k, b = 2i + 3j - 5k, c = 3i - j + lambda*k. Let r be a unit vector along b+c. If r.a = 3, then 3*lambda equals:
(1) 27  (2) 25  (3) 28  (4) 21
Ans_des: b+c = 5i+2j+(lambda-5)k. r = k*(b+c), |r|=1. r.a = k*(b.a + c.a) = k*(2+6-15+3-2+3*lambda) = k*(-6+3*lambda) = 3. |r| = k*sqrt(25+4+(lambda-5)^2) = 1. From equation: k = 3/(3*lambda-6) = 1/(lambda-2). 1/(lambda-2) * sqrt(29+(lambda-5)^2) = 1. (lambda-5)^2 + 29 = (lambda-2)^2. lambda^2-10*lambda+25+29 = lambda^2-4*lambda+4. -10*lambda+54 = -4*lambda+4. 6*lambda = 50. 3*lambda = 25.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If alpha != a, beta != b, gamma != c and |alpha b c; a beta c; a b gamma| = 0, then a/(alpha-a) + b/(beta-b) + c/(gamma-c) equals:
(1) 2  (2) 3  (3) 0  (4) 1
Ans_des: Apply row operations R1->R1-R2, R2->R2-R3. Expanding the resulting determinant: gamma*(alpha-a)*(beta-b) - b*(alpha-a)*(c-gamma) + a*(b-beta)*(c-gamma) = 0. Divide throughout: gamma/(gamma-c) + b/(beta-b) + a/(alpha-a) = 0.

question:
The number of ways five alphabets can be chosen from MATHEMATICS (not necessarily distinct) equals:
(1) 175  (2) 181  (3) 177  (4) 179
Ans_des: Distinct letters in MATHEMATICS: A(x2), M(x2), T(x2), H, I, C, S, E - 8 distinct, 3 pairs. (1) All 5 distinct: 8C5 = 56. (2) One pair + 3 different: 3C1 * 7C3 = 3*35 = 105. (3) Two pairs + 1 different: 3C2 * 6C1 = 3*6 = 18. Total = 56+105+18 = 179.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Sum of all possible values of theta in [-pi, 2*pi] for which (1 + i*cos(theta))/(1 - 2*i*cos(theta)) is purely imaginary equals:
(1) 2*pi  (2) 3*pi  (3) 5*pi  (4) 4*pi
Ans_des: For purely imaginary, real part = 0. Multiply numerator and denominator by conjugate: Real part = (1-2*cos^2(theta))/(1+4*cos^2(theta)) = 0 leads to 1-2*cos^2(theta) = 0... Actually: setting Z = -Z-bar gives 2-4*cos^2(theta) = 0. cos^2(theta) = 1/2. theta = +-pi/4, +-3pi/4, 5pi/4, 7pi/4 in [-pi,2pi]. Sum = -pi/4 - 3pi/4 + pi/4 + 3pi/4 + 5pi/4 + 7pi/4 = 3*pi.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the system x + 4y - z = lambda, 7x + 9y + mu*z = -3, 5x + y + 2z = -1 has infinitely many solutions, then (2*mu + 3*lambda) equals:
(1) 2  (2) -3  (3) 3  (4) -2
Ans_des: For infinite solutions, main determinant Delta = 0. Expanding: (18-mu) - 4(14-5*mu) - (7-45) = 0. 18-mu - 56+20*mu + 38 = 0. 19*mu = 0. mu = 0. Then using Delta_x = 0 with mu=0: lambda(18) + 4(6-0) - (-3+9) = 0. Actually: lambda(18) - 4(-6) - 6 = 0... Working: 18*lambda + 24 - 6 = 0. 18*lambda = -18. lambda = -1. 2*mu + 3*lambda = 0 + 3*(-1) = -3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If shortest distance between lines (x-lambda)/2 = (y-4)/3 = (z-3)/4 and (x-2)/4 = (y-4)/6 = (z-7)/8 is 13/sqrt(29), then a value of lambda is:
(1) -13/25  (2) 13/25  (3) 1  (4) -1
Ans_des: Direction vector b = 2i+3j+4k. a1 = lambda*i+4j+3k, a2 = 2i+4j+7k. a2-a1 = (2-lambda)i+4k. SD = |b x (a2-a1)|/|b| = 13/sqrt(29). b x (a2-a1): i(3*4-4*0)-j(2*4-4*(2-lambda))+k(2*0-3*(2-lambda)) = 12i-j(8-4(2-lambda))+k(-3(2-lambda)) = 12i+4*lambda*j+(3*lambda-6)k. |12i-4*lambda*j+(3*lambda-6)k|^2 = 144+16*lambda^2+(3*lambda-6)^2 = 169. 16*lambda^2+(3*lambda-6)^2 = 25. 25*lambda^2-36*lambda+36 = 25. Solving gives lambda = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the value of (3*cos36 + 5*sin18)/(5*cos36 - 3*sin18) = (a*sqrt(5) - b)/c where gcd(a,c)=1, then a+b+c equals:
(1) 50  (2) 40  (3) 52  (4) 54
Ans_des: Using cos36 = (sqrt(5)+1)/4 and sin18 = (sqrt(5)-1)/4: Numerator = 3*(sqrt(5)+1)/4 + 5*(sqrt(5)-1)/4 = (3*sqrt(5)+3+5*sqrt(5)-5)/4 = (8*sqrt(5)-2)/4. Denominator = 5*(sqrt(5)+1)/4 - 3*(sqrt(5)-1)/4 = (5*sqrt(5)+5-3*sqrt(5)+3)/4 = (2*sqrt(5)+8)/4. Ratio = (8*sqrt(5)-2)/(2*sqrt(5)+8) = (4*sqrt(5)-1)/(sqrt(5)+4). Rationalize: multiply by (sqrt(5)-4)/(sqrt(5)-4): = (4*5-16*sqrt(5)-sqrt(5)+4)/(5-16) = (24-17*sqrt(5))/(-11) = (17*sqrt(5)-24)/11. a=17, b=24... wait per solution b=27 seems error; a=17, b=27, c=11: checking... Actually per sol: a=17, b=27, c=11, a+b+c=52.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let y(x) satisfy differential equation sec(y)*dy/dx + 2x*sin(y) = x^3*cos(y), y(1) = 0. Then y(sqrt(3)) equals:
(1) pi/3  (2) pi/6  (3) pi/4  (4) pi/12
Ans_des: Multiply by sec(y): sec^2(y)*dy/dx + 2x*tan(y) = x^3. Let t = tan(y): dt/dx + 2x*t = x^3. IF = e^(x^2). Solution: t*e^(x^2) = integral(x^3*e^(x^2))dx. Let z=x^2: = (1/2)*integral(z*e^z)dz = (e^z/2)*(z-1)+C. tan(y)*e^(x^2) = (e^(x^2)/2)*(x^2-1)+C. y(1)=0: 0 = 0+C, C=0. 2*tan(y) = x^2-1. At x=sqrt(3): 2*tan(y) = 2. tan(y) = 1. y = pi/4.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If f(x) = 2x^3 - 9a*x^2 + 12a^2*x + 1, a>0 has local maximum at x=alpha and local minimum at x=alpha^2, then alpha and alpha^2 are roots of:
(1) x^2-6x+8=0  (2) 8x^2+6x-8=0  (3) 8x^2-6x+1=0  (4) x^2+6x+8=0
Ans_des: f'(x) = 6x^2 - 18ax + 12a^2 = 0. Roots are alpha and alpha^2. Sum: alpha+alpha^2 = 3a. Product: alpha*alpha^2 = 2a^2. From (alpha+alpha^2)^3 = 27a^3: (3a)^3 = 27a^3 checks. Expanding (alpha+alpha^2)^3 and using product: 2a^2+4a^4+18a^3 = 27a^3. 4a^2-9a+2=0. (4a-1)(a-2)=0. a=2 (a=1/4 gives alpha=1/2 not valid for distinct roots). Sum = 6, product = 8. Equation: x^2-6x+8=0.

question:
Let integral from alpha to ln4 of dx/sqrt(e^x-1) = pi/6. Then e^alpha and e^(-alpha) are roots of equation:
(1) 2x^2-5x+2=0  (2) x^2-2x-8=0  (3) 2x^2-5x-2=0  (4) x^2+2x-8=0
Ans_des: Let e^x-1 = t^2, e^x dx = 2t dt. Integral = 2*arctan(sqrt(e^x-1)) evaluated from alpha to ln4. = 2[arctan(sqrt(3)) - arctan(sqrt(e^alpha-1))] = pi/6. arctan(sqrt(3)) = pi/3. pi/3 - arctan(sqrt(e^alpha-1)) = pi/12. arctan(sqrt(e^alpha-1)) = pi/4. sqrt(e^alpha-1) = 1. e^alpha = 2. e^(-alpha) = 1/2. Roots are 2 and 1/2. Equation: (x-2)(x-1/2) = 0, 2x^2-5x+2=0.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f(x) = {-a if -a<=x<=0; x+a if 0<x<=a} where a>0 and g(x) = (f(|x|) - |f(x)|)/2. Then g:[-a,a]->[-a,a] is:
(1) Neither one-one nor onto  (2) Both one-one and onto  (3) One-one  (4) Onto
Ans_des: Analyzing f(x): for -a<=x<=0, f(x)=-a (constant). For 0<x<=a, f(x)=x+a (from a to 2a). g(x)=(f(|x|)-|f(x)|)/2. After computing g(x) for all intervals, g(x) is constant (=0) for -a<=x<=0 and equals some function for 0<x<=a. The function is not injective (many-to-one for x<=0) and not surjective (misses some values in [-a,a]). Hence neither one-one nor onto.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let A={2,3,6,8,9,11}, B={1,4,5,10,15}. R on AxB defined by (a,b)R(c,d) iff 3ad-7bc is even. R is:
(1) Reflexive but not symmetric  (2) Transitive but not symmetric  (3) Reflexive and symmetric but not transitive  (4) Equivalence relation
Ans: Reflexive and symmetric but not transitive
Ans_des: Reflexive: (a,b)R(a,b): 3ab-7ba = -4ab, always even. True. Symmetric: If 3ad-7bc is even, then 3cb-7da = 3bc-7ad = -(7ad-3bc). Even means 7ad-3bc is even iff 3ad-7bc is even (same parity structure). True. Transitive: Counterexample: (3,4)R(6,4) -> 3*12-7*24=36-168=-132 even. (6,4)R(3,1) -> 3*6-7*12=18-84=-66 even. (3,4)R(3,1) -> 3*3-7*12=9-84=-75 odd. Not transitive.

subject: Mathematics
question:
For a, b > 0, f(x) = {tan((a+1)x)+b*tan(x)}/x for x<0; = 3 for x=0; = {sqrt(ax+b^2*x^2)-sqrt(ax)}/(b*sqrt(a)*x*sqrt(x)) for x>0. Continuous at x=0. Then b/a equals:
(1) 5  (2) 4  (3) 8  (4) 6
Ans_des: Left limit as x->0^-: (tan((a+1)x)+b*tan(x))/x -> (a+1)+b = a+b+1. Set = 3: a+b = 2... not matching. Right limit: [sqrt(ax+b^2*x^2)-sqrt(ax)]/(b*sqrt(a)*x*sqrt(x)) = sqrt(ax)*(sqrt(1+b^2*x/a)-1)/(b*sqrt(a)*x*sqrt(x)). As x->0: approx (sqrt(ax))*(b^2*x/(2a)) / (b*sqrt(a)*x*sqrt(x)) = b/(2a). Set = 3... actually from solution: b/(2a)=3, so b/a=6.

question: If alpha = lim as x->0+ of (e^(sqrt(tanx)) - e^(sqrt(x)))/(sqrt(tanx)-sqrt(x)) and beta = lim as x->0 of (1+sin(x/2))^(cot(x)) are roots of a*x^2 + b*x - sqrt(e) = 0, then 12*ln(a+b) equals ______.
Ans_des: alpha: let t = sqrt(tanx)-sqrt(x) -> 0. Limit = e^(sqrt(x)) * (e^t - 1)/t -> e^0 = 1. alpha = 1. beta: lim (1+sin(x/2))^(cot x) = e^(lim cot(x)*sin(x/2)) = e^(lim cos(x)*sin(x/2)/sin(x)) = e^(1/2). beta = sqrt(e). Roots 1 and sqrt(e) of ax^2+bx-sqrt(e)=0. By Vieta: product of roots = -sqrt(e)/a = sqrt(e). a=-1. Sum = -b/a = 1+sqrt(e). b = -(1+sqrt(e))*(-1) = 1+sqrt(e). a+b = -1+1+sqrt(e) = sqrt(e). 12*ln(sqrt(e)) = 12*(1/2) = 6.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let S be focus of hyperbola x^2/3 - y^2/5 = 1 on positive x-axis. Let C be circle with centre at A(sqrt(6),sqrt(5)) passing through S. O is origin, SAB is diameter of C. Square of area of triangle OSB equals ______.
Ans_des: For hyperbola x^2/3 - y^2/5 = 1: a^2=3, b^2=5. c^2 = a^2+b^2 = 8. S = (sqrt(8),0) = (2*sqrt(2),0). Center A = (sqrt(6),sqrt(5)). SAB is diameter so B is reflection of S through A. B = (2*sqrt(6)-2*sqrt(2), 2*sqrt(5)). OS = sqrt(8) = 2*sqrt(2). Height from B to OS (x-axis) = 2*sqrt(5). Area = (1/2)*2*sqrt(2)*2*sqrt(5) = 2*sqrt(10). (Area)^2 = 4*10 = 40.

question: An arithmetic progression is written in triangular array (row 1: 2; row 2: 5,8; row 3: 11,14,17; row 4: 20,23,26,29; ...). Sum of all terms of 10th row is ______.
Ans_des: First term of row n: using pattern 2,5,11,20... General term = (3n^2-3n+4)/2. T10 = (300-30+4)/2 = 137. Row 10 has 10 terms with common difference 3. Sum = (10/2)*(2*137 + 9*3) = 5*(274+27) = 5*301 = 1505.

subject: Mathematics
question: Number of distinct real roots of |x+1||x+3| - 4|x+2| + 5 = 0 is ______.
Ans_des: Case x<=-3: (x+1)(x+3)+4(x+2)+5=0. x^2+8x+16=0. (x+4)^2=0. x=-4 (valid). Case -3<=x<=-2: -(x^2+4x+3)+4(x+2)+5=0. -x^2+10=0. x=+-sqrt(10). x=-sqrt(10) is approx -3.16 (valid). Case -2<=x<=-1: -(x^2+4x+3)-4(x+2)+5=0. -x^2-8x-6=0. x=-4+-sqrt(10). x=-4+sqrt(10) approx -0.84 (not in range). Case x>=-1: x^2+4x+3-4(x+2)+5=0. x^2=0. x=0 (valid). Total: x=-4 and x=-sqrt(10) and x=0 but... per solution answer=2. Checking: x=0 and x=-4 are valid; x=-sqrt(10) needs checking interval. 2 distinct roots.

question: Let alpha*|x| = |y|*e^(x*y-beta), alpha,beta in N be solution of x*dy - y*dx + x*y*(x*dy+y*dx) = 0, y(1)=2. Then alpha+beta equals ______.
Ans_des: Rearrange: dy/y - dx/x + (x*dy+y*dx) = 0. Integrate: ln|y| - ln|x| + xy = C. ln|y/x| + xy = C. y(1)=2: ln2 + 2 = C. ln|y| - ln|x| + xy = 2 + ln2. ln|x| = ln|y/2| - 2 + xy. |x| = |y/2|*e^(xy-2). 2|x| = |y|*e^(xy-2). alpha=2, beta=2. alpha+beta=4.

subject: Physics
question:
A proton, an electron and an alpha particle have the same energies. Their de-Broglie wavelengths will be compared as:
(1) lambda_e > lambda_alpha > lambda_p
(2) lambda_alpha < lambda_p < lambda_e
(3) lambda_p < lambda_e < lambda_alpha
(4) lambda_p > lambda_e > lambda_alpha
Ans_des: de Broglie wavelength: lambda = h/p = h/sqrt(2mK). For same kinetic energy K, lambda is proportional to 1/sqrt(m). Since m_alpha > m_p > m_e, we get lambda_alpha < lambda_p < lambda_e.

subject: Physics
question:
A particle moving in a straight line covers half the distance with speed 6 m/s. The other half is covered in two equal time intervals with speeds 9 m/s and 15 m/s respectively. The average speed of the particle during the motion is:
(1) 8.8 m/s  (2) 10 m/s  (3) 9.2 m/s  (4) 8 m/s
Ans_des: Let each half distance = S. Second half covered in two equal times t each: S = 9t + 15t = 24t. First half: S = 6*t1, so 24t = 6*t1, giving t1 = 4t. Average speed = total distance/total time = 2S/(t1 + 2t) = 48t/(4t + 2t) = 48t/6t = 8 m/s.

subject: Physics
question:
A plane EM wave propagates along x-direction with wavelength 4 mm. Electric field is in y-direction with maximum magnitude 60 V/m. The equation for magnetic field is:
(1) Bz = 60*sin[(pi/2)*(x - 3*10^8*t)] k-hat T
(2) Bz = 2*10^-7*sin[(pi/2)*10^3*(x - 3*10^8*t)] k-hat T
(3) Bx = 60*sin[(pi/2)*(x - 3*10^8*t)] i-hat T
(4) Bz = 2*10^-7*sin[(pi/2)*(x - 3*10^8*t)] k-hat T
Ans_des: B0 = E0/c = 60/(3*10^8) = 2*10^-7 T. Frequency f = c/lambda = (3*10^8)/(4*10^-3) = (3/4)*10^11 Hz. omega = 2*pi*f = (pi/2)*10^3 * c. Wave vector k = omega/c = (pi/2)*10^3. EM wave propagates in x, E in y, so B must be in z-direction. Bz = 2*10^-7 * sin[(pi/2)*10^3*(x - 3*10^8*t)] k-hat T.

subject: Physics
question:
Given: Statement I - When object is at centre of curvature of a concave lens, image forms at centre of curvature on other side. Statement II - Concave lens always forms a virtual and erect image.
(1) I false, II true  (2) Both false  (3) I true, II false  (4) Both true
Ans: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans_des: For a concave lens with real objects, image is always virtual, erect, and diminished - never at centre of curvature on other side. Statement I is false. Statement II is correct for real objects (for a virtual object at u = f/2, a real image can form, but for all real objects, image is always virtual and erect). Answer: Statement I is false, Statement II is true (considering only real objects as intended).
