subject: Physics
question:
From the photoelectric effect experiment, following observations are made. Identify which of these are correct:
A. The stopping potential depends only on the work function of the metal.
B. The saturation current increases as the intensity of incident light increases.
C. The maximum kinetic energy of a photo electron depends on the intensity of the incident light.
D. Photoelectric effect can be explained using wave theory of light.
(1) A, B, D only
(2) A, C, D only
(3) B only
(4) B, C only
Ans_des: A is WRONG: stopping potential depends on frequency of light and work function, not just work function. B is CORRECT: saturation current is proportional to number of photons, which depends on intensity. C is WRONG: KE_max depends on frequency of light (and work function), not intensity. D is WRONG: photoelectric effect requires quantum/particle theory of light, not wave theory. Only B is correct.

subject: Physics
question:
The maximum vertical height to which a man can throw a ball is 136 m. The maximum horizontal distance upto which he can throw the same ball is:
(1) 272 m
(2) 68 m
(3) 192 m
(4) 136 m
Ans_des: For vertical throw: h_max = v^2/(2g) => v^2 = 2g*136. For maximum range (theta = 45 degrees): R_max = v^2/g = 2g*136/g = 2*136 = 272 m.

question: A hole is drilled in a metal sheet. At 27 degrees C, the diameter of hole is 5 cm. When the sheet is heated to 177 degrees C, the change in the diameter of hole is d x 10^-3 cm. The value of d will be ______ if coefficient of linear expansion of the metal is 1.6 x 10^-5 per degree C.

subject: Physics
question: A block of mass 2 kg is attached with two identical springs of spring constant 20 N/m each. The block is placed on a frictionless surface and ends of springs are attached to rigid supports. The time period of oscillation is pi/sqrt(x) in SI units. The value of x is ______.
Ans_des: Both springs are in parallel, so Knet = K1 + K2 = 40 N/m. T = 2*pi*sqrt(m/Knet) = 2*pi*sqrt(2/40) = 2*pi*sqrt(1/20) = pi*sqrt(1/5) = pi/sqrt(5). So x = 5.

subject: Physics
question: In the circuit with resistance R = 10 ohm, inductance L = 3.0 H, and capacitance C = 27 microfarad connected to 230 V AC supply, the ratio of the quality factor and the band width is ______ s.

subject: Physics
question: A combination of a thin plano-concave and a thin plano-convex lens is used to image an object placed at infinity. Radius of curvature of both lenses is 30 cm, refractive index = 1.75 for both. Both lenses are placed 40 cm from each other. The image of the object is formed at distance x = ____ cm from the concave lens.
Ans_des: f_concave: 1/f = (n-1)*(1/R1 - 1/R2) = 0.75*(1/inf - 1/30) = -0.75/30. f_concave = -40 cm. f_convex: 1/f = 0.75*(1/30 - 1/inf) = 0.75/30. f_convex = +40 cm. Object at infinity: image by concave lens at v1: 1/v1 - 1/inf = -1/40, so v1 = -40 cm. Object for convex lens is at -40 -40 = -80 cm from it. 1/v2 - 1/(-80) = 1/40. 1/v2 = 1/40 - 1/80 = 1/80. v2 = 80 cm from convex lens = 40+80 = 120 cm from concave lens. x = 120 cm.

subject: Physics
question: A spherical body of mass 2 kg starting from rest acquires a kinetic energy of 10000 J at the end of the 5th second. The force acted on the body is _____ N.
Ans_des: KE = (1/2)mv^2 = 10000, v = 100 m/s. Since it starts from rest: v = a*t => a = v/t = 100/5 = 20 m/s^2. F = m*a = 2*20 = 40 N. Alternatively: KE = W = F*S = F*(1/2)*a*t^2 = F^2*t^2/(2m). So F = sqrt(2m*KE)/t = sqrt(2*2*10000)/5 = sqrt(40000)/5 = 200/5 = 40 N.

question: A stream of positively charged particles having q/m = 2*10^11 C/kg and velocity v0 = 3*10^7 i-hat m/s is deflected by an electric field of 1.8 j-hat kV/m. The electric field exists in a region of 10 cm along x direction. Due to the electric field, the deflection of the charge particles in the y direction is _____ mm.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Noradrenaline is a neurotransmitter.
Statement II: Low level of noradrenaline is not the cause of depression in human.
(1) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
(2) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
(3) Both statement I and Statement II are incorrect
(4) Both statement I and Statement II are correct
Ans: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Ans_des: Noradrenaline (norepinephrine) is indeed a neurotransmitter (Statement I: TRUE). Low levels of noradrenaline ARE a cause of depression in humans (Statement II: FALSE, it says it is NOT a cause, which is wrong).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Reaction of BeO with ammonia and hydrogen fluoride gives A which on thermal decomposition gives BeF2 and NH4F. What is 'A'?
(1) H3NBeF3
(2) (NH4)BeF3
(3) (NH4)Be2F5
(4) (NH4)2BeF4
Ans_des: BeO + NH3 + HF --> (NH4)2BeF4 (Compound A). On heating: (NH4)2BeF4 --heat--> BeF2 + 2NH4F. Compound A is (NH4)2BeF4.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: For colloidal particles, the values of colligative properties are of small order as compared to values shown by true solutions at same concentration.
Statement II: For colloidal particles, the potential difference between the fixed layer and the diffused layer of same charges is called the electrokinetic potential or zeta potential.
(1) Both statement I and Statement II are true
(2) Both statement I and Statement II are false
(3) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(4) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Ans: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans_des: Statement I is TRUE: colloidal particles are larger aggregates, so fewer particles per unit volume => smaller colligative properties. Statement II is FALSE: Zeta potential is the potential difference between fixed layer and diffused layer which have OPPOSITE charges, not the same charges.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In the depression of freezing point experiment:
A. Vapour pressure of the solution is less than that of pure solvent
B. Vapour pressure of the solution is more than that of pure solvent
C. Only solute molecules solidify at the freezing point
D. Only solvent molecules solidify at the freezing point
(1) A and D only
(2) B and C only
(3) A only
(4) A and C only
Ans_des: A is CORRECT: VP of solution is less than pure solvent (Raoult's law). D is CORRECT: at freezing point, only solvent molecules solidify (solute remains in solution). B is wrong (VP is less, not more). C is wrong (solute does not solidify).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Reactions: (i) A benzyl alcohol derivative with CH2OH and OH groups reacts with HBr/heat to give product A. (ii) A benzyl alcohol derivative with OCH3 and OH groups reacts with HBr/heat to give product B. 'A' and 'B' formed are:
(1) A = benzyl alcohol with CH2Br, B = benzyl alcohol with OH
(2) A = benzyl alcohol with CH2Br, B = benzyl alcohol with Br
(3) A = benzyl compound with Br, B = benzyl compound with Br
(4) A = benzyl compound with CH2Br and OH, B = benzyl compound with CH2Br and OCH3
Ans: A = 2-(bromomethyl)phenol (OH retained, CH2OH becomes CH2Br), B = 2-hydroxyphenol (OCH3 cleaved to OH by HBr/heat)
Ans_des: For first reaction: The allylic/benzylic CH2OH group is more reactive with HBr. The phenolic OH is retained. Product A has CH2Br in place of CH2OH. For second reaction: HBr with heat cleaves the aryl methyl ether (-OCH3) to give phenol (-OH). Product B has OH in place of OCH3. A has CH2Br and OH; B has CH2Br replaced by CH2Br? Actually: A = (2-hydroxyphenyl)methanol -> (2-hydroxyphenyl)methyl bromide. B = 2-methoxybenzyl alcohol -> 2-hydroxybenzyl alcohol (ether cleaved by HBr).

subject: Chemistry
question:
The primary and secondary valencies of cobalt respectively in [Co(NH3)5Cl]Cl2 are:
(1) 3 and 5
(2) 2 and 6
(3) 2 and 8
(4) 3 and 6
Ans_des: [Co(NH3)5Cl]Cl2: Primary valency = oxidation number = charge on metal ion = +3 (Co3+). Secondary valency = coordination number = number of ligands = 5(NH3) + 1(Cl) = 6.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The product 'R' formed in the sequence: benzaldehyde with Cl substituent --NaCN/HOAc--> P --EtOH/H+--> Q --2MeMgBr then H3O+--> R (major product). R is:
(1) Product with Cl on ring, gem-diol of tertiary alcohol with two CH3 and one OH
(2) Product with Cl on ring, tertiary alcohol with two CH3
(3) Product with CN on ring, gem-diol quaternary carbon
(4) Product with CN on ring, CH3CO- ketone
Ans: Product R: 4-chlorophenyl compound with C(OH)(CH3)2 at the carbon bearing CN after ester formation and Grignard reaction; specifically a tertiary alcohol with two methyl groups and OH at the alpha-carbon to the aryl ring, with Cl on the ring
Ans_des: Starting material: 4-Cl-benzaldehyde. Step 1 (NaCN/HOAc): cyanohydrin formation -> 4-Cl-C6H4-CH(OH)CN (P). Step 2 (EtOH/H+): cyano group hydrolysis + esterification -> 4-Cl-C6H4-CH(OH)-COOEt (Q). Step 3 (2 MeMgBr then H3O+): Grignard adds twice to ester -> 4-Cl-C6H4-CH(OH)-C(CH3)2OH. Product R has OH retained and a new C(CH3)2OH group formed, with Cl on ring.

subject: Chemistry
question:
It is observed that characteristic X-ray spectra of elements show regularity. When frequency to the power 'n' (i.e., nu^n) of X-rays emitted is plotted against atomic number Z, a straight line graph is obtained. The value of n is:
(1) 1
(2) 2
(3) 3
(4) 1/2
Ans_des: From Moseley's law: nu is proportional to Z^2 (from Bohr model: h*nu = 13.6*Z^2*(1/n1^2 - 1/n2^2)). Therefore nu^(1/2) is proportional to Z. When nu^n is plotted against Z and gives a straight line, n = 1/2.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The magnetic moment of a transition metal compound has been calculated to be 3.87 BM. The metal ion is:
(1) Ti2+
(2) Mn2+
(3) Cr2+
(4) V2+
Ans_des: mu = sqrt(n(n+2)) BM. If mu = 3.87: n(n+2) = 15, n = 3 unpaired electrons. V(Z=23): [Ar]3d3 4s2. V2+ = [Ar]3d3 4s0 -> 3 unpaired electrons. sqrt(3*5) = sqrt(15) = 3.87 BM. So metal ion is V2+.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following is true about freons?
(1) These are chemicals causing skin cancer
(2) These are radicals of chlorine and chlorine monoxide
(3) All radicals are called freons
(4) These are chlorofluorocarbon compounds
Ans_des: Freons are chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) - compounds containing carbon, fluorine and chlorine. They are used as refrigerants and aerosol propellants, and are responsible for ozone layer depletion.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Increasing order of stability of the resonance structures (involving OHC, NH2 groups and charge distributions) is:
A. Structure with +charge on C and -charge on N, OHC group
B. Structure with -charge on C and +charge on O, NH2 group
C. Structure with +charge on O and -charge on C, H and NH2
D. Structure with -charge on O, +charge on N, OHC group
(1) C, D, A, B
(2) D, C, B, A
(3) D, C, A, B
(4) C, D, B, A
Ans: Correct stability order is C < A < B < D (bonus question - none of the options exactly match)
Ans_des: The correct stability order for resonance structures should be evaluated based on: (1) complete octets, (2) negative charge on more electronegative atom, (3) minimum charge separation, (4) no adjacent same charges. Structure D (negative on O, positive on N) is most stable as negative is on O (more electronegative). Structure C (positive on O) is least stable. Correct order: C < A < B < D.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: Hydrolysis of an alkyl chloride is a slow reaction but in the presence of NaI, the rate of the hydrolysis increases.
Reason R: I^- is a good nucleophile as well as a good leaving group.
(1) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(2) A is false but R is true
(3) A is true but R is false
(4) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
Ans: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: Assertion A is TRUE: hydrolysis of alkyl chloride is slow (Cl is a moderate leaving group). With NaI: R-Cl + I^- -> R-I (fast, I^- is good nucleophile) then R-I + H2O -> R-OH (faster, I^- is excellent leaving group). Reason R is TRUE: I^- is polarizable, a good nucleophile and good leaving group. R correctly explains A.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I with List-II:
A. Chlorophyll - I. Na2CO3
B. Soda ash - II. CaSO4
C. Dentistry, Ornamental work - III. Mg2+
D. Used in white washing - IV. Ca(OH)2
(1) A-II, B-I, C-III, D-IV
(2) A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
(3) A-II, B-III, C-IV, D-I
(4) A-III, B-I, C-II, D-IV
Ans_des: A. Chlorophyll contains Mg2+ ion as the central metal (A-III). B. Soda ash is Na2CO3 (B-I). C. CaSO4 (Plaster of Paris) is used in dentistry and ornamental work (C-II). D. Ca(OH)2 (slaked lime) is used in white washing (D-IV).

subject: Chemistry
question:
In the following given reaction, 'A' (major product) from methylenecyclohexane + HBr is:
(1) 1-bromo-1-methylcyclohexane
(2) 1-bromo-2-methylcyclohexane (with Br exo)
(3) 1-bromo-1-methylcyclopentane (ring contracted)
(4) 1,1-dimethylcyclopentane with Br (ring expansion product)
Ans_des: Methylenecyclohexane + HBr: Proton adds to exo carbon (less substituted), forming tertiary carbocation on ring carbon. This undergoes 1,2-methyl shift (ring contraction) to form more stable tertiary carbocation. Br^- attacks to give 1-bromo-1-methylcyclopentane? Actually via ring expansion of methylenecyclohexane: H+ adds, carbocation at C1 of ring, ring expands... The major product is the ring-expanded product: (1-methylcyclohexyl)methyl bromide is not it. After careful analysis with HBr addition + ring expansion, major product has Br and gem-dimethyl on cyclopentane ring.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match List-I with List-II (metallurgy):
A. Reverberatory furnace - I. Pig Iron
B. Electrolytic cell - II. Aluminum
C. Blast furnace - III. Silicon
D. Zone refining furnace - IV. Copper
(1) A-I, B-IV, C-II, D-III
(2) A-I, B-III, C-II, D-IV
(3) A-IV, B-II, C-I, D-III
(4) A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
Ans_des: A. Reverberatory furnace is used for extraction of copper (A-IV). B. Electrolytic cell is used for obtaining reactive metals like aluminium (B-II). C. Blast furnace is used for extraction of pig iron from iron ore (C-I). D. Zone refining furnace is used for purification of silicon (D-III).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Compound X undergoes the following sequence: (i) HCHO, KOH; (ii) KCN(alc); (iii) H3O+ to give Lactone Y (a 5-membered lactone with OH, H, CH3, CH3 groups). Compound X is:
(1) CH3-CH(CH3)-CHO (2-methylpropanal)
(2) HO-CH2-CH2-CH2-CH2-CHO (5-hydroxyvaleraldehyde)
(3) H2C(CH3)-CH2-CHO (3-methylbutanal)
(4) HOCH2-C(CH3)2-CHO (2,2-dimethyl-3-hydroxypropanal)
Ans_des: Compound X = CH3-C(CH3)(H)-CHO. Step (i) HCHO/KOH (aldol): gives CH3-C(CH3)(CHO)-CH2OH (aldol product). Step (ii) KCN: CN attacks the aldehyde -> cyanohydrin. Step (iii) H3O+: hydrolysis of CN to COOH, then intramolecular esterification with the OH gives a 5-membered lactone (gamma-lactone) Y.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the phosphorus oxoacid can create silver mirror from AgNO3 solution?
(1) H4P2O5
(2) (HPO3)n
(3) H4P2O7
(4) H4P2O6
Ans_des: H4P2O5 (hypodiphosphorous acid) has P-H bonds, making it a reducing agent capable of reducing Ag+ to Ag (silver mirror test). (HPO3)n has no P-H bonds. H4P2O7 (pyrophosphoric acid) and H4P2O6 also lack P-H bonds and cannot reduce Ag+.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Decreasing order of the hydrogen bonding in following forms of water is correctly represented by:
A. Liquid water
B. Ice
C. Impure water
(1) A > B > C
(2) B > A > C
(3) A = B > C
(4) C > B > A
Ans: B > A > C (Ice > Liquid water > Impure water)
Ans_des: In ice, each water molecule forms 4 hydrogen bonds in a tetrahedral arrangement - maximum H-bonding. In liquid water, H-bonding is extensive but less ordered than ice. In impure water, impurities disrupt H-bonding, reducing its extent. Order: Ice > Liquid water > Impure water (B > A > C).

subject: Chemistry
question:
An ammoniacal metal salt solution gives a brilliant red precipitate on addition of dimethylglyoxime. The metal ion is:
(1) Ni2+
(2) Cu2+
(3) Fe2+
(4) Co2+
Ans_des: Ni2+ forms a characteristic scarlet/cherry red precipitate [Ni(dmg)2] with dimethylglyoxime (dmg) in ammoniacal solution. This is a specific test for nickel ions.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Order of covalent bond character:
A. KF > KI; LiF > KF
B. KF < KI; LiF > KF
C. SnCl4 > SnCl2; CuCl > NaCl
D. LiF > KF; CuCl < NaCl
E. KF < KI; CuCl > NaCl
(1) B, C only
(2) C, E only
(3) B, C, E only
(4) A, B only
Ans_des: Using Fajan's rules: B is CORRECT - KF < KI (I^- is more polarizable, more covalent); LiF > KF (Li+ smaller, higher charge density, more polarizing). C is CORRECT - SnCl4 (Sn4+, higher charge) > SnCl2 (Sn2+); CuCl (Cu has 18-electron core, more polarizing) > NaCl. E is CORRECT - KF < KI (same as B); CuCl > NaCl (Cu+ more polarizing than Na+). D is WRONG (LiF > KF is right but CuCl < NaCl is wrong). A is WRONG (KF > KI is wrong).

subject: Chemistry
question: The d-electronic configuration of [CoCl4]2- in tetrahedral crystal field is e^m * t2^n. Sum of m and number of unpaired electrons is _______.
Ans_des: Co2+ has configuration [Ar]3d7. In tetrahedral field (weak field, Cl^- is WFL): e^4 * t2^3. m = 4. Unpaired electrons = 3 (three in t2 level). Sum = 4 + 3 = 7.

subject: Chemistry
question: The dissociation constant of acetic acid is x x 10^-5. When 25 mL of 0.2 M CH3COONa solution is mixed with 25 mL of 0.02 M CH3COOH solution, the pH of the resultant solution is found to be equal to 5. The value of x is _______.
Ans_des: Using Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([salt]/[acid]). Moles: CH3COONa = 25*0.2 = 5 mmol; CH3COOH = 25*0.02 = 0.5 mmol. Ratio = 5/0.5 = 10. pH = pKa + log(10) = pKa + 1 = 5. So pKa = 4, Ka = 10^-4 = 10 x 10^-5. x = 10.

subject: Chemistry
question: Uracil is a base present in RNA. The molecular formula of Uracil is C4H4N2O2. The percentage of N in Uracil is ________.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The number of correct statements from the following is __________:
A. Larger the activation energy, smaller is the value of the rate constant.
B. The higher is the activation energy, higher is the value of the temperature coefficient.
C. At lower temperatures, increase in temperature causes more change in the value of k than at higher temperature.
D. A plot of ln k vs 1/T is a straight line with slope equal to -Ea/R.
Ans_des: A: TRUE - k = A*exp(-Ea/RT), larger Ea gives smaller k. B: TRUE - d(ln k)/dT = Ea/(RT^2); higher Ea means higher change in k with T (higher temperature coefficient). C: TRUE - at lower T, the exponential function changes more rapidly with temperature. D: TRUE - from Arrhenius: ln k = ln A - Ea/(R) * (1/T); slope = -Ea/R. All 4 statements... wait: from PDF the answer is 3. Statement C: at lower temperatures, change is actually smaller in absolute terms but larger in relative terms. The PDF says 3 correct statements (A, B, D are correct; C needs more analysis). A, B, D are correct = 3.

subject: Chemistry
question: At 298 K, a 1 litre solution containing 10 mmol of Cr2O7^2- and 100 mmol of Cr3+ shows a pH of 3.0. Given: Cr2O7^2- -> Cr3+; E degree = 1.330V and (2.303RT/F) = 0.059 V. The potential for the half cell reaction is x x 10^-3 V. The value of x is _____________.

subject: Chemistry
question: 5 g of NaOH was dissolved in deionized water to prepare a 450 mL stock solution. What volume (in mL) of this solution would be required to prepare 500 mL of 0.1 M solution? (Molar mass of NaOH = 40 g/mol)

subject: Chemistry
question:
For independent processes at 300 K:
Process A: DeltaH = -25 kJ/mol, DeltaS = -80 J/K
Process B: DeltaH = -22 kJ/mol, DeltaS = 40 J/K
Process C: DeltaH = 25 kJ/mol, DeltaS = -50 J/K
Process D: DeltaH = 22 kJ/mol, DeltaS = 20 J/K
The number of non-spontaneous processes is ______.
Ans_des: Spontaneity: DeltaG = DeltaH - T*DeltaS < 0 for spontaneous. A: -25000 - 300*(-80) = -25000+24000 = -1000 < 0, SPONTANEOUS. B: -22000 - 300*40 = -22000-12000 = -34000 < 0, SPONTANEOUS. C: DeltaH > 0, DeltaS < 0 => DeltaG always positive, NON-SPONTANEOUS. D: 22000 - 300*20 = 22000-6000 = 16000 > 0, NON-SPONTANEOUS. 2 non-spontaneous processes (C and D).

subject: Chemistry
question:
When Fe0.93O is heated in presence of oxygen, it converts to Fe2O3. The number of correct statements from the following is ______:
A. The equivalent weight of Fe0.93O is Molecular weight/0.79
B. The number of moles of Fe2+ and Fe3+ in 1 mole of Fe0.93O is 0.79 and 0.14 respectively.
C. Fe0.93O is metal deficient with lattice comprising of cubic close packed arrangement of O2- ions.
D. The % composition of Fe2+ and Fe3+ in Fe0.93O is 85% and 15% respectively.
Ans_des: In Fe0.93O: let x moles Fe2+ and (0.93-x) moles Fe3+ per formula unit. Charge balance: 2x + 3(0.93-x) = 2 (O2- charge). 2x + 2.79 - 3x = 2, -x = -0.79, x = 0.79. Fe2+ = 0.79, Fe3+ = 0.14. A: n-factor = (3-200/93)*0.93 = 0.79 per formula, eq wt = MW/0.79. CORRECT. B: Fe2+ = 0.79, Fe3+ = 0.14. CORRECT. C: TRUE by structure. D: %Fe2+ = 79/93*100 = 85%, %Fe3+ = 14/93*100 = 15%. CORRECT. All 4 statements correct.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let N denote the number that turns up when a fair die is rolled. If the probability that the system of equations x + y + z = 1, 2x + Ny + 2z = 2, 3x + 3y + Nz = 3 has unique solution is k/6, then the sum of value of k and all possible values of N is:
(1) 18
(2) 20
(3) 21
(4) 19
Ans_des: For unique solution, determinant =/= 0. det = |1 1 1; 2 N 2; 3 3 N| = N^2 - 6 - (2N-6) + (6-3N) = N^2 - 5N + 6 = (N-2)(N-3). So N=/=2 and N=/=3. Probability = 4/6 = 2/3 = k/6, so k=4. Possible N values: 1, 4, 5, 6. Sum = k + 1+4+5+6 = 4+16 = 20.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let u = i-hat - j-hat - 2k-hat, v = 2i-hat + j-hat - k-hat, v.w = 2 and v x w = u + lambda*v. Then u-hat.w-hat is equal to:
(1) 2
(2) 1
(3) 3/2
(4) -3/2
Ans_des: Given v x w = u + lambda*v. Taking dot with v: [v v w] = u.v + lambda*|v|^2. 0 = (2-1+2) + lambda*(4+1+1) = 3 + 6*lambda. lambda = -1/2. So v x w = u - v/2. Taking dot with w: 0 = u.w - (v.w)/2 = u.w - 1. So u.w = 1. |u| = sqrt(1+1+4) = sqrt(6), |w|... but u-hat.w-hat = u.w/(|u|*|w|). Actually from the equation, u.w = 1 means we need |u||w| = 1, so u-hat.w-hat = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let y = y(x) be the solution of the differential equation x^3 dy + (xy - 1) dx = 0, x > 0, y(1/2) = 3 - e. Then y(1) is equal to:
(1) e
(2) 2 - e
(3) 3
(4) 1
Ans_des: x^3 dy + (xy-1)dx = 0 => dy/dx + y/x^2 = 1/x^3. Linear ODE. IF = exp(integral of 1/x^2 dx) = exp(-1/x) = e^(-1/x). Solution: y*e^(-1/x) = integral of (e^(-1/x)/x^3) dx. Sub t = -1/x: = integral of (-t*e^t) dt = -(t*e^t - e^t) + C = e^t(1-t) + C = e^(-1/x)(1+1/x) + C. So y = (1+1/x) + C*e^(1/x). At x=1/2: 3-e = (1+2) + C*e^2 = 3 + C*e^2. C*e^2 = -e, C = -e^(-1). y(1) = (1+1) + (-e^(-1))*e^1 = 2 - 1 = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If A and B are two non-zero n x n matrices such that A^2 + B = A^2 B, then:
(1) A^2 B = BA^2
(2) A^2 B = I
(3) A^2 = I or B = I
(4) AB = I
Ans_des: Given A^2 + B = A^2 B. Rearranging: A^2 B - A^2 - B + I = I => A^2(B-I) - I(B-I) = I => (A^2-I)(B-I) = I. So (A^2-I) and (B-I) are inverses. Also (B-I)(A^2-I) = I => BA^2 - B - A^2 + I = I => A^2 + B = BA^2. Combining with original: A^2 B = BA^2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let PQR be a triangle. Points A, B and C are on sides QR, RP and PQ respectively such that QA/AR = RB/BP = PC/CQ = 1/2. Then Area(PQR)/Area(ABC) is equal to:
(1) 5/2
(2) 4
(3) 3
(4) 2
Ans_des: Using the formula for ratio of areas when cevians divide sides in ratio 1:2: Area(ABC)/Area(PQR) = (PC*QA*RB + CQ*AR*BP)/(PQ*QR*RP) = (1*1*1 + 2*2*2)/(3*3*3) = (1+8)/27 = 9/27 = 1/3. So Area(PQR)/Area(ABC) = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
For three positive integers p, q, r, x^(pq^2) = y^(qr) = z^(p^2*r) and r = pq+1 such that 3, 3*log_y(x), 3*log_z(y), 7*log_x(z) are in A.P with common difference 1/2. Then r - p - q is equal to:
(1) 12
(2) 2
(3) -6
(4) 6
Ans_des: AP with first term 3 and cd 1/2: terms are 3, 7/2, 4, 9/2. 3*log_y(x) = 7/2 => log_y(x) = 7/6 => x = y^(7/6). 3*log_z(y) = 4 => log_z(y) = 4/3 => y = z^(4/3). 7*log_x(z) = 9/2 => log_x(z) = 9/14 => z = x^(9/14). From pq^2 = qr (exponents equal): 7pq = 6r. From qr = p^2*r: 4q = 3p^2. r = pq+1. From 7pq = 6r = 6(pq+1): pq=6, r=7. From 4q = 3p^2 and pq=6: q = 6/p, 4*(6/p) = 3p^2, 24 = 3p^3, p=2, q=3. r-p-q = 7-2-3 = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The relation R = {(a,b): gcd(a,b) = 1, 2a =/= b, a,b are integers} is:
(1) Reflexive but not symmetric
(2) Neither symmetric nor transitive
(3) Symmetric but not transitive
(4) Transitive but not reflexive
Ans_des: Not reflexive: gcd(a,a) = a =/= 1 for a > 1. Symmetric: if gcd(a,b) = 1 then gcd(b,a) = 1, so (b,a) is also in R. Not transitive: gcd(a,b)=1 and gcd(b,c)=1 does not imply gcd(a,c)=1. Counterexample: a=2, b=3, c=4: gcd(2,3)=1, gcd(3,4)=1 but gcd(2,4)=2 =/= 1.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The value of lim(t->0) of (1/(1*sin^2(t)) + 1/(2*sin^2(t)) + ... + 1/(n*sin^2(t)))^(sin^2(t)) is equal to:
(1) n^2
(2) n^2 + n
(3) n(n+1)/2
(4) n
Ans_des: Rewrite as: [n*((1/n)^cosec^2(t) + (2/n)^cosec^2(t) + ... + 1)]^(sin^2(t)). Let l = n * lim(t->0) [sum of (r/n)^cosec^2(t)]^(sin^2(t)). As t->0, cosec^2(t)->inf. (r/n)^cosec^2(t) -> 0 for r < n, and (n/n)^cosec^2(t) = 1. So the sum approaches 1. l = n*1^0 = n.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The value of the sum from r=0 to 22 of (22Cr * 23Cr) is:
(1) 44C22
(2) 45C24
(3) 44C23
(4) 45C23
Ans_des: Using Vandermonde identity: sum of (22Cr * 23Cr) for r=0 to 22 = sum of (22Cr * 23C(23-r)) ... Using the identity: sum of (mCr * nCr) = (m+n)C(m) = (m+n)C(n). Here sum of (22Cr)*(23Cr) = 45C22 = 45C23. By multiplying expansions of (1+x)^22 and (x+1)^23 and comparing coefficient of x^23: we get 45C23.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let a tangent to the curve y^2 = 24x meet the curve xy = 2 at the points A and B. Then the mid points of such line segments AB lie on a parabola with the:
(1) Directrix 4x = 3
(2) Length of latus rectum 3/2
(3) Length of latus rectum 2
(4) Directrix 4x = -3
Ans_des: Tangent to y^2=24x at parameter t: ty = x + 6t^2. This meets xy=2: substituting y=2/x: t*(2/x) = x+6t^2 => 2t = x^2+6t^2*x => x^2+6t^2*x-2t=0. Sum of roots: x1+x2 = -6t^2, so 2h = -6t^2, h = -3t^2. Product: x1*x2 = -2t. Sum of y coords: y1+y2 = 2/x1+2/x2 = 2(x1+x2)/(x1*x2) = 2*(-6t^2)/(-2t) = 6t. 2k=6t, k=3t. Eliminate t: t=k/3, h=-3(k/3)^2=-k^2/3 => k^2 = -3h => y^2 = -3x. Vertex at origin, opens left. Directrix: x = 3/4, i.e., 4x = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The equation x^2 - 4x + [x] + 3 = x*[x], where [x] denotes the greatest integer function, has:
(1) Exactly two solutions in (-inf, inf)
(2) No solution
(3) A unique solution in (-inf, 1)
(4) A unique solution in (-inf, inf)
Ans_des: x^2 - 4x + [x] + 3 = x*[x] => (x-1)(x-3) = (x-1)*[x] => (x-1)(x-3-[x]) = 0. Either x=1, or x-3-[x]=0 i.e., {x}=x-[x]=x-3+... means {x}=3 which is impossible (0 <= {x} < 1). So x=1 is the unique solution.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The compound statement (NOT(P AND Q)) OR ((NOT P) AND Q) OR ((NOT P) AND (NOT Q)) is equivalent to:
(1) ((NOT P) OR Q) AND (NOT Q)
(2) (NOT Q) AND P
(3) (NOT P) OR Q
(4) ((NOT P) OR Q) AND ((Q) OR P)
Ans: ((NOT P) OR Q) AND ((Q) OR P)
Ans_des: ~(P^Q) v (~P^Q) v (~P^~Q) = (~P v ~Q) v (~P^Q) v (~P^~Q) = ~P v (~Q v Q^~P) v ~P^~Q... Simplifying using distribution laws: = (~P v ~Q) v ~P = ~P v ~Q. Also equivalent to (P v Q) => (~P ^ ~Q) ... Final simplification gives ((~P v Q) ^ (~Q v P)).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let Omega be the sample space and A is a subset of Omega be an event. Given two statements: (S1): If P(A) = 0, then A = empty set. (S2): If P(A) = 1, then A = Omega. Then:
(1) Both (S1) and (S2) are false
(2) Only (S1) is true
(3) Only (S2) is true
(4) Both (S1) and (S2) are true
Ans_des: Both statements are correct in the standard probability theory context where we consider events with definite probabilities. If P(A) = 0, A = empty set (null event). If P(A) = 1, A = Omega (certain event). Both S1 and S2 are true.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let alpha be a root of the equation (a-c)x^2 + (b-a)x + (c-b) = 0 where a, b, c are distinct real numbers such that the matrix [[alpha^2, alpha, 1],[1,1,1],[a,b,c]] is singular. Then the value of (a-c)^2/((b-a)(c-b)) + (b-a)^2/((a-c)(c-b)) + (c-b)^2/((a-c)(b-a)) is:
(1) 12
(2) 6
(3) 9
(4) 3
Ans_des: The expression = [(a-c)^3 + (b-a)^3 + (c-b)^3] / [(b-a)(c-b)(a-c)]. Let p=a-c, q=b-a, r=c-b. Then p+q+r=0. For p+q+r=0: p^3+q^3+r^3 = 3pqr. So expression = 3pqr/pqr = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let f(x) = {x^2*sin(1/x), x =/= 0; 0, x = 0}. Then at x = 0:
(1) f is continuous but not differentiable
(2) f and f' both are continuous
(3) f' is continuous but not differentiable
(4) f is continuous but f' is not continuous
Ans: f is continuous but f' is not continuous
Ans_des: At x=0: lim(x->0) x^2*sin(1/x) = 0 = f(0), so f is continuous. f'(0) = lim(h->0) h^2*sin(1/h)/h = lim h*sin(1/h) = 0. So f is differentiable. f'(x) = 2x*sin(1/x) - cos(1/x) for x=/=0, f'(0)=0. lim(x->0) f'(x) = lim(-cos(1/x)) which oscillates; does not exist. So f' is not continuous at x=0.

subject: Mathematics
question:
tan^-1((1+sqrt(3))/(3+sqrt(3))) + sec^-1(sqrt((8+4*sqrt(3))/(6+3*sqrt(3)))) is equal to:
(1) pi/3
(2) pi/6
(3) pi/2
(4) pi/4
Ans_des: First term: (1+sqrt(3))/(3+sqrt(3)) = (1+sqrt(3))/(sqrt(3)(sqrt(3)+1)) = 1/sqrt(3). So tan^-1(1/sqrt(3)) = pi/6. Second term: (8+4*sqrt(3))/(6+3*sqrt(3)) = 4(2+sqrt(3))/(3(2+sqrt(3))) = 4/3. sec^-1(sqrt(4/3)) = sec^-1(2/sqrt(3)) = cos^-1(sqrt(3)/2) = pi/6. Total = pi/6 + pi/6 = pi/3.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let C be the largest circle centred at (2, 0) and inscribed in the ellipse x^2/36 + y^2/16 = 1. If (1, alpha) lies on C, then 10*alpha^2 is equal to _________.
Ans_des: Ellipse: x^2/36 + y^2/16 = 1. Circle centered at (2,0): x = 2+r*cos(t), y = r*sin(t). For largest circle: substitute in ellipse and set discriminant of resulting equation in sin(t) to zero. (2+r*cos)^2/36 + r^2*sin^2/16 = 1. Maximizing r: setting D=0 for quadratic in cos(t) gives r^2 = 128/10. If (1,alpha) on C: (1-2)^2 + alpha^2 = r^2 = 128/10. 1 + alpha^2 = 128/10. alpha^2 = 118/10. 10*alpha^2 = 118.

subject: Mathematics
question: The number of 9-digit numbers that can be formed using all the digits of the number 123412341 so that the even digits occupy only even places, is ___________.
Ans_des: Digits in 123412341: 1,2,3,4,1,2,3,4,1. Even digits: 2,2,4,4 (4 even digits). Odd digits: 1,1,1,3,3 (5 odd digits). Even places (2,4,6,8): 4 places for 4 even digits (2,2,4,4). Arrangements = 4!/(2!2!) = 6. Odd places (1,3,5,7,9): 5 places for 5 odd digits (1,1,1,3,3). Arrangements = 5!/(3!2!) = 10. Total = 6*10 = 60.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let a tangent to the curve 9x^2 + 16y^2 = 144 intersect the coordinate axes at the points A and B. Then the minimum length of the line segment AB is ____________.
Ans_des: Ellipse: x^2/16 + y^2/9 = 1. Point P = (4cos(t), 3sin(t)). Tangent: x*cos(t)/4 + y*sin(t)/3 = 1. A = (4/cos(t), 0) = (4*sec(t), 0), B = (0, 3/sin(t)) = (0, 3*cosec(t)). AB = sqrt(16*sec^2(t) + 9*cosec^2(t)) = sqrt(16+9 + 16*tan^2(t) + 9*cot^2(t)). By AM-GM: 16*tan^2(t) + 9*cot^2(t) >= 2*sqrt(16*9) = 24. AB_min = sqrt(25+24) = sqrt(49) = 7.

question: A boy needs to select five courses from 12 available courses, out of which 5 courses are language courses. If he can choose at most two language courses, then the number of ways he can choose five courses is _____________.
Ans_des: Non-language courses = 12-5 = 7. Case 1 (0 language): 7C5 = 21. Case 2 (1 language): 7C4 * 5C1 = 35*5 = 175. Case 3 (2 language): 7C3 * 5C2 = 35*10 = 350. Total = 21+175+350 = 546.

question: Let lambda be in R and the equation E be |x|^2 - 2|x| + |lambda - 3| = 0. Then the largest element in the set S = {x + lambda: x is an integer solution of E} is ________.
Ans_des: Let u = |x| >= 0. u^2 - 2u + |lambda-3| = 0. Discriminant >= 0: 4 - 4|lambda-3| >= 0 => |lambda-3| <= 1 => 2 <= lambda <= 4. |x| = (2 +/- sqrt(4-4|lambda-3|))/2 = 1 +/- sqrt(1-|lambda-3|). For integer x: |x| must be non-negative integer. Maximum |x| = 1+1 = 2 when lambda=3. x_largest = 2 (when lambda=3). Largest element of S = x + lambda = 2 + 3 = 5.

question: The value of (8/pi) * integral from 0 to pi/2 of (cos(x))^2023 / ((sin(x))^2023 + (cos(x))^2023) dx is __________.
Ans_des: Let I = (8/pi) * integral_0^(pi/2) of cos^2023(x)/(sin^2023(x)+cos^2023(x)) dx. Using property: integral_0^(pi/2) f(x)dx = integral_0^(pi/2) f(pi/2-x)dx, adding gives 2I = (8/pi) * integral_0^(pi/2) 1 dx = (8/pi)*(pi/2) = 4. So I = 2.

subject: Physics
question:
In an Isothermal change, the change in pressure and volume of a gas can be represented for three different temperatures T3 > T2 > T1 as:
(1) T3, T2, T1 curves from top
(2) T1, T2, T3 curves from top
(3) T3 at left, T2 middle, T1 at right
(4) T1, T2, T3 from left with T1 highest pressure at given volume
Ans: Option (4): T1, T2, T3 ordered from left where at constant volume P3 > P2 > P1
Ans_des: For isothermal process PV = nRT. At constant volume V', P is proportional to T. So T3 > T2 > T1 implies P3 > P2 > P1 at same V. The correct graph shows T1 leftmost (highest pressure curve), T2 middle, T3 rightmost.

subject: Physics
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Acceleration due to earth's gravity decreases as you go 'up' or 'down' from earth's surface.
Statement II: Acceleration due to earth's gravity is same at a height 'h' and depth 'd' from earth's surface, if h = d.
(1) Both statements correct
(2) Statement I correct, Statement II incorrect
(3) Both incorrect
(4) Statement I incorrect, Statement II correct
Ans: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Ans_des: Statement I is correct as g decreases both above and below earth's surface. Statement II is incorrect because the formulae for g above (g_h = g*(1 - 2h/R)) and below (g_d = g*(1 - d/R)) the surface are different, so g at height h does not equal g at depth h.

subject: Physics
question:
Assertion A: Steel is used in construction of buildings and bridges.
Reason R: Steel is more elastic and its elastic limit is high.
(1) Both A and R correct, R is correct explanation of A
(2) A correct but R not correct
(3) Both correct but R not correct explanation
(4) A not correct but R correct
Ans: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: Steel is indeed more elastic (larger Young's modulus) and has a high elastic limit, which makes it suitable for construction. R correctly explains A.

subject: Physics
question:
If the distance of the earth from Sun is 1.5*10^6 km. Then the distance of an imaginary planet from Sun, if its period of revolution is 2.83 years is:
(1) 6*10^6 km
(2) 3*10^7 km
(3) 3*10^6 km
(4) 6*10^7 km
Ans_des: By Kepler's third law: T^2 is proportional to r^3. (Te/Tp)^2 = (re/rp)^3. (1/2.83)^2 = (1.5*10^6/rp)^3. (1/2)^(2/3) is approximately 1/2, so rp = 2*1.5*10^6/2 = 3*10^6 km.

subject: Physics
question:
The frequency (nu) of an oscillating liquid drop may depend upon radius (r), density (rho) and surface tension (s) as: nu = r^a * rho^b * s^c. The values of a, b and c respectively are:
(1) (-3/2, 1/2, 1/2)
(2) (3/2, -1/2, 1/2)
(3) (-3/2, -1/2, 1/2)
(4) (3/2, 1/2, -1/2)

subject: Physics
question:
A cell of emf 90 V is connected across series combination of two resistors each of 100 ohm resistance. A voltmeter of resistance 400 ohm is used to measure the potential difference across each resistor. The reading of the voltmeter will be:
(1) 45 V
(2) 40 V
(3) 80 V
(4) 90 V
Ans_des: Voltmeter (400 ohm) parallel with one resistor (100 ohm): equivalent = (400*100)/(400+100) = 80 ohm. Total resistance = 80 + 100 = 180 ohm. Current = 90/180 = 0.5 A. Voltage across parallel combination = 0.5*80 = 40 V. Voltmeter reads 40 V.

subject: Physics
question:
When a beam of white light is allowed to pass through convex lens parallel to principal axis, the different colours of light converge at different points on the principal axis after refraction. This is called:
(1) Spherical aberration
(2) Chromatic aberration
(3) Polarisation
(4) Scattering
Ans_des: Different colours (wavelengths) have different refractive indices causing them to focus at different points. This phenomenon is called chromatic aberration.

subject: Physics
question:
An alpha-particle, a proton and an electron have the same kinetic energy. Which one of the following is correct in case of their de-Broglie wavelength?
(1) lambda_alpha > lambda_p < lambda_e
(2) lambda_alpha = lambda_p = lambda_e
(3) lambda_alpha > lambda_p > lambda_e
(4) lambda_alpha < lambda_p < lambda_e
Ans_des: lambda = h/sqrt(2mK). For same K, lambda is proportional to 1/sqrt(m). Since m_alpha > m_p > m_e, we get lambda_alpha < lambda_p < lambda_e.

subject: Physics
question:
The logic gate equivalent to the given circuit diagram (two inputs A and B with NPN transistors and LED output Y) is:
(1) NAND
(2) NOR
(3) AND
(4) OR
Ans_des: Truth table of the circuit: A=0,B=0 => Y=1; A=0,B=1 => Y=1; A=1,B=0 => Y=1; A=1,B=1 => Y=0. This matches the NAND gate truth table.

subject: Physics
question:
Assertion A: A pendulum clock when taken to Mount Everest becomes fast.
Reason R: The value of g is less at Mount Everest than its value on the surface of earth.
(1) A not correct but R is correct
(2) A correct but R not correct
(3) Both A and R correct but R not correct explanation
(4) Both A and R correct and R is correct explanation
Ans: A is not correct but R is correct
Ans_des: At Mount Everest, g decreases. Time period T = 2*pi*sqrt(L/g) increases when g decreases. So the pendulum oscillates slower and the clock becomes slow (not fast). Assertion A is wrong. Reason R is correct.

subject: Physics
question:
A metallic rod of length L is rotated with angular speed omega normal to a uniform magnetic field B about an axis passing through one end of rod. The induced emf will be:
(1) (1/2)*B^2*L^2*omega
(2) (1/2)*B*L^2*omega
(3) (1/4)*B*L^2*omega
(4) (1/4)*B*L*omega
Ans_des: The centre of the rod moves with velocity v = omega*L/2. emf = B*v*L = B*(omega*L/2)*L = (1/2)*B*L^2*omega.

subject: Physics
question: A spherical ball of radius 1 mm and density 10.5 g/cc is dropped in glycerine of coefficient of viscosity 9.8 poise and density 1.5 g/cc. Viscous force on the ball when it attains constant velocity is 3696*10^(-x) N. The value of x is (g = 9.8 m/s^2, pi = 22/7).

subject: Physics
question: A parallel plate capacitor with air between the plates has a capacitance of 15 pF. The separation between the plates becomes twice and the space between them is filled with a medium of dielectric constant 3.5. Then the capacitance becomes (x/4) pF. The value of x is ___.

subject: Physics
question: A mass m attached to free end of a spring executes SHM with a period of 1 s. If the mass is increased by 3 kg the period of oscillation increases by one second, the value of mass m is ____ kg.
Ans_des: T1 = 2*pi*sqrt(m/k) = 1. T2 = 2*pi*sqrt((m+3)/k) = 2. Dividing: sqrt((m+3)/m) = 2. (m+3)/m = 4. 3m = 3. m = 1 kg.

subject: Physics
question: A single turn current loop in the shape of a right angle triangle with sides 5 cm, 12 cm, 13 cm is carrying current of 2 A. The loop is in a uniform magnetic field of 0.75 T whose direction is parallel to the current in the 13 cm side. The magnitude of magnetic force on the 5 cm side will be (x/130) N. The value of x is ___.
Ans_des: sin(theta) = 12/13 (angle between 5 cm side and B direction). F = I*L*B*sin(theta) = 2*(5/100)*0.75*(12/13) = 9/130 N. So x = 9.

subject: Physics
question: A uniform solid cylinder with radius R and length L has moment of inertia I1. A concentric solid cylinder of radius R/2 and length L/2 is carved out. If I2 is the moment of inertia of carved out portion, then I1/I2 = ___.

subject: Physics
question: A convex lens of refractive index 1.5 and focal length 18 cm in air is immersed in water. The change in focal length of the lens will be _____ cm. (Refractive index of water = 4/3)

subject: Physics
question: A body of mass 1 kg begins to move under the action of a time dependent force F = (t*i + 3*t^2*j) N. The power developed by above force, at the time t = 2 s, will be _____ W.
topics: Work, Energy and Power / Newton's Laws
Ans_des: a = F/m = t*i + 3*t^2*j. v = integral of a dt from 0 to 2 = (t^2/2)*i + t^3*j at t=2: v = 2i + 8j. F at t=2: F = 2i + 12j. P = F.v = (2)(2) + (12)(8) = 4 + 96 = 100 W.

subject: Physics
question: Three identical resistors R = 12 ohm and two identical inductors L = 5 mH are connected to a 12 V ideal battery. The current through the battery long after the switch has been closed will be _____ A.
Ans_des: Long after switch closed, inductors act as short circuits (wires). The three resistors (12 ohm each) end up in parallel. R_eq = 12/3 = 4 ohm. I = V/R_eq = 12/4 = 3 A.

subject: Physics
question: The energy released per fission of nucleus of 240X is 200 MeV. The energy released if all the atoms in 120 g of pure 240X undergo fission is _____ x 10^25 MeV. (NA = 6*10^23)

subject: Physics
question: If a copper wire is stretched to increase its length by 20%. The percentage increase in resistance of the wire is _____ %.
Ans_des: Volume constant: L0*A0 = 1.2*L0*A_f => A_f = A0/1.2. R = rho*L/A. R_new/R_old = (1.2*L0)/(A0/1.2) / (L0/A0) = 1.2^2 = 1.44. Percentage increase = 44%.

subject: Chemistry
question:
In which of the following reactions does hydrogen peroxide act as a reducing agent?
(1) HOCl + H2O2 -> H3O+ + Cl- + O2
(2) PbS + 4H2O2 -> PbSO4 + 4H2O
(3) 2Fe2+ + H2O2 -> 2Fe3+ + 2OH-
(4) Mn2+ + H2O2 -> Mn4+ + 2OH-
Ans_des: In reaction (1), Cl goes from +1 in HOCl to -1 in Cl- (reduction). H2O2 is oxidized (O goes from -1 to 0 in O2), so H2O2 acts as a reducing agent.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Choose the correct colour of the product when a diazonium compound reacts with 1-Naphthyl amine:
(1) Blue
(2) Red
(3) Yellow
(4) White
Ans_des: The coupling reaction of a diazonium salt with 1-Naphthylamine gives a red-coloured azo dye.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which one amongst the following are good oxidizing agents?
A. Sm2+  B. Ce2+  C. Ce4+  D. Tb4+
(1) C only
(2) A and B only
(3) D only
(4) C and D only
Ans: C and D only (Ce4+ and Tb4+)
Ans_des: The common oxidation state of lanthanides is +3. Ce4+ and Tb4+ are strong oxidizing agents because they tend to revert back to the stable +3 state. Sm2+ and Ce2+ are reducing agents as they would go to +3.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Pure Aniline and other arylamines are usually colourless.
Statement II: Arylamines get coloured on storage due to atmospheric reduction.
(1) Statement I correct, II incorrect
(2) Both incorrect
(3) Statement I incorrect, II correct
(4) Both correct
Ans: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Ans_des: Statement I is correct: pure arylamines are colourless. Statement II is incorrect: arylamines get coloured on storage due to atmospheric OXIDATION (not reduction).

subject: Chemistry
question:
The metal which is extracted by oxidation and subsequent reduction from its ore is:
(1) Al
(2) Cu
(3) Fe
(4) Ag
Ans_des: Ag is first oxidized: 4Ag + 8NaCN + 2H2O + O2 -> 4Na[Ag(CN)2] + 4NaOH. Then reduced: 2Na[Ag(CN)2] + Zn -> Na2[Zn(CN)4] + 2Ag.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: Benzene is more stable than hypothetical cyclohexatriene.
Reason R: The delocalized pi electron cloud is attracted more strongly by nuclei of carbon atoms.
(1) Both correct but R is not correct explanation
(2) Both correct and R is correct explanation
(3) A true but R false
(4) A true but R false
Ans: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: Benzene is resonance stabilized. The delocalized pi electrons are attracted more strongly by carbon nuclei, making benzene more stable than cyclohexatriene. R correctly explains A.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: Beryllium has less negative value of reduction potential compared to other alkaline earth metals.
Reason R: Beryllium has large hydration energy due to small size of Be2+ but relatively large value of atomization enthalpy.
(1) Both correct but R not correct explanation
(2) A correct but R not correct
(3) Both correct and R is correct explanation
(4) A not correct but R correct
Ans: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Ans_des: Be has the least negative reduction potential among alkaline earth metals. High atomisation and ionisation enthalpies outweigh high hydration enthalpy of Be2+. R correctly explains A.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following cannot be explained by crystal field theory?
(1) Stability of metal complexes
(2) The order of spectrochemical series
(3) Magnetic properties of transition metal complexes
(4) Colour of metal complexes
Ans_des: CFT cannot explain the spectrochemical series because according to CFT, anionic ligands should exert the greatest splitting effect. However, they actually lie at the lower end of the spectrochemical series.

subject: Chemistry
question:
What is the number of unpaired electron(s) in the highest occupied molecular orbital of the following species: N2, N2+, O2, O2+?
(1) 0, 1, 0, 1
(2) 0, 1, 2, 1
(3) 2, 1, 2, 1
(4) 2, 1, 0, 1
Ans_des: N2: HOMO is sigma2p (no unpaired). N2+: HOMO is pi*2p with 1 electron (1 unpaired). O2: HOMO is pi*2p with 2 electrons in degenerate orbitals (2 unpaired). O2+: HOMO has 1 electron in pi*2p (1 unpaired).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Choose the correct representation of conductometric titration of benzoic acid vs sodium hydroxide:
(1) Decreases then increases (V-shape)
(2) Shape with minimum and then increase
(3) Continuously increasing
(4) Increases then decreases
Ans: Conductance decreases to a minimum at equivalence point then increases (V-shape curve, option 1)
Ans_des: Benzoic acid is a weak acid with low conductance. As NaOH is added, weakly conducting acid is replaced by sodium benzoate. At equivalence point conductance is minimum. After that excess NaOH increases conductance. Graph shows a V-shape.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which will undergo deprotonation most readily in basic medium among: a) CH2(CO)2 (1,3-diketone), b) MeO-CH(CO)-CH(CO)-OMe, c) CH3CO-CH2-CO-OMe?
(1) c only
(2) a only
(3) Both a and c
(4) b only
Ans_des: Compound (a) has a CH2 flanked by two carbonyl groups giving maximum acidic character without cross-conjugation restrictions. In (b) and (c) deprotonation tendency is less due to cross-conjugation effects.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify the correct statements about alkali metals:
A. Order Na > Rb > Li for standard reduction potential
B. CsI is highly soluble in water
C. Lithium carbonate is highly stable to heat
D. Potassium in concentrated liquid ammonia is blue and paramagnetic
E. All alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids
(1) A,B,E  (2) A,B,D  (3) C,E  (4) A and E only
Ans_des: A: Correct order. B: Wrong - CsI is less soluble due to low hydration enthalpy. C: Wrong - Li2CO3 decomposes on heating. D: Wrong - in concentrated ammonia the solution becomes diamagnetic. E: Correct - alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The hybridization and magnetic behaviour of cobalt ion in [Co(NH3)6]3+ complex are:
(1) d2sp3 and paramagnetic
(2) sp3d2 and diamagnetic
(3) sp3d2 and paramagnetic
(4) d2sp3 and diamagnetic
Ans_des: NH3 is a strong field ligand causing spin pairing. Co3+ has d6 configuration. All 6 electrons pair up in t2g. Hybridisation is d2sp3 (inner orbital complex). No unpaired electrons => diamagnetic.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Correct statement about air and food consumption:
(1) Average human consumes ~15 times more air than food
(2) Average human consumes more food than air
(3) Average human consumes 100 times more air than food
(4) Equal amounts of food and air
Ans: An average human being consumes nearly 15 times more air than food
Ans_des: Factual knowledge: An average human being consumes about 15 times more air than food.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Find the major products A and B:
[Alkene] + BH3/THF then H2O2/OH- gives A (anti-Markovnikov alcohol)
[Same alkene] + Hg(OAc)2/H2O then NaBH4 gives B (Markovnikov alcohol)
(1) A=branched-OH, B=branched-OH
(2) A=anti-Markovnikov OH, B=Markovnikov OH
(3) A=Markovnikov OH, B=anti-Markovnikov OH
(4) different regiochemistry products
Ans: A = anti-Markovnikov alcohol (OH at less substituted carbon), B = Markovnikov alcohol (OH at more substituted carbon)
Ans_des: Hydroboration-oxidation (BH3/THF then H2O2/OH-) gives anti-Markovnikov addition of OH (syn addition). Oxymercuration-demercuration (Hg(OAc)2/H2O then NaBH4) gives Markovnikov alcohol.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Match drug types with drug names:
A. Antifertility drug - I. Norethindrone
B. Tranquilizer - II. Meprobomate
C. Antihistamine - III. Seldane
D. Antibiotic - IV. Ampicillin
(1) A-II,B-I,C-III,D-IV  (2) A-I,B-II,C-III,D-IV  (3) A-I,B-III,C-II,D-IV  (4) A-IV,B-III,C-II,D-I
Ans_des: Norethindrone is an antifertility drug. Meprobomate is a tranquilizer. Seldane is an antihistamine. Ampicillin is an antibiotic.

subject: Chemistry
question:
K2Cr2O7 paper acidified with dilute H2SO4 turns green when exposed to:
(1) Hydrogen sulphide
(2) Carbon dioxide
(3) Sulphur dioxide
(4) Sulphur trioxide
Ans_des: SO2 reduces Cr2O7(2-) to Cr3+ (green colour) in acidic medium. This is the standard test for SO2.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Statement I: H2N-CH2-CO-CH2-CO (with NH2 group) under Clemmensen reduction gives HOOC-CH2-CH2-CH2 product.
Statement II: Compound with Cl-CH2-CO under Wolff-Kishner gives Cl-CH2-CH2 product.
(1) Statement I true but II false
(2) Both false
(3) Both true
(4) Statement I false but II true
Ans: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Ans_des: Clemmensen reduction converts C=O to CH2, and does not affect NH2 or COOH groups; Statement I is correct. Wolff-Kishner uses basic conditions (NH2NH2/OH-); acid-sensitive Cl would not survive, so Statement II is false.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The number of s-electrons present in an ion with 55 protons in its unipositive state is:
(1) 10
(2) 8
(3) 9
(4) 12
Ans_des: 55 protons = Cs. Cs+ has 54 electrons. Electronic configuration of Cs+: 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p6 3d10 4s2 4p6 4d10 5s2 5p6. s-electrons: 1s2+2s2+3s2+4s2+5s2 = 2+2+2+2+2 = 10.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A student studied decomposition of gas AB3 at 25C and obtained data: p(mmHg): 50, 100, 200, 400; relative t(1/2)(s): 4, 2, 1, 0.5. The order of the reaction is:
(1) 1
(2) 0
(3) 2
(4) 0.5
Ans_des: t(1/2) is proportional to 1/p (as p doubles, t(1/2) halves). For order n: t(1/2) proportional to p^(1-n). Here 1-n = -1, so n = 2. Order of reaction = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question: Maximum number of isomeric monochloro derivatives which can be obtained from 2,2,5,5-tetramethylhexane by chlorination is ___.
Ans_des: Structure of 2,2,5,5-tetramethylhexane: (CH3)3C-CH2-CH2-C(CH3)3. Distinct H environments: (1) 18 equivalent CH3 hydrogens, (2) 4 equivalent CH2 hydrogens (giving 2 enantiomers = 2 isomers). Total = 1 + 2 = 3 isomeric monochloro derivatives.

subject: Chemistry
question: Total number of tripeptides possible by mixing of valine and proline is ___.
Ans_des: With 2 amino acids and 3 positions, each position can be either valine (V) or proline (P). Total = 2^3 = 8 tripeptides.

subject: Chemistry
question: Sum of pi-bonds present in peroxodisulphuric acid (H2S2O8) and pyrosulphuric acid (H2S2O7) is ___.
Ans_des: H2S2O8 (peroxodisulphuric acid): each S has 2 S=O bonds; total 4 pi bonds + the structure gives 4 pi bonds. H2S2O7 (pyrosulphuric acid): each S has 2 S=O double bonds = 4 pi bonds. Total = 4 + 4 = 8.

subject: Chemistry
question: Total pressure by mixing two liquids A and B is 350 mmHg when mole fractions are 0.7 and 0.3. Total pressure becomes 410 mmHg if mole fractions are 0.2 and 0.8. Vapour pressure of pure A is ___ mmHg.

subject: Chemistry
question: If the pKa of lactic acid is 5, then the pH of 0.005 M calcium lactate solution at 25C is ___ x 10^-1 (Nearest integer).
Ans_des: Calcium lactate is a salt of weak acid and strong base. pH = 7 + (1/2)*(pKa + log c) = 7 + (1/2)*(5 + log 0.005) = 7 + (1/2)*(5 - 2.3) approx 7 + 1.5 = 8.5. So 8.5 x 10^-1 * 10 = 85.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The number of statements which are characteristics of physisorption is ___:
A. It is highly specific in nature
B. Enthalpy of adsorption is high
C. It decreases with increase in temperature
D. It results into unimolecular layer
E. No activation energy is needed
Ans_des: Correct characteristics of physisorption: C (decreases with temp) and E (no activation energy). A is wrong (non-specific), B wrong (low enthalpy), D wrong (multimolecular layer). Count = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question: Following figure shows spectrum of an ideal black body at four temperatures T1>T2>T3>T4. The number of correct statements from: A. T4>T3>T2>T1; B. Particles perform SHM; C. Peak shifts to shorter wavelength as temp increases; D. T1/v1=T2/v2=T3/v3=constant; E. Explained by quantisation of energy.
Ans_des: C is correct (Wien's displacement law: peak shifts to shorter wavelength at higher temp). E is correct (Planck explained black body radiation using energy quantisation). A is wrong (T1>T2>T3>T4 from graph). B is wrong. D is wrong. Count = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question: The number of correct statements about compression of carbon dioxide from point (a) in Andrews isotherm: A. CO2 remains gas up to point (b); B. Liquid CO2 appears at point (c); C. Liquid and gas coexist between (b) and (c); D. As volume decreases from (b) to (c), amount of liquid decreases.
Ans_des: A is correct (gas until point b). B is wrong (liquid appears at b not c). C is correct (two phases coexist between b and c). D is wrong (as volume decreases liquid increases). Count = 2.

subject: Chemistry
question: One mole of ideal monoatomic gas is subjected to cyclic process 1->2->3->1 (isothermal then constant volume then isothermal). Magnitude of work done is ___ J (log 2=0.3, ln 10=2.3).

subject: Chemistry
question: The number of units used to express concentration of solutions from: Mass percent, Mole, Mole fraction, Molarity, ppm, Molality is ___.
Ans_des: Concentration units: Mass percent, Mole fraction, Molarity, ppm, Molality = 5 units. 'Mole' alone is not a concentration unit; it measures amount of substance.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The set of all values of a for which lim(x->a) ([x-5] - [2x+2]) = 0, where [] denotes greatest integer function, is equal to:
(1) [-7.5, -6.5)
(2) (-7.5, -6.5]
(3) [-7.5, -6.5]
(4) (-7.5, -6.5)
Ans_des: Condition: [x-5] = [2x+2] => [x]-5 = [2x]+2 => [x] = [2x]+7. For x in (-7.5,-7): -8=-15+7=true. For x in (-7,-6.5): -7=-14+7=true. At x=-6.5: -7 not equal to -13+7=-6. At x=-7.5: condition holds at point but limit requires open neighborhood. So a in (-7.5,-6.5).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let p and q be two statements. Then negation of (p AND (p => NOT q)) is equivalent to:
(1) (NOT p) OR q
(2) p OR ((NOT p) AND q)
(3) p OR (p AND q)
(4) p OR (p AND (NOT q))
Ans_des: Using truth table: negation of (p AND (p => NOT q)) is equivalent to NOT p OR q. Verify: p=T,q=T: expression=NOT(T AND F)=T=NOT p OR q=T. p=T,q=F: NOT(T AND T)=F=NOT p OR q=F. p=F: NOT(F AND ...)=T=NOT p OR q=T.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The locus of midpoints of chords of circle C1:(x-4)^2+(y-5)^2=4 which subtend angle theta_i at centre, is a circle of radius r_i. If theta_1=pi/3, theta_3=2*pi/3 and r1^2 = r2^2 + r3^2, then theta_2 equals:
(1) 3*pi/4
(2) pi/4
(3) pi/6
(4) pi/2
Ans_des: r_i = 2*cos(theta_i/2) (radius of circle = 2). r1^2 = r2^2 + r3^2. 4*cos^2(pi/6) = 4*cos^2(theta_2/2) + 4*cos^2(pi/3). 3 = 4*cos^2(theta_2/2) + 1. cos^2(theta_2/2) = 1/2. theta_2/2 = pi/4. theta_2 = pi/2.

subject: Mathematics
question:
If the system x+2y+3z=3, 4x+3y-4z=4, 8x+4y-lambda*z=9+mu has infinitely many solutions, then ordered pair (lambda,mu) equals:
(1) (-72/5, 21/5)
(2) (72/5, -21/5)
(3) (72/5, 21/5)
(4) (-72/5, -21/5)
Ans_des: For infinite solutions: D=0. det[[1,2,3],[4,3,-4],[8,4,-lambda]]=0. Solving gives lambda=72/5. Then D_z=0 (with constants 3,4,9+mu) gives mu=-21/5.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let the plane containing line of intersection of planes P1: x+(lambda+4)y+z=1 and P2: 2x+y+z=2 pass through (0,1,0) and (1,0,1). Distance of point (2*lambda, lambda, -lambda) from P2 is:
(1) 4*sqrt(6)
(2) 3*sqrt(6)
(3) 5*sqrt(6)
(4) 2*sqrt(6)
Ans_des: Family of planes: (P1) + k*(P2)=0. Passing through (0,1,0): lambda+4-1+k*(-1)=0. Passing through (1,0,1): 1+k=0 => k=-1. Then lambda=-4. Point = (-8,-4,4). Distance from 2x+y+z=2: |(-16-4+4-2)|/sqrt(6) = 18/sqrt(6) = 3*sqrt(6).

subject: Mathematics
question:
If foot of perpendicular drawn from (1,9,7) to line through (3,2,1) and parallel to planes x+2y+z=0 and 3y-z=3 is (alpha,beta,gamma), then alpha+beta+gamma equals:
(1) -1
(2) 1
(3) 3
(4) 5
Ans_des: Direction of line = b = n1 x n2 = |i j k; 1 2 1; 0 3 -1| = -5i + j + 3k. Line: (x-3)/(-5) = (y-2)/1 = (z-1)/3 = t. Foot = (-5t+3, t+2, 3t+1). Perpendicular condition: dot product with (-5,1,3) = 0. 25t-10+t-7+9k-18=0 => 35t=35 => t=1. Foot = (-2,3,4). Sum = 5.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let A be a 3x3 matrix such that |adj(adj(adj A))| = 12^4. Then |A^(-1) * adj A| equals:
(1) 12
(2) 2*sqrt(3)
(3) sqrt(6)
(4) 1

subject: Mathematics
question:
The value of ((1+sin(2*pi/9)+i*cos(2*pi/9))/(1+sin(2*pi/9)-i*cos(2*pi/9)))^3 is:
(1) (1/2)*(sqrt(3)+i)
(2) -(1/2)*(1-i*sqrt(3))
(3) (1/2)*(1-i*sqrt(3))
(4) -(1/2)*(sqrt(3)-i)
Ans_des: 1+sin(2pi/9)+i*cos(2pi/9) = 2*cos(5pi/36)*exp(i*5pi/36). Denominator = 2*cos(5pi/36)*exp(-i*5pi/36). Ratio = exp(i*10pi/36) = exp(i*5pi/18). Cube = exp(i*5pi/6) = cos(5pi/6)+i*sin(5pi/6) = -sqrt(3)/2 + i/2 = -(1/2)*(sqrt(3)-i).

subject: Mathematics
question:
The number of square matrices of order 5 with entries from {0,1}, such that sum of all elements in each row is 1 and sum of all elements in each column is also 1, is:
(1) 120
(2) 225
(3) 150
(4) 125
Ans_des: Each row has exactly one 1 and four 0s, each column has exactly one 1. These are permutation matrices of order 5. Number = 5! = 120.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The number of real solutions of the equation 3*(x^2+1/x^2) - 2*(x+1/x) + 5 = 0 is:
(1) 3
(2) 0
(3) 2
(4) 4
Ans_des: Let t = x+1/x. Note |t| >= 2 for real x. Then x^2+1/x^2 = t^2-2. Equation: 3(t^2-2)-2t+5=0 => 3t^2-2t-1=0 => (3t+1)(t-1)=0 => t=1 or t=-1/3. Both |t|<2, impossible for real x. So 0 real solutions.

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let alpha = 4i+3j+5k and beta = i+2j-4k. Let beta_1 be parallel to alpha and beta_2 perpendicular to alpha. If beta = beta_1 + beta_2, then value of 5*beta_2.(i+j+k) is:
(1) 7
(2) 9
(3) 6
(4) 11
Ans_des: beta_1 = lambda*alpha. beta_2.alpha = 0. beta.alpha = lambda*|alpha|^2. lambda = (4+6-20)/50 = -10/50 = -1/5. beta_1 = (-4/5)i - (3/5)j - k. beta_2 = beta - beta_1 = (9/5)i + (13/5)j - 3k. 5*beta_2.(i+j+k) = 9+13-15 = 7.

question:

subject: Mathematics
question:
The number of integers greater than 7000 that can be formed using the digits 3,5,6,7,8 without repetition is:
(1) 48
(2) 168
(3) 220
(4) 120
Ans_des: 4-digit numbers > 7000: first digit 7 gives 4*3*2=24; first digit 8 gives 4*3*2=24; total 4-digit = 48. 5-digit numbers: 5! = 120. Total = 48+120 = 168.

subject: Mathematics
question:
The equations of sides AB and AC of triangle ABC are (lambda+1)x+lambda*y=4 and lambda*x+(1-lambda)y+lambda=0. Vertex A is on y-axis and orthocentre is (1,2). Length of tangent from C to parabola y^2=6x in first quadrant is:
(1) 2*sqrt(2)
(2) sqrt(6)
(3) 2
(4) 4
Ans_des: A is on y-axis. Setting x=0 in both line equations: from AB: lambda*y=4 and from AC: (1-lambda)y+lambda=0 gives y=-lambda/(1-lambda). Equating: lambda*(-lambda/(1-lambda))=4 => lambda=2. AB: 3x+2y=4, AC: 2x-y=-2. With orthocentre (1,2), find C=(-1/2,1). Tangent length from C(-1/2,1) to y^2=6x: sqrt(y1^2-6x1)/... = sqrt(S1) = sqrt(1+3) = 2. So length = 2*sqrt(2).

subject: Mathematics
question:
Let y=y(x) be solution of (x^2-3y^2)dx+3xy*dy=0, y(1)=1. Then 6*y^2(e) equals:
(1) 3*e^2
(2) (3/2)*e^2
(3) e^2
(4) 2*e^2
Ans_des: Divide by 3xy*dy: rearrange to get dy/dx in terms. Let v=y^2. dv/dx - (2v/x) = (-2x/3). IF = 1/x^2. v/x^2 = (-2/3)*ln|x| + c. y^2/x^2 = (-2/3)*ln|x| + 1 (using y(1)=1). At x=e: y^2/e^2 = (-2/3)+1 = 1/3. y^2 = e^2/3. 6y^2 = 2e^2.

(3) 220
(4) 210

subject: Mathematics
question: Let f be differentiable on [0,pi/2] with f(x)>0 and f(x) + integral from 0 to x of f(t)*sqrt(1-(log_e f(t))^2) dt = e. Then (6*log_e f(pi/6))^2 equals ___.
Ans_des: Differentiating: f'(x)+f(x)*sqrt(1-(ln f(x))^2)=0. Let t=ln f(x). dt/sqrt(1-t^2)=-dx. sin^(-1)(t)=-x+c. f(0)=e so t=1, c=pi/2. t=sin(pi/2-x)=cos(x). ln f(pi/6)=cos(pi/6)=sqrt(3)/2. (6*sqrt(3)/2)^2 = (3*sqrt(3))^2 = 27.

subject: Mathematics
question: If (1^3+2^3+3^3+...+n terms)/(1.3+2.5+3.7+...+n terms) = 9/3, then value of n is ___.
Ans_des: Numerator = (n(n+1)/2)^2. Denominator = sum of r(2r+1) = 2n(n+1)(2n+1)/6 + n(n+1)/2 = n(n+1)*(2n+1)/3 + n(n+1)/2. After simplification and equating to 3: gives 5n^2-19n-30=0 => (5n+6)(n-5)=0 => n=5.

subject: Mathematics
question: Equations of sides AB, BC, CA of triangle ABC: 2x+y=0, x+p*y=21a (a not 0), x-y=3. P(2,a) is centroid. Then (BC)^2 = ___.
Ans_des: Using centroid condition and solving simultaneous equations: p=11, a=3. B=(-3,6), C=(8,5). BC^2=(8-(-3))^2+(5-6)^2=121+1=122.

subject: Mathematics
question: The minimum number of elements that must be added to the relation R={(a,b),(b,c),(b,d)} on set {a,b,c,d} so that it is an equivalence relation is ___.
Ans_des: Need: reflexive pairs (a,a),(b,b),(c,c),(d,d): +4. Symmetric: (b,a),(c,b),(d,b): +3. Transitive from (a,b),(b,c): need (a,c): +1. From (a,b),(b,d): need (a,d): +1. Then symmetric (c,a),(d,a): +2. From (b,c),(c,b): (b,b) already. From (c,b),(b,d): (c,d): +1. From (d,b),(b,c): (d,c): +1. Total = 4+3+1+1+2+1+1 = 13.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let S={theta in [0,2*pi): tan(pi*cos(theta))+tan(pi*sin(theta))=0}. Then sum over S of sin^2(theta+pi/4) equals ___.
Ans_des: tan(pi*cos(theta))=-tan(pi*sin(theta)). pi*cos(theta)=n*pi - pi*sin(theta). sin+cos=n. Solutions where sin+cos in {-1,0,1}: theta=0,pi/2,3pi/4,7pi/4,pi,3pi/2. Compute sum of sin^2(theta+pi/4): 1/2+1/2+0+0+1/2+1/2 = 2.

subject: Mathematics
question: If area of region bounded by curves y^2-2y=-x and x+y=0 is A, then 8A equals ___.
Ans_des: y^2-2y=-x is parabola x=(2y-y^2). x+y=0 is line x=-y. Intersection: 2y-y^2=-y => y^2-3y=0 => y=0 or y=3. Area = integral from 0 to 3 of [(2y-y^2)-(-y)] dy = integral of (3y-y^2) dy = [3y^2/2-y^3/3] from 0 to 3 = 27/2-9 = 9/2. 8A = 36.

subject: Mathematics
question: If shortest distance between lines (x+sqrt(6))/2=(y-sqrt(6))/3=(z-sqrt(6))/4 and (x-lambda)/3=(y-2*sqrt(6))/4=(z+2*sqrt(6))/5 is 6, then square of sum of all possible values of lambda is ___.
Ans_des: b1=2i+3j+4k, b2=3i+4j+5k. a2-a1=(lambda-sqrt(6))i+sqrt(6)j-3*sqrt(6)k. d=|(a2-a1).(b1xb2)|/|b1xb2|=6. Compute: b1xb2=(-1)i+(2)j+(-1)k... Solving gives |-lambda+4*sqrt(6)|=6*sqrt(6). lambda1=-2*sqrt(6), lambda2=10*sqrt(6). Sum^2=(8*sqrt(6))^2=384.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let sum of first three terms in expansion of (x-3/x^2)^n, x not 0, n in N, be 376. Then coefficient of x^4 is ___.

subject: Mathematics
question: Three urns A,B,C contain 4 red 6 black; 5 red 5 black; lambda red 4 black balls. One urn selected randomly. A red ball is drawn. Probability it is from urn C is 0.4. Then square of length of side of largest equilateral triangle inscribed in parabola y^2=lambda*x with one vertex at vertex is ___.
Ans_des: P(C|red)=(1/3)*(lambda/(lambda+4))/[(1/3)*(4/10)+(1/3)*(5/10)+(1/3)*(lambda/(lambda+4))]=0.4. Solving: 10*lambda/(19*lambda+36)=2/5 => 50*lambda=38*lambda+72 => lambda=6. Parabola: y^2=6x. Let side=L. Point (sqrt(3)*L/2, L/2) on parabola: (L/2)^2=6*(sqrt(3)*L/2). L^2/4=3*sqrt(3)*L. L=12*sqrt(3). L^2=432.

subject: Mathematics
question: Let a=i+2j+lambda*k, b=3i-5j-lambda*k, a.c=7, 2*b.c+43=0, a x c = b x c. Then |a.b| equals ___.
Ans_des: a x c = b x c => (a-b) x c = 0 => c || (a-b) = (-2i+7j+2*lambda*k). c = alpha*(-2i+7j+2*lambda*k). a.c = alpha*(12+2*lambda^2) = 7. 2*b.c = 2*alpha*(-41-2*lambda^2) = -43. alpha*(12+2*lambda^2)/alpha*(-41-2*lambda^2) = 7/(-43/2). Solving: lambda^2=1. |a.b| = |3-10-lambda^2| = |3-10-1| = 8.

question:
A message signal of frequency 5 kHz is used to modulate a carrier signal of frequency 2 MHz. The bandwidth for amplitude modulation is:
(1) 5 kHz
(2) 2.5 kHz
(3) 10 kHz
(4) 20 kHz
Ans_des: Bandwidth for AM = 2 x frequency of modulating signal = 2 x 5 kHz = 10 kHz.

subject: Physics
question:
Electron beam used in an electron microscope, when accelerated by a voltage of 20 kV, has a de-Broglie wavelength of lambda_0. If the voltage is increased to 40 kV, then the de-Broglie wavelength associated with the electron beam would be:
(1) 9*lambda_0
(2) lambda_0/2
(3) lambda_0/sqrt(2)
(4) 3*lambda_0
Ans_des: lambda = h/sqrt(2meV). lambda_0 = h/sqrt(2m*e*20000). lambda_new = h/sqrt(2m*e*40000) = lambda_0/sqrt(2).

subject: Physics
question:
The root mean square velocity of molecules of gas is:
(1) Proportional to square root of temperature (sqrt(T))
(2) Inversely proportional to square root of temperature (1/sqrt(T))
(3) Proportional to temperature (T)
(4) Proportional to square of temperature (T^2)
Ans: Proportional to square root of temperature (sqrt(T))
Ans_des: Vrms = sqrt(3RT/M). Therefore Vrms is proportional to sqrt(T).

subject: Physics
question:
Match List I with List II:
A. Surface tension - I. kg m^-1 s^-1
B. Pressure - II. kg m s^-1
C. Viscosity - III. kg m^-1 s^-2
D. Impulse - IV. kg s^-2
Options: (1) A-III,B-IV,C-I,D-II  (2) A-II,B-I,C-III,D-IV  (3) A-IV,B-III,C-I,D-II  (4) A-IV,B-III,C-II,D-I
Ans_des: Surface tension = N/m = kg s^-2 (IV). Pressure = N/m^2 = kg m^-1 s^-2 (III). Viscosity = Pa.s = kg m^-1 s^-1 (I). Impulse = N.s = kg m s^-1 (II).

subject: Physics
question:
A bowl filled with very hot soup cools from 98C to 86C in 2 minutes when the room temperature is 22C. How long will it take to cool from 75C to 69C?
(1) 2 minutes
(2) 1.4 minutes
(3) 0.5 minute
(4) 1 minute
topics: Thermal Physics / Newton's Law of Cooling
Ans_des: Newton's law: dT/dt = -k(T - Ts). Case I: 12/2 = k*(92-22) = 70k. Case II: 6/dt = k*(72-22) = 50k. From ratio: dt = 6*2*70/(12*50) = 1.4 min.

subject: Physics
question:
T is the time period of simple pendulum on the earth's surface. Its time period becomes xT when taken to a height R (equal to earth's radius) above the earth's surface. Then the value of x will be:
(1) 4
(2) 1/2
(3) 2
(4) 1/4
Ans_des: g at height R: g_R = GM/(2R)^2 = g/4. T = 2*pi*sqrt(L/g). T_R = 2*pi*sqrt(L/(g/4)) = 2*T. So x = 2.

subject: Physics
question:
A Carnot engine with efficiency 50% takes heat from a source at 600 K. In order to increase the efficiency to 70%, keeping the temperature of sink same, the new temperature of the source will be:
(1) 360 K
(2) 300 K
(3) 900 K
(4) 1000 K
Ans_des: eta = 1 - T_sink/T_source. At 50%: 0.5 = 1 - T_sink/600, so T_sink = 300 K. At 70%: 0.7 = 1 - 300/T_source, so T_source = 300/0.3 = 1000 K.

question:

(1) 2:1
(2) 8:1
(3) 1:1
(4) 4:1
Ans_des: Nuclear density is constant for all nuclei (it does not depend on mass number). Therefore rho_oxygen / rho_helium = 1.

subject: Physics
question:
A car travels a distance x with speed v1 and then same distance x with speed v2 in the same direction. The average speed of the car is:
(1) (v1+v2)/2
(2) v1*v2/(2*(v1+v2))
(3) 2*v1*v2/(v1+v2)
(4) 2x/(v1+v2)
Ans_des: v_avg = 2x / (x/v1 + x/v2) = 2x / (x*(v1+v2)/(v1*v2)) = 2*v1*v2/(v1+v2). This is the harmonic mean.

subject: Physics
question:
An electromagnetic wave is transporting energy in the negative z direction. At a certain point and certain time the direction of electric field of the wave is along positive y direction. What will be the direction of the magnetic field of the wave at that point and instant?
(1) Negative direction of x
(2) Negative direction of y
(3) Positive direction of z
(4) Positive direction of x
Ans_des: For EM wave: E x B = direction of propagation. E is in +y and propagation is in -z. (+y) x B = -z. So B must be in +x direction (since y_hat x x_hat = -z_hat).

question:
In an LC oscillator, if values of inductance and capacitance become twice and eight times respectively, then the resonant frequency of oscillator becomes x times its initial resonant frequency omega_0. The value of x is:
(1) 1/16
(2) 1/4
(3) 4
(4) 16
Ans_des: omega_0 = 1/sqrt(LC). New omega = 1/sqrt(2L * 8C) = 1/sqrt(16LC) = omega_0/4. So x = 1/4.

subject: Physics
question:
Assertion A: Photodiodes are used in forward bias usually for measuring the light intensity.
Reason R: For a p-n junction diode, at applied voltage V the current in the forward bias is more than the current in reverse bias for |Vz| > +/-V >= |V0|.
(1) A is false but R is true
(2) Both A and R are true and R is correct explanation of A
(3) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(4) A is true but R is false
Ans: A is false but R is true
Ans_des: Photodiodes are used in REVERSE bias (not forward bias) for measuring light intensity. So Assertion A is false. Reason R is a correct statement about p-n junction diodes.

subject: Physics
question:
Assume a tunnel is dug along the diameter of earth. A particle of mass 100 g is released in this tunnel. It executes SHM. The time period will be approximately (g = 10 m/s^2, radius of earth = 6400 km):
(1) 1 hour 40 minutes
(2) 12 hours
(3) 24 hours
(4) 1 hour 24 minutes
Ans_des: T = 2*pi*sqrt(R/g) = 2*pi*sqrt(6400000/10) = 2*pi*800 = 5024 s = approx 1 hour 24 minutes.

question:
Match List I (current configuration) with List II (magnitude of magnetic field at point O):
A. Semicircle with two straight wires - I. B0 = (mu_0*I/4*pi*r)*(pi+2)
B. Three-quarter circle with straight segment - II. B0 = mu_0*I/(4r)
C. Quarter circle with two straight wires - III. B0 = (mu_0*I/2*pi*r)*(pi-1)
D. Straight line only - IV. B0 = (mu_0*I/4*pi*r)*(pi+1)
Options: (1) A-III,B-IV,C-I,D-II  (2) A-I,B-III,C-IV,D-II  (3) A-III,B-I,C-IV,D-II  (4) A-II,B-I,C-IV,D-III
Ans_des: A (semicircle + 2 wires): B = mu_0*I/(2*pi*r)*(pi-1) => A-III. B (3/4 circle + straight): B = mu_0*I/(4*pi*r)*(pi+2) => B-I. C (quarter circle + 2 wires): B = mu_0*I/(4*pi*r)*(pi+1) => C-IV. D (straight wire only): B = mu_0*I/(4r) => D-II.

subject: Physics
question: An object of mass m initially at rest on a smooth horizontal plane starts moving under the action of force F = 2N. The angle theta between the force and horizontal varies as theta = k*x, where k is a constant and x is the distance covered. The expression of kinetic energy of the object will be E = (n/k)*sin(theta). The value of n is ___.
Ans_des: E = Work done = integral of F*cos(kx) dx from 0 to x = integral of 2*cos(kx) dx = (2/k)*[sin(kx)]_0^x = (2/k)*sin(kx) = (2/k)*sin(theta). So n = 2.

question: A uniform electric field of 10 N/C is created between two parallel charged plates. An electron enters the field symmetrically between the plates with a kinetic energy 0.5 eV. The length of each plate is 10 cm. The angle (theta) of deviation of the path of electron as it comes out of the field is ___ degrees.
Ans_des: tan(theta) = v_y/v_x = (eE*t/m)/u = eE*R/(2*KE) = e*10*0.1/(2*0.5*e) = 1. So theta = 45 degrees.

subject: Physics
question: The distance between two consecutive points with phase difference of 60 degrees in a wave of frequency 500 Hz is 6.0 m. The velocity with which wave is traveling is ___ km/s.
Ans_des: Phase difference = (2*pi/lambda)*delta_x. pi/3 = (2*pi/lambda)*6. lambda/6 = 6 => lambda = 36 m. v = f*lambda = 500*36 = 18000 m/s = 18 km/s.

subject: Physics
question: I_CM is the moment of inertia of a circular disc about axis through center perpendicular to plane. I_AB is moment of inertia about an axis perpendicular to plane and parallel to CM at distance (2/3)*R from center. The ratio of I_AB and I_CM is x:9. The value of x is ___.

subject: Physics
question: A ray of light is incident from air on a glass plate having thickness sqrt(3) cm and refractive index sqrt(2). The angle of incidence equals the critical angle for glass-air interface. The lateral displacement of the ray when it passes through the plate is ___ x 10^-2 cm. (sin 15 deg = 0.26)
Ans_des: Critical angle: sin(ic) = 1/sqrt(2), so ic = 45 deg = angle of incidence. Snell's law: sqrt(2)*sin(r) = sin(45) = 1/sqrt(2), so sin(r) = 1/2, r = 30 deg. Lateral displacement = t*sin(i-r)/cos(r) = sqrt(3)*sin(15)/cos(30) = sqrt(3)*0.26/(sqrt(3)/2) = 2*0.26 = 0.52 cm = 52*10^-2 cm.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Identify the products A and E in the reaction sequence: Methylnitrobenzene -> (Br2) -> A -> (Sn/HCl) -> B -> (NaNO2/HCl, 273-278K) -> C -> (H3PO2/H2O) -> D -> (i) KMnO4/KOH, (ii) H3O+) -> E:
(1) A=methyl-dibromo-nitrobenzene, E=bromo-benzaldehyde
(2) A=bromo-methyl-nitrobenzene, E=bromo-benzoic acid
(3) A=dibromo-methyl-nitrobenzene, E=bromo-dicarboxybenzoic acid
(4) A=bromo-methyl-nitrobenzene, E=bromo-hydroxy-benzene
Ans: A = bromo-methyl-nitrobenzene (Br at ortho to NO2), E = bromo-benzoic acid (COOH at position where CH3 was, Br at ortho)
Ans_des: Br2 on methyl-nitrobenzene gives ortho-bromo product (A). Sn/HCl reduces NO2 to NH2 (B). NaNO2/HCl forms diazonium salt (C). H3PO2/H2O removes diazonium (D). KMnO4/KOH oxidizes CH3 to COOH giving E = bromo-benzoic acid.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A cubic solid is made up of two elements X and Y. Atoms of X are present on every alternate corner and one at the center of cube. Y is at 1/3 rd of the total faces. The empirical formula of the compound is:
(1) XY2.5
(2) X1.5Y2
(3) X2Y1.5
(4) X2.5Y
Ans: X3Y2 (No option is correct as given)
Ans_des: X atoms: 4 alternate corners * (1/8) + 1 center = 0.5 + 1 = 1.5. Y atoms: 6 faces * (1/3) * (1/2) = 1. Empirical formula X1.5Y = X3Y2. No given option matches.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Reaction of thionyl chloride with white phosphorus forms compound [A], which on hydrolysis gives [B], a dibasic acid. [A] and [B] are respectively:
(1) POCl3 and H3PO4
(2) PCl3 and H3PO3
(3) PCl5 and H3PO4
(4) P4O6 and H3PO3
Ans_des: P4 + 8SOCl2 -> 4PCl3(A) + 4SO2 + 2S2Cl2. PCl3 + 3H2O -> H3PO3(B) + 3HCl. H3PO3 is a dibasic acid.

subject: Chemistry
question:
25 volume' hydrogen peroxide means:
(1) 1 L marketed solution contains 250 g of H2O2
(2) 100 mL marketed solution contains 25 g of H2O2
(3) 1 L marketed solution contains 25 g of H2O2
(4) 1 L marketed solution contains 75 g of H2O2
Ans: 1 L marketed solution contains 75 g of H2O2
Ans_des: Molarity = Volume strength/11.2 = 25/11.2 = 2.23 M. Amount in 1 L = 2.23 * 34 = 75 g.

(1) Epoxide of cumene
(2) Cumene hydroperoxide (C6H5-C(CH3)2-O-O-H)
(3) Cumene peroxide ester
(4) Benzaldehyde derivative
Ans_des: Cumene + O2 -> cumene hydroperoxide (intermediate). Cumene hydroperoxide + H+ -> phenol + acetone.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which one of the following reactions does not occur during extraction of copper?
(1) CaO + SiO2 -> CaSiO3
(2) FeO + SiO2 -> FeSiO3
(3) 2Cu2S + 3O2 -> 2Cu2O + 2SO2
(4) 2FeS + 3O2 -> 2FeO + 2SO2
Ans_des: In copper extraction, FeO is removed as FeSiO3 slag by reacting with SiO2. CaO is used in iron metallurgy (not copper). Reactions (2), (3), (4) all occur in copper extraction.

(iv) beta-D-(minus)-Glucopyranose

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect for antibiotics?
(1) An antibiotic should promote the growth or survival of microorganisms
(2) An antibiotic is a synthetic substance produced as a structural analogue of naturally occurring antibiotic
(3) An antibiotic should be effective in low concentrations
(4) An antibiotic must be a product of metabolism
Ans: An antibiotic should promote the growth or survival of microorganisms
Ans_des: An antibiotic should INHIBIT (not promote) the growth or survival of microorganisms. All other statements are correct characteristics of antibiotics.

subject: Chemistry
question:
The compound which will have the lowest rate towards nucleophilic aromatic substitution on treatment with OH- is:
(1) 4-chloronitrobenzene (para)
(2) 3-chloronitrobenzene (meta)
(3) 2-chloronitrobenzene (ortho)
(4) 2,4-dichloronitrobenzene
Ans_des: For nucleophilic aromatic substitution, electron-withdrawing groups at ortho or para positions activate the ring. Meta-NO2 does not stabilize the intermediate as effectively. Hence meta-isomer has the lowest rate.

subject: Chemistry
question:
A hydrocarbon P reacts with reagents to give PhCOOH (Q) and PhCH2OH (R). The correct sequence of reagents for preparation of Q and R is:
(1) (i) KMnO4/OH-; (ii) Mo2O3; (iii) NaOH; (iv) H3O+
(2) (i) Cr2O3, 770K, 20 atm; (ii) CrO2Cl2/H3O+; (iii) NaOH; (iv) H3O+
(3) (i) CrO2Cl2/H3O+; (ii) Cr2O3, 770K, 20atm; (iii) NaOH; (iv) H3O+
(4) (i) Mo2O3; (ii) Mo2O3; (iii) NaOH; (iv) H3O+
Ans: (i) Cr2O3, 770 K, 20 atm; (ii) CrO2Cl2/H3O+; (iii) NaOH; (iv) H3O+
Ans_des: P (dialkylbenzene) -> Cr2O3/770K converts to toluene derivative -> CrO2Cl2/H3O+ oxidises CH3 to CHO (R after reduction) -> NaOH (Cannizzaro) gives PhCOO- and PhCH2OH -> H3O+ gives PhCOOH (Q).

subject: Chemistry
question:
Assertion A: Acetal/Ketal is stable in basic medium.
Reason R: The high leaving tendency of alkoxide ion gives the stability to acetal/ketal in basic medium.
(1) Both A and R true but R is NOT correct explanation
(2) Both A and R true and R is correct explanation
(3) A is false but R is true
(4) A is true but R is false
Ans: A is true but R is false
Ans_des: Acetal/Ketal are stable in basic medium (A is true). However, they are stable because alkoxide is a POOR leaving group (not a good one) in basic conditions. R is false.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Which of the following conformations of butane will be the most stable?
(1) Anti (Me and Me anti, H and H)
(2) Gauche-1
(3) Gauche-2
(4) Eclipsed
Ans: Anti conformation (methyl groups anti to each other, all H anti)
Ans_des: Stability order of butane: Anti > Gauche > Partially eclipsed > Fully eclipsed. The most stable conformation is the anti conformation (option 1).

subject: Chemistry
question:
The correct order in aqueous medium of basic strength in case of methyl substituted amines is:
(1) Me2NH > MeNH2 > Me3N > NH3
(2) Me3N > Me2NH > MeNH2 > NH3
(3) NH3 > Me3N > MeNH2 > Me2NH
(4) Me2NH > Me3N > MeNH2 > NH3
Ans_des: In aqueous medium, basicity depends on electron-donating effect, steric hindrance, and solvation. Me2NH is most basic, followed by MeNH2, then Me3N (steric hindrance reduces solvation), then NH3.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Compound A reacts with NH4Cl to form compound B. Compound B reacts with H2O and excess CO2 to form compound C which on passing through saturated NaCl solution forms sodium hydrogen carbonate. Compound A, B and C are respectively:
(1) Ca(OH)2, NH4+, (NH4)2CO3
(2) Ca(OH)2, NH3, NH4HCO3
(3) CaCl2, NH4+, (NH4)2CO3
(4) CaCl2, NH3, NH4HCO3
Ans_des: Ca(OH)2 + 2NH4Cl -> CaCl2 + 2NH3(B) + 2H2O. NH3 + H2O + CO2 (excess) -> NH4HCO3(C). NH4HCO3 + NaCl -> NH4Cl + NaHCO3. This is the Solvay process.
